## SEC-001. An empty AAD in SessionCipher — binding the header to the ciphertext (High)

**Component:** `crypto/session_cipher.rs:67,75`

**Vulnerability description:**

`SessionCipher::seal()` and `open()` call ChaCha20-Poly1305 with `aad: b""` (an empty
Associated Authenticated Data). Field `FrameHeader` (family, msg\_type, peer\_id) **not enabled
in authenticated data**, so AEAD tag protects only the body frame, but not its type.

```rust
// session_cipher.rs:67
.encrypt(&nonce, Payload { msg: plaintext, aad: b"" })
```

**Attack scenario:**

Active MITM between two sessions (for example, a compromised relay) can take
the encrypted the body frame type `APP_DATA` and place its in slot `ROUTE_ANNOUNCE` with the same
nonce counter. Tag will pass verification, and the deserializer the recipient handles data
in the wrong context (invalid type), that can lead to an incorrect state
routing or disclosure data on a partial decoding.

**Exploitation precondition:** control over relay--node or MitM on TCP-the connection between two
peers.

**Mitigation:** pass in `aad` at minimum `[family_byte, msg_type_u16_be]` from the header
frame, so that type was cryptographically bound to the ciphertext.

| # | Task | Status |
|---|--------|--------|
| SEC-001.1 | `SessionCipher::seal(plaintext, aad: &[u8])` — add a parameter AAD; form AAD as `family ‖ msg_type` (3 bytes) | ✅ done |
| SEC-001.2 | `SessionCipher::open(ciphertext, aad: &[u8])` — symmetrically | ✅ done |
| SEC-001.3 | Update all call-sites in `session/runner.rs`: pass the header in AAD | ✅ done |
| SEC-001.4 | Test: `seal` with `aad=[1,0,1]` not opens on `aad=[1,0,2]` (another msg_type) | ✅ done |

---

## SEC-002. Signature RouteAnnounce/RouteWithdraw not is verified for gossip hops (High)

**Component:** `node/dispatcher.rs:1232–1238`, `1279–1285`

**Vulnerability description:**

Verification signature RouteAnnounce and RouteWithdraw kicks in only when
`p.via_node_id == peer_id` (the sender — direct neighbor). If announcement arrived via relay
(`via_node_id != peer_id`), **signature not is verified at all**:

```rust
// dispatcher.rs:1232
if p.via_node_id == peer_id
 && !self.verify_routing_sig(&peer_id, &p.signable_bytes(), &p.signature)
{ … Violation }
// Otherwise — accept without verification.
```

**Attack scenario:**

A compromised relay forwards a forged `RouteAnnounce`, in which
`origin_node_id = victim`, `via_node_id = some-that another node` (not relay).
All recipients accept its without verification signature and add a record in RouteCache.
Traffic to the victim is redirected via relay-the attacker — a classic route hijack.

**Exploitation precondition:** control over although would one relay--node in the network.

**Mitigation:** on gossip hop (`via_node_id != peer_id`) needed either (a) verify
signature the originator over the cached pubkey if it known, or (b) reject
announcements from unknown originators, for which pubkey not yet cached.

| # | Task | Status |
|---|--------|--------|
| SEC-002.1 | `dispatcher.rs:dispatch_routing`: on `via_node_id != peer_id` — check signature the originator (`origin_node_id`) over `peer_pubkeys` if pubkey known; reject Violation on a mismatch | ✅ done |
| SEC-002.2 | On `origin_node_id` unknown (pubkey not in cache) — RouteAnnounce accept conditionally, **not** add in RouteCache before obtaining RouteResponse c signature from target | ✅ done |
| SEC-002.3 | Test: relay forwards a forged RouteAnnounce with `via != peer` — the recipient not adds a record in RouteCache | ✅ done |

---

## SEC-003. TOFU-attack on ML-KEM key in RouteResponse — compromise E2E encryption (High)

**Component:** `node/dispatcher.rs:1402–1425`

**Vulnerability description:**

On first receipt `RouteResponse` for an unfamiliar node (`key_known = false`) ML-KEM
public key is accepted and is cached **without verification signature**:

```rust
// dispatcher.rs:1417
if !key_known || sig_valid {
 // We cache mlkem_pubkey without verification when !key_known
 peer_mlkem_keys.insert(p.target_node_id, (ek.clone(), …));
}
```

**Attack scenario (Trust On First Use / TOFU):**

1. Alice sends `RouteRequest` to Bob.
2. Evil relay intercepts `RouteResponse` from Bob, substitutes `mlkem_pubkey` on its own.
3. Alice caches **key the attacker** under `bob_node_id` (pubkey Bob'and not yet known → no verification).
4. All subsequent E2E-messages Alice to Bob encrypted a key the attacker.
5. Attacker decrypts, reads plaintext, on if desired re-encrypts the real a key Bob'and.

Attack runs one once, after which E2E-channel compromised forever (before update the key).

**Exploitation precondition:** control over relay--node on the path RouteRequest/RouteResponse.

**Mitigation:** ML-KEM key accept only together with a valid signature from target\_node\_id.
On `!key_known` — defer caching before obtaining signature via direct handshake
(ML-KEM pubkey already in is enabled in `IdentityPayload` during handshake — use this path
as primary, RouteResponse — only as update).

| # | Task | Status |
|---|--------|--------|
| SEC-003.1 | `dispatcher.rs`: cache `mlkem_pubkey` from RouteResponse **only** on `sig_valid` (remove `!key_known` from the condition) | ✅ done |
| SEC-003.2 | On `!sig_valid && !key_known` — store pending key, confirm on a direct handshake or a repeated RouteResponse with signature | ✅ done |
| SEC-003.3 | Test: RouteResponse from unknown node with a substituted mlkem\_pubkey not is cached if signature is missing/is invalid | ✅ done |
| SEC-003.4 | Test: a legitimate RouteResponse with a valid signature caches mlkem\_pubkey correctly (regression) | ✅ done |

---

## SEC-004. PowChallenge is accepted without verification signature acceptor — CPU exhaustion (Medium)

**Component:** `node/dispatcher.rs:1449–1457`

**Vulnerability description:**

Having received `PowChallenge` with `requester_node_id == local_node_id`, node returns
`DispatchResult::SolvePow(p)` and launches an expensive PoW-solver **without verification signature
acceptor**:

```rust
// dispatcher.rs:1454–1456
if p.requester_node_id == self.local_node_id {
 return DispatchResult::SolvePow(p); // signature not is verified
}
```

Over protocol (TASKS.md Epic 61) `PowChallengePayload` contains `signature [u8;64]` from
the private the key acceptor. This signature not is verified before launch solver'and.

**Attack scenario:**

Any a connected peer sends stream fake `PowChallenge` with `difficulty=255`
and arbitrary signatures. Node-victim launches `spawn_blocking` for each,
that saturates the pool streams tokio and calls DoS.

**Exploitation precondition:** the presence at least one established session with the victim.

**Mitigation:** before `SolvePow` verify `p.signature` pubkey' `p.acceptor_node_id`
(must be known from handshake); invalid signature → Violation.
Add the global rate limit on incoming `PowChallenge` (not more N in second).

| # | Task | Status |
|---|--------|--------|
| SEC-004.1 | `dispatcher.rs:PowChallenge`: verify `p.signature` pubkey' `p.acceptor_node_id` before `SolvePow`; invalid → `Violation` | ✅ done |
| SEC-004.2 | Add rate limit on `PowChallenge` in `PerPeerLimiter`: not more `pow_challenge_per_sec` (default 1) | ✅ done |
| SEC-004.3 | Test: `PowChallenge` with invalid signature → `Violation`, solver not starts up | ✅ done |
| SEC-004.4 | Test: stream `PowChallenge` beyond limit → rate limit kicks in, the excess are dropped | ✅ done |

---

## SEC-005. TOCTOU on recreation UNIX-socket + error chmod is ignored (Low)

**Component:** `node/ipc/server.rs:293–312`

**Vulnerability description 1 - TOCTOU:**

```rust
fn prepare_socket(path: &Path) -> std::io::Result<()> {
 if path.exists() { // time-of-check
 std::fs::remove_file(path)?;
 }
 // ... gap ...
 let listener = UnixListener::bind(&self.socket_path)?; // time-of-use
```

Between `remove_file` and `bind` attacker (local user) can create file with the same
name, that will lead to failure bind or to that, that bind' to socket-the file the attacker.

**Vulnerability description 2 - chmod error ignored:**

```rust
let _ = std::fs::set_permissions(&self.socket_path, perms); // errors are ignored
```

Socket is created `UnixListener::bind()` with permissions umask process. If `set_permissions`
fails (nosuid FS, raceCon and ..), socket can remain world-accessible before the next
a restart.

**Exploitation precondition:** local user on the same machine.

**Mitigation 1:** remove TOCTOU — bind in a temp name, then atomic `rename()`.
**Mitigation 2:** return an error from `run()` if `set_permissions` failed, and not
continue with unsafe permissions.

| # | Task | Status |
|---|--------|--------|
| SEC-005.1 | `ipc/server.rs`: bind to `{path}.tmp`, then `std::fs::rename(tmp, path)` — atomic recreation without TOCTOU | ✅ done |
| SEC-005.2 | Remove `let _ = …`: if `set_permissions` returned an error — return `Err` from `run()`, not continue | ✅ done |
| SEC-005.3 | Test: `prepare_socket` correctly establishes permissions 0o600 and returns an error when the path is unavailable | ✅ done |

---

## Epic 75. Session Keepalive & Inactivity Timeout

**Problem:** `SessionRunner` — an infinite select without a timeout. A hung peer (not closed TCP, not responds) holds resources indefinitely. No OVL1-level heartbeat — cannot distinguish a live quiet session from dead peer.

**Deliverables:**
- Idle timeout in `SessionRunner`: if behind N seconds not arrived nor one bytes — close session
- OVL1-level keepalive: `ControlMsg::Keepalive` / `ControlMsg::KeepaliveAck` in `proto/family.rs`
- Sender loop in `SessionRunner`: once in `keepalive_interval` send Keepalive, wait Ack
- Config: `session.keepalive_interval_secs` (default 30), `session.idle_timeout_secs` (default 90)

**Done criteria:** Test: two node connected, one stops read socket — via `idle_timeout_secs` session closed the side the sender.

| # | Task | Status |
|---|--------|--------|
| 75.1 | Add `ControlMsg::Keepalive` / `KeepaliveAck` in `proto/family.rs` and handler in `dispatch_control` | ✅ done |
| 75.2 | Config: `session.keepalive_interval_secs`, `session.idle_timeout_secs` in `cfg/model.rs` | ✅ done |
| 75.3 | `SessionRunner`: add keepalive sender loop + idle deadline — close session on exceeding the limit | ✅ done |
| 75.4 | Test: peer stops respond → session is closed via `idle_timeout_secs` | ✅ done |

## Epic 76. Gateway Attachment Lifecycle: Keepalive & Reattach

**Problem:** Three related gap make attachment unreliable under churn:
1. Expired leases not are cleared — no background eviction task on gateway.
2. The leaf never not sends KEEPALIVE — lease silently goes stale on gateway.
3. On reconnecting the leaf not re-sends ATTACH — gateway its more not remembers, messages are lost.

**Deliverables:**
- A background eviction task on gateway: each 10s call `gateway_service.evict_expired()`
- Leaf keepalive sender: background loop, once in N seconds send `ControlMsg::Keepalive` to gateway-sessions
- Reattach on reconnect: `runtime.on_session_opened` for leaf-role sends ATTACH if peer — gateway
- Config: `gateway.attachment_lease_ttl_secs` (default 300), `gateway.keepalive_interval_secs` (default 60)

**Done criteria:** Test: the leaf connects to gateway, gateway goes down and comes back up again, the leaf automatically re-sends ATTACH — message via mailbox is delivered without manual intervention.

| # | Task | Status |
|---|--------|--------|
| 76.1 | `cfg/model.rs`: add `GatewayConfig { attachment_lease_ttl_secs, keepalive_interval_secs }` | ✅ done |
| 76.2 | `runtime.rs`: add a background eviction loop — `gateway_service.evict_expired()` each 10s | ✅ done |
| 76.3 | `session/runner.rs` or `runtime.rs`: leaf keepalive sender loop to gateway-peer | ✅ done |
| 76.4 | `runtime.rs`: in `on_session_opened` for leaf-role — if peer announced role Gateway, send ATTACH | ✅ done |
| 76.5 | Test: gateway eviction removes the stale lease after `attachment_lease_ttl_secs` | ✅ done |
| 76.6 | Test: the leaf reconnects to gateway and automatically re-sends ATTACH | ✅ done |

## Epic 79. Bootstrap with a known node_id

**Problem:** `bootstrap_peers` accepts only a URI without a node_id. The OVL1 handshake requires identity verification, so connecting to a bootstrap peer without a known node_id beforehand is impossible. A new node can join the network only through a static `peers` config.

**Solution:** Extend `bootstrap_peers` to a `BootstrapPeer { transport, node_id, public_key, nonce }` struct — the same information as in `PeerConfig`, but with "initial DHT bootstrap only" semantics. At startup the runtime connects to each bootstrap peer as a regular `PeerConfig`, runs `FIND_NODE(self)` after the session is established to populate the routing table, then closes the connection if the peer is not in the `peers` list.

**Done criteria:** Test: a node with an empty `peers` and a single `bootstrap_peers` starts, runs FIND_NODE through bootstrap, and receives a list of contacts in the DHT routing table.

| # | Task | Status |
|---|--------|--------|
| 79.1 | `cfg/model.rs`: replace `bootstrap_peers: Vec<String>` with `bootstrap_peers: Vec<BootstrapPeer>` where `BootstrapPeer { transport, node_id, public_key, nonce }` | ✅ done |
| 79.2 | `runtime.rs`: `spawn_bootstrap_task` — connect to each bootstrap peer as a temporary `PeerConfig`, run FIND_NODE(self), add the response to the DHT routing table | ✅ done |
| 79.3 | `runtime.rs`: after the bootstrap FIND_NODE — if the peer is not in `config.peers`, close the session (do not hold a connection for the sake of a single lookup) | ✅ done |
| 79.4 | Test: a bootstrap peer returns a list of contacts → they appear in the DHT routing table | ✅ done |

---

## Epic 80. Mailbox Replication via DHT

**Problem:** A mailbox is stored only on a single gateway. If the gateway goes down, all pending messages are lost. There is no mechanism to deliver offline messages when the primary gateway is unavailable.

**Solution:** On `MAILBOX_PUT` the gateway additionally stores the envelope in the DHT as `STORE(recipient_node_id, envelope)`. On `MAILBOX_FETCH` — if the local mailbox is empty, it runs `FIND_VALUE(recipient_node_id)` through the DHT. This gives eventual durability without full consensus replication.

**Limitations:** Only for small envelopes (≤ MAX_DHT_VALUE_BYTES). Large payloads require a separate mechanism. DHT TTL ≤ mailbox TTL.

**Done criteria:** Test: node A puts a message for B through gateway G1. G1 goes down. Node B does MAILBOX_FETCH through gateway G2 — G2 finds the envelope in the DHT and delivers it to B.

| # | Task | Status |
|---|--------|--------|
| 80.1 | `cfg/model.rs`: add `mailbox.dht_replication: bool` (default false) and `mailbox.max_dht_value_bytes: usize` (default 4096) | ✅ done |
| 80.2 | `dispatcher.rs`: on `MAILBOX_PUT` — if `dht_replication` is enabled and envelope ≤ max_dht_value_bytes, call `dht.store(recipient_id, envelope)` | ✅ done |
| 80.3 | `dispatcher.rs`: on `MAILBOX_FETCH` — if the local mailbox is empty, try `dht.find_value(recipient_id)` and return what is found | ✅ done |
| 80.4 | Test: PUT → gateway A stores in DHT → fetch through gateway B (no direct mailbox) → found through DHT | ✅ done |

---

## Epic 81. Partition Detection & Observability

**Problem:** A node does not know it is isolated from the network. ROUTE_REQUEST silently fails. There is no "I cannot see the network" metric. An operator cannot distinguish "network is healthy" from "node is isolated".

**Solution:** A counter of consecutive route misses without recovery + a `network_reachability_score` metric (0.0–1.0 = fraction of successful deliveries over the last N attempts). When score < threshold — log a warn "possible network partition".

**Done criteria:** Test: after N consecutive route misses without recovery, `network_reachability_score` drops below the threshold and a warn log is generated.

| # | Task | Status |
|---|--------|--------|
| 81.1 | `observability.rs`: add a `route_recovery_total` counter (successful route discovery after miss) | ✅ done |
| 81.2 | `runtime.rs` / `dispatcher.rs`: increment `route_recovery_total` on successful route discovery | ✅ done |
| 81.3 | `observability.rs`: compute and export `network_reachability_score` = recoveries / (recoveries + persistent_failures) over a sliding window | ✅ done |
| 81.4 | `runtime.rs`: when score < 0.2 (configurable threshold) — `logger.warn("network.partition_suspected", ...)` | ✅ done |
| 81.5 | Test: 5 route misses without recovery → score drops, warn is generated | ✅ done |

---

## Epic 82. Bootstrap: real connection and FIND_NODE over the network

**Problem:** `spawn_bootstrap_task` (Epic 79) only adds a contact to the local DHT without a real network connection. FIND_NODE(self) is not executed — the routing table is not populated with responses from the network.

**Solution:** Add bootstrap peers as temporary `PeerConfig`, wait for the session to be established, send FIND_NODE(self) through DHT RPC, add the received contacts to the routing table, close the session if the peer is not in the `peers` list.

**Done criteria:** Integration test: node A with an empty `peers` and bootstrap peer B starts, establishes a session with B, receives contacts from the FIND_NODE response, and they appear in the DHT routing table.

| # | Task | Status |
|---|--------|--------|
| 82.1 | `runtime.rs`: `spawn_bootstrap_task` — add bootstrap peers to a temporary `peers` config and call `spawn_outbound_peers` | ✅ done |
| 82.2 | `runtime.rs`: after the session with a bootstrap peer is established — send `FIND_NODE(local_node_id)` through the DHT | ✅ done |
| 82.3 | `runtime.rs`: handle the `FIND_NODE` response — add the received contacts to the DHT routing table | ✅ done |
| 82.4 | `runtime.rs`: after FIND_NODE — close the session with the bootstrap peer if it is not in `config.peers` | ✅ done |
| 82.5 | Test: `bootstrap_only_peer_registered_in_state` in `runtime.rs` — the bootstrap peer appears in state.peers with `bootstrap_only=true` | ✅ done |

## Epic 85. Config validation hardening

**Problem:** The config is only partially validated. `partition_score_threshold` may be > 1.0 or < 0.0. `keepalive_interval_secs` may be larger than `idle_timeout_secs`. `bootstrap_peers` with an invalid base64 pubkey are silently ignored.

**Solution:** Extend validation in `cfg/validate/` for the new fields from epics 75–81.

**Done criteria:** An invalid config returns a clear error, does not panic, and does not keep running.

| # | Task | Status |
|---|--------|--------|
| 85.1 | `cfg/validate/`: `partition_score_threshold` ∈ [0.0, 1.0] | ✅ done |
| 85.2 | `cfg/validate/`: `keepalive_interval_secs < idle_timeout_secs` | ✅ done |
| 85.3 | `cfg/validate/`: `bootstrap_peers[*].public_key` — valid base64 and 32 bytes long | ✅ done |
| 85.4 | `cfg/validate/`: `bootstrap_peers[*].transport` — parses as `TransportUri` | ✅ done |
| 85.5 | Tests: invalid values → clear errors | ✅ done |

## Epic 86. Multi-App Isolation: ephemeral bind mode

**Problem:** `app_id = BLAKE3(node_id || namespace || name)` is deterministic over
`(namespace, name)`. Two instances of the same application on one node get the
same `app_id` and conflict when binding the same `endpoint_id` →
`ALREADY_BOUND`. `client_token` is issued at `APP_HELLO_OK` but is used nowhere.

**Solution:** Add an `EPHEMERAL = 0x0001` bit to `AppBindPayload.flags`.
In ephemeral mode the IPC server mixes `client_token` into the derivation:
`app_id = BLAKE3(node_id || client_token || namespace || name)`.
Each connection gets a unique `client_token` → a unique `app_id` →
no conflicts without manual coordination.

**Named mode** (`flags=0`, current): behavior unchanged, one bind per
`(namespace, name, endpoint_id)` across the whole node — for services with a fixed address.
**Ephemeral mode** (`flags & EPHEMERAL`): multiple instances of the same application
coexist; the address does not survive a reconnect — for user applications.

**Done criteria:** Two IPC clients with the same `(namespace, name, endpoint_id)`
both bind successfully in ephemeral mode and get different `app_id` in `APP_BIND_OK`.

| # | Task | Status |
|---|--------|--------|
| 86.1 | `proto/ipc.rs`: add `pub mod ipc_bind_flags { pub const EPHEMERAL: u16 = 0x0001; }` | ✅ done |
| 86.2 | `node/app/address.rs`: add `pub fn ephemeral_app_id(node_id, client_token: &[u8;16], namespace, name) -> [u8;32]` — `BLAKE3(node_id \|\| client_token \|\| namespace \|\| name)` | ✅ done |
| 86.3 | `node/ipc/server.rs`: store `client_token: [u8;16]` in `IpcClientState`; pass it into `handle_bind` | ✅ done |
| 86.4 | `node/ipc/server.rs`: in `handle_bind` — if `flags & EPHEMERAL != 0`, use `ephemeral_app_id`; otherwise the current `app_id` | ✅ done |
| 86.5 | `veilclient`: `AppHandle::bind(ns, name, endpoint_id)` sends the `EPHEMERAL` flag by default; `AppHandle::bind_named(ns, name, endpoint_id)` — without the flag | ✅ done |
| 86.6 | Test: two IPC clients with the same `(namespace, name, endpoint_id)` in ephemeral mode → both bind, `app_id` differ | ✅ done |
| 86.7 | Test: named mode — a second bind of the same `(namespace, name, endpoint_id)` → `ALREADY_BOUND` (regression) | ✅ done |
| 86.8 | Test: ephemeral `app_id` changes on a new connection (different `client_token` → different `app_id`) | ✅ done |

---

## Epic 87. DELIVERY_FORWARD integrity

**Problem:** `DELIVERY_FORWARD` is vulnerable to replay attacks and does not check TTL
on the relay side. `content_id` is always `[0u8; 32]` — the relay cannot
deduplicate repeated frames.

**Solution:**
- Generate `content_id = BLAKE3(rand32 || sender_node_id || dst_node_id || payload)` on send.
- The relay checks TTL (`created_at + ttl_secs < now → drop`).
- The relay keeps a seen-set keyed by `content_id` (LRU 10k / 30s) — a repeated frame is dropped.
- `ipc_send_err::NO_E2E_KEY = 3` — a separate error code when the E2E key is unavailable.

| # | Task | Status |
|---|--------|--------|
| 87.1 | `ipc/server.rs`: generate `content_id = BLAKE3(rand32 \|\| sender \|\| dst \|\| payload)` in `handle_ipc_send` | ✅ done |
| 87.2 | `dispatcher.rs`: TTL check on the relay — drop if `now > created_at + ttl_secs` | ✅ done |
| 87.3 | `dispatcher.rs`: `ForwardSeenSet` (LRU 10k / 30s) — dedup by `content_id` | ✅ done |
| 87.4 | `proto/ipc.rs`: add `ipc_send_err::NO_E2E_KEY = 3`; `ipc/server.rs`: use it instead of `NO_ROUTE` on E2E errors | ✅ done |
| 87.5 | Test: the relay drops a replay (the same `content_id` twice) | ✅ done |
| 87.6 | Test: the relay drops an expired envelope (TTL expired) | ✅ done |

---

## Epic 88. IPC src_app_id validation

**Problem:** An IPC client can specify an arbitrary `src_app_id` in `APP_IPC_SEND`,
impersonating another application. The server does not verify that `src_app_id` belongs
to one of the registered endpoints of this connection.

**Solution:** Before handling `APP_IPC_SEND` — verify that `src_app_id`
matches one of the `app_id`s issued to the client via `APP_BIND_OK`.
On a mismatch — return an `ipc_send_err::SPOOFED_SRC = 4` error.

| # | Task | Status |
|---|--------|--------|
| 88.1 | `ipc/server.rs`: check `src_app_id` before `handle_ipc_send`; `IpcClientState::has_app_id()`; `ipc_send_err::SPOOFED_SRC = 4` | ✅ done |
| 88.2 | Test: `APP_IPC_SEND` with a foreign `src_app_id` → `SPOOFED_SRC` error | ✅ done |

---

## Epic 89. DoS / Security hardening

**Problem:** Several paths let a remote peer trigger OOM or
bypass protections.

**Vectors:**
- `MailboxFetchResponse::decode` — `Vec::with_capacity(count)` with `count = u32::MAX` → a 32 GB allocation.
- `runner.rs` — allocates `vec![0u8; body_len as usize]` without checking `body_len` first.
- `pending_responses` HashMap — unbounded growth on a fake `request_id` in FIND_NODE_RESPONSE.
- `DeliveryEnvelope` — no limit on `payload_len` and `ttl_secs`.
- `ForwardSeenSet` — a zero `content_id` is accepted instead of rejected.
- `lock!` macro — mutex poisoning is swallowed without a log, data may be incorrect.

| # | Task | Status |
|---|--------|--------|
| 89.1 | `proto/delivery.rs`: `MailboxFetchResponse::decode` — `count.min(MAX_FETCH_ENTRIES)` before `with_capacity`; constant `MAX_FETCH_ENTRIES = 1024` | ✅ done |
| 89.2 | `session/runner.rs`: check `body_len <= MAX_FRAME_BODY` before allocation; return an error if exceeded | ✅ done |
| 89.3 | `session/runner.rs`: `pending_responses` — bound the capacity (MAX=256), evict by age (>30s) | ✅ done |
| 89.4 | `proto/delivery.rs`: `DeliveryEnvelope::decode` — limit `payload_len <= MAX_ENVELOPE_PAYLOAD = 1 MiB` | ✅ done |
| 89.5 | `proto/delivery.rs`: on encode/decode of `DeliveryEnvelope` — clamp `ttl_secs <= MAX_TTL_SECS = 604_800 (7 days)` | ✅ done |
| 89.6 | `dispatcher.rs`: `ForwardSeenSet` — reject a zero `content_id` (`return NoResponse`) instead of skipping dedup | ✅ done |
| 89.7 | `lib.rs`: `lock!` macro — log mutex poisoning via `eprintln!` before `into_inner()` | ✅ done |
| 89.8 | Tests for 89.1–89.6 | ✅ done |

---

## Epic 92. Security (P0)

**Problem:** Four vulnerabilities of varying severity: unstable sorting
on a NaN score, no limit on the bind-payload size, a zero node_id in the
handshake, and a non-obvious lock acquisition order.

| # | Task | Status |
|---|--------|--------|
| 92.1 | `routing/cache.rs`: replace `f32` with `u32` for the `score` field in `RouteCacheEntry`; drop `partial_cmp().unwrap_or()` — `u32::cmp` is total | ✅ done |
| 92.2 | `proto/ipc.rs`: `AppBindPayload::decode` — add constant `MAX_BIND_NS_LEN / MAX_BIND_NAME_LEN = 255` and return `ProtoError::ValueTooLarge` when exceeded | ✅ done |
| 92.3 | `proto/ipc.rs`: `AppBindPayload::encode` — check `namespace.len() ≤ 65535` and `name.len() ≤ 65535`, replace the panic on violation with a `Result` | ✅ done |
| 92.4 | `node/handshake.rs` / `dispatcher.rs`: reject a peer with `node_id = [0u8;32]` as `Violation("zero node_id")` at the handshake stage | ✅ done |
| 92.5 | `dispatcher.rs` + `session/runner.rs`: add a header comment `// Lock order: session_tx_registry → route_cache → ban_list → violation_tracker` and a `debug_assert!` on order violation in dev builds | ✅ done |

## Epic 96. Security — resource limits (P1)

**Problem:** Three unbounded structures allow OOM/DoS from a remote peer:
`AppStreamTable` is unbounded neither globally nor per-peer; `send_window` can be
inflated to `u32::MAX`; `pow_pending` accumulates entries without cleanup.

| 96.2 | `node/app/streams.rs`: `apply_window_update` — add `MAX_SEND_WINDOW = 16 * 1024 * 1024 (16 MiB)`, clamp after saturating_add | ✅ done |
| 96.3 | `proto/budget.rs`: add `MAX_POW_PENDING = 256`; `dispatcher.rs`: `handle_route_request` — check the limit before inserting into `pow_pending` and evict the oldest entry (store `(requester, difficulty, Instant)`) | ✅ done |
| 96.4 | Tests for 96.1–96.3 | ✅ done |

## Epic 101. Protocol hardening

**Problem:** Residual technical debt at the protocol level: no forward secrecy
for session keys, node_id takes 32 bytes everywhere in gossip, mailbox ACK is one-by-one.

| # | Task | Status |
|---|--------|--------|
| 101.1 | `proto/session.rs`: SessionMsg::Rekey; session/runner.rs: rekey via ECDH on threshold | ✅ done |
| 101.2 | `proto/routing.rs` / `dispatcher.rs`: a short 8-byte `SessionAlias` for node_id within a single session — negotiated in the handshake, used in gossip frames instead of the 32-byte node_id | ✅ done |
| 101.3 | `proto/delivery.rs`: `MailboxAckPayload` → batch ACK: `recipient_node_id(32) + count(2) + seq[count](8*count)`; reduces overhead during mass delivery | ✅ done |
| 101.4 | Tests: rekey completes successfully, data after rekey is encrypted with the new key; batch ACK encodes/decodes correctly | ✅ done |

---

## Epic 102. Dispatcher decomposition

**Problem:** `dispatcher.rs` (~3500 lines) and `runtime.rs` (~2000 lines) violate SRP.
Each `dispatch_*` method is a separate logical unit, but they are all in one file.

| # | Task | Status |
|---|--------|--------|
| 102.1 | Move `dispatch_session` + related types into `node/dispatcher/session.rs` | ✅ done |
| 102.2 | Move `dispatch_delivery` + `relay_forward` into `node/dispatcher/delivery.rs` | ✅ done |
| 102.3 | Move `dispatch_routing` (RouteAnnounce/Withdraw/Request/Response/PoW) into `node/dispatcher/routing.rs` | ✅ done |
| 102.4 | Move `dispatch_discovery` + `dispatch_app` + `dispatch_diag` into `node/dispatcher/discovery.rs`, `app.rs`, `diag.rs` | ✅ done |
| 102.5 | `NodeRuntime` → split into `NodeRuntime` (lifecycle: start/stop/reload) + `NodeServices` (stateless Arc-handles to services) | ✅ done |
| 102.6 | `cargo build` + `cargo test` — no regressions | ✅ done |

---

## Epic 103. Fuzzing & property-based tests

**Problem:** The `proto/` decoders accept arbitrary external input and none of them
have been checked with a fuzzer. Property tests for data structures are absent.

| # | Task | Status |
|---|--------|--------|
| 103.1 | `fuzz/`: set up `cargo-fuzz`; add fuzz targets for `proto/session.rs`, `proto/delivery.rs`, `proto/routing.rs`, `proto/app.rs`, `proto/ipc.rs` — each `decode()` | ✅ done |
| 103.2 | `fuzz/`: a fuzz target for `SessionCipher::open` — arbitrary ciphertext must not panic | ✅ done |
| 103.3 | `node/routing/cache.rs`: proptest — `insert` + `lookup` invariants (a bucket does not grow > MAX_ROUTES_PER_DST, lookup always returns the best score) | ✅ done |
| 103.4 | `node/abuse/violation_tracker.rs`: proptest — size never exceeds MAX_VIOLATION_TRACKER_SIZE; `record` + `evict_stale` are idempotent | ✅ done |
| 103.5 | `node/app/streams.rs`: proptest — `open` + `close` never violate the per_peer counter invariant; `apply_window_update` never exceeds MAX_STREAM_SEND_WINDOW | ✅ done |
| 103.6 | CI: add `cargo test` for property tests to the regular test run; run fuzz targets separately (not in CI by default — too slow) | ✅ done |

---

## Epic 104. Performance & security hardening

**Problem:** Code analysis revealed specific bottlenecks: O(n) eviction on hot paths,
nested mutexes in relay-forward, TTL overflow allows eternal packets, double
envelope serialization, body allocation on every frame.

| # | Task | Status |
|---|--------|--------|
| 104.1 | `dispatcher/mod.rs`: extract a generic `ExpiryCache<K>` — eliminate the `RouteSeenSet`/`ForwardSeenSet` duplication; replace the O(n) LRU eviction with a `BinaryHeap` | ✅ done |
| 104.2 | `dispatcher/delivery.rs`: `relay_forward()` — copy the needed data from `route_cache`/`vivaldi` outside the locks beforehand; remove `sort_by_key` from the hot path | ✅ done |
| 104.3 | `dispatcher/delivery.rs`: eliminate the double envelope serialization on the DHT path; compute the size from the struct without `encode()` | ✅ done |
| 104.4 | `dispatcher/mod.rs`: in `dispatch()` check capture-subscriber activity before `body.to_vec()`; guard around the allocation | ✅ done |
| 104.5 | `dispatcher/delivery.rs`: add an explicit limit on `ttl_secs` when receiving an envelope (e.g. `MAX_ENVELOPE_TTL_SECS = 7 * 24 * 3600`); protection against TTL-saturation bypass | ✅ done |
| 104.6 | `node/routing/cache.rs`: per-source (via_node_id) rate limit on insertion — protection against cache-thrashing DoS; `MAX_ROUTES_PER_VIA = 256` | ✅ done |
| 104.7 | `dispatcher/delivery.rs`: add a log + metric on a decode/decrypt error of an E2E envelope instead of a silent `NoResponse` | ✅ done |
| 104.8 | `dispatcher/routing.rs`: document the scoring formula (the `10.0` coefficient, the `as u32` cast); add constants instead of magic numbers | ✅ done |
| 104.9 | Tests: `ttl_saturation_rejected`, `relay_forward_no_nested_locks` (verified via timeout), `expiry_cache_eviction_o1`, `e2e_error_logged` | ✅ done |

---

## Epic 105. NAT Traversal: from scaffold to production

**Problem:** Epic 32 laid the foundation (`nat/discovery.rs`, `nat/puncher.rs`, `nat/relay.rs`,
proto messages), but all the code is dead: the dispatcher replies `NoResponse` to all NAT messages,
`NatCoordinator` does not exist, `outbound_connector` does not start hole-punching when a
direct connection fails, and there is no `NatConfig` in the config. Most real nodes behind NAT cannot
connect directly without this layer.

**Goal:** fully wired NAT traversal: STUN-like external address → ICE candidate exchange →
UDP hole-punch (QUIC) → relay fallback through a core node.

| # | Task | Status |
|---|--------|--------|
| 105.1 | `cfg/model.rs`: add `NatConfig` — `enabled: bool`, `punch_timeout_ms: u64`, `stun_servers: Vec<String>`, `relay_enabled: bool`; integrate into `Config` and validation | ✅ done |
| 105.2 | `proto/control.rs`: extend `NatCandidate` with a `candidate_type: u8` field (0=host, 1=srflx, 2=relay) and `priority: u32` (ICE RFC 8445 §5.1.2 formula); update encode/decode/tests | ✅ done |
| 105.3 | `nat/puncher.rs`: sort candidates by `priority` before punch; add `CandidateList` with `add_host()`, `add_srflx()`, `add_relay()` helpers; update tests | ✅ done |
| 105.4 | `dispatcher/control.rs`: implement `handle_nat_probe_request()` — the core node replies with `NatProbeReply` containing the peer's observed `remote_addr` (server-side STUN) | ✅ done |
| 105.5 | `dispatcher/control.rs`: implement `handle_nat_relay_request()` — the core node registers a relay tunnel; traffic is proxied through `ForwardPayload` between two leaf nodes | ✅ done |
| 105.6 | `node/nat/coordinator.rs` (new file): `NatCoordinator` — async state machine: `Idle → Discovering → Exchanging → Punching → Relaying → Connected/Failed`; encapsulates the whole flow | ✅ done |
| 105.7 | `node/outbound_connector.rs`: on a `ConnectError` over the direct transport, start `NatCoordinator` as a fallback; pass `NatConfig` and the veil sender | ✅ done |
| 105.8 | `node/runtime.rs`: initialize `NatCoordinator` from `NatConfig`; pass it into `FrameDispatcher` and `outbound_connector` | ✅ done |
| 105.9 | Tests: `nat_probe_dispatched_and_replied`, `relay_tunnel_forwards_frames`, `ice_candidates_sorted_by_priority`, `punch_fallback_to_relay_on_timeout` | ✅ done |

---

## Epic 106. Security & correctness audit fixes

**Problem:** The code audit revealed security vulnerabilities, incorrect comments,
missing authorization, and resource leaks. This epic closes all the findings.

| # | Task | Status |
|---|--------|--------|
| 106.1 | `proto/budget.rs`: fix the `DELIVERY_ENVELOPE_HEADER` comment — add `src_app_id(32)` to the field list, change `148` → `180` | ✅ done |
| 106.2 | `dispatcher/control.rs`: `NatRelayRequest` — add an authorization check: reject if `peer_id != request.node_a && peer_id != request.node_b` | ✅ done |
| 106.3 | `dispatcher/routing.rs` + `dispatcher/mod.rs`: clean up `relay_tunnels` in `on_session_closed` — remove all tunnels where `node_a == peer` or `node_b == peer` | ✅ done |
| 106.4 | `dispatcher/routing.rs`: `verify_routing_sig` — replace the `bool` return with `enum SigResult { Valid, Invalid, UnknownKey }` to distinguish "signature is wrong" from "key is unknown" | ✅ done |
| 106.5 | `dispatcher/routing.rs`: `PowChallenge` — reject if `difficulty > MAX_POW_DIFFICULTY`; add `MAX_POW_DIFFICULTY = 24` in `proto/budget.rs` | ✅ done |
| 106.6 | `node/runtime.rs`: start a background `pow_pending` cleanup task on a timer (every `POW_CHALLENGE_TTL_SECS`), not only on the arrival of a new challenge | ✅ done |
| 106.7 | `dispatcher/routing.rs`: `peer_pubkeys` — on insert when the cache is full, evict the LRU entry instead of silently dropping the new one | ✅ done |
| 106.8 | `cargo build` + `cargo test` — no regressions | ✅ done |

---

## Epic 107. Security & correctness audit fixes (round 2)

**Problem:** The second code audit revealed vulnerabilities in the proto decoders, cryptography,
mailbox, DHT, and supporting code.

| # | Task | Status |
|---|--------|--------|
| 107.1 | `proto/*.rs`: replace all `.try_into().unwrap()` with `.ok_or(ProtoError::BufferTooShort{..})?` — 309 occurrences across 11 files; a panic on a malformed buffer → DoS | ✅ done |
| 107.2 | `node/session/handshake.rs`: replace `remote_confirm.mac != mac` with a constant-time comparison (`subtle::ConstantTimeEq`) — prevents a timing attack | ✅ done |
| 107.3 | `node/mailbox/service.rs`: in `handle_put` check `envelope.sender_node_id == sender_id` — otherwise an authenticated peer could forge the sender field | ✅ done |
| 107.4 | `node/dht/iterative.rs`: add `MAX_ROUNDS = 20` in `find_node_iterative` and `find_value_iterative` — without a limit an attacker keeps a lookup alive indefinitely | ✅ done |
| 107.5 | `node/ipc/server.rs`: apply `chmod 0o600` to the final socket path **after** `rename` (currently chmod is on `.tmp`, after rename the permissions are not guaranteed) | ✅ done |
| 107.6 | `dispatcher/routing.rs`: `routing.pow_accept` — log level `INFO` → `DEBUG` (reveals internal topology in prod logs) | ✅ done |
| 107.7 | `node/outbound_connector.rs`: jitter reconnect-backoff — replace `SystemTime::now().subsec_nanos()` with `rand_core::OsRng` | ✅ done |
| 107.8 | `cargo build` + `cargo test` — no regressions | ✅ done |

---

## Epic 108. Security & correctness audit fixes (round 3)

**Problem:** The third (agentic) audit revealed vulnerabilities in the proto encoders/decoders, the crypto module,
the app layer, config validation, and IPC. All findings were verified against the source code.

| # | Task | Priority | Status |
|---|--------|-----------|--------|
| 108.1 | `proto/routing.rs` + `proto/discovery.rs`: `as u8` / `as u16` on encode without a bounds check — if a list has > 255 elements, the count silently wraps → the receiver sees a wrong number and loses data.  Add `MAX_*` constants in `budget.rs` (MAX_TRANSPORT_ADDRS=32, MAX_RELAY_IDS=32, MAX_GATEWAYS=32, MAX_MAILBOXES=32) and return `ProtoError::ValueTooLarge` when exceeded. | HIGH | ✅ done |
| 108.2 | `proto/routing.rs` + `proto/discovery.rs`: on **decode** the maximum element count and string length are not checked — an attacker sends count=255 × len=255 → unbounded memory allocation.  Add `if count > MAX_* { return Err(ProtoError::ValueTooLarge{..}) }` and `if len > MAX_TRANSPORT_STR_LEN { … }` before `Vec::with_capacity`. | HIGH | ✅ done |
| 108.3 | `proto/routing.rs` + `proto/discovery.rs`: `String::from_utf8_lossy()` silently replaces invalid UTF-8 with `U+FFFD` — a protocol violation goes unrecorded.  Replace with `String::from_utf8(…).map_err(\|_\| ProtoError::InvalidUtf8)?` (add an `InvalidUtf8` variant to `ProtoError`). | MEDIUM | ✅ done |
| 108.4 | `crypto/signature.rs`: `decode_private_key()` returns a `Vec<u8>` with the private key without clearing it — the key stays in memory after use.  Change the return type to `zeroize::Zeroizing<Vec<u8>>` everywhere key material is returned or temporarily held. | HIGH | ✅ done |
| 108.5 | `crypto/session_kdf.rs`: `SessionKeys` derives `Clone` — cloning breaks the `ZeroizeOnDrop` guarantee (the clone lives independently).  Remove `#[derive(Clone)]` from `SessionKeys`; where shared access is needed, use `Arc<SessionKeys>`. | HIGH | ✅ done |
| 108.6 | `node/app/streams.rs`: in `replenish_recv_window()`, `recv_window` is not capped with `.min(MAX_STREAM_SEND_WINDOW)` — a peer can issue an endless stream of UPDATEs and inflate the window without limit.  Add `.min(MAX_STREAM_SEND_WINDOW)` when incrementing `recv_window`. | MEDIUM | ✅ done |
| 108.7 | `node/runtime.rs`: the check-and-increment of `sessions_per_ip` happens in two separate lock acquisitions → a race window under high load lets the `MAX_SESSIONS_PER_IP` limit be exceeded.  Combine the check and increment in one lock scope (entry API). | MEDIUM | ✅ done (was already implemented) |
| 108.8 | `node/ipc/server.rs`: `read_frame()` allocates `vec![0u8; header.body_len as usize]` from a wire value without checking `MAX_FRAME_BODY` — a local IPC client can trigger a 4 GB allocation.  Add `if header.body_len > MAX_FRAME_BODY { return Err(...) }` like the session runner. | MEDIUM | ✅ done (decode_header already does this check) |
| 108.9 | `cfg/validate/structural.rs`: timeout/interval config params are only checked from below — there are no upper bounds.  Add: `keepalive_interval_secs <= 3600`, `idle_timeout_secs <= 86400`, `punch_timeout_ms <= 30_000`. | MEDIUM | ✅ done |
| 108.10 | `node/app/registry.rs`: `send_to()` on `TrySendError::Full` / `TrySendError::Closed` does not log the lost message if `self.metrics.is_none()` — the losses are invisible.  Add `log::warn!` unconditionally on a drop. | LOW | ✅ done |
| 108.11 | `node/mesh/beacon.rs`: a `MeshBeaconPayload` decode error is silently ignored (the `if let Ok(..) = decode(..)` pattern).  Add an `else` with `log::debug!` to distinguish a "duplicate beacon" from a "malformed beacon". | LOW | ✅ done |
| 108.12 | `crypto/session_cipher.rs`: `CipherError::NonceOverflow` is neither logged nor counted as a metric — a cryptographic failure without observability.  Add `log::error!` / `self.logger.error` at the `NonceOverflow` handling points in `session/runner.rs`. | LOW | ✅ done |
| 108.13 | `cargo build` + `cargo test` — no regressions | — | ✅ done (858 passed) |

---

## Epic 109. Security & correctness audit fixes (round 4)

**Problem:** The fourth audit revealed new vulnerabilities: unbounded growth of the DHT store,
no limit on the number of relay tunnels, and an inconsistency in recv_window initialization.
`pow_pending` and `pending_diag` were checked — they already have limits (auditor false positives).

| # | Task | Priority | Status |
|---|--------|-----------|--------|
| 109.1 | `node/dht/kademlia.rs`: `handle_store()` and `store_local()` insert values into `self.inner.store` without checking the maximum number of entries — an attacker (or several peers within the 200 ops/min rate-limit each) can fill memory. Add `MAX_DHT_STORE_ENTRIES` in `budget.rs` and in `handle_store()` / `store_local()` when `store.len() >= MAX_DHT_STORE_ENTRIES` evict the LRU entry (oldest by `Instant`) before inserting. | HIGH | ✅ done |
| 109.2 | `node/dispatcher/control.rs:163`: `lock!(self.relay_tunnels).insert(...)` without a map-size check — an authenticated peer can send NatRelayRequests with different `session_token`s, filling the map. Add `MAX_RELAY_TUNNELS` in `budget.rs` and reject the request with `DispatchResult::Violation` when the limit is exceeded. | MEDIUM | ✅ done |
| 109.3 | `node/dispatcher/routing.rs:578`: `lock!(self.peer_observed_addrs).insert(...)` without a limit on the number of entries — a single peer is not inserted twice (since it is cleared on session close), but a defensive limit is absent. Add `MAX_PEER_OBSERVED_ADDRS` in `budget.rs` and skip insertion when the limit is reached. | LOW | ✅ done |
| 109.4 | `node/app/streams.rs:162`: `recv_window: initial_recv_window` is not capped by `MAX_STREAM_SEND_WINDOW` unlike `send_window` on line 161. This lets a peer declare a recv_window larger than allowed. Add `.min(MAX_STREAM_SEND_WINDOW)` like line 161. | LOW | ✅ done |
| 109.5 | `cargo build` + `cargo test` — no regressions | — | ✅ done (858 passed) |

---

## Epic 110. Security & correctness audit fixes (round 5)

**Problem:** The fifth audit (exhaustive, every .rs file) revealed new issues.
False positives were filtered out: ExpiryCache is correct (heap/set stay in sync),
DhtQuota has no race (called under a Mutex), IPC `read_frame` is protected via `decode_header`.

| # | Task | Priority | Status |
|---|--------|-----------|--------|
| 110.1 | `proto/mesh.rs:79`: `self.payload.len() as u16` without a check — if payload > 65,535 bytes, the length is silently truncated, the decoder reads fewer bytes than there are, and the tail is interpreted as the next frame. Add `debug_assert!(self.payload.len() <= u16::MAX as usize)` and return `ProtoError::ValueTooLarge` on violation (or limit it at construction time in `MeshFrame::new`). | HIGH | ✅ done |
| 110.2 | `node/mailbox/store.rs:74`: `queues.entry(recipient).or_default()` creates an entry for any `recipient_node_id` without limiting the number of unique recipients — an attacker sends MAILBOX_PUT with N different recipients, and the `queues` HashMap grows unbounded. Add `MAX_MAILBOX_RECIPIENTS` in `budget.rs` and reject `put()` when `queues.len() >= MAX_MAILBOX_RECIPIENTS && !queues.contains_key(recipient)`. | MEDIUM | ✅ done |
| 110.3 | `node/runtime.rs:2166`: `perform_ovl1_handshake(...)` is not wrapped in `tokio::time::timeout()` — an attacker opens a TCP connection, sends HELLO, and goes silent forever, occupying a session slot indefinitely (Slow Loris). With `MAX_SESSIONS_PER_IP` simultaneous such connections from one IP, a legitimate peer cannot connect. Wrap the call in `timeout(Duration::from_secs(HANDSHAKE_TIMEOUT_SECS), ...)`. | HIGH | ✅ done |
| 110.4 | `node/abuse/dht_quota.rs`: `DhtQuota::evict_stale()` is called nowhere in production code — the `entries` HashMap grows by one entry per unique peer that sends DHT requests and is never cleaned (entries live forever). Call `evict_stale()` periodically from the dispatcher (e.g. once every `DHT_QUOTA_WINDOW_SECS` seconds). | MEDIUM | ✅ done |
| 110.5 | `cargo build` + `cargo test` — no regressions | — | ✅ done |

---
## Epic 111. Automatic replacement of 0.0.0.0 in listen_transports with the external IP

**Problem:** The node binds to `tcp://0.0.0.0:7001` (all interfaces), but in `RouteResponse`
it advertises to peers the literal string `tcp://0.0.0.0:7001` — a non-routable address.
Other peers cannot connect automatically.

**Solution:** After receiving the first `NatProbeReply` from a core node containing an srflx candidate
with a public IP, update `listen_transports` — replace 0.0.0.0 / [::] with the discovered IP.

| # | Task | Priority | Status |
|---|--------|-----------|--------|
| 111.1 | `transport/uri.rs`: add `pub fn rewrite_wildcard_host(uri: &str, new_ip: IpAddr) -> Option<String>` — parses the URI, replaces the wildcard host (0.0.0.0 / `::`) with `new_ip`, returns `Some(new_uri)` if the host was wildcard, `None` otherwise | MEDIUM | ✅ done |
| 111.2 | `dispatcher/mod.rs`: change `listen_transports: Vec<String>` → `Arc<RwLock<Vec<String>>>`, add a method `update_wildcard_listen_addr(&self, ip: IpAddr)` — replaces wildcard hosts via `rewrite_wildcard_host`; update all 4 init sites (2 tests, 2 tests with direct assignment) | MEDIUM | ✅ done |
| 111.3 | `dispatcher/routing.rs`: update the 2 `listen_transports` read sites — use `.read().unwrap()` instead of direct access | MEDIUM | ✅ done |
| 111.4 | `runtime.rs`: change the 2 `listen_transports` construction sites — wrap in `Arc::new(RwLock::new(...))` | MEDIUM | ✅ done |
| 111.5 | `dispatcher/control.rs`: in the `NatProbeReply` handler — decode, find an srflx candidate with a non-empty, non-loopback, non-unspecified IP, call `self.update_wildcard_listen_addr(ip)` (once: do not overwrite if already replaced) | HIGH | ✅ done |
| 111.6 | Tests: unit test `rewrite_wildcard_host`; unit test `update_wildcard_listen_addr`; dispatcher test: `NatProbeReply` with an srflx candidate updates `listen_transports` | MEDIUM | ✅ done |
| 111.7 | `cargo build` + `cargo test` — no regressions | — | ✅ done |

---
## Epic 112. Windows compatibility: compilation under `x86_64-pc-windows-msvc`

**Problem:** The code builds only on Linux/macOS. The main blocker is that the Unix-socket API
(`tokio::net::{UnixListener, UnixStream}`, `std::os::unix::*`) is used without `#[cfg(unix)]`
in several key files. On Windows these types do not exist and the compiler fails.
Daemon/IPC will stay Unix-only, but the protocol core (TCP/TLS/QUIC, DHT, sessions, mailbox)
must compile and work on Windows.

| # | Task | Priority | Status |
|---|--------|-----------|--------|
| 112.1 | `transport/mod.rs:10`: add `#[cfg(unix)]` before `pub mod unix;` — otherwise the module compiles on all platforms and pulls in Unix-only types | HIGH | ✅ done |
| 112.2 | `transport/unix.rs`: add `#![cfg(unix)]` at the top of the file — the entire file uses `tokio::net::{UnixListener, UnixStream}`, which do not exist on Windows | HIGH | ✅ done |
| 112.3 | `transport/registry.rs:25`: wrap `registry.register(Arc::new(super::unix::UnixTransport))` in `#[cfg(unix)]` — a direct compilation blocker (the `with_defaults()` call runs on all platforms) | HIGH | ✅ done |
| 112.4 | `node/ipc/server.rs`: add `#![cfg(unix)]` at the top of the file — the entire IPC server is built on Unix sockets; on Windows this functionality is unavailable in principle | HIGH | ✅ done |
| 112.5 | `node/admin.rs`: add `#[cfg(unix)]` on the import `use tokio::net::{UnixListener, UnixStream}` (line 13) and on the functions `run_foreground()`, `set_socket_permissions()`, `peer_uid_matches()` — or add `#![cfg(unix)]` to the whole file and move the Windows stub to a separate `admin_stub.rs`; the Unix-dependent functions are called from `cmd/node_cmd.rs` through `#[cfg(unix)]` blocks, so a Windows stub is sufficient | HIGH | ✅ done |
| 112.6 | `cmd/node_cmd.rs`: make sure all `admin::run_foreground()` and `spawn_background_node()` calls are wrapped in `#[cfg(unix)]`; add a `#[cfg(not(unix))]` branch with a clear error "daemon mode is Unix-only" | MEDIUM | ✅ done |
| 112.7 | Check `node/ipc/` for other files with Unix dependencies (`client.rs`, `streams.rs`, `mod.rs`) — add `#[cfg(unix)]` where needed and make sure the public API does not export Unix-only types without a guard | MEDIUM | ✅ done |
| 112.8 | Add a CI step (`.github/workflows/ci.yml`) `cargo check --target x86_64-pc-windows-msvc` under `runs-on: ubuntu-latest` with `mingw-w64` installed or via `cross` — so regressions are caught automatically | MEDIUM | ✅ done |
| 112.9 | `cargo check --target x86_64-pc-windows-msvc` — make sure all blockers are removed and the code passes type-checking without errors | HIGH | ✅ done |
| 112.10 | `cargo test` on Linux — make sure the changes did not break existing tests | — | ✅ done |

---

## Epic 113. Introspection CLI: commands to inspect node state

**Goal:** Add CLI commands to view the internal state of a running node: metrics,
DHT, route table, discovery announcements, the list of nodes connected to a gateway.

All commands work through the admin Unix socket and return data synchronously.

| # | Task | Priority | Status |
|---|--------|-----------|--------|
| 113.1 | `node/admin.rs`: add `AdminCommand` variants (Metrics, DhtList, DhtRouting, DhtGet, DhtPut, DiscoveryList, GatewayList, Routes) and the corresponding `AdminResult` variants with types `AdminMetricsSnapshot`, `AdminDhtEntry`, `AdminDhtContact`, `AdminAttachmentEntry`, `AdminRouteEntry` | HIGH | ✅ done |
| 113.2 | `node/routing/cache.rs`: add a method `all_routes_with_score()` — returns `(dst, next_hop, score, hop_count)` for all live entries | HIGH | ✅ done |
| 113.3 | `node/discovery/directory.rs`: add a free function `all_attachments_alive()` — returns all live attachment entries | HIGH | ✅ done |
| 113.4 | `node/runtime.rs`: add introspection methods `metrics_snapshot()`, `dht_stored_entries()`, `dht_contacts()`, `dht_get_local()`, `dht_put_local()`, `discovery_all_attachments()`, `gateway_attached_nodes()`, `route_cache_all()` | HIGH | ✅ done |
| 113.5 | `node/admin.rs`: implement handlers in `execute_admin_command` for all 8 new command variants | HIGH | ✅ done |
| 113.6 | `cmd/cli.rs`: add `NodeCommand` variants (Metrics, DhtList, DhtRouting, DhtGet, DhtPut, DiscoveryList, GatewayList, Routes) | HIGH | ✅ done |
| 113.7 | `cmd/node_cmd.rs`: add dispatch and render functions for all the new commands | HIGH | ✅ done |
| 113.8 | `cargo build` + `cargo test` — verify everything compiles without errors | — | ✅ done |

### Commands (usage example)
```
veil-cli node metrics          # all metric counters
veil-cli node dht-list         # local DHT K/V store
veil-cli node dht-routing      # Kademlia contact table
veil-cli node dht-get <KEY>    # look up a key in the local DHT
veil-cli node dht-put <K> <V>  # direct write to the local DHT
veil-cli node discovery-list   # attachment entries in the discovery dir
veil-cli node gateway-list     # nodes connected to the gateway
veil-cli node routes           # route cache (dst, next-hop, score)
```

---

## Epic 114. DHT Name Namespace: record validation and node names

**Goal:** Prevent arbitrary garbage being written to the DHT; add a namespace for registering human-readable node names with PoW protection against squatting.

**Design:**
- Magic `[0x4E, 0x52]` ("NR") in the first 2 bytes of a DHT value — a name-record marker
- `NameRecord` — name, node_id, nonce (u64 LE), algo, public_key (raw), signature, expires_at, difficulty
- DHT key: `BLAKE3(name)` (32 bytes)
- PoW: `sign_input = name_bytes ‖ node_id ‖ nonce_le`, the signature is computed over it; `hash_input = sign_input ‖ signature`; `difficulty = leading_zero_bits(BLAKE3(hash_input))`
- Replacement: no record → accept; owner signature invalid → accept; otherwise challenger.difficulty > incumbent.difficulty → accept; equal difficulty → keep incumbent
- Validation in the `dispatcher/discovery.rs` Store handler: if magic matches → full check; otherwise accept (backward compat for mailbox)

| # | Task | Priority | Status |
|---|--------|-----------|--------|
| 114.1 | `proto/name.rs` — `NameRecord` struct, `NAME_RECORD_MAGIC`, encode/decode, `name_key()`, `signable_body()`, `difficulty_input()` | HIGH | ✅ done |
| 114.2 | `proto/mod.rs` — add `pub mod name` and re-export `NameRecord`, `NAME_RECORD_MAGIC`, `name_key` | HIGH | ✅ done |
| 114.3 | `node/name/validation.rs` — `validate_name_syntax`, `compute_difficulty`, `verify_name_record`, `can_replace`, `mine_nonce` | HIGH | ✅ done |
| 114.4 | `node/name/service.rs` — `NameService` wrapping `Arc<KademliaService>`: `store_name`, `lookup_name`, `all_local_names` | HIGH | ✅ done |
| 114.5 | `node/name/mod.rs` — pub use re-exports | MEDIUM | ✅ done |
| 114.6 | `dispatcher/discovery.rs` — in the `DiscoveryMsg::Store` handler: if magic matches → validate + can_replace | HIGH | ✅ done |
| 114.7 | `node/runtime.rs` — add `name: Arc<NameService>`, methods `name_store`, `name_lookup`, `name_list` | HIGH | ✅ done |
| 114.8 | `node/admin.rs` — add `AdminCommand::{NameCheck,NameNonce,NameClaim,NameRenew,NameList,NameLookup}` and `AdminNameEntry`, handlers | HIGH | ✅ done |
| 114.9 | `cmd/cli.rs` — add `NodeCommand::Name(NameArgs)` with subcommand `NameCommand::{Check,Nonce,Claim,Renew,List,Lookup}` | HIGH | ✅ done |
| 114.10 | `cmd/node_cmd.rs` — CLI handlers + render functions (inlined in node_cmd.rs by analogy with DHT) | HIGH | ✅ done |
| 114.11 | `cmd/mod.rs` + `run.rs` — no changes needed (wired via NodeCommand::Name in node_cmd.rs) | MEDIUM | ✅ done |
| 114.12 | Tests: `NameRecord` encode/decode roundtrip, validate_name_syntax, PoW difficulty, can_replace | HIGH | ✅ done |
| 114.13 | `cargo build` + `cargo test` — no regressions (881 passed, 1 pre-existing failure unrelated) | — | ✅ done |

### Commands (usage example)
```
veil-cli node name check <NAME>                                              # check name syntax
veil-cli node name nonce <NAME> --algo ed25519 --pubkey <B64> --privkey <B64> --difficulty <N>  # mine a nonce
veil-cli node name claim <NAME> --pubkey <B64> --privkey <B64> [--algo ed25519] [--difficulty 8] [--ttl 604800]  # claim a name
veil-cli node name renew <NAME> --pubkey <B64> --privkey <B64> [--ttl 604800]  # extend the TTL
veil-cli node name list                                                      # names on the current node
veil-cli node name lookup <NAME>                                             # find a record in the DHT
```

id        = 1
transport = "ws://127.0.0.1:7001/veil"

relay     = "base64NodeId32bytes=="

## Epic 117 — Configurable limits: move hardcoded limits into the config

**Goal:** Move all significant operational limits, timeouts, and buffers — currently baked in as `const` or inline literals — into `Config` structs with sane defaults. Existing code without a config behaves identically (defaults = old hardcodes).

**Principle:** Do not touch _protocol_ security limits (`budget.rs` — MAX_FRAME_BODY, TLV_MAX_ENTRY_VALUE, etc.) — they define protocol compatibility, not operational tuning. We only move _operational_ limits that an operator might want to change for their scenario.

**Config sections and affected fields:**

### 117-A: DHT (`[dht]` — `DhtConfig` already exists in model.rs)
Add fields to `DhtConfig`:
- `k` (u8, default 20) — k-bucket size, the Kademlia K parameter (`dht/routing.rs:K`, `dht/network_querier.rs:K_QUERY`)
- `alpha` (u8, default 3) — iterative-lookup parallelism (`dht/iterative.rs:ALPHA`)
- `max_rounds` (u8, default 20) — max iterative-lookup rounds (`dht/iterative.rs:MAX_ROUNDS`)
- `find_node_timeout_ms` (u64, default 2000) — timeout of one FIND_NODE/FIND_VALUE RPC (`dht/network_querier.rs:FIND_NODE_TIMEOUT`)

### 117-B: Session (`[session]` — `SessionConfig` already exists in model.rs)
Add fields to `SessionConfig`:
- `max_concurrent` (usize, default 1024) — max concurrent OVL1 sessions (`budget.rs:MAX_CONCURRENT_SESSIONS`)
- `max_per_ip` (usize, default 32) — max inbound sessions from one IP (`budget.rs:MAX_SESSIONS_PER_IP`)
- `max_pending_responses` (usize, default 256) — max pending RPC responses per session (`session/runner.rs:MAX_PENDING_RESPONSES`)
- `pending_response_ttl_ms` (u64, default 30000) — pending-response TTL (`session/runner.rs:PENDING_RESPONSE_TTL`)

### 117-C: Connections / reconnection (a new `[connection]` section or in `[global]`)
- `reconnect_backoff_min_ms` (u64, default 1000) — min reconnect delay (`outbound_connector.rs:BACKOFF_MIN`)
- `reconnect_backoff_max_ms` (u64, default 300000) — max reconnect delay (`outbound_connector.rs:BACKOFF_MAX`)

### 117-D: Routing (`[routing]` — `RoutingConfig` already exists in model.rs)
Add fields to `RoutingConfig`:
- `route_seen_capacity` (usize, default 4096) — size of the route dedup cache (`dispatcher/mod.rs:ROUTE_SEEN_CAPACITY`)
- `route_seen_window_secs` (u64, default 120) — dedup-cache window (`dispatcher/mod.rs:ROUTE_SEEN_WINDOW`)
- `max_gossip_hops` (u8, default 8) — max gossip-hop TTL (`dispatcher/routing.rs:MAX_GOSSIP_HOPS`)

### 117-E: PoW (`[pow]` — new, add it or put in `[global]`)
- `challenge_rate` (f64, default 1.0) — PoW challenge rate/s (`runtime.rs:POW_CHALLENGE_RATE`)
- `challenge_burst` (f64, default 2.0) — PoW challenge burst (`runtime.rs:POW_CHALLENGE_BURST`)
- `challenge_window_secs` (u64, default 300) — PoW rate-limiter window (`runtime.rs:POW_CHALLENGE_WINDOW`)

### 117-F: WebSocket transport (in `ListenConfig` or a separate section)
- `ws_bridge_buffer` (usize, default 65536) — WS bridge buffer (`transport/websocket.rs:WS_BRIDGE_BUFFER_SIZE`)
- `ws_message_queue` (usize, default 128) — WS message-queue depth (`transport/websocket.rs:WS_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE`)

### 117-G: Mailbox WAL (`MailboxConfig` already exists)
- `wal_compact_threshold` (usize, default 1000) — WAL compaction threshold (`mailbox/backend.rs:WAL_COMPACT_THRESHOLD`)

---

| #     | Task | Status |
|-------|--------|--------|
| 117.1 | `DhtConfig`: add `k`, `alpha`, `max_rounds`, `find_node_timeout_ms`; wire into `KademliaService` / `NetworkQuerier` / iterative walk | ✅ done |
| 117.2 | `SessionConfig`: add `max_concurrent`, `max_per_ip`, `max_pending_responses`, `pending_response_ttl_ms`; wire into the session runner and init | ✅ done |
| 117.3 | New `ConnectionConfig`: `reconnect_backoff_min_ms`, `reconnect_backoff_max_ms`; wire into `OutboundConnector` | ✅ done |
| 117.4 | `RoutingConfig`: add `route_seen_capacity`, `route_seen_window_secs`, `max_gossip_hops`; wire into `FrameDispatcher` | ✅ done |
| 117.5 | New `PowConfig`: `challenge_rate`, `challenge_burst`, `challenge_window_secs`; wire into the runtime | ✅ done |
| 117.6 | `ListenConfig` or a separate `TransportConfig`: `ws_bridge_buffer`, `ws_message_queue`; wire into the WS transport | ⏭ skip — requires transport-layer refactoring |
| 117.7 | `MailboxConfig`: add `wal_compact_threshold`; wire into the WAL backend | ✅ done |
| 117.8 | Update all formats (INI, TOML, JSON, YAML): serialize/deserialize the new fields with `#[serde(default)]` | ✅ done |
| 117.9 | Tests: default values match the old hardcodes; custom values are applied | ✅ done |

## Epic 119. Code audit 2: bugs, leaks, dead code (round 2)

**Goal:** fix the issues found in the second code audit (April 2026).

### Findings table

| File | Lines | Category | Problem | Severity |
|------|--------|-----------|----------|-------------|
| `node/dispatcher/discovery.rs` | 211-258 | Security | DHT STORE does not check the size of `payload.value` for non-NameRecord values. An attacker can store a value up to 16 MiB (MAX_FRAME_BODY), which at MAX_DHT_STORE_ENTRIES=65536 gives potentially 1 TiB of RSS | High |
| `node/session/quic_transport.rs` | 1-372 | Dead code | `QuicSessionTransport` is used nowhere outside `session/mod.rs`; 372 lines of a complete QUIC veil-multiplexer implementation (Epic 30) without consumers | Medium |
| `node/dht/kademlia.rs` | 62 | Dead code | `KademliaService::role` is marked `#[allow(dead_code)]`, read nowhere in the code; the field is used only as a constructor parameter | Low |
| `node/dispatcher/mod.rs` | 78 | Dead code | `CryptoContext::peer_mlkem_keys` is marked `#[allow(dead_code)]`, although the field is used in `routing.rs:394` — the attribute became stale after Epic 118 | Low |
| `node/dispatcher/mod.rs` | 106, 117 | Dead code | `DiagEvent` and `CaptureEvent` are marked `#[allow(dead_code)]`; both structs are used in `diag.rs` and `mod.rs` — the attributes are outdated | Low |
| `node/observability.rs` | 147, 205 | Dead code | `impl NodeLogger` and `impl NodeMetrics` are marked `#[allow(dead_code)]` — a blanket suppress over the whole impl block instead of targeted suppression | Low |
| `node/control.rs` | 30 | Dead code | `impl ControlPlaneService` is marked `#[allow(dead_code)]` — a whole impl block; the methods `new`, `with_rtt_table`, `handle_reply`, `rtt_ms` are used in the runtime | Low |
| `node/dht/kademlia.rs` | 151, 170, 184, 195, 204, 213, 218, 235, 240, 245, 251, 256 | Lock poisoning | 12+ `.lock().unwrap()` calls without mutex-poison handling | Medium |
| `node/discovery/service.rs` | 69, 74, 82, 90, 105, 112, 116, 121 | Lock poisoning | 8 `.lock().unwrap()` calls in `DiscoveryService` | Medium |
| `node/gateway/service.rs` | 80, 89, 105, 116, 122, 126, 130 | Lock poisoning | 7 `.lock().unwrap()` calls in `GatewayService` | Medium |
| `node/session/outbox.rs` | 46, 52, 66, 74 | Lock poisoning | 4 `.lock().unwrap()` calls in `SessionOutbox` | Medium |
| `node/mailbox/backend.rs` | 86, 100, 104, 108, 112, 116, 241, 264, 299 | Lock poisoning | 9 `.lock().unwrap()` calls in the mailbox backend | Medium |

| `node/mesh/link.rs` | 62, 72, 75, 80 | Lock poisoning | 4 `.lock().unwrap()` calls in MeshLink | Medium |
| `node/mesh/bridge.rs` | 91, 129, 133 | Lock poisoning | 3 `.lock().unwrap()` calls in MeshBridge | Medium |

| `node/session/runner.rs` | 886 | Lock poisoning | 1 `.lock().unwrap()` call when registering a relay tunnel | Medium |
| `node/dispatcher/routing.rs` | 232, 502 | Lock poisoning | 2 `.read().unwrap()` calls on `RwLock<listen_transports>` | Medium |

## Epic 120. Code audit 3: bugs, lock-poison, hot-path performance (round 3)

**Goal:** Fix a real TLV-encoding bug, close the missed `lock().unwrap()` from Epic 119, remove hot cloning in the broadcast path.

### Findings

| File | Lines | Category | Description | Severity |
|------|--------|-----------|----------|-------------|
| `proto/tlv.rs` | 23, 41 | **Bug** | `TLV_MAX_ENTRY_VALUE = 65_536 > u16::MAX`; `encode()` does `as u16` → wrap-around to 0 at len=65536, frame corruption | 🔴 HIGH |
| `node/ipc/streams.rs` | 74, 99, 117, 127, 135, 147 | Lock poisoning | 6 `.lock().unwrap()` calls in production code; the file was missed in Epic 119 | 🟠 MEDIUM |
| `node/runtime.rs` | 746, 761–770 | Lock poisoning | 8 `.lock().unwrap()` calls in `reload_config`; missed in Epic 119 | 🟠 MEDIUM |
| `node/session/tx_registry.rs` | 54–58, 76–82 | Performance | `bytes.clone()` for each peer in broadcast — N allocations per call | 🟠 MEDIUM |
| `node/ipc/streams.rs` | 103 | Bug (minor) | `data.len() as u32` — silent truncation at len > u32::MAX | 🟡 LOW |

### Plan

| # | Task | Status |
|---|--------|--------|
| 120.1 | `proto/budget.rs` — fix `TLV_MAX_ENTRY_VALUE`: `65_536` → `u16::MAX as usize` (65_535); add a `debug_assert` in `encode()` | ✅ done |
| 120.2 | `node/ipc/streams.rs` — replace 6 `.lock().unwrap()` calls with `lock!()` | ✅ done |
| 120.3 | `node/runtime.rs` — replace `.lock().unwrap()` in `reload_config` (~8 calls) with `lock!()` | ✅ done |
| 120.4 | `node/session/tx_registry.rs` — replace `PriorityFrame = (u8, Vec<u8>)` with `(u8, Arc<[u8]>)`; remove `clone()` in `send_to_all_with_priority` and `send_to_all_except` | ✅ done |
| 120.5 | `node/ipc/streams.rs:103` — `data.len() as u32` → `u32::try_from(data.len()).unwrap_or(u32::MAX)` | ✅ done |

## Epic 121. Code audit 4: assert-panic, silent truncation, O(N) eviction (round 4)

**Goal:** Replace `assert!` panics in constructors with `Result` returns, eliminate silent `as u32` truncations, document per-frame memory bounds.

### Findings

| File | Lines | Category | Description | Severity |
|------|--------|-----------|----------|-------------|
| `node/routing/score.rs` | 51 | Panic in production | `assert!(alpha > 0.0 && alpha <= 1.0)` — panic instead of `Result` | 🟠 MEDIUM |
| `node/abuse/rate_limiter.rs` | 34 | Panic in production | `assert!(capacity > 0.0 && refill_rate > 0.0)` — panic instead of `Result` | 🟠 MEDIUM |
| `node/dht/routing.rs` | 90 | Panic in production | `assert!(window_size > 0)` — panic instead of `Result` | 🟠 MEDIUM |
| `node/abuse/mailbox_quota.rs` | 22 | Panic in production | `assert!(max_per_sender > 0)` — panic instead of `Result` | 🟠 MEDIUM |
| `node/ipc/server.rs` | 407, 423, 176 | Silent truncation | `body.len() as u32` without a bounds check — silent truncation at len > 4 GiB | 🟠 MEDIUM |
| `crypto/pow/search.rs` | 81, 94 | Silent truncation | `thread_index as u32` / `threads as u32` — truncation at > 2³² threads | 🟡 LOW |
| `node/abuse/per_peer_limiter.rs` | 46–68 | O(N) eviction | `min_by_key` linear scan on eviction with thousands of peers | 🟡 LOW |

### Plan

| # | Task | Status |
|---|--------|--------|
| 121.1 | `node/routing/score.rs`, `node/abuse/rate_limiter.rs`, `node/abuse/replay_window.rs`, `node/abuse/mailbox_quota.rs` — replace `assert!` with `debug_assert!` (panic only in debug/test builds) | ✅ done |
| 121.2 | `node/ipc/server.rs` — `encode_ipc_frame`: `debug_assert!`; `write_frame_wh`/`write_frame_stream`: `u32::try_from(...)?` with `io::ErrorKind::InvalidInput` | ✅ done |
| 121.3 | `crypto/pow/search.rs` — add a `debug_assert!` guard that `params.threads` fits in `u32` | ✅ done |
| 121.4 | Add safety comments about the `MAX_FRAME_BODY` bound in `ipc/server.rs` (2 allocations) — `session/runner.rs` already had a comment | ✅ done |

## Epic 122. Code audit 5+6: unbounded maps, LIFO bugs, rekey/stream correctness (rounds 5–6)

**Goal:** Eliminate unbounded HashMap growth in the dispatcher, LIFO bugs in veilclient, config-validation errors, bugs in rekey and stream windows.

### Findings

| File | Lines | Category | Description | Severity |
|------|--------|-----------|----------|-------------|
| `veilclient/client.rs` | 226, 235, 260, 269 | Logic bug | `Vec::pop()` (LIFO) instead of `VecDeque::pop_front()` (FIFO) — responses are matched in reverse order | 🔴 HIGH |
| `veilclient/stream.rs` | 115, 140 | Data loss | `poll_write` returns `Ok(len)` without checking for a partial write | 🔴 HIGH |
| `node/ipc/server.rs` | 634–635 | Input validation | `from_utf8().unwrap_or("")` — invalid UTF-8 in namespace/name collides with an empty binding | 🔴 HIGH |
| `node/mailbox/store.rs` | 75–79, 100–104 | TOCTOU race | `contains_key()` + `len()` in separate calls under one lock | 🔴 HIGH |
| `node/mesh/neighbor.rs` | 51 | TOCTOU race | Likewise: contains_key and len checked separately | 🔴 HIGH |
| `node/dispatcher/mod.rs` | ~345 | Unbounded growth | `pending_diag` HashMap without TTL/eviction — DoS via ping flood | 🔴 HIGH |
| `node/dispatcher/mod.rs` | ~398 | Unbounded growth | `alias_registry` is never cleaned after sessions end | 🔴 HIGH |
| `node/dispatcher/mod.rs` | ~406 | Unbounded growth | `peer_observed_addrs` keeps addresses forever, no TTL | 🔴 HIGH |
| `node/session/runner.rs` | 313–332 | Logic bug | `pending_responses` evicts entries via `pop()` (LIFO) — starving legitimate RPCs | 🔴 HIGH |
| `node/session/runner.rs` | ~399 | Numeric edge case | `bytes_since_rekey.saturating_add()` freezes at `u64::MAX`, rekey does not fire | 🟠 MEDIUM |
| `node/app/streams.rs` | 162, 227 | Logic bug | `recv_window` is capped by `MAX_STREAM_SEND_WINDOW` instead of a recv constant | 🟠 MEDIUM |
| `node/dispatcher/routing.rs` | ~368–371 | Security | On cache overflow it evicts a random key via `keys().next()` instead of LRU | 🟠 MEDIUM |
| `node/dispatcher/routing.rs` | ~503 | Protocol | `listen_transports.first().unwrap_or_default()` — an empty string in PowAccept | 🟠 MEDIUM |
| `cfg/model.rs` | ~152–163 | Config validation | `cleanup_interval_secs=0` is accepted without an error — disables mailbox cleanup | 🟠 MEDIUM |
| `cfg/validate/structural.rs` | ~477 | Off-by-one | `ka >= idle_timeout` should be `ka > idle_timeout` | 🟡 LOW |
| `node/session/runner.rs` | ~545–546 | Crypto state | After `RekeyAck` it swaps ciphers without flushing in-flight frames of the old cipher | 🟡 LOW |

### Plan

| # | Task | Status |
|---|--------|--------|
| 122.1 | `veilclient/client.rs` + `handle.rs` — `Vec` → `VecDeque` + `pop_front()` for `pending_binds` and `pending_stream_opens` | ✅ done |
| 122.2 | `veilclient/stream.rs` — fix `poll_write`: add `write_pending: Vec<u8>`, a loop with drain + saving the tail on partial write / mutex contention | ✅ done |
| 122.3 | `node/ipc/server.rs:634` — replace `unwrap_or("")` with returning `APP_BIND_ERR(INVALID_REQUEST)` on invalid UTF-8 in namespace/name | ✅ done |
| 122.4 | `node/mailbox/store.rs` + `node/mesh/neighbor.rs` — **false positive**: both use `&mut self` / a single lock, TOCTOU is impossible | ⛔ rejected |
| 122.5 | `node/dispatcher/mod.rs` — **false positive**: `pending_diag` is bounded by `MAX_PENDING_DIAG`, `alias_registry` is cleared by `unregister_session_aliases`, `peer_observed_addrs` is cleared on close | ⛔ rejected |
| 122.6 | `node/session/runner.rs` — **false positive**: eviction is already FIFO via `min_by_key` on insertion time | ⛔ rejected |
| 122.7 | `node/session/runner.rs` — **false positive**: `saturating_add` to `u64::MAX` → immediate rekey trigger (threshold is always < MAX) | ⛔ rejected |
| 122.8 | `node/app/streams.rs` — **false positive**: `recv_window` is intentionally capped by the same constant by design | ⛔ rejected |
| 122.9 | `node/dispatcher/routing.rs` — add an empty-transport check before sending PowAccept | ✅ done |
| 122.10 | `cfg/validate/structural.rs` — add rules `mailbox_cleanup_interval_zero` and `dht_cleanup_interval_zero` (panic on `Duration::from_secs(0)` in a tokio interval) | ✅ done |

## Epic 123. Code audit 7: proxy/tun cleanup (round 7)

**Goal:** Eliminate orphaned spawn tasks in the proxy, break-on-error in the TUN device loop, a code smell in send_reply.

### Findings

| File | Lines | Category | Description | Severity |
|------|--------|-----------|----------|-------------|
| `node/proxy/exit.rs` | 115–125 | Async resource | `tokio::spawn` × 2 + `select!` — the second task keeps running unobserved | 🟡 LOW |
| `node/proxy/socks5.rs` | 185–199 | Async resource | The same pattern | 🟡 LOW |
| `node/tun/device.rs` | 95 | Error handling | `let _ = write_all(pkt).await` — TUN errors are silently ignored, the loop does not terminate | 🟡 LOW |
| `node/proxy/socks5.rs` | 210 | Code smell | `bnd_addr.parse().unwrap_or(UNSPECIFIED)` — a `&str` parameter instead of `Ipv4Addr` | 🟡 LOW |

### Plan

| # | Task | Status |
|---|--------|--------|
| 123.1 | `proxy/exit.rs` + `proxy/socks5.rs` — replace `tokio::spawn` × 2 + `select!` with direct futures in `select!` (the losing future is cancelled on drop, orphaned tasks eliminated) | ✅ done |
| 123.2 | `tun/device.rs:95` — on a `write_all` error in TUN: `break` out of the event loop | ✅ done |
| 123.3 | `proxy/socks5.rs:210` — parameter `bnd_addr: &str` → `Ipv4Addr`, removed `parse().unwrap_or(...)` | ✅ done |

---

## Epic 124. Code audit 9: TLV truncation, priority-queue deadlock, replay-window doc (round 9)

**Goal:** Eliminate silent truncation in TLV encode via public fields, the zero-weight deadlock in PriorityQueue, add a security comment to ReplayWindow.

### Findings

| File | Lines | Category | Description | Severity |
|------|--------|-----------|----------|-------------|
| `proto/tlv.rs` | 40–46 | Bug / silent truncation | `encode()` contains a `debug_assert!` + `as u16`, but the `TlvEntry`/`TlvBlock` fields are public — `push()` can be bypassed and produce silent truncation in release | 🟠 MEDIUM |
| `node/session/priority_queue.rs` | 42–48 | Bug / data loss | `PriorityQueue::new([0,0,0,0])` — pop() is always `None` with non-empty queues, frames are lost silently | 🟡 LOW |
| `node/abuse/replay_window.rs` | — | Doc / security | No comment on the minimum safe `window_size`; too small a window opens a replay attack after a nonce is evicted | 🟡 LOW |

### Plan

| # | Task | Status |
|---|--------|--------|
| 124.1 | `proto/tlv.rs` — replace `debug_assert!` + `as u16` in `encode()` with `u16::try_from(...).expect(...)` | ✅ done |
| 124.2 | `node/session/priority_queue.rs` — add a `debug_assert!` in `new()` that at least one weight > 0 | ✅ done |
| 124.3 | `node/abuse/replay_window.rs` — add a doc comment describing the `window_size` security trade-off | ✅ done |

## Epic 125. Code audit 10: debug_assert in encode, O(n) eviction (round 10)

**Goal:** Add `debug_assert!` before `as u16`/`as u8` in the encode functions of session.rs/ipc.rs; replace the O(8192) linear scan on eviction in PerPeerLimiter and BanList with O(1).

### Findings

| File | Lines | Category | Description | Severity |
|------|--------|-----------|----------|-------------|
| `proto/session.rs` | 90–91, 101, 246 | Code quality | `as u16`/`as u8` without a `debug_assert!` in encode — inconsistent with the ipc.rs pattern | 🟡 LOW |
| `proto/ipc.rs` | 120, 319 | Code quality | `detail.len() as u16` without a `debug_assert!` in `AppBindErrPayload`/`StreamOpenErrPayload` | 🟡 LOW |
| `node/abuse/per_peer_limiter.rs` | 54–56 | Performance | O(8192) `min_by_key` scan on every eviction under an IP flood | 🟡 LOW |
| `node/abuse/ban_list.rs` | 57–70 | Performance | A double O(8192) scan (find expired + min_by_key) on eviction | 🟡 LOW |

### Plan

| # | Task | Status |
|---|--------|--------|
| 125.1 | `proto/session.rs` — add a `debug_assert!` before `as u16`/`as u8` in `IdentityPayload::encode()` and `KeyAgreementPayload::encode()` | ✅ done |
| 125.2 | `proto/ipc.rs` — add a `debug_assert!` before `detail.len() as u16` in `AppIpcHelloErrPayload::encode()` and `AppBindErrPayload::encode()` | ✅ done |
| 125.3 | `node/abuse/per_peer_limiter.rs` — replace the O(n) `min_by_key` with `BTreeMap<u64, [u8;32]>` (seq-number index) for O(log n) eviction | ✅ done |
| 125.4 | `node/abuse/ban_list.rs` — a similar replacement of the double O(n) scan with a seq-number `BTreeMap` | ✅ done |

## Epic 126. Code audit 11: wrong error type, missing debug_assert, PoW mutex contention (round 11)

**Goal:** Fix the wrong error type in control.rs, add a `debug_assert!` in the delivery.rs encode function, optimize PoW `update_best` via an `AtomicU32` pre-check.

### Findings

| File | Lines | Category | Description | Severity |
|------|--------|-----------|----------|-------------|
| `proto/control.rs` | 221 | Bug | On an invalid `atyp` it returns `BufferTooShort` even though the buffer is of normal length — incorrect diagnostics | 🔴 HIGH |
| `proto/delivery.rs` | 300–301 | Code quality | `MailboxAckPayload::encode()` silently truncates seqs without a `debug_assert!` — inconsistent with the project pattern | 🟡 MED |
| `crypto/pow/state.rs` | 74–77 | Performance | The `Mutex` is taken on every PoW candidate, not only on improvement — extra contention during a multi-thread search | 🟡 MED |

### Plan

| # | Task | Status |
|---|--------|--------|
| 126.1 | `proto/control.rs:221` — replace `BufferTooShort` with `ValueTooLarge` for an invalid `atyp` | ✅ done |
| 126.2 | `proto/delivery.rs:300` — add a `debug_assert!` before `.min()` in `MailboxAckPayload::encode()` | ✅ done |
| 126.3 | `crypto/pow/state.rs` — add an `AtomicU32 best_zero_bits` for a lock-free check, take the mutex only on a real update | ✅ done |

## Epic 127. External audit: quick fixes (bugs and incorrect semantics)

**Goal:** Fix the confirmed functional bugs from the external audit: a wrong error type in the cipher, a WAL write-ordering violation, IPv6 in URIs, blocking code in an async context, a wrong `algo` when computing a peer node_id, a wrong `is_tx` for the rx-cipher.

### Findings

| File | Lines | Category | Description | Severity |
|------|--------|-----------|----------|-------------|
| `crypto/session_cipher.rs` | 82 | Bug | `seal()` returns `CipherError::DecryptFailed` on an encrypt operation — incorrect semantics | 🟡 MED |
| `node/session/runner.rs` | 545–546, 565–566 | Bug | `rx_cipher = SessionCipher::new(&rx_key, true)` — the rx-cipher is created with `is_tx=true`, both directions use the same salt | 🟡 MED |
| `node/mailbox/backend.rs` | 307–312 | Bug | `WalBackend::put()` writes to memory BEFORE the WAL — a WAL-semantics violation; the docstring claims the opposite | 🟠 HIGH |
| `node/runtime.rs` | 383–448, 695–730 | Bug | On a WAL/RocksDB backend error — a silent fallback to `InMemoryBackend` without warning the operator (only a warn log) | 🟠 HIGH |
| `transport/uri.rs` | 239–278, 317–325 | Bug | `Display` for TCP/TLS/QUIC does not wrap IPv6 addresses in `[...]` → invalid URIs; `is_wildcard_host` does not handle `"::"` | 🟠 HIGH |
| `node/runtime.rs` | 1789–1806 | Bug | `mine_nonce()` is called synchronously inside `tokio::spawn` — blocks a worker thread without `spawn_blocking` | 🟡 MED |
| `node/session/runner.rs` | 329–330 | Code quality | Eviction from `pending_responses` sends `vec![]` to the oneshot — an empty Vec is indistinguishable from a valid empty response | 🟡 MED |
| `node/runtime.rs` | 2283 | Bug | `build_state()`: `NodeId::from_public_key(identity.algo, &peer.public_key)` uses the local `algo` instead of the peer's algorithm | 🟡 MED |

### Plan

| # | Task | Status |
|---|--------|--------|
| 127.1 | `crypto/session_cipher.rs` — add `CipherError::EncryptFailed`, `seal()` returns it instead of `DecryptFailed` | ✅ done |
| 127.2 | `node/session/runner.rs` — ~~fix is_tx for rx_cipher~~ **FALSE BUG**: the test `tx_and_rx_ciphers_are_compatible` explicitly requires the same flag; direction is determined by different keys (tx_key vs rx_key), not the salt. Only rename the API parameter to `same_dir` or `dir_index`. | ✅ done (false positive, no change required) |
| 127.3 | `transport/uri.rs` — fix `Display` for IPv6 (add `[...]`), extend `is_wildcard_host` to `"::"` | ✅ done |
| 127.4 | `node/runtime.rs` — move `mine_nonce()` to `tokio::task::spawn_blocking` | ✅ done |
| 127.5 | `node/mailbox/backend.rs` — fix the WAL order: first `append_put()`, then `inner.put()`, fix the docstring | ✅ done |
| 127.6 | `node/runtime.rs` — add a config option `mailbox.strict_backend` (bool): when `true` — panic on backend failure instead of fallback | ✅ done |
| 127.7 | `node/session/runner.rs` — replace `tx.send(vec![])` on eviction with a typed error via a separate Result type | ✅ done |
| 127.8 | `node/runtime.rs:2283` — store `algo` in `PeerConfigEntry`, use it in `from_public_key` | ✅ done |
