## Epic 128. External audit: bounded queues and backpressure

**Goal:** Eliminate the risk of unbounded memory growth from the unbounded channels in `SessionOutbox` and `SessionTxRegistry`.

### Findings

| File | Lines | Category | Description | Severity |
|------|--------|-----------|----------|-------------|
| `node/session/outbox.rs` | 35, 45 | Memory | `UnboundedSender<OutboxRequest>` — no limit; a slow peer inflates the queue | 🔴 CRITICAL |
| `node/session/tx_registry.rs` | 34, 47 | Memory | `UnboundedSender<PriorityFrame>` — no limit; broadcast under a flood is unbounded | 🔴 CRITICAL |

### Plan

| # | Task | Status |
|---|--------|--------|
| 128.1 | `node/session/outbox.rs` — replace `unbounded_channel` with `bounded_channel` with a configurable capacity; on overflow — drop oldest or close session | ✅ done |
| 128.2 | `node/session/tx_registry.rs` — likewise for `PriorityFrame`; define an overflow policy by priority (drop BACKGROUND first) | ✅ done |
| 128.3 | Add metrics `session.tx_queue_depth` and `session.outbox_depth` for monitoring | ✅ done |

## Epic 129. Code audit: PoW fixes — correctness and performance

**Goal:** Fix bugs in the PoW search: a global interrupt flag, a silent timeout, key decoding on every iteration, fragile string matching.

### Findings

| File | Lines | Category | Description | Severity |
|------|--------|-----------|----------|-------------|
| `crypto/pow/search.rs` | 67–68 | Bug | The global interrupt flag is reset to `false` on every `search_nonce()`: if Search A is running, the user presses Ctrl-C, then Search B starts — it resets the flag and Search A ignores the interrupt | 🔴 HIGH |
| `crypto/pow/state.rs` | 43–55 | Bug | If the initial nonce (nonce=0) already has ≥ target zero bits, `update_best()` is never called for it (fast-path: `score <= best_zero_bits`), `stop` is not set, the search runs until timeout | 🟠 MED |
| `crypto/pow/score.rs` | 26 | Performance | `decode_public_key()` (base64 + allocation) is called on every PoW iteration — the main cause of timeouts on loaded machines | 🟠 MED |
| `crypto/pow/interrupt.rs` | 21 | Code quality | Duplicate registration is detected via `err.to_string().contains("already registered")` — fragile string matching, breaks when the `ctrlc` crate is updated | 🟡 LOW |

### Plan

| # | Task | Status |
|---|--------|--------|
| 129.1 | `crypto/pow/search.rs` — remove `interrupted.store(false, ...)` from `search_nonce`; add a separate pub function `reset_interrupt_flag()` called only from the CLI | ✅ done |
| 129.2 | `crypto/pow/state.rs` — in `PowSharedState::new()` check `initial_score.zero_bits >= target_zero_bits` and set `stop=true` immediately | ✅ done |
| 129.3 | `crypto/pow/score.rs` + `search.rs` — add `pow_score_raw` without base64; decode the keys once before the loop in `search_nonce` | ✅ done |
| 129.4 | `crypto/pow/interrupt.rs` — use `std::sync::OnceLock` + a successful-registration flag instead of string matching | ✅ done |

---

## Epic 130. Code audit: routing cache and session handshake fixes

**Goal:** Fix the false LRU in the route cache, the wrong fallback nonce in the handshake, optimize the dispatcher hot path.

### Findings

| File | Lines | Category | Description | Severity |
|------|--------|-----------|----------|-------------|
| `node/routing/cache.rs` | 156–159 | Bug | LRU eviction uses the `.first()` entry of the bucket (sorted by score), not by last-use time — it evicts the bucket with the best route, not the oldest one | 🟠 MED |
| `node/session/handshake.rs` | 290–291 | Bug | If the remote nonce is not valid UTF-8, a silent fallback: the nonce field becomes base64 of the raw bytes instead of rejecting the connection | 🟠 MED |
| `node/dispatcher/delivery.rs` | 265–290 | Performance | 3–4 consecutive mutex lock/unlock per relay frame (route_cache, rtt_table, control_plane) — contention under high traffic | 🟡 LOW |

### Plan

| # | Task | Status |
|---|--------|--------|
| 130.1 | `node/routing/cache.rs` — use `max(last_used)` over all bucket entries instead of `first().last_used` | ✅ done |
| 130.2 | `node/session/handshake.rs:290` — replace `unwrap_or_else` with an explicit `return Err(...)` on a non-UTF-8 nonce | ✅ done |
| 130.3 | `node/dispatcher/delivery.rs` — combine the route_cache + rtt_table lookup into one lock or use `DashMap` for lock-free reads | ✅ done |

---

## Epic 131. Code audit: session runner refactoring and memory guard

**Goal:** Simplify the 8-way `await_next_input`, bound the size of `pending_responses`, add access to the error reason in `SessionPhase::Failed`.

### Findings

| File | Lines | Category | Description | Severity |
|------|--------|-----------|----------|-------------|
| `node/session/runner.rs` | 103–190 | Maintainability | 8-way `match (outbox, rpc_outbox, sleep_until)` → 8 identical `tokio::select!` blocks; a timeout change requires editing 8 places | 🟠 MED |
| `node/session/runner.rs` | 256–257 | Memory | `pending_responses: HashMap` can grow when there are no responses, the TTL cleanup runs only on incoming traffic | 🟡 LOW |
| `node/session/fsm.rs` | ~45 | Dead code | `SessionPhase::Failed(String)` stores the error reason, but there is no method to extract it — the field is unused | 🟡 LOW |
| `proto/codec.rs` | 8 | Memory | `MAX_FRAME_BODY = 16 MB` — each frame can allocate 16 MB; with several hundred sessions OOM is possible | 🟠 MED |

### Plan

| # | Task | Status |
|---|--------|--------|
| 131.1 | `node/session/runner.rs` — refactor `await_next_input`: a single function with `tokio::select!` using `if let Some` guards instead of 8 match-arms | ✅ done |
| 131.2 | `node/session/runner.rs` — add periodic TTL cleanup of `pending_responses` on a timer (not only on incoming traffic) | ✅ done |
| 131.3 | `node/session/fsm.rs` — add `fn failed_reason(&self) -> Option<&str>` and use it when logging session death | ✅ done |
| 131.4 | `proto/codec.rs` — add a per-session `max_frame_body` (configurable via `SessionConfig`), default 1 MB; 16 MB as the absolute hard ceiling | ✅ done |

## Epic 132. Code audit: dead code — mesh backend stubs and BLE seq overflow

**Goal:** Fix the seq overflow bug in BLE, move the architectural stubs outside the main crate.

### Findings

| File | Lines | Category | Description | Severity |
|------|--------|-----------|----------|-------------|
| `node/mesh/ble_backend.rs` | 61 | Bug | `i as u16` — for a message > 1,310,700 bytes (65535 fragments × 20 bytes) the sequence number wraps; reassemble treats fragments as duplicates and returns `None` | 🟠 MED |
| `node/mesh/wifi_direct_backend.rs` | 1–108 | Dead code | An architectural stub with no transport implementation; no `LocalTransport` impl; requires platform APIs | 🟡 LOW |
| `node/mesh/ble_backend.rs` | 1–127 | Dead code | An architectural stub; only fragment/reassemble utilities; no GATT/scan/connect | 🟡 LOW |
| `transport/external_quic.rs` | whole file | Dead code | The `quic-session` feature is marked `not yet wired into the runtime`; the file compiles but is not connected to anything | 🟡 LOW |

| File | Lines | Category | Description | Severity |
|------|--------|-----------|----------|-------------|
| `proto/name.rs` | ~141–203 | Panic | `try_into().unwrap()` in `NameRecord::decode()` when parsing incoming wire data | 🔴 HIGH |

| `node/session/runner.rs` | ~542 | Panic | `unreachable!()` in the FSM `RekeyState::Idle` branch — on a state race it panics instead of closing the session | 🔴 HIGH |
| `proto/tlv.rs` | ~47 | Panic | `.expect("TLV entry value exceeds u16::MAX bytes")` — panic on internal misuse instead of a `Result` | 🟠 MED |

## Epic 134. Code audit: memory leaks — unbounded collections

**Goal:** Bound the growth of unbounded HashMaps/collections, add eviction policies.

### Findings

| File | Lines | Category | Description | Severity |
|------|--------|-----------|----------|-------------|
| `node/dispatcher/mod.rs` | ~676–718 | Memory | 6 unbounded `HashMap`s without TTL/eviction: `peer_pubkeys`, `pow_pending`, `peer_vivaldi`, `alias_registry`, `peer_observed_addrs`, `relay_tunnels` | 🟠 MED |
| `node/session/runner.rs` | ~256 | Memory | `pending_responses: HashMap` without a hard cap — grows unbounded under an RPC flood | 🟠 MED |
| `transport/quic.rs` | ~602 | Memory | `Arc<Mutex<HashMap>>` of QUIC sessions without eviction — accumulates on short connections | 🟠 MED |
| `tls_terminator.rs` | ~494–502 | Memory | Two `tokio::spawn` for child-process I/O without a timeout — block forever on a hung child | 🟠 MED |

### Plan

| # | Task | Status |
|---|--------|--------|
| 134.1 | `node/session/runner.rs` — add `MAX_PENDING_RESPONSES = 256`, evict the oldest entries when exceeded | ✅ done (131.2) |
| 134.2 | `node/dispatcher/mod.rs` — `pow_pending`: reuse `ExpiryCache`, add TTL eviction | N/A (already implemented: retain + MAX_POW_PENDING in routing.rs) |
| 134.3 | `node/dispatcher/mod.rs` — `peer_vivaldi`, `peer_pubkeys`: add an LRU cap | ✅ done (`peer_pubkeys` already had a cap; added `MAX_PEER_VIVALDI_CACHE=32768` for `peer_vivaldi`) |
| 134.4 | `tls_terminator.rs` — wrap both spawns in `tokio::time::timeout(Duration::from_secs(60), ...)` | N/A (already has a shutdown_tx/drop mechanism; a timeout on io::copy would break long-lived connections) |

## Epic 135. Code audit: performance and dead code

**Goal:** Remove busy-wait loops, add comments to stubs, remove `#[allow(dead_code)]` where possible.

### Findings

| File | Lines | Category | Description | Severity |
|------|--------|-----------|----------|-------------|
| `cmd/node_cmd.rs` | ~625–643 | Perf | A busy-wait loop without sleep while waiting for the daemon process to start | 🟠 MED |
| `node/dispatcher/mod.rs` | ~182–189 | Perf | O(n) eviction drain in `ExpiryCache::insert` — noticeable delays with >10K entries | 🟠 MED |
| `node/observability.rs` | ~295–410 | Dead code | 6× `#[allow(dead_code)]` on telemetry fields | 🟡 LOW |
| `node/e2e.rs` | ~55,85,169 | Dead code | 3× `#[allow(dead_code)]` on crypto fields — a refactoring leftover | 🟡 LOW |
| `node/dispatcher/mod.rs` | ~108–115 | Dead code | `PingDiagnostic`/`TraceHop` structs — Epic 63 diagnostics without use | 🟡 LOW |
| `sim/loss.rs` | ~70 | Perf | `&params as *const _ as u64` as an RNG seed — a stack address, non-portable | 🟡 LOW |

### Plan

## Epic 136. Load-aware routing and admission control

**Goal:** An overloaded node stops receiving new traffic. Implemented in three layers:
1. **Hard admission control** — the node itself refuses relay sessions when it hits the cap and withdraws its routes
2. **Congestion score** — `max(relay_ratio, queue_ratio)` with hysteresis; carried in ROUTE_REPLY
3. **Weighted random selection** — instead of a deterministic "best hop", a probabilistic choice among the top-N

**Why max(), not product():**
`max(0.5, 0.5) = 0.5` — correctly reflects the load.
`product(0.5, 0.5) = 0.25` — underestimates by half under uniform load.
`max(1.0, 0.1) = 1.0` — the bottleneck is visible immediately.

**Why weighted random, not "always best":**
A deterministic choice → all senders pick one node → oscillation.
Weighted random → the load naturally spreads across comparable candidates.

### Data schema

```
NodeCapacityConfig          CongestionMonitor              RttProbe
─────────────────           ─────────────────              ────────
max_relay_sessions    ───▶  relay_ratio: f64    ──max()──▶ congestion: u8  (0–255)
max_total_sessions    ───▶  session_ratio: f64   │         rtt_ms: u32
tx_queue_high_wm      ───▶  queue_ratio: f64  ───┘
                            score: f64  (hysteresis 80%↑ / 60%↓)
                            congested: bool
```

```
ROUTE_REPLY
──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
RouteReplyPayload + congestion: u8   ──▶  recorded in the neighbor's RttProbe
```

```
relay_forward (delivery.rs)
──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
candidates = route_cache.lookup_all(dst)
for each candidate: effective_score = rtt_ms × (1 + K × peer_congestion)
weighted_random(candidates, weight = 1/effective_score)   ← not sort, a probabilistic choice
```

### Admission control flow

```
on_session_opened (relay) ──▶ CongestionMonitor.relay_count++
                          ──▶ if relay_count >= max_relay_sessions:
                                  send ROUTE_WITHDRAW for all routes through self
                                  set the flag refuse_new_relay = true

incoming relay ATTACH     ──▶ if refuse_new_relay: return SessionError::CapacityExceeded

on_session_closed (relay) ──▶ CongestionMonitor.relay_count--
                          ──▶ if relay_count < soft_cap (60% of max):
                                  clear refuse_new_relay
                                  re-announce routes through self
```

### Plan

| # | Task | File | Status |
|---|--------|------|--------|

| 136.1 | `cfg/model.rs` — `NodeCapacityConfig`, add it to `Config` as `pub capacity` | `cfg/model.rs` | ✅ done |
| 136.2 | `node/congestion.rs` — `CongestionMonitor` with atomic counters, `score()`, hysteresis | `node/congestion.rs` | ✅ done |
| 136.3 | `proto/control.rs` — `congestion: u8` in `RouteReplyPayload`; backward-compat decode | `proto/control.rs` | ✅ done |
| 136.4 | `node/routing/probe.rs` — `congestion: u8` in `RttProbe`; update `record()` | `node/routing/probe.rs` | ✅ done |
| 136.5 | `node/dispatcher/control.rs` — fill `congestion` from `CongestionMonitor.score_u8()` on `ROUTE_REPLY` | `node/dispatcher/control.rs` | ✅ done |
| 136.6 | `node/dispatcher/routing.rs` — update the session counters in `CongestionMonitor` on open/close | `node/dispatcher/routing.rs` | ✅ done |
| 136.7 | `node/dispatcher/delivery.rs` — weighted random selection: `effective_score = rtt × (1 + 2 × congestion/255)` | `node/dispatcher/delivery.rs` | ✅ done |
| 136.8 | `node/runtime.rs` — create `CongestionMonitor`, pass it into `FrameDispatcher`; update `tx_queue_depth` in the maintenance loop | `node/runtime.rs` | ✅ done |

## Epic 137 — Routing quality improvements

### Goal

Close the feedback loops missing after Epic 136:
- RTT is measured by a single point — noise distorts route selection
- A failed `send_to()` does not affect the RouteCache — a bad route lives until TTL
- The node knows it is overloaded (`is_admitting()==false`), but does not withdraw itself from neighbors' routing
- Hop count does not participate in `effective_score`
- Peer flaps cause an avalanche of gossip messages
- Fallback candidates are chosen deterministically — all nodes switch to one hop simultaneously

### Subtasks

#### High priority

| # | Task | File | Status |
|---|--------|------|--------|
| 137.1 | `node/routing/probe.rs` — EWMA RTT smoothing: `rtt_smoothed = α×new + (1−α)×prev`, α=0.2; store `rtt_smoothed` alongside `rtt_ms`; use `rtt_smoothed` in `delivery.rs` | `node/routing/probe.rs`, `node/routing/score.rs` | ✅ done |
| 137.2 | `node/dispatcher/delivery.rs` — delivery failure feedback: on `send_to(hop) == false` call `route_cache.invalidate_hop(dst, hop)` immediately, do not wait for TTL | `node/dispatcher/delivery.rs`, `node/routing/cache.rs` | ✅ done |
| 137.3 | `node/dispatcher/routing.rs` — self-withdrawal on overload: when `CongestionMonitor::is_admitting()` goes `true→false`, send `ROUTE_WITHDRAW(origin=local_node_id)`; on `false→true` — `ROUTE_ANNOUNCE(origin=local_node_id, hop=0)` (re-announce self) | `node/dispatcher/routing.rs`, `node/congestion.rs` | ✅ done |
| 137.4 | `node/dispatcher/delivery.rs` — account for hop count in `effective_score`: `score × (1 + 0.1 × hop_count)` — penalize long routes; take `hop_count` from `route_cache.lookup_all_with_hops()` | `node/dispatcher/delivery.rs`, `node/routing/cache.rs` | ✅ done |

#### Medium priority

| # | Task | File | Status |
|---|--------|------|--------|
| 137.5 | `node/dispatcher/routing.rs` — flap dampening: on `on_session_closed` hold a ~3 s pause before sending `ROUTE_WITHDRAW`; cancel if the peer reconnected within that time | `node/dispatcher/routing.rs` | ✅ done |
| 137.6 | `node/routing/cache.rs` — update a route's score on receiving `ROUTE_REPLY`: on every RTT measurement to the `via` node, recompute the score of related routes | `node/routing/cache.rs`, `node/dispatcher/control.rs` | ✅ done |
| 137.7 | `node/routing/score.rs` — asymmetric EMA in `NeighborScorer`: `α_down=0.5` (react quickly to degradation), `α_up=0.1` (recover slowly) | `node/routing/score.rs` | ✅ done |

#### Low priority

| # | Task | File | Status |
|---|--------|------|--------|
| 137.8 | `node/dispatcher/delivery.rs` — weighted random for fallback candidates: apply the same weighted-choice logic not only to the first attempt but to subsequent retries on failure | `node/dispatcher/delivery.rs` | ✅ done |
| 137.9 | `node/routing/probe.rs` — confidence decay: `effective_weight = 1 − elapsed/max_age`; multiply the RTT score by weight when sorting candidates | `node/routing/probe.rs`, `node/dispatcher/delivery.rs` | ✅ done |

## Epic 138 — Gateway autodiscovery via mesh beacon

### Goal

A leaf node currently learns about a Gateway only from `config.peers` (hardcoded). In a real mesh a Gateway should announce itself via a periodic beacon, and leaf nodes should automatically add it as a peer without manual configuration. This is a basic prerequisite for operating in a dynamic mesh without pre-known addresses.

### Architecture

```
Gateway                              Leaf
   |                                   |
   |──── MeshBeacon{role=GATEWAY} ────▶|
   |     (UDP broadcast, 5 s)          |
   |                                   | BeaconReceiver detects IS_GATEWAY
   |                                   | → auto-add peer (veil addr from beacon)
   |◀─── OVL1 session ────────────────|
```

`MeshBeaconPayload` is extended with a `role_flags: u8` field:
- `0x01 = IS_GATEWAY` — the node bridges the mesh into the global veil
- `0x02 = HAS_INTERNET` — the node has internet access
- `0x04 = IS_RELAY` — the node can serve as a relay (NAT punch, store-and-forward)

### Known risks

| Risk | Description | Mitigation |
|------|----------|-----------|
| **Connection surge** | If a Gateway starts announcing itself, every Leaf within beacon range reaches for it at once | The existing backoff (1 s → 300 s, ±20% jitter) spreads out the attempts; a 10-beacons/60-s-per-IP limit prevents flooding. Additionally: in 138.4 initiate no more than `mesh.autodiscover_max_concurrent` simultaneous connects (default 3) |
| **Fake Gateway** | A malicious node sets `IS_GATEWAY` and catches Leaf connections | The handshake verifies cryptographic identity; a node without a valid key will not authenticate. The connection fails — backoff prevents flooding. Extra task 138.6 |
| **Beacon replay** | An attacker replays an old beacon with `IS_GATEWAY` after the Gateway has left the network | Entries in `AutoDiscoveredPeers` have a 60-s TTL and are refreshed only on a fresh beacon. If the Gateway is silent — the entry expires and no connect is initiated |

### Subtasks

| # | Task | File | Status |
|---|--------|------|--------|
| 138.1 | `node/mesh/beacon.rs` — add `role_flags: u8` and `veil_addr: Option<String>` to `MeshBeaconPayload`; backward-compat decode (old beacons without flags → `role_flags=0`) | `node/mesh/beacon.rs` | ✅ done |
| 138.2 | `node/runtime.rs` — when a Gateway node starts, fill `role_flags` from `NodeRole` and `config.listen` (first public address → `veil_addr`); pass it into `BeaconSender` | `node/runtime.rs`, `node/mesh/udp.rs` | ✅ done |
| 138.3 | `node/mesh/beacon.rs` — in `BeaconReceiver::on_beacon()`: if `role_flags & IS_GATEWAY != 0` and `veil_addr` is present — add it to the list of auto-discovered gateway peers (`AutoDiscoveredPeers`) with a 60-s TTL | `node/mesh/beacon.rs` | ✅ done |
| 138.4 | `node/runtime.rs` — autodiscovery connect loop + beacon receive loop | `node/runtime.rs` | ✅ done |
| 138.5 | `cfg/model.rs` — add `mesh.autodiscover_gateway: bool` and `mesh.autodiscover_max_concurrent: usize` | `cfg/model.rs` | ✅ done |
| 138.6 | `node/mesh/beacon.rs` — `MAX_AUTODISCOVERED_GATEWAYS = 8` in `AutoDiscoveredPeers` | `node/mesh/beacon.rs` | ✅ done |

## Epic 139 — Persistent RouteCache (offline operation)

### Goal

`RouteCache` is in-memory only, with a 120-s TTL. After a node restart or a brief loss of Core, all routes disappear and re-convergence is required. In a mesh scenario with unstable internet, the node should keep routing traffic by the last known routes, gradually lowering their priority until fresh data arrives.

### Known risks

| Risk | Description | Mitigation |
|------|----------|-----------|
| **Stale cache after a long offline** | The node was offline for >1 hour, the topology changed significantly; loaded stale routes send traffic nowhere | Stale entries get a ×2.0 penalty in `effective_score` → they are evicted on the first fresh `ROUTE_ANNOUNCE`. When loading a snapshot older than `cache_persist_max_age` (default 1 hour) — do not restore at all |
| **Disk artifacts on improper shutdown** | The snapshot was written partially (crash mid-write) → the node loads a corrupt cache at startup | Write via a tmp file + atomic rename; on a parse error — log and ignore the snapshot |
| **Privacy leak** | The on-disk snapshot reveals the network topology (neighbors' node_ids) under physical device access | Task 139.5: optional snapshot encryption with a key from `identity` (AES-256-GCM) |

### Architecture

- On every RouteCache change (insert/invalidate) — asynchronously save a snapshot to disk (bincode)
- At startup — load the snapshot, mark the loaded routes as `stale` (reduced score)
- `stale` routes are used for routing, but with a ×2.0 penalty in `effective_score`
- On a fresh `ROUTE_ANNOUNCE` for the same dst — replace the stale entry, remove the penalty

### Subtasks

| # | Task | File | Status |
|---|--------|------|--------|
| 139.1 | `node/routing/cache.rs` — add `is_stale: bool` to `RouteCacheEntry`; in `lookup_all()` include stale entries with a doubled `effective_score`; in `insert()` — reset `is_stale=false` for updated entries | `node/routing/cache.rs` | ✅ done |
| 139.2 | `node/routing/cache.rs` — add `fn snapshot(&self) -> Vec<RouteCacheEntry>` and `fn restore(entries: Vec<RouteCacheEntry>)`; on restore set `is_stale=true` for all loaded entries | `node/routing/cache.rs` | ✅ done |
| 139.3 | `cfg/model.rs` — add `routing.cache_persist_path: Option<String>` (path to the file, `None` = disabled) and `routing.cache_persist_interval_secs: u64` (default 30) | `cfg/model.rs` | ✅ done |
| 139.4 | `node/runtime.rs` — at startup, if `cache_persist_path` is set: read the file → `route_cache.restore(...)`; spawn a periodic snapshot task (every `cache_persist_interval_secs`); on clean shutdown — a final flush | `node/runtime.rs` | ✅ done |
| 139.5 | `node/runtime.rs` — safe snapshot write: write to a temp file (`<path>.tmp`), atomic `rename`; on a parse error during load — log a warning and continue without the cache; do not panic | `node/runtime.rs` | ✅ done |
| 139.6 | `cfg/model.rs` — add `routing.cache_persist_max_age_secs: u64` (default 3600); when loading a snapshot check the file `mtime`: if older than `max_age` — skip restore and delete the file | `cfg/model.rs`, `node/runtime.rs` | ✅ done |

## Epic 140 — Local NAT coordinator (without dependency on Core)

### Known risks

| Risk | Description | Mitigation |
|------|----------|-----------|
| **Gateway as MITM** | The local Gateway sees all `NAT_PROBE_REQUEST`s and knows who wants to connect to whom (metadata) | Architecturally unavoidable for rendezvous; session contents are E2E-encrypted. Document it as a threat model |
| **Gateway overload** | Many Leaf nodes use one Gateway as a signaling server at once | Rate limiting on `NAT_PROBE_REQUEST` is already implemented via `pow_challenge_limiter`; sufficient |
| **No fallback when both Core and a local Gateway are absent** | Both nodes behind different NATs, no relay at all | Honest behavior: return a `NatError::NoRelayAvailable` error, route the traffic through the mailbox |

### Goal

Currently NAT hole-punching requires a global Core node as a signaling server: `NAT_PROBE_REQUEST` is forwarded through veil, and STUN reflection is done by the Core node. In an isolated mesh (no internet) hole-punching is unavailable entirely. A Gateway in the mesh can act as a local signaling server and STUN relay for Leaf nodes in the same network.

### Architecture

```
Leaf A (behind NAT)      Gateway (local relay)         Leaf B (behind NAT)
    |                           |                            |
    |── NAT_PROBE_REQUEST ──────▶|                            |
    |   (via mesh, not internet) |── NAT_PROBE_REQUEST ──────▶|
    |                           |◀─ NAT_PROBE_REPLY ─────────|
    |◀─ NAT_PROBE_REPLY ────────|                            |
    |                           |                            |
    |════════ direct QUIC / UDP punch ═══════════════════════|
    |         (if successful)                                |
    |                           |                            |
    |── NAT_RELAY_REQUEST ──────▶|                            | (fallback)
    |◀═══════════ relay tunnel via Gateway ══════════════════|
```

A Gateway with the `IS_RELAY` flag (from Epic 138) takes on the role of local rendezvous.

### Subtasks

| # | Task | File | Status |
|---|--------|------|--------|
| 140.1 | `node/nat/coordinator.rs` — add `local_relay: Option<[u8; 32]>` — the node_id of a Gateway from `AutoDiscoveredPeers` (Epic 138.3); when there is no global Core, try local_relay as a fallback signaling server | `node/nat/coordinator.rs` | ✅ done |
| 140.2 | `node/dispatcher/control.rs` — Leaf nodes may forward `NAT_PROBE_REQUEST` through a Gateway (already works via veil routing); make sure a Gateway with the `Relay` role accepts and forwards requests even without internet | `node/dispatcher/control.rs` | ✅ done |
| 140.3 | `node/nat/discovery.rs` — add `local_stun`: the Gateway replies to `NAT_PROBE_REQUEST` with the sender's observed address (Core already does this) — make sure it works for a Relay node without IS_GATEWAY | `node/nat/discovery.rs` | ✅ done |
| 140.4 | `node/nat/relay.rs` — `RelayFallback`: prioritize local_relay (from the mesh beacon) over the global Core; logic: try local_relay, timeout 3 s → fallback to Core if there is internet | `node/nat/relay.rs` | ✅ done |

---

## Epic 141 — Multi-gateway failover

### Known risks

| Risk | Description | Mitigation |
|------|----------|-----------|
| **Connection storm on Gateway failure** | N Leaf nodes lose the Gateway at once and all rush to the next one; at N=100 that is ~83 attempts/s in the first second | Task 141.6: staggered reconnect — initial delay = `rank_in_gateway_list × 500 ms + jitter(±200 ms)`; rank is the index in `GatewayList` |
| **Oscillation between Gateways** | Gateway A recovers → nodes switch back → A fails again → loop | Hysteresis: switch to the best Gateway only if its score exceeds the current one by 20% and it has been stable for >30 s (the `stable_since` counter) |
| **False failover** | A brief disconnect (< 5 s) triggers an unnecessary failover and extra gossip traffic | Failover delay: initiate a Gateway change only after `gateway_failover_delay_secs` (default 5) of continuous unavailability |

### Goal

A leaf node knows only one Gateway from the config. If it is unavailable, the node is fully offline. With autodiscovery (Epic 138) a node can know several Gateways. Selection and switching logic is needed: on loss of the current Gateway, automatically use the next one from the list, preferring a Gateway with better RTT and `HAS_INTERNET`.

### Architecture

```
GatewayList (ordered by score):
  [Gateway A: RTT=15ms, HAS_INTERNET=true,  active=true ]  ← current
  [Gateway B: RTT=40ms, HAS_INTERNET=true,  active=true ]  ← backup
  [Gateway C: RTT=8ms,  HAS_INTERNET=false, active=true ]  ← mesh only

On loss of Gateway A:
  → Gateway A's score is zeroed
  → Gateway B is chosen (HAS_INTERNET=true, best RTT among the available)
  → a session to B is initiated
  → routes through A are invalidated (ROUTE_WITHDRAW)
  → new routes are announced through B
```

`HAS_INTERNET` preference: for delivery to global-veil nodes use only a Gateway with `HAS_INTERNET=true`; for local mesh nodes any Gateway works.

### Subtasks

| # | Task | File | Status |
|---|--------|------|--------|
| 141.1 | `node/runtime.rs` — `GatewayList`: a `Vec<GatewayEntry { node_id, veil_addr, score, has_internet, last_seen }>` struct, filled from `AutoDiscoveredPeers` (Epic 138.3) + static `config.peers` | `node/runtime.rs` | ✅ done |
| 141.2 | `node/runtime.rs` — active Gateway selection: `preferred_gateway()` → the Gateway with the maximum `score × (1 + has_internet as f64)` among those with an active session; if none are active — initiate a connect to the best by score | `node/runtime.rs` | ✅ done |
| 141.3 | `node/runtime.rs` — on `on_session_closed(gateway_id)`: if the preferred Gateway closed — immediately call `preferred_gateway()`, initiate a session to the next one; log `gateway.failover` | `node/runtime.rs` | ✅ done |
| 141.4 | `node/dispatcher/delivery.rs` — on `DELIVERY_FORWARD` to a global-veil node: if the direct route is unavailable, check GatewayList and send through the preferred Gateway with `HAS_INTERNET`; do not fall to the mailbox immediately | `node/dispatcher/delivery.rs` | ✅ done |
| 141.5 | `cfg/model.rs` — add `connection.prefer_internet_gateway: bool` (default `true`) and `connection.gateway_failover_delay_secs: u64` (default 5); when `prefer_internet_gateway=false` the Leaf uses the nearest Gateway regardless of `HAS_INTERNET` | `cfg/model.rs` | ✅ done |
| 141.6 | `node/runtime.rs` — staggered reconnect on failover: initial delay before connecting to the next Gateway = `rank × 500 ms + jitter(±200 ms)`; rank = position in `GatewayList` by score; prevents a connection storm | `node/runtime.rs` | ✅ done |
| 141.7 | `node/runtime.rs` — switch-back hysteresis: return to a higher-priority Gateway only if it has been stable for >30 s (`stable_since`) and its score exceeds the current one by 20%; prevents oscillation | `node/runtime.rs` | ✅ done |

---

## Epic 142 — Multipath delivery

### Goal

Traffic goes over a single path. When several routes with a similar score exist, the load can be spread across them — this gives bandwidth aggregation and reduces tail latency when one path is congested.

### Modes

- **ECMP** (Equal-Cost Multipath) — frames are spread round-robin across routes with a close `effective_score` (spread ≤ 20%). For stateless traffic.
- **Redundant send** — for frames with the `CRITICAL` flag, send simultaneously over the 2 best paths, accept the first to arrive. Reduces p99 latency.
- **Flow pinning** — frames of one logical flow (same `src+dst`) go over one path (avoids reordering for TCP-over-veil).

### Subtasks

| # | Task | File | Status |
|---|--------|------|--------|
| 142.1 | `node/routing/cache.rs` — `lookup_all_with_scores() -> Vec<([u8;32], f64)>`: return routes with their `effective_score`; extract the ECMP group as the subset of routes within 20% of the best | `node/routing/cache.rs` | ✅ done |
| 142.2 | `node/dispatcher/delivery.rs` — ECMP round-robin: when an ECMP group of ≥2 routes exists, spread frames across them via an atomic `ecmp_cursor` counter; flow pinning: `cursor = hash(src_node_id ⊕ dst_node_id) % group_len` | `node/dispatcher/delivery.rs` | ✅ done |
| 142.3 | `node/dispatcher/delivery.rs` — redundant send: add a flag `DELIVERY_FLAG_REDUNDANT = 0x01` to `DeliveryEnvelope`; when the flag is set — send over the two best paths simultaneously; the receiver deduplicates by `envelope_id` | `node/dispatcher/delivery.rs`, `proto/delivery.rs` | ✅ done |
| 142.4 | `cfg/model.rs` — add `routing.ecmp_score_band: f64` (default 0.20) — the allowed score spread for inclusion in the ECMP group; `routing.redundant_send: bool` (default false) | `cfg/model.rs` | ✅ done |

---

## Epic 143 — Adaptive probe frequency

### Goal

`ROUTE_PROBE` is sent every 30 s regardless of state. In a stable network this is wasted traffic; on unstable connections it is too rare for fast degradation detection.

### Algorithm

```
stability = std_dev(last_5_rtt_samples) / mean_rtt
if stability < 5%  → interval = 120 s   (stable)
if stability < 20% → interval = 30 s    (normal)
if stability ≥ 20% → interval = 10 s   (unstable)
new peer             → interval = 5 s     (warm-up)
```

### Subtasks

| # | Task | File | Status |
|---|--------|------|--------|
| 143.1 | `node/routing/probe.rs` — add a sliding window of the last 5 RTT samples to `RttProbe`; a method `stability() -> f64` = `std_dev / mean`; if `<5` samples — treat as unstable | `node/routing/probe.rs` | ✅ done |
| 143.2 | `node/runtime.rs` — replace the fixed `route_probe_interval` with `adaptive_probe_interval(peer_id)`: reads stability from `RttTable`, returns an interval from the range `[probe_min_interval, probe_max_interval]` | `node/runtime.rs` | ✅ done |
| 143.3 | `cfg/model.rs` — add `routing.probe_min_interval_secs: u64` (default 5), `routing.probe_max_interval_secs: u64` (default 120), `routing.probe_stability_threshold: f64` (default 0.05) | `cfg/model.rs` | ✅ done |

## Epic 144 — QoS / Traffic classes

### Goal

All frames are handled identically. Under congestion, VoIP traffic competes with background sync. Prioritization is needed: real-time traffic gets bandwidth first, background gets only the leftovers.

### Classes

| Class | Value | Examples | Behavior under congestion |
|-------|----------|---------|--------------------------|
| `RT` (0) | Real-time | VoIP, video | Drop oldest, never wait |
| `IN` (1) | Interactive | API, messages | Current behavior (default) |
| `BK` (2) | Bulk | Files, backup | Throttle to free bandwidth |
| `BG` (3) | Background | DHT replication | Only when the queue is empty |

WFQ weights: RT=8, IN=4, BK=2, BG=1.

### Subtasks

| # | Task | File | Status |
|---|--------|------|--------|
| 144.1 | `proto/header.rs` — extend `priority` to an explicit `TrafficClass` enum (`RealTime=0, Interactive=1, Bulk=2, Background=3`); backward compat: map old `priority` values to `Interactive` | `proto/header.rs` | ✅ done |
| 144.2 | `node/session/tx_registry.rs` — WFQ scheduler: 4 separate queues per session, send by weights `[8,4,2,1]`; a method `send_with_class(peer_id, class, bytes)` | `node/session/tx_registry.rs` | ✅ done |
| 144.3 | `node/dispatcher/` — set `TrafficClass` based on the frame type: `ControlMsg::Keepalive` → RT; `DeliveryMsg::Forward` → IN; `DhtMsg::*` → BG; mailbox put/fetch → BK | `node/dispatcher/control.rs`, `delivery.rs` | ✅ done |
| 144.4 | `cfg/model.rs` — add `session.qos_weights: [u8; 4]` (default `[8,4,2,1]`) and `session.rt_queue_len: usize` (default 64), `session.bg_queue_len: usize` (default 256) | `cfg/model.rs` | ✅ done |

---

## Epic 145 — Predictive route prefetch

### Goal

After a start or reconnect, the node waits for gossip to learn routes. For known frequent peers (from the config or history), `ROUTE_PROBE` can be started right away without waiting, reducing the latency of the first message.

### Subtasks

| # | Task | File | Status |
|---|--------|------|--------|

## Epic 146 — Epidemic broadcast

### Goal

There is no mechanism for efficiently broadcasting a message to the whole network (e.g. a public-key update, a system announcement). Unicast to each recipient does not scale. Gossip-based epidemic broadcast covers the whole network in `O(log N)` steps.

### Protocol

```
Sender → K random neighbors: EPIDEMIC{msg_id, ttl, payload}
Each recipient:
  - if msg_id already seen (RouteSeenSet) → drop
  - else → deliver + forward to K random neighbors with ttl-1
  - at ttl=0 → deliver, do not forward
```
Fanout K=3, TTL=6 (covers ~3^6 = 729 nodes; for larger networks TTL=log3(N)).

### Subtasks

| # | Task | File | Status |
|---|--------|------|--------|
| 146.1 | EpidemicBroadcast proto | `proto/epidemic.rs` | ✅ done |
| 146.2 | epidemic dispatch + K-fanout forward | `node/dispatcher/control.rs` | ✅ done |
| 146.3 | broadcast_epidemic public API | `node/runtime.rs` | ✅ done |
| 146.4 | epidemic config fields | `cfg/model.rs` | ✅ done |

## Epic 147 — Battery/energy-aware routing

### Goal

In a mesh on mobile devices, routing traffic through a node at 5% battery is undesirable — it will soon power off and break the route. Add `battery_level` to the beacon and account for it when selecting a relay.

### Subtasks

| # | Task | File | Status |
|---|--------|------|--------|
| 147.1 | `node/mesh/beacon.rs` — add `battery_level: u8` to `MeshBeaconPayload` (0=unknown/AC, 1..100=% charge); backward-compat: a missing byte → 0 (unknown, no penalty applied). Implement reading the battery level in a platform-specific way: Linux `/sys/class/power_supply/`, Android JNI, a stub for the rest | `node/mesh/beacon.rs` | ✅ done |
| 147.2 | `node/routing/probe.rs` — store `battery_level: u8` in `RttProbe` alongside `congestion`; update it on receiving a beacon from a peer | `node/routing/probe.rs` | ✅ done |
| 147.3 | `node/dispatcher/delivery.rs` — add a battery penalty to `effective_score`: `× (1 + battery_penalty)`, where `battery_penalty = if level > 0 && level < 20 { 3.0 } else if level < 40 { 0.5 } else { 0.0 }` | `node/dispatcher/delivery.rs` | ✅ done |
| 147.4 | `cfg/model.rs` — add `routing.battery_penalty_low: f64` (default 3.0, threshold <20%), `routing.battery_penalty_medium: f64` (default 0.5, threshold <40%), `routing.battery_threshold_low: u8` (default 20), `routing.battery_threshold_medium: u8` (default 40) | `cfg/model.rs` | ✅ done |

## Epic 148 — Distributed tracing (trace_id)

### Goal

On delivery problems it is impossible to tell which nodes a frame passed through (or did not). Add an optional `trace_id` — each hop logs its participation, and an admin command assembles the full path.

### Architecture

```
Sender: DELIVERY_FORWARD { trace_id=0xABCD1234, ... }
  → Node B logs: trace 0xABCD1234 hop=1 from=A to=C rtt=12ms
  → Node C logs: trace 0xABCD1234 hop=2 from=B to=DST rtt=8ms

Admin: veil trace 0xABCD1234
  → collects logs from all nodes → draws the path
```

Sampling: by default 1% of frames are traced (a random `trace_id != 0`).

### Subtasks

| # | Task | File | Status |
|---|--------|------|--------|
| 148.1 | `proto/delivery.rs` — add `trace_id: u64` to `DeliveryEnvelope` (0 = not traced); backward-compat decode (a missing field → 0) | `proto/delivery.rs` | ✅ done |
| 148.2 | `node/dispatcher/delivery.rs` — when `trace_id != 0`: log `delivery.trace` with fields `trace_id`, `from_peer`, `to_peer`, `hop_rtt_ms`, `timestamp_ms`; pass `trace_id` into the outgoing `DELIVERY_FORWARD` unchanged | `node/dispatcher/delivery.rs` | ✅ done |
| 148.3 | `node/runtime.rs` — on send: if `rand() < trace_sample_rate` — generate a random `trace_id != 0` and insert it into the envelope; return `trace_id` to the caller via IPC | `node/runtime.rs` | ✅ done |
| 148.4 | `node/admin.rs` — a command `AdminCommand::TraceQuery { trace_id: u64 }`: collect all `delivery.trace` records with the given `trace_id` from an in-memory ring buffer (last 10,000 records); return a list of hop records sorted by `timestamp_ms` | `node/admin.rs` | ✅ done |
| 148.5 | `cfg/model.rs` — add `routing.trace_sample_rate: f64` (default 0.01 = 1%) and `routing.trace_buffer_size: usize` (default 10_000) | `cfg/model.rs` | ✅ done |

## Epic 149 — Topology-aware DHT placement

### Goal

Kademlia chooses nodes by the XOR metric on node_id without regard to physical proximity. Among several XOR-equidistant nodes it is better to pick the physically closer one (lower RTT by Vivaldi). It reduces DHT get/put latency without changing Kademlia correctness.

### Algorithm

```
composite_score(node) = xor_distance(node) × (1 + vivaldi_weight × vivaldi_factor)
vivaldi_factor = vivaldi_distance(local, node) / vivaldi_reference_ms
```
`vivaldi_weight=0.3` — XOR still dominates, Vivaldi only breaks ties.

### Subtasks

| # | Task | File | Status |
|---|--------|------|--------|
| 149.1 | `node/dht/kademlia.rs` — in `find_closest_nodes`: after sorting by XOR, apply a secondary sort via `composite_score`; `vivaldi_factor = local_viv.distance_estimate(peer_viv) / 100.0` (normalized to 100 ms); when there are no Vivaldi coordinates for a peer — `vivaldi_factor = 0` (no penalty) | `node/dht/kademlia.rs` | ✅ done |
| 149.2 | `cfg/model.rs` — add `dht.vivaldi_weight: f64` (default 0.3); at 0.0 — behavior identical to current (pure XOR) | `cfg/model.rs` | ✅ done |

## Epic 150 — RTT table persistence

### Goal

`RttTable` is reset on every restart — the node starts without knowledge of latencies to neighbors and spends several probing rounds to rebuild the picture. Persist the smoothed RTT values to disk (like RouteCache in Epic 139) so that right after a restart the historical data is used as an initial approximation.

| 150.2 | `cfg/model.rs` — add `routing.rtt_persist_path: Option<String>` (default `None`) and `routing.rtt_persist_interval_secs: u64` (default 60) | `cfg/model.rs` | ✅ done |
| 150.3 | `node/runtime.rs` — at startup: if `rtt_persist_path` is set — read the JSON file, call `rtt_table.restore()`; spawn a periodic snapshot task every `rtt_persist_interval_secs`; on clean shutdown — a final flush; atomic write via a tmp file + rename | `node/runtime.rs` | ✅ done |

## Epic 151 — DHT republish jitter

### Goal

The republish task (Epic 82) republishes all keys at once every 30 minutes — creating a DHT traffic spike. Add a staggered schedule: each key has its own offset `key_offset = hash(key) % republish_interval`, so the load spreads evenly over time.

### Algorithm

```
next_publish(key) = last_publish(key) + republish_interval + jitter
jitter = (FNV1a(key) % (republish_interval / 4)) as secs
```
On first enabling (no `last_publish`) — `next_publish = now + jitter`, so not all keys publish at once on startup.

### Subtasks

| # | Task | File | Status |
|---|--------|------|--------|
| 151.1 | `node/dht/republish.rs` (new file) — `RepublishScheduler { schedule: HashMap<[u8;32], Instant> }`; a method `next_due(&mut self, key: [u8;32], interval: Duration) -> bool` — returns `true` if it is time to republish, updates `schedule[key]` to `now + interval + jitter(key, interval/4)` | `node/dht/republish.rs` | ✅ done |
| 151.2 | `node/runtime.rs` — in the republish task (Epic 82): instead of "publish all keys every N seconds" use `RepublishScheduler::next_due()` per key; the task wakes every 1 s and publishes only the keys for which `next_due` returned `true` | `node/runtime.rs` | ✅ done |

## Epic 152 — Beacon deduplication window

### Goal

`BeaconReceiver` processes each incoming UDP beacon independently. If a node sends beacons every 1–2 s or a packet is duplicated by the network, the processing repeats needlessly. Add a short dedup window: ignore a second beacon from the same `source_id` within `dedup_window_secs`.

### Subtasks

| # | Task | File | Status |
|---|--------|------|--------|
| 152.1 | `node/mesh/beacon.rs` — add `dedup: HashMap<[u8;32], Instant>` to `BeaconReceiver`; in `handle_beacon()`: if `dedup[source_id]` exists and `elapsed < dedup_window` — return (drop duplicate); otherwise insert/update `dedup[source_id] = now`; GC: on insert remove entries older than `2 × dedup_window` | `node/mesh/beacon.rs` | ✅ done |
| 152.2 | `cfg/model.rs` — add `mesh.beacon_dedup_window_secs: u64` (default 3); a `BeaconReceiver::with_dedup_window(secs: u64)` builder | `cfg/model.rs` | ✅ done |

## Epic 156 — LRU eviction for peer_vivaldi map (P1)

### Goal

When the 32,768-entry limit of `peer_vivaldi` is reached, it throws out the first key it finds (`HashMap::iter().next()`). This may evict frequently used peers. Replace with eviction by least-recently-used.

### Algorithm

Store `(VivaldiCoord, last_used: Instant)` instead of just `VivaldiCoord`. When the limit is reached, find the entry with the smallest `last_used` and remove it. On every read (`set_vivaldi`, lookup in `handle_find_node`) — update `last_used`.

### Subtasks

| # | Task | File | Status |
|---|--------|------|--------|
| 156.1 | `node/dht/kademlia.rs` — change the `PeerVivaldiMap` value from `VivaldiCoord` to `(VivaldiCoord, Instant)`; on eviction pick the key with the minimum `last_used`; on read/write update `last_used = Instant::now()` | `node/dht/kademlia.rs` | ✅ done |

## Epic 165 — Route Discovery via Random Walk + PoW

### Goal

A node does not know about new nodes beyond its direct neighbors. Implement a mechanism for actively discovering new routes through a random walk of a packet across the network with PoW protection against flooding.

### Protocol

**Packet:**
```
RouteDiscoveryPacket {
    src_node_id: [u8; 32],
    timestamp:   u64,        // unix seconds
    pow_nonce:   [u8; 32],
    ttl:         u8,         // initial: 16
}
```

**PoW:** `BLAKE3(src_node_id ‖ timestamp ‖ nonce)`, difficulty 16.

**Timestamp validity window:**
```
window = max(10 minutes, expected_solve_time(difficulty) * 10)
expected_solve_time = 2^difficulty / estimated_hash_rate
```
`estimated_hash_rate` — a conservative-estimate constant (weak hardware).

**Forwarding:**
1. Verify PoW and timestamp (using the window from the formula) — otherwise drop
2. Rate-limit: no more than 3 packets / 10 minutes from one `src_node_id` — otherwise drop
3. Decrement TTL
4. TTL == 0 → reply (see below)
5. Pick the next hop from non-Leaf neighbors, excluding the sender:
   - 75% — the neighbor with the maximum `neighbor_id XOR src_node_id`
   - 25% — a random neighbor
6. If there are no candidates — drop

**Reply at TTL == 0:**
- A node that can accept inbound (Relay/Gateway/Core): discovery lookup by `src_node_id` → get the initiator's `gateway_node_id` → connect through it → exchange session keys → send the encrypted transport address
- A node that cannot accept inbound (Leaf): drop

**Flood protection (forwarder):**
```
per_src:  TokenBucket { max_burst: 3, refill: 1 / 10 min }
global:   TokenBucket { max_burst: 50 packets/sec }
```

**Initiator — send frequency:**

| Routes in RouteCache | Interval |
|---|---|
| 0–3 | 1 hour |
| 4–7 | exponentially up to 24 hours |
| 8+ | 48 hours |

A node with `role = Leaf` or the flag `route_discovery = manual` — manual start only via the CLI.

### Subtasks

| # | Task | File | Status |
|---|--------|------|--------|
| 165.1 | Proto: `RouteDiscoveryPacket` struct + encode/decode | `proto/routing.rs` | ✅ done |
| 165.2 | PoW verify with timestamp checking by the window formula | `node/routing/pow.rs` | ✅ done |
| 165.3 | Forwarder: validation, TTL, biased random walk, Leaf filter | `node/routing/discovery_forwarder.rs` | ✅ done |
| 165.4 | Rate-limit on the forwarder (per-src TokenBucket + global) | `node/routing/discovery_forwarder.rs` | ✅ done |
| 165.5 | TTL=0 handler: ForwardDecision::Respond + runtime stub | `node/routing/discovery_forwarder.rs` | ✅ done |
| 165.6 | Initiator: adaptive interval + build/solve | `node/routing/discovery_initiator.rs` | ✅ done |
| 165.7 | CLI: a command for manual discovery start | `cmd/cli.rs`, `node/admin.rs` | ✅ done |
| 165.8 | Unit tests: PoW window, forwarding, rate-limit, biased walk | `node/routing/` | ✅ done |

| 167.2 | DHT `evict_oldest_if_full()`: O(n) scan → BTreeMap keyed by insertion time | `node/dht/kademlia.rs` | ✅ done |

## Epic 168 — Code audit: bugs, panics, performance (round 2)

### Goal

Eliminate issues found during the audit (April 2026): panics in production code, memory leaks, blocking I/O in async context, duplicated code.

### Findings table

| Category | File | Lines | Description |
|-----------|------|--------|----------|
| **Panic in prod** | `node/runtime.rs` | 3616, 3627 | `.lock().unwrap()` instead of the `lock!()` macro — panic risk on poisoned mutex |
| **Panic in prod** | `node/admin.rs` | 1239 | `.lock().unwrap()` on `trace_buffer()` — does not use `lock!()` |
| **Panic in prod** | `node/dht/kademlia.rs` | 645, 673 | `.lock().unwrap()` in `NetworkPeerQuerier::find_node` / `find_value` — outside the test block |
| **Memory leak** | `node/mesh/beacon.rs` | 66 | `BeaconRateLimiter::evict_stale()` is defined but **never called anywhere** → `counts: HashMap<IpAddr, _>` grows unbounded by the number of unique IPs |
| **Blocking I/O** | `node/runtime.rs` | 2134–2135, 2238–2239, 2312–2313, 2381–2382, 2451–2452, 2523–2524, 2593–2594, 2676–2677 | 8 persist tasks call `std::fs::write` / `std::fs::rename` / `std::fs::read_to_string` directly from `tokio::spawn(async move {})` without `spawn_blocking` → risk of blocking the event loop |
| **O(n) in hot path** | `node/dispatcher/routing.rs` | 272–277 | `pow_pending` eviction: O(n) `min_by_key` scan over up to MAX_POW_PENDING entries on every new RouteRequest — the same pattern that was fixed in kademlia.rs |
| **Duplicated code** | `node/admin.rs` | 1379, 1423, 1448 | Three nearly identical hex-parsing functions: `parse_node_id`, `parse_hex_bytes`, `parse_node_id_hex` — the first two duplicate the logic; `parse_node_id_hex` already calls `parse_hex_bytes` |
| **Dead code** | `node/observability.rs` | 295, 313, 407 | 5 metrics functions with `#[allow(dead_code)]` and TODO: `inc_session_handshake_failures`, `inc_dht_store`, `inc_dht_lookup`, `inc_rt_frames_tx`, `inc_rt_seq_gaps` |
| **Unfinished stub** | `node/runtime.rs` | 3638 | `trigger_discovery_search()` — empty stub, the discovery_initiator signal is not implemented (Epic 165 TODO) |
| **debug_assert in encode** | `proto/routing.rs` | 258, 263, 268, 292, 297, 303 | `debug_assert!` on length limits during serialization — do not fire in release builds, silent data truncation |

### Subtasks

| # | Task | File | Status |
|---|--------|------|--------|
| 168.1 | `lock!()` instead of `.lock().unwrap()` in runtime.rs:3616,3627 and admin.rs:1239 and kademlia.rs:645,673 | `node/runtime.rs`, `node/admin.rs`, `node/dht/kademlia.rs` | ✅ done |
| 168.2 | Call `BeaconRateLimiter::evict_stale()` in `handle_beacon` (every N calls) | `node/mesh/beacon.rs` | ✅ done |
| 168.3 | 8 persist tasks: wrap `flush_*_sync` in `tokio::task::spawn_blocking` | `node/runtime.rs` | ✅ done |
| 168.4 | `pow_pending` eviction: replace O(n) `min_by_key` with a BTreeMap keyed by `Instant` (analogous to kademlia 167.2) | `node/dispatcher/routing.rs` | ✅ done |
| 168.5 | Remove `parse_node_id` (1379), replace calls with `parse_node_id_hex` | `node/admin.rs` | ✅ done |
| 168.6 | Wire up 3 of 5 metrics: `inc_session_handshake_failures` (runtime.rs), `inc_dht_store/lookup` (kademlia.rs + metrics threading); `inc_rt_frames_tx/seq_gaps` — the RT-frame send path is not implemented yet | `node/observability.rs`, `node/runtime.rs`, `node/dht/kademlia.rs` | ✅ done |
| 168.7 | `debug_assert!` → `assert!` in encode for routing payloads | `proto/routing.rs` | ✅ done |

## Epic 169 — Code audit: panics in constructors, fragile unwrap, config inconsistency, dead code (round 3)

### Goal

Eliminate issues found during the audit (April 2026): `assert!` in production constructors → `Result`, fragile `.unwrap()` in guarded paths → `if let`, ConfigKey key-case inconsistency, dead / unpopulated code.

### Findings table

| Category | File | Lines | Description |
|-----------|------|--------|----------|
| **Panic in prod** | `node/storage_policy.rs` | 40 | `assert!(max_entries > 0)` in the constructor — panics on invalid input; needs a `Result` |
| **Panic in prod** | `node/abuse/violation_tracker.rs` | 48 | `assert!(ban_threshold > 0)` in the constructor — same |
| **Panic in prod** | `node/abuse/attachment_quota.rs` | 22 | `assert!(max_per_gateway > 0)` in the constructor — same |
| **Fragile unwrap** | `node/session/runner.rs` | 520 | `tx_cipher.as_mut().unwrap()` inside an `if is_some()` guard — safe but fragile under refactoring |
| **Fragile unwrap** | `node/routing/cache.rs` | 168 | `get_mut(&dst_node_id).unwrap()` inside an `if contains_key()` guard — same |
| **Fragile unwrap** | `node/session/handshake.rs` | 207 | `try_into().unwrap()` right after a manual length check — replace with `map_err` |
| **Logic error** | `cfg/keys.rs` | 50–54, 89–93 | `parse()` accepts `"identity.*"` and `"Identity.*"`, `as_str()` returns `"Identity.*"` — round-trip is broken for lowercase input; the other keys are lowercase |
| **Dead code** | `node/session/quic_transport.rs` | 34–38 | The `QuicSendChannel` enum is marked `#[allow(dead_code)]` entirely; the `Uni` variant is never used |
| **Unpopulated field** | `node/metrics_http.rs` | 30–31 | `RuntimeSummary::active_sessions` is marked `#[allow(dead_code)]` — the field is not populated, always 0 in the HTTP response |

### Subtasks

| # | Task | File | Status |
|---|--------|------|--------|
| 169.1 | `DefaultPressurePolicy::new` → `Result<Self, &'static str>`, update all call-sites | `node/storage_policy.rs` | ✅ done |
| 169.2 | `ViolationTracker::new` → `Result<Self, &'static str>`, update all call-sites | `node/abuse/violation_tracker.rs` | ✅ done |
| 169.3 | `AttachmentQuota::new` → `Result<Self, &'static str>`, update all call-sites | `node/abuse/attachment_quota.rs` | ✅ done |
| 169.4 | `runner.rs:520` `tx_cipher.as_mut().unwrap()` → `let Some(cipher) = ... else { return }` | `node/session/runner.rs` | ✅ done |
| 169.5 | `cache.rs:168` `get_mut(...).unwrap()` → `let Some(bucket) = ... else { return }` | `node/routing/cache.rs` | ✅ done |
| 169.6 | `handshake.rs:207` `.try_into().unwrap()` → `.try_into().map_err(\|_\| NodeError::Handshake(...))` | `node/session/handshake.rs` | ✅ done |

## Epic 170 — Code audit: silent no-op API, dead fields, duplication, logging, cosmetics (round 4)

### Assessment of the issues found

All 14 items verified. Summary by reality/severity:

| # | Issue | Real? | Severity | Verdict |
|---|----------|----------|-------------|---------|
| 1 | `trigger_discovery_search()` — silent no-op | ✅ | **High** — the admin command silently does nothing | Fix: log a warning |
| 2 | `eprintln!` in the WAL code of mailbox/backend.rs (219, 232, 249) | ✅ | **Medium** — stderr is lost in daemon mode | Replace with `logger.warn()` |
| 3 | Two incompatible `RateLimiter`s in the crate | ✅ | **Medium** — different semantics (token-bucket vs sliding window), but duplicate naming | Replace the IPC one with `TokenBucket` |
| 4 | `write_frame`/`read_frame` in 3 places | ✅ partially | **Low** — different stream types and error types in each place; full unification is not worthwhile | Leave as is (different contexts) |
| 5 | `NodeId` vs `[u8; 32]` in the public SDK | ✅ | **Low** — API design, breaking change | Defer to a separate epic |
| 6 | RT metrics `inc_rt_frames_tx/seq_gaps` always 0 | ✅ | **Low** — RT send path is not implemented, explicitly documented in Epic 168.6 | Leave until RT send is implemented |
| 7 | `DiagEvent` fields with `#[allow(dead_code)]` — seq is not read | ✅ | **Low** — `responder`/`echo_ts_us` are actually read in admin.rs, `seq` is not read | Remove the redundant attributes, keep or drop the seq field |
| 8 | `pub _client_token` — public field with a `_` prefix | ✅ | **Low** — convention violation | Remove the `_` or make it private |
| 9 | `reannounce_secs` — dead config field | ✅ **CRITICAL** | **High** — the field is parsed from INI, assigned in the model, but **never used** in node code; only `reannounce_interval_secs` is used in runtime | Either wire it up or remove it |
| 10 | `is_empty()` vs `is_default()` — inconsistency | ✅ | **Low** — cosmetic | Rename for consistency |
| 11 | `DiagMsg` starts at 1 | ✅ | **No** — wire protocol, breaking change | Leave as is, document it |
| 12 | `node/handshake.rs` and `node/session/handshake.rs` | ✅ | **Low** — different responsibilities, confusing names | Rename for clarity |
| 13 | edition 2024 vs 2021 in the workspace | ✅ | **Low** — Cargo supports it; edition 2024 brings new capabilities | Update veilclient to 2024 |
| 14 | `temp.md` in the repository root | ✅ | **Low** — temporary file, should not be in VCS | Remove |

## Epic 171. Code audit: fragile unwrap in crypto/kdf, from_utf8_lossy in TLS fingerprint, lock! consistency (round 5)

### Goal

Eliminate new findings from the April 2026 audit: fragile `.unwrap()` on crypto invariants, silent UTF-8 replacement when parsing network ALPN/SNI, `lock().unwrap()` in sim code without the lock! macro.

Additionally: all items of Epics 108–110 were verified — most are already implemented in the code, status updated.

### Findings table

| Category | File | Lines | Description | Severity |
|-----------|------|--------|----------|-------------|
| **Fragile unwrap** | `crypto/session_kdf.rs` | 77–80, 119–122 | `.try_into().unwrap()` on fixed-size slices of HKDF output — a protocol invariant (HKDF always yields exactly 96 bytes), but unwrap without a message | LOW |
| **Fragile unwrap** | `node/routing/cache.rs` | 178 | `bucket.pop().unwrap()` — called only after checking `len() >= MAX_ROUTES_PER_DST`, but unwrap without context | LOW |
| **Silent data loss** | `transport/fingerprint.rs` | 895, 926 | `from_utf8_lossy` when parsing SNI/ALPN from a network ClientHello — invalid UTF-8 is replaced with `U+FFFD` without logging; may break fingerprint matching | MEDIUM |
| **lock() inconsistency** | `sim/loss.rs` | 163 | `rng.lock().unwrap()` in the production method `should_drop()` — violates the lock! macro convention (poison-resilient) | LOW |

### Verification status of Epics 108–110

| Epic | Item | Verdict |
|------|-------|---------|
| 108.1 | `as u8`/`as u16` without a check in encode | N/A — encode already uses `assert!` + `.min()` |
| 108.2 | decode does not check max lengths | N/A — decode checks via `ValueTooLarge` |
| 108.3 | `from_utf8_lossy` in routing/discovery | N/A — already `String::from_utf8()` |
| 108.4 | `decode_private_key` returns `Vec<u8>` | N/A — already `Zeroizing<Vec<u8>>` |
| 108.5 | `SessionKeys` derives Clone | N/A — only `Zeroize, PartialEq, Eq` |
| 108.6 | `recv_window` not bounded | N/A — `initial_recv_window.min(MAX_STREAM_SEND_WINDOW)` |
| 108.7 | `sessions_per_ip` race | N/A — check-and-increment under a single lock! |
| 108.8 | `read_frame` allocates without a limit | N/A — `decode_header` rejects > MAX_FRAME_BODY |
| 108.9 | Config timeout params without validation | N/A — `structural.rs` validates |
| 108.10 | `send_to()` on TrySendError | N/A — `TrySendError::Full/Closed` are logged |
| 108.11 | beacon decode error is ignored | N/A — `log::debug!(...)` already present |
| 108.12 | `NonceOverflow` is not logged | N/A — `log::error!` in runner.rs |
| 109.1 | DHT store unbounded | N/A — `evict_if_full()` is called in store |
| 109.2 | `relay_tunnels` unbounded | N/A — `MAX_RELAY_TUNNELS` is checked |
| 109.3 | `peer_observed_addrs` unbounded | N/A — `MAX_PEER_OBSERVED_ADDRS` is checked |
| 109.4 | `recv_window` not capped at MAX | N/A — `.min(MAX_STREAM_SEND_WINDOW)` |
| 110.1 | MeshFrame payload overflow | N/A — `debug_assert! + .min(u16::MAX)` |
| 110.2 | mailbox `or_default()` on probe | N/A — the capacity check runs before `or_default` |
| 110.3 | `perform_ovl1_handshake` without timeout | N/A — wrapped in `tokio::time::timeout` |
| 110.4 | `DhtQuota::evict_stale()` is not called | N/A — called inside `allow()` every N iterations |

### Subtasks

| # | Task | File | Status |
|---|--------|------|--------|
| 171.1 | `session_kdf.rs:77-80, 119-122` `.try_into().unwrap()` → `.expect("HKDF output is exactly 96 bytes")` | `crypto/session_kdf.rs` | ✅ done |
| 171.2 | `cache.rs:178` `bucket.pop().unwrap()` → `expect("bucket non-empty: len >= MAX checked above")` | `node/routing/cache.rs` | ✅ done |
| 171.3 | `fingerprint.rs:895,926` `from_utf8_lossy` → `std::str::from_utf8()`; invalid UTF-8 is logged at debug and skipped | `transport/fingerprint.rs` | ✅ done |
| 171.4 | `sim/loss.rs:163` `rng.lock().unwrap()` → `lock!(self.rng)` | `sim/loss.rs` | ✅ done |
| 171.5 | `cargo build --workspace && cargo test --workspace` — 954 passed, 0 failed | — | ✅ done |

## Epic 172. Security hardening: Sybil/Eclipse/Flood/Replay/Spoofing mitigations

**Goal:** Reduce the exploitability of six attack models: Sybil, Eclipse, Mailbox Flood, DHT Poisoning (replay AnnounceAttachment), Gateway Spoofing, Replay in the routing plane.

### Findings table

| Attack | File | Issue | Severity | Fix |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|-------------|
| **Sybil** | `identity_policy.rs` | `DEFAULT_POW_DIFFICULTY = 16` bits — ~65k hashes, a GPU mints 10,000 nodes in 0.65 s | CRITICAL | Raise to 24 bits (+256× cost) |
| **Mailbox Flood** | `node/mailbox/store.rs` | On queue overflow `pop_front()` discards **old legitimate** messages; 100 Sybil senders evict any content | HIGH | Return `false` when the queue is full, do not discard |
| **Eclipse (DHT)** | `node/dht/routing.rs` | No IP diversity in the k-bucket; an attacker fills the bucket with nodes from a single /24 | HIGH | `MAX_NODES_PER_SUBNET_PER_BUCKET = K/4`; on insert prefer evicting from the most-represented subnet |
| **Replay (routing)** | `node/dispatcher/routing.rs` | ROUTE_ANNOUNCE verifies the signature, but there is no per-origin monotonic sequence window; an old signed announce can be replayed | MEDIUM | `ExpiryCache<([u8;32], u32)>` = `(origin_node_id, seq_no)` dedup with TTL |
| **DHT Poisoning** | `node/discovery/service.rs` | `handle_announce_attachment` does not check `payload.expires_at` — a stale record is accepted | MEDIUM | Check `payload.expires_at > now_unix_secs` before store |
| **Gateway Spoofing** | `node/dispatcher/discovery.rs` | Any node can send `AnnounceAttachment` with `role = GATEWAY`; the dispatcher does not know the role from the handshake | MEDIUM | Add `peer_roles: Arc<Mutex<HashMap<[u8;32], u8>>>` to `CryptoContext`; populate at handshake; check in the AnnounceAttachment handler |

### Subtasks

| # | Task | File | Status |
|---|--------|------|--------|
| 172.A | `DEFAULT_POW_DIFFICULTY` 16 → 24; `#[cfg(test)] = 16` for sim tests | `identity_policy.rs` | ✅ done |
| 172.B | `MailboxStore::put` / `put_with_seq` on a full queue — return `false`, not `pop_front`; update `service.rs` to return `REJECTED` | `node/mailbox/store.rs`, `node/mailbox/service.rs` | ✅ done |
| 172.C | Subnet diversity: `MAX_NODES_PER_SUBNET_PER_BUCKET = K/4`; `RoutingTable::insert` evicts from the most-represented subnet or drops if the attacking subnet is full | `node/dht/routing.rs` | ✅ done |
| 172.D | `RouteSeenSet` — two layers: `full` per-(origin,via,seq) and `replay` per-(origin,seq); protection against replay via different via | `node/dispatcher/mod.rs` | ✅ done |
| 172.E | `handle_announce_attachment`: check `payload.expires_at > SystemTime::now()` | `node/discovery/service.rs` | ✅ done |
| 172.F | `CryptoContext.peer_roles: Arc<Mutex<HashMap<[u8;32], u8>>>`; populate from `remote_capabilities.roles_supported` in `register_connection_session`; check in the AnnounceAttachment handler | `node/dispatcher/mod.rs`, `node/runtime.rs`, `node/dispatcher/discovery.rs` | ✅ done |
| 172.G | `cargo build --workspace && cargo test --workspace` — 958 passed, 0 failed | — | ✅ done |

---

## Epic 173. Mailbox quota: fix logic bugs and close the remaining flood vectors

**Goal:** Eliminate three vulnerabilities remaining after Epic 172.B: quota bypass via FORWARD, quota never released, Sybil flood via N sender identities.

### Findings

| # | File | Issue | Severity |
|---|------|----------|-------------|
| 173-A | `node/mailbox/service.rs:216` | `store_forward_fallback` calls `backend.put()` directly without `quota.charge()` — any peer sends `FORWARD` and fills the mailbox without limits | HIGH |
| 173-B | `node/mailbox/service.rs:195–201` | `handle_ack` calls `backend.ack_batch()` but not `quota.release()` — the sender's counter grows and never decreases; the quota degrades into a one-time budget | HIGH |
| 173-C | `node/abuse/mailbox_quota.rs` | Per-sender quota: N Sybil nodes yield N × `max_per_sender` slots; mitigated by 172.A (24-bit PoW), but not eliminated | MEDIUM |

### Subtasks

| # | Task | File | Status |
|---|--------|------|--------|
| 173.A | `store_forward_fallback`: add a `sender_id` parameter (from `envelope.sender_node_id`) and call `quota.charge()` before `backend.put()` | `node/mailbox/service.rs` | ✅ done |
| 173.B | `handle_ack`: after `backend.ack_batch()` call `quota.release(sender)` for each acknowledged seq — restore slots in the sliding window | `node/mailbox/service.rs`, `node/mailbox/backend.rs` | ✅ done |
| 173.C | Add a global limit `max_senders_per_mailbox` to `MailboxQuota`; when exceeded, reject messages from new senders | `node/abuse/mailbox_quota.rs` | ✅ done |
| 173.D | `cargo build --workspace && cargo test --workspace` — no regressions | — | ✅ done |

## Epic 174. Peer nonce mismatch: graceful nonce update without WARN spam

**Goal:** Eliminate false-alarm `WARN peer.nonce_mismatch` during a normal nonce rotation on a remote peer.

### Problem description

When receiving a new nonce from a peer, the node logs:
```
WARN  peer.nonce_mismatch peer_id=0x00000002 link_id=… error=peer nonce mismatch for 0x00000002: expected configured nonce AAAzXQ==, got ATi7Ew==
```
The nonce in the peer config becomes stale after a normal identity rotation (re-mine). The current code treats this as a security violation (`WARN`/`Violation`), although it is a normal operation provided the handshake signature is verified.

### Subtasks

| # | Task | File | Status |
|---|--------|------|--------|
| 174.A | Find the nonce check: `verify_remote_peer_identity` in `node/runtime.rs:4465` — the nonce check is separated from crypto verification | `node/runtime.rs` | ✅ done |
| 174.B | `NonceMismatch` → `INFO peer.nonce_updated`; update `state.peers[peer_id].nonce`; persist to config via `spawn_blocking`; continue the connection. `IdentityMismatch` remains `WARN` + disconnect | `node/runtime.rs` | ✅ done |
| 174.C | `cargo build --workspace && cargo test --workspace` — 958 passed, 0 failed | — | ✅ done |

---

## Epic 175. Node name: fix nonce/DHT registration

**Goal:** Fix four issues with the name registration mechanism:
1. `node name nonce` does not save the result — on `claim` the PoW is performed again
2. After a node restart the nonce is lost and the DHT registration is not restored
3. `spawn_name_autoclaim_task` always re-mines with difficulty=8, ignoring the saved record
4. The `can_replace` logic allowed taking a name with the same difficulty (broken ownership) and did not account for expiry

### Subtasks

| # | Task | File | Status |
|---|--------|------|--------|
| 175.A | `can_replace`: add rule 2 (expired = always replaceable) and rule 3 (same owner = difficulty ≥ incumbent); it was strictly greater for all | `node/name/validation.rs` | ✅ done |
| 175.B | `IdentityConfig`: add a `name_record: Option<String>` field (base64-encoded NameRecord); update INI/YAML/JSON/TOML parsers and all struct literals | `cfg/model.rs`, `cfg/identity.rs`, `cfg/format/*.rs` | ✅ done |
| 175.C | `NameClaim` and `NameRenew` in `admin.rs`: after a successful publish, save the encoded record to the config file's `[identity] name_record` via `spawn_blocking` | `node/admin.rs`, `node/runtime.rs` | ✅ done |
| 175.D | `spawn_name_autoclaim_task`: if a valid `name_record` is present in the config — republish without mining; when mining, determine difficulty from the incumbent (same owner = match, other owner = beat+1); save the new record to config | `node/runtime.rs` | ✅ done |
| 175.E | `cargo build --workspace && cargo test --workspace` — 967 passed, 0 failed | — | ✅ done |

---

## Epic 176. Name security: eliminate vulnerabilities in the DHT-name mechanism

**Goal:** Close three critical vulnerabilities in the name system found by the audit:
1. Replay expired record — `verify_name_record` does not check the challenger's `expires_at`
2. `node_id ≠ BLAKE3(public_key)` — the binding is not checked, impersonation is possible
3. Permanent lockout — the incumbent can take difficulty=255 and lock the name forever

### Subtasks

| # | Task | File | Status |
|---|--------|------|--------|
| 176.A | `verify_name_record`: add a check `expires_at > now` (reject already-expired records); add the binding check `BLAKE3(public_key) == node_id` | `node/name/validation.rs` | ✅ done |
| 176.B | `can_replace` + `verify_name_record`: introduce `MAX_NAME_DIFFICULTY=24` in `proto/name.rs`; in `verify_name_record` reject `difficulty > MAX`; in `can_replace` rule 4 — when incumbent.difficulty≥MAX the threshold = MAX-1 so a challenger with MAX can evict; `#[ignore]` test for the slow mining test | `node/name/validation.rs`, `proto/name.rs` | ✅ done |
| 176.C | `cargo build --workspace && cargo test --workspace` — 989 passed, 0 failed, 1 ignored | — | ✅ done |

---

**Goal:** Close four remaining vulnerabilities from the name-system audit.

### Subtasks

| # | Attack | Task | File | Status |
|---|-------|--------|------|--------|

## Epic 178. Optional delivery guarantee (at-least-once E2E ACK)

**Goal:** Add an opt-in reliable-delivery mode on top of the existing best-effort
veil routing.  The infrastructure (content_id, DeliveryStatus, route cache) already exists;
the ACK loop needs to be closed and a sender-side retransmit added.

**Semantics:** at-least-once.  Exactly-once is the application's responsibility
(content_id is the idempotency key).

### Subtasks

| # | Task | File | Status |
|---|--------|------|--------|
| 178.A | `require_ack` flag in `DeliveryEnvelope`: `DELIVERY_FLAG_REQUIRE_ACK = 0x8000_0000` in the high bit of the `ttl_secs` wire field (backward-compatible: old nodes clamp the TTL and route as usual); `require_ack: bool` in the AppIpcSendPayload field via `flags u32` (IPC-local); encode/decode updated | `proto/delivery.rs`, `proto/ipc.rs` | ✅ done |
| 178.B | The receiver sends `DeliveryStatus(DELIVERED, content_id)` back: on successful local delivery (if `require_ack`) it calls `send_delivery_ack` via `session_tx_registry` | `node/dispatcher/delivery.rs` | ✅ done |
| 178.C | `PendingAckTracker`: `HashMap<content_id, (frame_bytes, dst, deadline, attempts)>`; `ack()` removes the entry; `tick()` returns `Retransmit`/`Failed` per expired entry; 3 tests | `node/dispatcher/pending_ack.rs` (new) | ✅ done |
| 178.D | Integration: `pending_ack: Arc<Mutex<PendingAckTracker>>` in `FrameDispatcher` and `IpcServer`; registration on send in `handle_ipc_send`; `DeliveryStatus(DELIVERED)` → `ack()` in the dispatcher; `spawn_pending_ack_tick()` — background tick task with retransmit | `node/dispatcher/delivery.rs`, `node/dispatcher/mod.rs`, `node/ipc/server.rs`, `node/runtime.rs` | ✅ done |
| 178.E | Constants `MAX_DELIVERY_ATTEMPTS=3`, `DELIVERY_ACK_TIMEOUT_MS=5000`, `DELIVERY_ACK_CHECK_INTERVAL_MS=1000` in `proto/budget.rs`; the Failed path (application notification) is a stub, full implementation as needed | `proto/budget.rs` | ✅ done |
| 178.F | `cargo build --workspace && cargo test --workspace` — 992 passed, 0 failed, 1 ignored | — | ✅ done |

route manipulation, replay via forward-cache overflow, resource exhaustion
via IPC, unreliable trust in indirect route announces.
