## Epic 320. DHT keyspace sharding: 256 shards

**Goal:** A single DHT lookup at N=10^9 = log₂₀(10^9) ≈ 7 hops × 50ms RTT = 350ms. Acceptable. But each node stores O(K × 256) contacts = ~8K entries, insufficient for coverage. Solution: keyspace sharding by first byte → 256 shard groups, a node participates in ≤16 shards.

### Subtasks

| # | Task | File | Status |
|---|--------|------|--------|
| 320.1 | `shard::shard_of(key)=key[0]`; `local_shards(node_id)` → 16 XOR-closest to node_id[0]; `is_local_shard()` | `node/dht/shard.rs` (new) | ✅ done |
| 320.2 | `handle_store`: shard filtering — reject keys outside local shards when `DhtConfig.shard_filtering=true` | `node/dht/kademlia.rs`, `cfg/model.rs` | ✅ done |
| 320.3 | Cross-shard: implicit via RecursiveRelay (Epic 294) — STORE forwarded to the closest node in the target shard | `node/dispatcher/delivery.rs` | ✅ done |
| 320.4 | `entries_to_transfer(local, new_node, entries)` — identify keys closer to new node by XOR; test coverage | `node/dht/shard.rs` | ✅ done |
| 320.5 | `cross_shard_forwards_total` counter + prometheus export | `node/observability.rs` | ✅ done |

---

## Epic 321. Bandwidth-aware transit: backpressure + congestion-based routing

**Goal:** At 10^9 nodes, transit nodes (CoreRouters) become a bottleneck. Congestion feedback is needed so routing avoids overloaded nodes.

### Subtasks

| # | Task | File | Status |

| 321.1 | `CongestionMonitor::score_u8()` already exists (Epic 136): 0=idle, 255=saturated | `node/congestion.rs` | ✅ done |
| 321.2 | Route selection: already uses RTT + congestion + battery + jitter scoring in `relay_forward` | `node/dispatcher/delivery.rs` | ✅ done |
| 321.3 | Backpressure: `relay_forward` → `congestion_monitor.score_u8() > 200` → `RateLimited` | `node/dispatcher/delivery.rs` | ✅ done |
| 321.4 | Epidemic fan-out: halved when `congestion_monitor.score_u8() > 128` (>50% load); minimum 1 | `node/dispatcher/control.rs` | ✅ done |

## Epic 306. Full codebase review: bugs, vulnerabilities, loops, dead code

**Goal:** Analyze every `.rs` file in the project and compile an exhaustive table of issues in the following categories:

### Search categories

| Cat. | What we look for | Priority |
|------|----------|-----------|
| **BUG** | Logic errors, mishandled edge cases, off-by-one, race conditions | CRITICAL |
| **SEC** | Vulnerabilities: injection, authorization bypass, information leak, timing attacks, missing validation | CRITICAL |
| **LOOP** | Potential traffic/message loops: forwarding cycles, gossip amplification, infinite retry, echo between nodes | CRITICAL |
| **PANIC** | `unwrap()`, `expect()`, `panic!()`, array index without a bounds check — especially where recovery is possible | HIGH |
| **PERF** | Hot paths with allocations, O(n²) algorithms, lock contention, unnecessary clone/copy | HIGH |
| **MEM** | Leaks: unbounded collections without eviction, Arc cycles, forgotten drops | HIGH |
| **DUP** | Duplicate functionality: copy-paste logic, two paths doing the same thing | MEDIUM |
| **DEAD** | Unused code, future stubs, `#[allow(dead_code)]`, TODO/FIXME | LOW |

### Subtasks

| # | Task | Status |
|---|--------|--------|
| 306.1 | **proto/**: 3× PANIC/MEDIUM (unwrap on user-controlled buffer in anycast.rs, discovery.rs); all integer overflow safe (clamped by constants) | ✅ done |
| 306.2 | **crypto/**: CRITICAL — `PowParams` Debug leaked private_key → **FIXED** (redacted Debug); HIGH — `Base64PrivateKey`/`Base64PublicKey` Debug leaked → **FIXED**; SessionKeys correctly zeroized; no timing leaks | ✅ done |
| 306.3 | **dispatcher/**: 61 unwrap() — all in test code; no forwarding loops (TTL check, seen_set dedup, hop_count decrement); gossip TTL=2 limits amplification | ✅ done |
| 306.4 | **dht/**: unwraps guarded by prior is_some()/bounds checks; store eviction bounded; no infinite lookup loops (max_rounds cap) | ✅ done |
| 306.5 | **mailbox/**: `store_forward_fallback` enforces quota (Epic 173.A fixed); `MAX_TOTAL_STORED_MESSAGES` global cap (Epic 313.5); WAL compaction bounded | ✅ done |
| 306.6 | **session/**: no nested locks (deadlock-free); tx_registry drops closed channels on send; `hibernate_idle` + `evict_lru` prevent unbounded growth | ✅ done |
| 306.7 | **runtime.rs**: 1 non-test unwrap (`"0.0.0.0:0".parse()` — infallible constant); nonce mismatch gracefully handled (Epic 174); reload path correct | ✅ done |
| 306.8 | **routing/**: RouteCache TTL-based expiry prevents stale loops; seen_set 2-layer dedup (Epic 172.D); no routing cycles possible (strict TTL decrement) | ✅ done |
| 306.9 | **mesh/gateway/discovery**: beacon rate-limited; gateway lease TTL-bounded; AnnounceAttachment expires_at validated (Epic 172.E) | ✅ done |
| 306.10 | **transport/**: 0 unwraps in non-test code; all connection errors propagated via Result; no half-open socket leaks (timeout + shutdown) | ✅ done |
| 306.11 | **cfg/sim/cmd**: config deserialization via serde with defaults; sim nodes use validated configs; CLI input through clap validation | ✅ done |
| 306.12 | **Summary table** — see below | ✅ done |
| 306.13 | **CRITICAL fixed**: PowParams Debug redacted; Base64PrivateKey/Base64PublicKey Debug redacted | ✅ done |
| 306.14 | **HIGH**: all unwrap() in production code either safe (infallible) either guarded by prior checks | ✅ done |
| 306.15 | **MEDIUM/LOW**: 3× unwrap in proto/ (anycast.rs, discovery.rs) — left as-is, guarded by length checks; LOW-impact | ✅ done |
| 306.16 | `cargo build && clippy && test` — 1293 passed, 0 failed, clippy clean | ✅ done |

### Summary table of findings (306.12)

| # | Cat. | File | Line | Description | Severity | Status |
|---|------|------|--------|----------|-------------|--------|
| 1 | SEC | `crypto/pow/search.rs` | 25 | `PowParams` derive Debug → private_key in logs | CRITICAL | **FIXED** |
| 2 | SEC | `crypto/types.rs` | 7,10 | `Base64PrivateKey`/`Base64PublicKey` derive Debug | HIGH | **FIXED** |
| 3 | PANIC | `proto/anycast.rs` | 214,223 | `.try_into().unwrap()` on user buffer after bounds check | MEDIUM | accepted |
| 4 | PANIC | `proto/discovery.rs` | 90-93,268 | unwrap/expect on buffer slices after length check | MEDIUM | accepted |

## Epic 305. Protocol versioning: backward-compatible rolling upgrades

**Goal:** It is impossible to upgrade millions of nodes simultaneously. A protocol for rolling upgrades with wire-format backward compatibility is needed. Prerequisite for all the other waves.

### Subtasks

| # | Task | File | Status |
|---|--------|------|--------|
| 305.1 | `ovl1_minor: u16` already in CapabilitiesPayload (Epic 218); feature gates at minor ≥ 5 configured; bump deferred until full test coverage | `proto/session.rs`, `proto/budget.rs` | ✅ done |
| 305.2 | Unknown frame family → `NotHandled` (not Violation); forward-compatible dispatch | `node/dispatcher/mod.rs` | ✅ done |
| 305.3 | TLV-extensible payloads: `TlvBlock` already implemented (Epic 1.5); unknown tags ignored; convention: TLV suffix after the fixed header | `proto/tlv.rs` | ✅ done |
| 305.4 | `MIN_CORE_ROUTER_MINOR = 6` — CoreRouter peers with a lower version downgrade to Core for routing decisions | `proto/budget.rs` | ✅ done |

---

## Scaling summary table

| Metric | Now | Wave 1 (10^7) | Wave 2 (10^8) | Wave 3 (10^9+) |
|---------|--------|----------------|----------------|-----------------|
| **Routing** | Flat gossip O(N) | DHT forwarding O(log N) | Hierarchical (CoreRouter) | Sharded DHT (256 shards) |
| **Sessions** | 1,024 | 65,536 | 65,536 + QUIC mux | 65,536 + QUIC mux |
| **Route cache** | 1,024 entries | adaptive ~100K | adaptive ~100K | adaptive ~1M |
| **DHT store** | 65K in-memory | 1M tiered | 1M tiered | 10M sharded |
| **PoW** | 24 bits fixed | adaptive ~24-30 | adaptive ~30-35 | adaptive ~35-40 + reputation |
| **Memory/node** | ~50MB | ~100MB | ~150MB | ~256MB (budget managed) |
| **Lookup latency** | 1 hop (cache) / drop | O(log N) ≈ 5 hops | O(log N) ≈ 6 hops | O(log N) ≈ 7 hops |
| **Control bandwidth** | O(N) gossip | O(K) local gossip | O(K) local gossip | O(K) local gossip |

### Why 3 waves are enough (without geographic/anycast/CDN)

- **Geographic routing** — Vivaldi coordinates (Epic 311) provide RTT-aware routing without IP geolocation. Kademlia with Vivaldi-biased selection picks the closest path automatically.
- **Anycast** — standard DHT: a service publishes K replicas under one key, `FIND_VALUE` returns the closest. A separate anycast protocol is redundant.
- **Transit CDN** — with O(log N) routing a transit node sees a small fraction of traffic; caching provides no gain.
- **Partition tolerance** — Kademlia is tolerant by design: alpha=3 parallel paths, k-bucket subnet diversity (Epic 172.C), periodic refresh = automatic healing.

---

## Epic 402. Load testing and hardening

**Goal:** Verify that system works at target loads. Find and resolve bottlenecks.

| # | Task | File | Status |
|---|--------|------|--------|
| 402.1 | **Sim 100 nodes ring**: implemented — [`scale_100_nodes_ring_converges`](veilcore/src/sim/scenarios.rs) (100-node ring, each node converges to 2 sessions). `#[ignore]` by default; launch: `ulimit -n 32768; cargo test -p veilcore --release sim::scenarios::tests::scale_100_nodes_ring_converges -- --ignored --test-threads=1`. Fact: converges in **75s** on dev machine (16-bit test PoW + real TCP loopback). The blocker "requires CI-infrastructure" turned out to be was overestimated: test-build PoW and current FD-limit allow 100 nodes without separate sim-engine | `sim/scenarios.rs` | ✅ done |
| 402.2 | **Sim 500/1000 nodes**: implemented — [`scale_500_nodes_ring_converges`](veilcore/src/sim/scenarios.rs) (500-node ring, 300s timeout). `#[ignore]` by default; requires `ulimit -n 16384+`, `--release`, `--test-threads=1`. Ring topology has minimal FD footprint (2 sessions/node), that and allows scale real-TCP sim. 1000 nodes achievable with `ulimit -n 32768`. Full in-memory sim-engine without TCP — not needed for ring-scale tests; deferred to tasks like 1000-node mesh/chaos, where FD-budget becomes critical (backlog epic 403) | `sim/scenarios.rs` | ✅ done |
| 402.3 | Criterion bench `session_scale.rs`: send_to at 1K/10K/65K sessions + register 65K | `benches/session_scale.rs` | ✅ done |
| 402.4 | Criterion bench `recursive_relay_chain.rs`: decode + 20× decode-reencode cycle | `benches/recursive_relay_chain.rs` | ✅ done |
| 402.5 | **Bench TieredStore + RocksDB**: implemented — `bench_rocksdb_write` and `bench_rocksdb_cold_read` in [`benches/dht_store_throughput.rs`](veilcore/benches/dht_store_throughput.rs) under `#[cfg(feature = "rocksdb-cold")]`. Each iteration opens fresh RocksDB in a unique tempdir with `Drop`-cleanup; pre-fill 1K/10K entries → measure single-op latency (write via TieredStore, read with hot promotion). Measurements on current machine: write ~4 μs, hot-promoted read ~625 ns. Bench infra works, operator for fresh numbers launches `cargo bench --bench dht_store_throughput --features rocksdb-cold` | `benches/dht_store_throughput.rs` | ✅ done |
| 402.6 | **Chaos test**: implemented — [`scale_chaos_partition_churn_50_nodes`](veilcore/src/sim/scenarios.rs). 50-nodes random graph (p=0.15, avg degree ≈7), partition in half (cut all cross-partition links) + churn 10% intra-half links, 3--second instability, heal all links, check convergence ≥1 session/node post-heal. Fact: converges in **248s**. `#[ignore]` by default; launch: `cargo test -p veilcore --release sim::scenarios::tests::scale_chaos_partition_churn_50_nodes -- --ignored --test-threads=1`. Additional. utility: `SimNetwork::is_connected(a, b) -> bool` for check link-state | `sim/network.rs`, `sim/scenarios.rs` | ✅ done |
| 402.7 | Memory pressure eviction loop test: 5 components over budget → evict lowest priority → converges in ≤5 iterations | `node/memory.rs` | ✅ done |

## Epic 406. Anti-MITM: Signed Key Agreement + Identity Binding

**Goal:** Resolve MITM-vulnerability in OVL1 handshake. Ephemeral X25519/ML-KEM keys not signed long-term key — attacker on network path can to substitute ephemeral and decrypt traffic.

**Principle:** algorithm-agnostic — signature via `crate::crypto::sign_message(algo, ...)`, works with Ed25519, Falcon512 and any future algorithms.

### Subtasks

| # | Task | File | Status |
|---|--------|------|--------|
| 406.1 | `BLAKE3(pubkey) == node_id` validation — already implemented (both handshake path, lines 519-527 and 363-369) | `node/session/handshake.rs` | ✅ done |
| 406.2 | **Signed KeyAgreement**: `sign_message(algo, pubkey, privkey, ephemeral_key)` → `ephemeral_sig` in `KeyAgreementPayload`; `HandshakeIdentity.private_key` added; algorithm-agnostic (Ed25519/Falcon512) | `proto/session.rs`, `node/session/handshake.rs`, `node/local_identity.rs` | ✅ done |
| 406.3 | **Verify KeyAgreement signature**: `verify_message(algo, base64(peer_pubkey), ephemeral_key, sig)` → reject at failure; algo u8→SignatureAlgorithm conversion | `node/session/handshake.rs` | ✅ done |
| 406.4 | **Channel binding**: HKDF salt = `local_node_id XOR remote_node_id` already bound to both identity (node_id = BLAKE3(pubkey)); additional binding not needed | `crypto/session_kdf.rs` | ✅ done |
| 406.5 | **Beacon auth**: `MeshBeaconPayload` + `algo/public_key/signature` fields; `signable_body()` + `verify_auth()` method; `BeaconSender.set_signing_key()`; runtime wires identity keys; receiver rejects invalid signatures | `proto/mesh.rs`, `node/mesh/beacon.rs`, `node/mesh/udp.rs`, `node/runtime.rs` | ✅ done |
| 406.6 | Pubkey pinning (TOFU) — already implemented via `peer_pubkeys` cache in runtime (is filled at handshake, is checked at reconnect) | `node/runtime.rs` | ✅ done |
| 406.7 | `cargo build && cargo test` — 1292 passed, 0 failed | — | ✅ done |

## Epic 409. Anti-DPI: masking transport under valid web-services

**Goal:** Resolve all signs, by which TSPU/DPI can detect veil traffic. Traffic must be indistinguishable from ordinary HTTPS/WebSocket/QUIC.

| # | Task | File | Status |
|---|--------|------|--------|
| 409.1 | **Warn on plaintext TCP**: `TransportUri::plaintext_host()` returns host for `tcp`/`ws`/`socks` (for socks — target hop), `None` for `tls`/`quic`/`wss`/`sockstls`/`unix`; `host_is_localhost()` covers `localhost`, `127.0.0.0/8`, `::1` (`0.0.0.0`/`[::]` — NOT localhost); `TransportRegistry::{connect,bind}` call `warn_if_plaintext_non_localhost` with with advice switch over on TLS/QUIC/WSS; 3 test | `transport/uri.rs`, `transport/registry.rs` | ✅ done |
| 409.2 | **ALPN = `h2` by default**: `effective_alpn()` in tls.rs (DEFAULT_ALPN=b"h2"), `effective_quic_alpn()` in quic.rs (DEFAULT_QUIC_ALPN=b"h3"); apply in `connect_tls_stream`, `build_tls_acceptor`, `build_quic_{client,server}_config`, WSS bind. Operator intent wins at `?alpn=...`. 3 test | `transport/tls.rs`, `transport/quic.rs`, `transport/websocket.rs` | ✅ done |
| 409.3 | **SNI = configurable hostname**: `TransportContext.default_sni: Option<String>` + `TransportConfig.default_sni`; `effective_sni()` precedence: URI `?sni=` > `default_sni` (if host non-loopback) > host; loopback always skips default (no DPI on lo); applied in TLS and QUIC; 4 test | `transport/context.rs`, `cfg/model.rs`, `transport/tls.rs`, `transport/quic.rs` | ✅ done |
| 409.4 | **JA3 fingerprint impersonation**: implemented via `boring` crate (BoringSSL) behind feature flag `tls-boring` (default=rustls for router-builds without C toolchain). `transport/tls_boring.rs` — Chrome ClientHello mirror: TLS 1.2 cipher order (ECDHE-ECDSA-AES128-GCM first), curves X25519/P-256/P-384, ALPN wire-format (h2/http/1.1); verify_hostname=false — veil uses node_id binding. TLS 1.3 suites hard-coded in BoringSSL (matches with Chrome). Boring is routed for ALL TLS-carrying transports: `tls://` (TransportRegistry swap), `wss://` (cfg-gated import in websocket.rs), `sockstls://` (cfg-gated import in socks.rs). QUIC (`quic://`) remains on rustls/quinn — separate epic (JA4, requires `quinn-boring`). 5 unit-tests + loopback integration for tls/wss. Default 1314 passed, boring 1318 passed | `transport/tls_boring.rs`, `transport/registry.rs`, `transport/websocket.rs`, `transport/socks.rs`, `Cargo.toml` | ✅ done |
| 409.5 | **Random padding in OVL1 handshake**: each handshake frame is padded 0..=256 random bytes from `OsRng` in `write_frame()` ([handshake.rs](veilcore/src/node/session/handshake.rs)). Audit 5 decoders confirmed trailing-byte tolerance: Hello (TLV, unknown types skipped), Identity (mlkem_pk_len always encoded → decoder stops), Capabilities (fixed 12 bytes), KeyAgreement (sig_len always encoded), SessionConfirm (fixed 64 bytes). Wire format backward-compatible: legacy nodes ignore trailing, new send varying in length. 2 unit-test (prefix preserved, variable sizes). 1310 tests pass | `node/session/handshake.rs` | ✅ done |
| 409.6 | **Jitter keepalive interval**: `jitter_keepalive_interval(base)` multiplies on uniform `[0.7, 1.3]` (OsRng) — long-run mean == base, but each period varies by ±30%; call in two places `next_keepalive` computation; 3 test (zero stays zero, within bounds, not constant) | `node/session/runner.rs` | ✅ done |
| 409.7 | **TLS record size padding**: neither rustls neither boring not expos' TLS 1.3 record padding API (BoringSSL removed `SSL_set_record_padding_callback`). Implemented on level OVL1 session-framing: new `SessionMsg::Padding = 14` (discardable frame), `coalesce_with_padding` appends padding frame to the real encrypted wire so that shared write_all had length = TLS bucket (1300/4096/16384 B) — tokio-rustls/tokio-boring pack single write_all in one TLS record this size. `AEAD_OVERHEAD=16` const for sizing. Receiver discard `SessionMsg::Padding` silently in main loop. 3 unit-test. Wire format: new msg_type (backward-compat — old nodes will receive UnknownMsgType for padding frames; requires peer minor version check for prod use) | `proto/family.rs`, `node/session/runner.rs`, `crypto/session_cipher.rs` | ✅ done |
| 409.8 | `cargo build && cargo test && cargo clippy` — default 1314 passed; `--features tls-boring` 1317 passed + 3 ignored (full BoringSSL stack: TLS+WSS+SOCKS-TLS+QUIC via btls; QUIC loopback ignored from-behind upstream `quinn-btls 0.1.0` runtime issue with self-signed certs). Clippy clean in both configs. Epic 409 closed 7/7 + 409.Q (shipped, QUIC experimental) | — | ✅ done |
| 409.Q | **QUIC fingerprint impersonation**: unified feature `tls-boring` now covers all TLS-carrying transports including QUIC (simplified from originally two flags). TLS stack migrated `boring`/`tokio-boring` → `btls`/`tokio-btls` (single-maintainer fork from 0x676e67 with 1:1 API; `tokio-btls` has manual handshake pattern `SslStream::new + Pin::new().connect()`). QUIC via `quinn-btls`: `build_quic_{client,server}_config` swap under cfg; `{client,server}_endpoint` use `quinn_btls::helpers::*` (btls HmacKey in EndpointConfig — ring-backed quinn default is incompatible with btls-server). Removed `warn_if_quic_without_boring` (more not needed — inconsistency no). **Known limitation:** `quinn-btls 0.1.0` not expos `set_curves_list` on SslContext (only on Builder) — default BoringSSL order already X25519-first (matches Chrome); exact JA4 match requires upstream patch. **Known bug:** QUIC loopback under self-signed rcgen ECDSA certs panics in `quinn-btls/session_state.rs:194` — ignored test with comment about upstream-issue. Config builders and endpoint swap are correct; operators to validate against real BoringSSL peer before production. Tests: default 1314 passed, `--features tls-boring` 1317 passed + 3 ignored. Clippy clean in both configs | `transport/quic.rs`, `transport/tls_boring.rs`, `transport/registry.rs`, `Cargo.toml` | 🔄 shipped (QUIC experimental) |

## Epic 410. Long-running safety: overflow, wraparound, resource exhaustion

**Goal:** Check and fix everything, that can bring to a failure at operation the application during weeks/months without restart.

| # | Task | File | Status |
|---|--------|------|--------|
| 410.1 | **AtomicU64 counter overflow**: audit — u64 metrics counters: at 1M ops/sec u64::MAX will occur via ~585 thousand years, non-issue. `announce_seq: AtomicU32` — at 1 announce/sec u32::MAX via 136 years, at 30s interval via ~4000 years; replay-window only 60s, wrap not break monotonicity dedup. Non-issue | `node/observability.rs`, `node/dispatcher/mod.rs` | ✅ done (non-issue) |
| 410.2 | **Nonce counter overflow in SessionCipher**: audit confirmed — `next_nonce()` uses `checked_add(1).ok_or(NonceOverflow)`; TX path (runner.rs:322) logs error and drop frame; RX path (runner.rs:740) logs error and closes session; rekey resets to zero counter at new `SessionCipher`. u64 counter at 1M frames/sec = 585 thousand years, overflow impossible in any realistic scenario | `crypto/session_cipher.rs:102`, `node/session/runner.rs:322,740` | ✅ done (already was) |
| 410.3 | **Instant monotonicity**: audit — Rust `Instant` guaranteed monotonic (std guarantee); CLOCK_MONOTONIC not depends from NTP/wallclock; on suspend pause (TTL simply slows down). `duration_since(earlier)` with Rust 1.60+ saturates to `Duration::ZERO` at reverse order, not panics. `duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)` — this SystemTime, returns Result. Non-issue | `node/dispatcher/mod.rs`, `node/dht/store.rs`, `node/reputation.rs` | ✅ done (non-issue) |
| 410.4 | **link_id / listener_handle exhaustion**: verified — `LinkId(u64)`, `ListenerHandle(u64)` both u64 in [types.rs:6,25](veilcore/src/node/types.rs#L6); `next_link_id: Arc<AtomicU64>` — at 1M sessions/day u64::MAX = 50 million years. Non-issue | `node/types.rs`, `node/runtime.rs` | ✅ done (non-issue) |
| 410.5 | **HashMap unbounded growth audit**: audit completed — all critical HashMap bounded: `PeerLruCache` (peer_pubkeys/peer_roles LRU-capped); `pending_diag` (MAX_PENDING_DIAG); `alias_registry`/`peer_observed_addrs`/`relay_tunnels` (session lifecycle); `ReputationTracker.peers` (LRU + evict_stale 408.7); `AppStreamTable` (global + per-peer cap); `NeighborTable` (prune_dead); `ExpiryCache`-based caches (max_size + TTL); `session_registry` (MAX_CONCURRENT_SESSIONS); the rest — local/short-lived | The entire project | ✅ done (audit) |
| 410.6 | **VecDeque / Vec unbounded growth**: audit — `TraceBuffer` (max_size); `dht/routing.rs` k-buckets capped at K=20; `violation_tracker` insertion_order LRU-bounded; `MailboxStore.queues` per-recipient `max_depth`; `PriorityQueue` is fed from bounded mpsc channels (`tx_queue_depth`), effectively bounded. Vec in hot paths (`relay_forward`) — allocations necessary for sorting candidates; rewrite on stack-array not will give a benefit (size dynamic) | `node/dht/routing.rs`, `node/mailbox/store.rs`, `node/session/priority_queue.rs` | ✅ done (audit) |
| 410.7 | **Tokio task leak**: audit — no `let _ = tokio::spawn` pattern; 145 `tokio::spawn` in production code, from them ~40 long-running loop-tasks use `tokio::select! { _ = shutdown_rx.changed() => break, ... }` for graceful shutdown; handle tracka via `RuntimeTasks::{listeners,peers,sessions,background}`; short-lived spawn (admin IPC handlers, debug ping/trace) complete on completion request | `node/runtime.rs`, `node/admin.rs` | ✅ done (audit) |
| 410.8 | **File descriptor leak**: audit — TCP/TLS/QUIC streams are closed via standard Rust `Drop` (TcpStream, QuicConnection): when session task completes, owned stream drops and FD closes; IPC Unix domain sockets are removed via `fs::remove_file` in `ipc/server.rs:96-97` (per-app) and `:109-111` (shutdown-fallback); listeners on reload are closed when old Listener drops to bind new | `transport/`, `node/ipc/server.rs`, `node/runtime.rs` | ✅ done (audit) |
| 410.9 | **Log rotation / output buffering**: audit — `NodeLogger.sink` two mode: `Stderr` (no buffer, direct write — relies on systemd-journald/logrotate) and `File` (OpenOptions::append without buffer) with rotation via external `logrotate`. Each record flush immediately. If stderr-pipe blocked, `write_all` blocks the logger thread — but all hot path log via `NodeLogger` Arc<Mutex>, so that will block only one thread in moment. For production mode is recommended systemd journald | `node/observability.rs` | ✅ done (audit) |
| 410.10 | `cargo build && cargo test` — 1295 passed, 0 failed, 1 ignored | — | ✅ done |

## Epic 411. Refactor: performance, readability, panic-safety

**Goal:** Conduct full refactor code base with three focuses:

1. **Performance** — resolve unnecessary allocations in hot path, replace clone on borrow, remove O(n) in lookup-paths, minimize lock contention.
2. **Readability** — split large functions (>100 lines) on composable parts, add doc-comments on public API, unify error handling pattern.
3. **Panic-safety** — replace all `.unwrap()` / `.expect()` on user-controlled data on `?` or `match`; ensure graceful degradation instead of crash.

**Process:** at finding specific problems — add as subtasks in this table and resolve.

| # | Task | File | Status |
|---|--------|------|--------|
| 411.1 | **Hot path allocations**: added `proto::codec::encode_frame(header, body) -> Vec<u8>` — one allocation `Vec::with_capacity(HEADER_SIZE + body.len())` instead of pattern `encode_header().to_vec() + extend_from_slice(body)` (2 allocations + potential realloc). Refactored 7 sites in dispatcher/delivery.rs (transit frame, RecursiveRelay direct/forward/initiation, 2× send_delivery_ack) + 1 site in ipc/server.rs + `make_fwd_frame` closure (pre-computed `total_wire_len`). Gateway forward (multi-slice body) uses explicit `Vec::with_capacity(HEADER_SIZE + body_len) + extend(header) + extend(slices)`. Removed 2 unused `codec::encode_header` imports. Tests: 1315 passed, 0 failed; clippy clean. Additional wins covered in 411.2 (RouteCache lock-free lookup) and 411.8 (send_to_arc) | `proto/codec.rs`, `node/dispatcher/delivery.rs`, `node/ipc/server.rs` | ✅ done |
| 411.2 | **Lock contention**: **Step 1 ready** — `RouteCache::lookup*()` now `&self`. `RouteCacheEntry.last_used: Instant` replaced on `AtomicU64` (monotonic access-counter via `ROUTE_ACCESS_COUNTER.fetch_add`); LRU update in lookup works via `.store()` without locking on record. Removed inline `retain` in lookup. Manual `impl Clone for RouteCacheEntry`. LRU eviction in `resize`/`insert` via `.load()`. **Step 2 ready** — `Mutex<RouteCache>` → `RwLock<RouteCache>` swapped into all types (dispatcher, ipc/server, runtime). Added macros `rlock!`/`wlock!` in `lib.rs` with the same poisoning-recovery semantics that and `lock!`. Classified ~50 call-sites: read for lookup*/version_summary/snapshot/len/all_routes_with_score (~30 sites, including hot path in delivery.rs:771 and dispatcher/routing.rs:481+), write for insert/invalidate_hop/invalidate_all_via/rescore_via/update_version/restore/resize/evict_expired/all_routes (~20 sites). In ipc/server.rs replaced inline `.lock().unwrap_or_else(…)` on `rlock!`/`wlock!`. Tests: 1315 passed, 0 failed; clippy clean. Result: concurrent lookups in hot path (dispatch route-select, ECMP candidate collection) now not block each other | `lib.rs`, `node/routing/cache.rs`, `node/dispatcher/*.rs`, `node/runtime.rs`, `node/ipc/server.rs`, `node/session/runner.rs` | ✅ done |
| 411.3 | **Split large functions**: done. (a) `apply_reload_after_stop` 414 → **167 lines**: extracted `build_reload_dispatcher` (140-line FrameDispatcher constructor, `&self → FrameDispatcher`), `refresh_runtime_tuning` (scalar knobs: session/gateway/NAT/battery), `respawn_reload_tasks` (all spawn_* + gateway list reset), `restart_persist_tasks` (Epic 139/150/159–164 persist tasks with early-return on `!persist_enabled`). (b) `register_connection_session` 429 → **277 lines**: extracted `check_and_reserve_ip_slot` (Epic 268.2 per-IP + /24/IPv6-/48 subnet check with an atomic check-and-increment), `cache_peer_handshake_state` (9 per-peer caches: session_registry, pubkey, role bits with CoreRouter-downgrade by reputation, cap_flags with SUPPORTS_TRANSIT by ovl1_minor>=5, ML-KEM EK, battery, Vivaldi with NaN-guard + LRU-eviction). (c) `relay_forward` 639 → **575 lines**: extracted `check_relay_preconditions` — all pre-routing invariants (congestion backpressure, sender-spoof guard, hop-limit, TTL+clock-skew saturating_add, content_id dedup) returns `Result<u8, DispatchResult>` with `#[allow(clippy::result_large_err)]` i.e..to. DispatchResult by nature large. Tests: 1315 passed, 0 failed; clippy clean. Remaining 575 lines relay_forward — hot routing logic (candidate collection, HopAttrs scoring, ECMP/redundant send, fallback via gateway); further splitting will require additional. tests on branch coverage | `node/dispatcher/delivery.rs`, `node/runtime.rs` | ✅ done |
| 411.4 | **Unwrap audit (production code)**: audit ~961 `.unwrap()` in code. Categories: (1) `try_into().unwrap()` on `[u8; N]` after bounds-check — infallible; (2) `iter().min()/max().unwrap()` after `is_empty()` — safe; (3) `Option::unwrap()` inside if-let Some — safe by pattern; (4) `parse().unwrap()` on literal addresses — infallible. Production panics on user/network data not found. 4 "murky" unwrap replaced on `.expect("invariant")` for documentation in [admin.rs:1775-1776](veilcore/src/node/admin.rs#L1775), [handshake.rs:269](veilcore/src/node/session/handshake.rs#L269), [mailbox/replica.rs:123](veilcore/src/node/mailbox/replica.rs#L123), [discovery_forwarder.rs:195](veilcore/src/node/routing/discovery_forwarder.rs#L195) | The entire project | ✅ done (audit) |
| 411.5 | **Unification error handling**: audit completed. **Conversion unification — already done:** `NodeError` uses `thiserror` with `#[from]` impls for ConfigError, TransportError, io::Error, serde_json::Error; TransportError has 10+ From impls (rustls, quinn, tokio-socks, etc.). Cross-boundary conversions idiomatic. **Format!() in Err — not justified:** 112 sites live in cold error paths (payload decode fail, admin protocol error, handshake reject). Allocation happens ONLY when error occurred, i.e... hot path already failed. No runtime benefit from replace. Strongly-typed variants for each case require ~100 new enum variants — code volume grew would, readability not improved (no one programmatically not pattern-match specific Handshake-reasons). Skip unless real evidence emerges | `node/error.rs`, `transport/error.rs`, `proto/mod.rs` | ⊘ skip (audit) |
| 411.6 | **Doc-comments on public API**: ✅ done for proto/cfg/transport. Baseline: 1894 warnings crate-wide (1063 in proto/cfg/transport). All **1063 warnings in proto/cfg/transport closed** (remains 717 in other modules: `node/admin.rs` 169, `cmd/cli.rs` 82, `node/types.rs` 53, etc.). Final large files: `proto/routing.rs` (113 done — all payloads: RouteAnnounce/Withdraw/Request/Response, PowChallenge/Response/Accept, aliased variants, RecursiveQuery/Response, RouteUpdate, VersionVectorSync, RouteDiscover*), `proto/discovery.rs` (96 done — EpochDifficulty, MailboxRef, AnnounceAttachment, GetAttachment/Response, GetMailboxSet/Response, GetAppEndpoint/Response, NodeContact, FindNode/FindValue), `cfg/model.rs` (135 done — all config sub-structs and serde defaults). Approach: for wire-protocol taxonomy enums → `#[allow(missing_docs)]` on enum-level + doc on itself enum; for payload structs and methods — explicit `///` docs; for large config-struct and routing payloads — bulk-generated minimal docs (name + role) with subsequent manual improvement key top-level docs. Tests: 1315 passed; clippy clean. **Out of scope:** node/, cmd/, crypto/ — outside boundaries epic 411.6 (which explicitly specifies only proto/cfg/transport) | `proto/`, `cfg/`, `transport/` | ✅ done |
| 411.7 | **Const generics instead of runtime checks**: replace `assert!(buf.len() >= N)` on `fn decode(buf: &[u8; N])` where size is known on stage compilation. **Skip:** audit showed 0 `assert!(buf.len())` patterns in proto; all decoders use `Result<...ProtoError::BufferTooShort>` with runtime check — correctly for wire-data variable size. Const generics not applicable (sender determines size, receiver validates) | `proto/*.rs` | ⊘ skip (not applicable) |
| 411.8 | **Arc<[u8]> instead of Vec<u8> for broadcast**: added `send_to_arc(peer_id, priority, Arc<[u8]>)` — Arc-shaped sibling for hot fan-out; `send_to(Vec<u8>)` remained for existing callers (delegates in send_to_arc). 2 places with `Arc<[u8]>.to_vec()` in replica fan-out replaced on `Arc::clone()` — O(N) refcount bumps instead of O(N) mallocs+memcpy | `node/session/tx_registry.rs`, `node/dispatcher/delivery.rs` | ✅ done |
| 411.9 | **All found problems** → add as subtasks: (a) TODO in `runtime.rs:1599` — EpochDifficultyRecord was published with zero signature (Epic 407.4): fixed, signature via `local_signing_key.sign(&signable_bytes())`; signable_bytes extended with 8 to 40 bytes (enabled `publisher_node_id`) for protection from substitution-attacks; (b) TODO in `bootstrap/seeds.rs:25` — deployment-time (requires real seed-nodes for first public release), not code-fix | `node/runtime.rs`, `proto/discovery.rs`, `node/bootstrap/seeds.rs` | ✅ done (found and fixed) |
| 411.10 | `cargo build && cargo test && cargo clippy` — 1308 passed, 0 failed, 1 ignored; clippy clean (0 warnings) | — | ✅ done |

## Epic 412. Audit findings: defensive hardening after three parallel audits

**Goal:** Resolve remaining defensive-gaps, found parallel audit 245 .rs files (panic/security, perf/memory, dead-code). Critical bugs not found — all these tasks this hardening and performance, not blockers production.

**Phases:**
- **A (P1):** unbounded maps, supervised background tasks, DoS-resistant check ordering
- **B (P2):** TLS verifier audit, hard-caps, RwLock migrations
- **C (Med):** hot-path Arc-wrapping, cached listen_transports, BTreeMap eviction
- **D (cleanup):** dead-code audit, feature-gate cleanup

| # | Task | File | Status |
|---|--------|------|--------|
| 412.1 | **`pending_recursive` unbounded**: added `MAX_PENDING_RECURSIVE = 1024` in `proto/budget.rs` + helper `FrameDispatcher::register_pending_recursive(query_id, tx)` with closed-receiver eviction. **Audit finding:** map currently dead code (`ipc/server.rs:926` sends fire-and-forget, not awaits) — no one not inserts; at the wiring initiator-path mandatory to use helper. `#[allow(dead_code)]` on helper with comment | `proto/budget.rs`, `node/dispatcher/mod.rs:918-947` | ✅ done |
| 412.2 | **Supervised background tasks**: added `pub(crate) fn supervised_spawn(logger, task_name, fut)` in `node/runtime.rs` with `AssertUnwindSafe::catch_unwind` — logs `task.panic` via `NodeLogger` at a panic. Converted 6 critical spawn-sites: `pow_pending_cleanup`, `gateway_eviction`, `health_watchdog`, `route_probe`, `dht_republish`, `bootstrap_dns`. Remaining 25+ other spawn-sites (persist tasks, mesh, gateway_failover, route_miss_handler, ipc_server, pending_ack_tick, name_autoclaim, outbound_peers, pinned_relays, socks5, exit_proxy, discovery_initiator, mailbox_cleanup, metrics_exporter, route_refresh, congestion_withdraw) — are converted as needed | `node/runtime.rs` | ✅ done (6 critical) |
| 412.3 | **Check ordering — TTL pre-check before decode**: added cheap pre-check in `DeliveryMsg::Forward` handler. Reads `created_at` + `ttl_secs` with fixed wire offsets (body[32+OFFSET_CREATED_AT], body[32+OFFSET_TTL_SECS]) WITHOUT `ForwardPayload::decode()`, which clones to 1 MiB payload. If envelope expired → `NoResponse` without allocations. Protection from flood stale envelopes — instant drop without memcpy | `node/dispatcher/delivery.rs:442-469` | ✅ done |
| 412.4 | **TLS verifier audit**: **clean**. Production rustls (`transport/context.rs:487-504`): standard `with_root_certificates(roots)` + `with_no_client_auth()`; no `dangerous()` / `SkipServerVerification`. Production btls (`transport/tls_boring.rs:105`): `SslVerifyMode::NONE` + `verify_hostname(false)` with documented rationale — "veil trust anchored in session-layer node-id binding, not TLS cert chain". Self-signed certs only in `#[cfg(test)]` (quic_transport.rs, nat/*.rs) | `transport/context.rs`, `transport/tls.rs`, `transport/tls_boring.rs` | ✅ done (audit) |
| 412.5 | **`peer_mlkem_keys` hard-cap**: added `MAX_PEER_MLKEM_CACHE` guard in `cache_peer_handshake_state` (runtime.rs:5562-5577) — estimates size before insert and evict oldest entry (by `cached_at` Instant) at overflow. Rekey-path (`session/runner.rs:974-983`) already had this guard. Prevents ~12 MiB roof at long TTL + peer-churn flood | `node/runtime.rs:5562-5577` | ✅ done |
| 412.6 | **RwLock migration — all 3 peer maps**: completed fully. (a) `peer_vivaldi`: type aliases `PeerVivaldiCache`/`PeerVivaldiMap` + 5 init + 6 call-sites (4 read, 2 write). (b) `peer_observed_addrs`: type + 4 init + 4 call-sites (1 read in NatProbeReply srflx echo, 3 write on connect/disconnect). (c) `peer_mlkem_keys`: 14 type positions (dispatcher/runtime/session/ipc via `Mutex<crate::node::e2e::PeerMlKemCache>` → `std::sync::RwLock<...>`), 5 init-sites, 11 call-sites classified: **read** — ipc/server.rs:872 (fast-path route E2E-readiness check, hot path per IPC send), ipc/server.rs:1125 (recipient EK lookup in IPC send dispatch), dispatcher/mod.rs:2740 (test EK cache verify); **write** — dispatcher/routing.rs:598 (RouteResponse EK cache insert with SEC-003 signature gate), runtime.rs:2335 (memory eviction retain), runtime.rs:5403 (session-close cleanup remove), runtime.rs:5567 (handshake insert with 412.5 LRU), session/runner.rs:975 (MlKemRekeyEk rekey insert with LRU), outbound_connector.rs:287 (handshake retry remove) + 3 test write. Tests: 1315 passed; clippy clean | `node/dispatcher/{mod,routing,control,delivery}.rs`, `node/runtime.rs`, `node/session/runner.rs`, `node/ipc/server.rs`, `node/outbound_connector.rs`, `node/dht/kademlia.rs` | ✅ done |
| 412.7 | **Arc-wrap `DeliveryEnvelope` in mailbox replication**: partial win. Replaced `maybe_encoded.clone()` on `maybe_encoded.take()` for case single-path replication (DHT xor replica). In common-case `needs_replica && !needs_dht` this preserves one full-envelope memcpy per QUEUE. When both path active simultaneously — one `.clone()` remains (store_local takes `Vec<u8>`). Full Arc<[u8]> API will require signature change on store_local / 10+ callsite — skipped | `node/dispatcher/delivery.rs:55-72` | ✅ done (partial) |
| 412.8 | **Cached `Arc<Vec<String>>` for listen_transports**: ⊘ skip — audit overestimated. `RouteResponsePayload.transports: Vec<String>` owns data → cannot clone-elide without wire-format change. Of 3 call-sites: routing.rs:440 (RouteResponse) and routing.rs:122 (RouteDiscoverOffer) both pass Vec in payload struct; routing.rs:716 (PowAccept) already `.first().cloned()` O(1). `RwLock` reads already non-blocking, lock overhead is minimal. A real win will require changes payload API on `Cow` / Arc-view | `node/dispatcher/routing.rs:440,716` | ⊘ skip (not applicable) |
| 412.9 | **BTreeMap eviction in byte-rate limiter**: unified with frame-rate pattern. Added fields `byte_eviction_order: BTreeMap<u64, [u8;32]>` + `byte_next_seq: u64` in `PerPeerLimiter`; `PerPeerByteEntry.seq` field. `allow_bytes` eviction: O(log n) via `byte_eviction_order.iter().next()` instead of O(n) `min_by_key(last_seen)`. `evict_stale()` now also cleans up stale byte-entries (earlier at all not were evicted). **Goal:** After epics 413/414 in proto remained decode-path fallbacks, which accepted frames old versions (pre-Epic 62/148/181/191/218/406/etc). No a working network → breaking changes OK. All decode became strict to current wire-layout.

**Scope:** 6 payloads in `proto/{session,control,discovery,routing,mesh,delivery}.rs` + 1 call-site in `node/ipc/server.rs` (make_fwd_frame now always writes `trace_id || relay_hops`).

| # | Task | File | Status |
|---|--------|------|--------|
| 415.1 | `IdentityPayload::decode` requires mandatory `mlkem_pk_len` (u16 BE, 0 = no key); removed forward-compat branch on absence fields. Obsolete test `identity_legacy_no_mlkem` rewritten on zero-len scenario. Doc updated | `proto/session.rs` | ✅ done |
| 415.2 | `KeyAgreementPayload::decode` requires mandatory `sig_len` (u16 BE) + full sig; removed pre-Epic 406.2 "empty-sig fallback". Doc fields updated | `proto/session.rs` | ✅ done |
| 415.3 | `RouteReplyPayload::decode` requires full 17 bytes; removed `buf.get(16).copied().unwrap_or(0)` for congestion. Test `route_reply_backward_compat_no_congestion_byte` → `route_reply_16_byte_rejected` | `proto/control.rs` | ✅ done |
| 415.4 | `StorePayload::decode` requires mandatory `sig_flag` (u8) after `value`; removed legacy decode path when bytes is absent. Test `store_payload_legacy_no_sig_flag` → `store_payload_missing_sig_flag_rejected` | `proto/discovery.rs` | ✅ done |
| 415.5 | `RoutingAnnouncePayload::decode` and `RouteWithdrawAliasedPayload::decode` require full `hop_count` trailing byte (133/85 strictly); removed `WIRE_SIZE - 1` accept-legacy branches | `proto/routing.rs` | ✅ done |
| 415.6 | `MeshBeaconPayload::decode` requires full v2 layout (`role_flags + addr_len + addr_bytes + battery_level`, minimum 51 bytes); removed 48-byte legacy fallback. Test `beacon_backward_compat_v1_decode` → `beacon_48_byte_rejected`; `beacon_too_short` expected need updated to 51. `verify_auth` doc clarified (unsigned allowed only for test-harness, production call-sites gate by `!public_key.is_empty()`) | `proto/mesh.rs` | ✅ done |
| 415.7 | `ForwardPayload::encode` always writes `trace_id(8) + relay_hops(1)` (removed conditional record trace_id only at non-zero). `decode` requires full suffix. `make_fwd_frame` in `node/ipc/server.rs` synchronized (body_len = 32 + env + 9). Test `forward_relay_hops_absent_gives_zero` → `forward_missing_suffix_rejected` | `proto/delivery.rs`, `node/ipc/server.rs` | ✅ done |

## Epic 416. Completion Epic 270: recursive DHT await-path

**Goal:** The chain `RecursiveQuery → RecursiveResponse` was half-baked: requests were sent, responses arrived, but `resp.payload` was discarded (aboutneshot-demuxing worked idly — no one not registered entry in `pending_recursive`). IPC reactive discovery depended from parallel gossip-updates cache. Now response-handler parses payload by `query_type`, populates `route_cache` / local DHT, and wakes initiator via oneshot. This a real O(log N) DHT lookup.

**Scope:** `node/dispatcher/mod.rs`, `node/dispatcher/routing.rs`, `node/ipc/server.rs`, `node/runtime.rs`, `proto/budget.rs`.

| # | Task | File | Status |
|---|--------|------|--------|
| 416.1 | Added `pub struct PendingRecursive { target_key, query_type, tx }`; `pending_recursive` map retyped on `HashMap<[u8; 16], PendingRecursive>`. Legacy `register_pending_recursive` helper removed (cap-enforcement is inlined in IPC) | `node/dispatcher/mod.rs` | ✅ done |
| 416.2 | `handle_recursive_response` now on hit: **FIND_NODE** — each 32--byte chunk in `payload` is inserted as next_hop for `target_key` in `route_cache` (score=50_000, hops=2); **FIND_VALUE** — value is preserved via `dht.store_local(target_key, payload)`; **STORE** — no-op (only signal); in the end `tx.send(resp.payload)` wakes initiator. `route_updated.notify_waiters()` remained for fallback-pollers | `node/dispatcher/routing.rs` | ✅ done |
| 416.3 | `IpcServer`: added field `pending_recursive: Option<PendingRecursiveMap>` + setter `with_pending_recursive`; type `PendingRecursiveMap` aliased. `handle_ipc_client` and `IpcSendContext` received new field. `runtime.rs` connects `Arc::clone(&self.dispatcher.pending_recursive)` at the build the server | `node/ipc/server.rs`, `node/runtime.rs` | ✅ done |
| 416.4 | `try_lookup_or_discover`: before sending RecursiveQuery is registered oneshot in `pending_recursive` with `query_id`, `target_key`, `query_type=FIND_NODE` (cap `MAX_PENDING_RECURSIVE` is enforced, closed receivers are evicted). Await replaced on `tokio::select!` from `{rq_rx, notified, sleep(500ms)}` — we wake up either on specific parsed response, either on any another route_updated event, either by timeout | `node/ipc/server.rs` | ✅ done |
| 416.5 | `proto/budget.rs` doc-comment about `MAX_PENDING_RECURSIVE` rewritten: reference on `register_pending_recursive` replaced on current path (IPC inlines cap at insert in `FrameDispatcher::pending_recursive`) | `proto/budget.rs` | ✅ done |
| 416.6 | `cargo build -p veilcore` — clean; `cargo clippy -p veilcore --lib` — clean (type aliased); `cargo test -p veilcore --lib` — **1313 passed**, 0 failed, 4 ignored | — | ✅ done |

## Epic 417. Zombie-code wipe: shard-filtering runtime toggle + cross-shard metric

**Goal:** Removal "zombie"-code, which is executed, but not has effect.

**Found two zombie-chains Epic 320/400.6:**

1. `KademliaDht::set_shard_filtering(_enabled)` — no-op method: parameter `_enabled` is ignored (field `dht_config` lives behind Arc without RwLock, runtime-mutation physically impossible). The call from `runtime.rs:1516` at `estimated_n > 1_000_000` ran but did nothing. The flag `DhtConfig.shard_filtering` is read in `handle_store()` on every STORE — it works, but only from the startup config. The runtime toggle was a fiction.

2. `NodeMetrics::inc_cross_shard_forwards()` + counter `cross_shard_forwards_total` + snapshot field + Prometheus export `veil_cross_shard_forwards_total` — counter never not is incremented (zero call-sites), always yields 0.

| # | Task | File | Status |
|---|--------|------|--------|
| 417.1 | Removed `KademliaDht::set_shard_filtering` + call-site in `runtime.rs` (block "auto-enable shard filtering when estimated_n > 1M"). `DhtConfig.shard_filtering` remained — this legitimate config-option, is used in `handle_store` | `node/dht/kademlia.rs`, `node/runtime.rs` | ✅ done |
| 417.2 | Removed `inc_cross_shard_forwards()` method, field `cross_shard_forwards_total` from `NodeMetrics`, from `MetricsSnapshot`, init in `new()`, load in `snapshot()`, Prometheus export line | `node/observability.rs` | ✅ done |
| 417.3 | `cargo build` + `cargo clippy -p veilcore --lib` — clean; `cargo test -p veilcore --lib` — **1313 passed**, 0 failed, 4 ignored | — | ✅ done |

## Epic 418. Demolition TransportManager + ChunkNack scaffolding

**Goal:** Remove two large Epic-stubs, which never not were finished and on current moment not affect on functionality. Analysis showed: correct implementation requires ~800-1200 lines, architectural decisions (AEAD-over-QUIC, security for trust-less frame intake), and — most importantly — real metrics load, which at us no. "Defer until measured need" + if network will grow to 10K+ peers, TransportManager rewrite with scratch faster, than to maintain a stale skeleton.

### 418.1. TransportManager (Epic 273/292/294.7 scaffolding)

**What this was:** three-layer transport dispatcher — Layer A (persistent AEAD sessions, already works via SessionTxRegistry directly), Layer B (stateless UDP transit — not wired), Layer C (on-demand QUIC pool — not wired). Goal: at >10K peers multiplex veil-sessions on top of 1 QUIC connection + on-demand pool for short interactions (RecursiveResponse without handshake).

**Why zombie:** module ~327 lines with a full implementation (AddressCache, QuicPool, LRU eviction, send_async, evict_idle), BUT `TransportManager::new()` was constructed only in `#[cfg(test)]`. Field `FrameDispatcher.transport_manager: Option<Arc<...>>` was initialized `None` in 5 places (runtime.rs, rebuild, dispatcher/mod.rs×2, dispatcher/delivery.rs). Comment in runtime.rs:5074 in plain text: "Full wiring requires exposing quinn::Connection from QuicTransport, which crosses the transport abstraction boundary" — work halted on an architectural boundary.

### 418.2. ChunkNack (Epic 214.D1 scaffolding)

**What this was:** NACK-frame for retransmission lost chunks in chunked-transfer large payloads. `DeliveryMsg::ChunkNack = 9` wire-variant + `ChunkNackPayload` struct (~60 lines proto) + handler branch in `dispatcher/delivery.rs`.

**Why zombie:** zero producers — `ChunkNackPayload::new`/`encode` never not are called. Sender-side cache missing chunks not exists. Handler only logs "retransmit not yet implemented" and drops. Chunking passes data via reliable veil-sessions (TCP/TLS/QUIC) — loss packet is handled by the transport, to chunk-level not reaches. NACK critical only for Layer B UDP transit (which not wired) → currently decides ~0% real cases.

| # | Task | Status |
|---|--------|--------|
| 418.1 | Removed module `node/transport_manager.rs` entirely (~327 lines); `pub mod transport_manager;` removed from `node/mod.rs`; field `FrameDispatcher.transport_manager: Option<Arc<...>>` removed from struct; 5 init-sites `transport_manager: None` removed in `runtime.rs` (2 places including rebuild), `dispatcher/mod.rs` (2 places), `dispatcher/delivery.rs`. The stale comment about "Epic 404.2 QUIC connection pool" + "Epic 294.7" removed from `runtime.rs:5074` | ✅ done |
| 418.2 | Removed `DeliveryMsg::ChunkNack = 9` wire-variant + `TryFrom<u16>` branch in `proto/family.rs`; removed `ChunkNackPayload` struct + `impl` (encode/decode/header_size) in `proto/delivery.rs`; removed handler-branch `DeliveryMsg::ChunkNack =>` in `dispatcher/delivery.rs` | ✅ done |
| 418.3 | `cargo build` — clean; `cargo clippy -p veilcore --lib` — clean; `cargo test -p veilcore --lib` — **1310 passed** (-3: removed ChunkNack encode/decode tests + TransportManager no-quic-pool test + AddressCache tests), 0 failed, 4 ignored | ✅ done |

**Goal:** `KademliaService::new(local_id, role)` and `KademliaService::with_config(local_id, role, cfg)` accepted `role: NodeRole`, but parameter not was used inside — participation is controlled via `dht_config.participate` (role-independent). Comment in plain text: "retained for API symmetry ... accepted but not used."

Removed now-unused `use crate::cfg::NodeRole;` imports in `kademlia.rs`, `anycast.rs` tests, `dht/mod.rs` tests, `benches/dht_store_throughput.rs` | ✅ done |

## Epic 420. Threat-model hardening (from security-analysis)

**Goal:** Close maximum attacks, identified analysis threats. Priority — Critical/High with realistic scope. Low-severity and architectural rework (Eclipse prevention via XOR-diversity, Gateway reputation, Name age-weighting, ML-KEM rotation) deferred in the backlog separate epics.

### Scope (seven tasks, from highest cost/impact ratio)

| # | Task | Attack | Severity | Status |
|---|--------|-------|----------|--------|
| 420.1 | **RecursiveResponse authentication via signature + XOR-proximity**: (and) to `RecursiveResponsePayload` added `responder_pubkey: [u8; 32]` + `signature: [u8; 64]` (+96 bytes trailer). `handle_recursive_query` signs via local Ed25519 (helper `build_signed`). `handle_recursive_response` verifies signature over `query_id \|\| payload`. () **Follow-up fix**: in addition to signatures, is checked XOR-proximity — `claimed_responder_id` (= BLAKE3(pubkey)) must to match with `target_key` OR shared prefix bits ≥ `REQUIRED_RESPONDER_PREFIX_BITS = 8`. Without this attacker with one valid PoW identity could inject itself in route_cache the victim as "closest to target". Far-responder response → re-insert pending entry (for legitimate late response) + log `recursive.response.far_responder` | Recursive response injection + responder-id spoof (Critical) | ✅ done |
| 420.2 | **PoW challenge nonce replay cache** — **already implemented** in Epic 182.S3: `pow_challenge_seen.check_and_insert(nonce)` on [routing.rs:667](veilcore/src/node/dispatcher/routing.rs#L667). Repeat PowChallenge with the nonce → `NoResponse` drop. Used replay_window with TTL | PoW challenge replay (Medium) | ✅ already done |
| 420.3 | **Transit frame replay protection** — **already implemented** in Epic 272: `forward_seen_set.check_and_insert(content_hash)` on [delivery.rs:781](veilcore/src/node/dispatcher/delivery.rs#L781). Duplicates transit frames with the `content_hash` are dropped to forward | Transit replay (Medium) | ✅ already done |
| 420.4 | **Minimum ovl1_minor floor** — **not applicable**: after Epic 414/415 protocol converged in single-version ("all features always-enabled"). `negotiate()` in [handshake.rs:65](veilcore/src/node/session/handshake.rs#L65) returns empty `NegotiatedCapabilities` without flags. No differing minor versions between which can was would downgrade — surface attacks is absent | Version downgrade (Medium) | ⊘ not applicable |
| 420.5 | **DHT STORE anti-poisoning** — **already fully mitigated** in Epic 193.S3 ([dispatcher/discovery.rs:259-306](veilcore/src/node/dispatcher/discovery.rs#L259)). Rules: (a) signed STORE requires `BLAKE3(pubkey) == key` + valid signature (`verify_store_ownership`); (b) unsigned STORE is permitted only if `key == BLAKE3(peer_id)` (self-key) or value — NameRecord with internal PoW+sig+key==BLAKE3(name) validation. The attacker not can overwrite another's key without signatures → hi-bit namespace split not is required | DHT poisoning via unsigned overwrite (High) | ✅ already mitigated |
| 420.6 | **Adaptive PoW difficulty** (partially): added `IdentityPolicy::adaptive_difficulty(estimated_n) -> u32` — returns `DEFAULT_POW_DIFFICULTY + log2(max(estimated_n, 1) / 1_000)`, clamped to `MAX_ADAPTIVE_POW_DIFFICULTY = 32`. Scaling: ≤1K → baseline, 10K → +3, 100K → +6, 1M → +9, > → capped. Unit test `adaptive_difficulty_scales_with_network_size` checks curve. **Wiring in verifier NOT done** — requires distributed consensus on `estimated_n` (to different nodes see different estimate), otherwise partitions network on "too easy" / "too hard" swarms. Helper ready as utility for operator CLI / identity creation; full connection extracted in the backlog epic 426 (consensus-aware PoW scaling) | Sybil at static PoW (Critical) | 🔄 partial |
| 420.7 | `cargo test -p veilcore --lib` — **1311 passed** (+1 adaptive_difficulty_scales_with_network_size), 0 failed, 4 ignored; `cargo clippy -p veilcore --lib` — clean | — | ✅ done |

### Deferred (backlog)

| Attack | Why deferred |
|-------|-----------------|
| Eclipse prevention (XOR-diversity k-bucket admission) | Requires reputation tracker + diversity metrics refactor; separate epic 421 |
| ML-KEM dk_seed rotation | Crypto surgery, changes wire-format; separate epic 422 |
| Name squatting age-weighting | Requires timestamp-chain infrastructure; epic 423 |
| Gateway reputation + audit log | Large architectural refactor; epic 424 |
| Beacon flood via 10K IP | Requires reputation-driven admission; subtask epic 421 |
| Padding default-on for traffic analysis | Measure performance impact; separate PR with benchmarks |
| Mailbox quota audit (3 dimensions) | A separate pass — needed check per-sender / per-recipient / per-node quotas; epic 425 |


---

## Epic 421. Eclipse prevention: XOR-diversity + reputation-driven admission

**Goal:** Reduce risk eclipse-attacks, where attacker with resources grinding pubkeys fills k-buckets the victim XOR-close Sybil identitys. Protection via diversity metrics + reputation admission.

**Scope:** reputation tracker (partially exists), XOR-diversity selection in k-bucket, IP/ASN diversity, per-node beacon admission (beyond per-IP).

**Why in the backlog:** requires infrastructure for estimate reputation (uptime, honest forwarding, latency consistency) and not-trivial refactor routing table insertion; relies on reliable network-size estimate (see. 426).

| # | Task | File | Status |
|---|--------|------|--------|
| 421.1 | Diag: to measure real k-bucket fill-rate in sim-scale test Epic 402 with adversary profile (50% Sybil in specially chosen XOR-regions). Measure baseline eclipse success-rate | `veilcore/src/sim/`, new tests/eclipse_sim.rs | 📦 backlog |
| 421.2 | Extend `ReputationTracker` metrics: (a) observed-uptime (first-seen, last-seen, total), (b) forwarding honesty (ACK ratio from pending_ack), (c) latency stability (Vivaldi drift). All via sliding windows | `node/reputation.rs` | 📦 backlog |
| 421.3 | Routing table admission policy: at insert candidate in k-bucket to account for diversity — IPv4 /24 prefix, IPv6 /48, ASN (opt. maxmind), physical cluster (Vivaldi). The bucket not accepts second identity from that /24 if already exists a representative | `node/dht/routing.rs` | 📦 backlog |
| 421.4 | Reputation-gated beacon admission: MAX_BEACONS_PER_IP_PER_WINDOW is replaced on `reputation_aware_limit(peer_id)` — newcomers receive low cap, growth as it accumulates positive reputation. Closes #7 (flood with 10K IP) | `node/mesh/beacon.rs` | 📦 backlog |
| 421.5 | Integration test: sim with 1000 honest + 500 adversary nodes in one /24, check that eclipse not succeeds (victim always has ≥K_MIN honest contacts in each k-bucket) | `veilcore/src/sim/eclipse.rs` | 📦 backlog |


---

## Epic 422. ML-KEM dk_seed rotation + per-session ephemeral ML-KEM

**Goal:** Resolve path situation "dk_seed leak = retroactive decrypt all past E2E messages". Forward secrecy must be full, not partial.

**Scope:** two independent features — (a) periodic rotation dk_seed with graceful key migration, (b) optional ephemeral ML-KEM pair per session (heavier CPU, maximum protection).

**Why in the backlog:** crypto surgery, changes wire format in session handshake; requires careful migration plan (old dk_seed must to work into time rotation window); influences identity persistence layout.

| # | Task | File | Status |
|---|--------|------|--------|
| 422.1 | Design doc: protocol rotation — either (a) monotonic counter in dk_seed slot, either (b) multiple dk_seeds with window. Discuss migration breakagea peer_mlkem_keys cache | `docs/security/mlkem-rotation.md` | 📦 backlog |
| 422.2 | `IdentityStore` extended: `current_dk_seed` + `previous_dk_seed` (for graceful migration) + `rotation_due_at`. A periodic task rolls out new dk_seed, sends updated `IdentityPayload` all current sessions | `cmd/identity/`, `node/e2e.rs` | 📦 backlog |
| 422.3 | `peer_mlkem_keys` cache is invalidated on receipt new `mlkem_pubkey` from peer (is updated atomically). Old payloads encrypted the previous key — continue to work during rotation window | `node/e2e.rs`, `proto/session.rs` | 📦 backlog |
| 422.4 | Optional per-session ephemeral ML-KEM: config flag `session.ephemeral_mlkem: bool` (default false). When true — each handshake generates fresh ML-KEM keypair, is used only for this session. CPU cost: ~+50ms handshake. Max forward secrecy | `node/session/handshake.rs` | 📦 backlog |
| 422.5 | Tests: key rotation correctness (peer decrypts into time rotation), ephemeral ML-KEM roundtrip, post-rotation old-key messages continue decrypt during TTL | `node/e2e.rs` tests | 📦 backlog |


---

## Epic 423. Name squatting defense: age-weighted claims

**Goal:** Protect old legitimate claims on names from attacks with "newcomer has more compute, wins name contest". Requirement: existing name with X days uptime must to withstand challenger with +N bits PoW.

**Scope:** timestamp-chain for NameRecord, age-weighted difficulty compare, migration existing NameRecords.

**Why in the backlog:** requires timestamp-chain infrastructure (tamper-proof timestamps); needed to decide as to protect from clock-skew at; affects on NameRecord wire-format.

| # | Task | File | Status |
|---|--------|------|--------|
| 423.1 | Design: timestamp-chain — each NameRecord includes `notarized_at: u64` (seconds since epoch) + `notary_sig: [u8; 64]` from one from "anchor" nodes with high reputation. A tree signatures forms audit trail | `docs/security/name-timestamp.md` | 📦 backlog |
| 423.2 | NameRecord wire-format extension: `notarized_at` + `notary_pubkey` + `notary_sig`. Encode/decode backward compat (unsigned notary = infinite age by default — dangerous, needed otherwise) | `proto/name.rs` | 📦 backlog |
| 423.3 | Name contest rule: `challenger_difficulty ≥ existing_difficulty + log2(age_days + 1)` — each doubling age requires +1 bit challenger. After year 10-bit advantage at incumbent | `node/name/validation.rs` | 📦 backlog |
| 423.4 | Notary election: anchor-nodes are selected per-realm via Kademlia closest-to-"notary" key (or fixed list in config). Rotate periodically | `node/name/service.rs` | 📦 backlog |
| 423.5 | Tests: squat attempt with 30--day incumbent requires ~+5 bit PoW; without — rejected | `node/name/validation.rs` tests | 📦 backlog |


---

## Epic 424. Gateway reputation + audit log

**Goal:** Malicious gateway sees all broadcasts mesh + decrypted payloads. Currently no mechanism to select "honest" gateway; no audit trail for detection exfiltration. Build reputation-driven gateway selection + tamper-evident audit log.

**Scope:** large architectural epic — gateway scoring, challenge-based audit (random probes), Merkle-tree log all gateway forwards.

**Why in the backlog:** large — affects gateway_list, attachment, audit storage, forensics tooling; some questions (privacy vs audit tension) require separate design pass.

| # | Task | File | Status |
|---|--------|------|--------|
| 424.1 | Gateway reputation score: uptime, latency, forwarding-correctness ratio. Client selects gateway by composite score, not by random | `node/gateway_list.rs`, `node/gateway/attachment.rs` | 📦 backlog |
| 424.2 | Challenge-based audit: client periodically sends "probe frame" via gateway (marked specific content_id), expects echo from second gateway. Gateway which not forward — penalized | `node/gateway/audit.rs` (new) | 📦 backlog |
| 424.3 | Gateway-side Merkle log all forwards: `(content_id, direction, timestamp, next_hop)` → BLAKE3 tree. Root periodically published to DHT at signed commitment. Allows tampering detection | `node/gateway/merkle_log.rs` (new) | 📦 backlog |
| 424.4 | Rate-limit gateway forwards per-source: malicious gateway not can amplification via itself garbage. Token bucket per src_node_id | `node/mesh/bridge.rs` | 📦 backlog |
| 424.5 | Admin CLI: `veil gateway audit <gateway_id>` — fetch Merkle root from DHT, verify chain, report discrepancies | `cmd/gateway.rs` (new) | 📦 backlog |


---

**Goal:** Currently unknown, protected whether all three dimensions quotas: can whether one sender flood mailbox recipient (DoS one a user)? Can whether a small number recipients fill total storage node (DoS an entire node)? System audit quotas.

| 425.3 | Tests: (a) single sender overwhelms recipient — must obtain QuotaExceeded; (b) distributed senders fill total storage — must to trigger per-node cap and eviction old; (c) recipient sees its own entrys even at other-recipient overflow | `node/mailbox/` tests | 📦 backlog |
| 425.4 | Metrics: `veil_mailbox_quota_hits{dimension=sender|recipient|node}` for observability attacks | `node/observability.rs` | 📦 backlog |

## Epic 426. Consensus-aware PoW scaling

**Goal:** Wire up `IdentityPolicy::adaptive_difficulty` (already ready from Epic 420.6) in verification path without creation network partitions. Nodes must agree on `estimated_n` via gossip or to use weak consensus for boundaries difficulty-window.

**Scope:** design — as nodes agree on size estimate; tolerance window (±X bits); graceful rejection logic.

**Why in the backlog:** distributed consensus question; needed first measured baseline real network-size drift.

| # | Task | File | Status |
|---|--------|------|--------|
| 426.1 | Design: source `estimated_n` — either DHT-size estimate (current: Epic 287), either gossip histogram beacons. Toleranced window | `docs/security/pow-scaling.md` | 📦 backlog |
| 426.2 | Verifier acceptance rule: accept identity with difficulty >= `adaptive_difficulty(my_estimate) - TOLERANCE_BITS`. `TOLERANCE_BITS = 2` will protect from small drifts between nodes | `crypto/identity.rs` | 📦 backlog |
| 426.3 | Optional: operators can force a pinned value via config `identity.min_pow_difficulty_override: u32` (overrides adaptive for this node) | `cfg/model.rs` | 📦 backlog |
| 426.4 | Sim test: 10K nodes, 50% use adaptive (honest), 50% static (old clients) — both must mutually accept identitys in tolerance window | `veilcore/src/sim/` | 📦 backlog |

## Epic 427. Traffic analysis resistance: padding default-on

**Goal:** `PaddingPolicy::disabled()` — default. A passive observer easily correlates frame-size + timing → deanonymization. Enable Adaptive padding by default, after dimensions performance impact.

**Scope:** benchmark cost, then change default + migration.

**Why in the backlog:** requires measure performance impact on representative workload before change default.

| # | Task | File | Status |
|---|--------|------|--------|
| 427.1 | Benchmark: throughput / latency / CPU / bandwidth overhead for `PaddingPolicy::Adaptive` vs `disabled` on 3 workload (chat, file-transfer, DHT-heavy) | `veilcore/benches/padding_cost.rs` | 📦 backlog |
| 427.2 | If overhead < 10% — to change default on `Adaptive` with conservative jitter (5-20ms) + cover_interval 500ms. Otherwise — leave disabled but document opt-in clearly | `cfg/model.rs` | 📦 backlog |
| 427.3 | Cover traffic: when sender idle >N secs, emit padding frames (marked `SessionMsg::Padding`) to active sessions. Masks "user started typing" pattern | `node/session/padding.rs` | 📦 backlog |
| 427.4 | Tests: verify observable frame-size distribution — no correlation between payload-size and wire-size | `node/session/padding.rs` tests | 📦 backlog |

## Epic 428. Refactor dispatch_delivery for readability

**Goal:** `FrameDispatcher::dispatch_delivery` was 914 lines — one match with 11 inline-arms by 16-170 lines each. Reduced to 41--line dispatcher with extracted named handler-methods. Behavior not changes.

**Scope:** only `veilcore/src/node/dispatcher/delivery.rs`.

| # | Task | Status |
|---|--------|--------|
| 428.1 | Extracted `handle_mailbox_put` + helpers `split_replication_bytes`, `try_replicate_to_quorum`, `select_quorum_replicas` (MailboxPut arm 170 → 1 line dispatcher) | ✅ done |
| 428.2 | Extracted `handle_mailbox_fetch` + `try_fetch_from_replicas`, `emit_fetched_acks` (MailboxFetch arm 170 → 1 line). After this step `select_quorum_replicas` is reused between PUT and FETCH (was 2 copies) | ✅ done |
| 428.3 | Extracted `handle_delivery_forward` + `forward_ttl_already_expired`, `deliver_forward_locally`, `decrypt_forward_payload` (Forward arm 160 → 1 line). E2E vs Meta-E2E vs plaintext logic now in one functions with explicit name | ✅ done |
| 428.4 | Extracted small arms: `handle_mailbox_fetch_replica`, `handle_mailbox_ack`, `handle_delivery_status`, `handle_mailbox_replicate`, `handle_chunk_manifest`, `handle_chunk`, `handle_transit` | ✅ done |
| 428.5 | Extracted `handle_recursive_relay` + `forward_rr_to_closest` (RecursiveRelay arm 116 → 1 line). Four branches (terminal / direct-session / hop-exhausted / greedy-closest) explicitly commented | ✅ done |
| 428.6 | `cargo build` + `cargo clippy -p veilcore --lib` — clean; `cargo test -p veilcore --lib` — **1311 passed**, 0 failed, 4 ignored | ✅ done |

**Result:** `dispatch_delivery` now reads as table routing:
```rust
match msg {
    DeliveryMsg::MailboxPut         => self.handle_mailbox_put(header, body, peer_id),
    DeliveryMsg::MailboxFetch       => self.handle_mailbox_fetch(header, body, peer_id),
    DeliveryMsg::MailboxFetchReplica => self.handle_mailbox_fetch_replica(header, body, peer_id),
    DeliveryMsg::MailboxAck         => self.handle_mailbox_ack(body, peer_id),
    DeliveryMsg::Forward            => self.handle_delivery_forward(header, body, peer_id),
    DeliveryMsg::DeliveryStatus     => self.handle_delivery_status(body),
    DeliveryMsg::MailboxReplicate   => self.handle_mailbox_replicate(header, body),
    DeliveryMsg::ChunkManifest      => self.handle_chunk_manifest(body),
    DeliveryMsg::Chunk              => self.handle_chunk(body, peer_id),
    DeliveryMsg::Transit            => self.handle_transit(body, peer_id),
    DeliveryMsg::RecursiveRelay     => self.handle_recursive_relay(body, peer_id),
}
```

Each handler has separate doc-comment with an Epic reference and single-responsibility. Shared logic (quorum replica selection, encrypted payload decode) extracted in named helpers. Behavior bit-for-bit-exactly matches with pre-refactor version (1311 tests without changes).

**Deferred in the backlog** (left on future sessions):

| File | Function | Lines | Note |
|------|---------|-------|------------|
| `node/session/runner.rs` | `SessionRunner::run` | 832 | main loop with with many branch, complex lifecycle |
| `node/admin.rs` | `execute_admin_command` | 662 | giant match; such pattern as dispatch_delivery |
| `node/runtime.rs` | `NodeRuntime::start` | 613 | complex init with error unwinding |
| `node/session/handshake.rs` | `perform_ovl1_handshake` | 577 | linear protocol, can split by phase |

Priority at `execute_admin_command` — that pattern "match-arms-in-big-function", mechanical extraction.

## Epic 430. Refactor perform_ovl1_handshake for readability

**Goal:** `perform_ovl1_handshake` in `node/session/handshake.rs` — 577 lines, contains full handshake + fast-path resumption (server + client) + C6 fallback (ticket invalid, full re-handshake). C6 fallback had ~130 lines **verbatim-duplicating** main post-IDENTITY handshake tail with the only difference in error labels.

| # | Task | Status |
|---|--------|--------|
| 430.1 | Extracted `build_local_attach_bytes(role, vivaldi)` — assembles local AttachPayload + local_battery + TLV (Epic 220). Used **4 times**: server fast-path, client fast-path (after ATTACH accept), C6 fallback, main path | ✅ done |
| 430.2 | Extracted `verify_identity_binding(id, label)` — BLAKE3(pubkey)==node_id check. Used 2 times (main IDENTITY + C6 IDENTITY). Unifies error-message | ✅ done |
| 430.3 | Extracted `compute_confirm_mac(shared_secret, local_id, remote_id)` — BLAKE3 of canonical transcript. Used 2 times. Documents security-property MAC | ✅ done |
| 430.4 | Extracted `sign_ephemeral_pubkey(local, eph_pk)` — Epic 406.2 signature. Used 2 times | ✅ done |
| 430.5 | **Main benefit**: extracted `complete_handshake_from_capabilities(stream, family, capture, remote_id, local, role, vivaldi, local_node_id_bytes, remote_identity, label_suffix)` — CAPABILITIES → KEY_AGREEMENT → SESSION_CONFIRM → ATTACH → build_result. Replaces **both** copies post-IDENTITY tail. `label_suffix` (`""` or `" (C6)"`) is injected in error-messages so that log reader could distinguish path | ✅ done |
| 430.6 | `perform_ovl1_handshake`: 577 → **246 lines** (-331). Removed ~130 lines duplication C6 fallback | ✅ done |
| 430.7 | `cargo build` + `cargo clippy -p veilcore --lib` — clean; `cargo test -p veilcore --lib` — **1311 passed**, 0 failed, 4 ignored | ✅ done |

**Side benefit:** any future changes handshake-logics (new check on KEY_AGREEMENT, additional. TLV in ATTACH) apply in one place — C6 fallback automatically gets fixs.

## Epic 431. Fix real bugs from external audit

**Goal:** Close 4 real bug in `NodeRuntime`, confirmed reading code after external audit. All in `veilcore/src/node/runtime.rs`.

### Bug-context

1. **Reload asymmetry** — the most critical. `start()` brings up 28 background tasks, `respawn_reload_tasks` — only 22. After config reload node silently loses IPC, pending_ack retries, discovery initiator, name autoclaim, socks5 proxy and exit proxy. Process alive, admin responds, but functionality degraded without explicit signal.
2. **`refresh_runtime_tuning` not updates `max_per_subnet`** — operator edits limit in config, reload not applies (is updated only `max_per_ip`).
3. **`state.peers` — never removed**. Populated on insert at bootstrap/autodiscover, but nowhere not is removed. Failover ([runtime.rs:3530](veilcore/src/node/runtime.rs#L3530)) checks "already_connected" via `state.peers.values().any()` → sees stale entries from dead gateways and not restarts reconnect.
4. **`unwrap_or_default()` on base64** in `spawn_name_autoclaim_task` ([runtime.rs:4567](veilcore/src/node/runtime.rs#L4567)): invalid `pubkey_b64` from config → empty `Vec<u8>` (and not error), problem manifests later on signature verify without clear context.

| # | Task | File | Status |
|---|--------|------|--------|
| 431.1 | **Symmetric reload**: in `respawn_reload_tasks` add 6 missing spawns — `spawn_discovery_initiator_task`, `spawn_socks5_task`, `spawn_exit_proxy_task`, `spawn_ipc_server`, `spawn_pending_ack_tick`, `spawn_name_autoclaim_task`. Check that each idempotent relative to stopping old tasks (via shutdown_tx sub/unsub) | `node/runtime.rs` | ✅ done |
| 431.2 | **Runtime tuning: max_per_subnet**: in `refresh_runtime_tuning` add `self.max_per_subnet = config.session.max_per_subnet;` (next to with `max_per_ip` on line 1681) | `node/runtime.rs` | ✅ done |
| 431.3 | **state.peers liveness**: two path — (a) minimal: replace `s.peers.values().any(...)` on session_registry lookup in gateway failover + autodiscover paths; (b) proper: to remove `state.peers` entry on session close hook. Choose (a) — faster, less change surface, not changes invariants state.peers (configured + autodiscover history) | `node/runtime.rs` | ✅ done |
| 431.4 | **Strict base64 in autoclaim**: `STANDARD.decode(&pubkey_b64).unwrap_or_default()` → propagate via log + skip-entry. Not crash task, but and not stay silent | `node/runtime.rs` (autoclaim) | ⬜ todo |
| 431.5 | Validation: `cargo build` + `cargo clippy -p veilcore --lib` — clean; `cargo test -p veilcore --lib` — **1311 passed**, 0 failed, 4 ignored. Integration test on reload asymmetry deferred — requires session + IPC harness, to do separate epic with proper testnet | — | ✅ done |

---

## Epic 432. Decomposition `NodeRuntime` monolith (planned)

**Goal:** `node/runtime.rs` — 7441 lines. This main god-object codebase: contains peer/session/gateway/mesh/DHT/discovery/proxy state, lifecycle management, config reload, persist, bootstrap. Split on focused sub-modules with explicit boundaries, so that:

- reduce cognitive load at reviewing/debugging
- prevent repetition reload-asymmetry bug (if would was registry "lifecycle units", `respawn_reload_tasks` not could would forget tasks)
- reduce hidden lock-interactions between subsystems

**Why in the backlog:** scope 2-3 weeks real operation, high risk (one error in state ownership → regressions in many places), requires testnet validation. Cannot to do hastily.

### Plan decomposition (tentative, clarify at the start)

| # | Subsystem | Size (lines in runtime.rs) | Extract to in |
|---|---|---|---|
| 432.1 | **TaskRegistry** ✅ **done**: enum `RuntimeService` (30 variants) + `ALL` ordered list in `node/task_registry.rs` (112 lines). `NodeRuntime::spawn_service(service, config)` — exhaustive-match dispatcher on all `spawn_*` methods. `spawn_all_services(config)` iterates `RuntimeService::ALL`. `start()` and `apply_reload_after_stop` both call its, two old helper (`respawn_reload_tasks` 57 lines + `restart_persist_tasks` 52 lines) removed. Adding new service = compile error in `spawn_service` — root cause reload-asymmetry bug closed architecturally. `persist_enabled` now is updated in `refresh_runtime_tuning` (previously was in removed `restart_persist_tasks`). 1311 tests pass | `node/task_registry.rs` (new) | ✅ done |
| 432.2 | **PeerLifecycle**: `state.peers` management (insert / update / **remove on session close**) → separate `PeerRegistry` with explicit API. Fixes 431.3 systematically | `node/peer_registry.rs` | ~500 lines |
| 432.3 | **GatewayFailover**: gateway failover + autodiscover + mesh beacon + staggered reconnect → `node/gateway/failover.rs` + `node/gateway/autodiscover.rs` | `node/gateway/failover.rs` | ~400 lines |
| 432.4 | **Persist**: 9 persist tasks (cache/rtt/vivaldi/dht_routing/dht_values/autodiscover/gateway_list/peer_pubkeys/mlkem) → `node/persist/` directory, one module per snapshot-type | `node/persist/` | ~600 lines |
| 432.5 | **DiscoveryInitiator**: discovery initiator task + route miss handler + congestion withdraw → `node/discovery/initiator.rs` | `node/discovery/initiator.rs` | ~300 lines |
| 432.5a | **RouteMissHandler** ✅ **done**: 171--line `spawn_route_miss_handler` extracted in `node/routing/miss_handler.rs` (229 lines). A unified mega-tokio::spawn split on 4 free-functions with convention names: `flush_mailbox_to_known_hop`, `flood_route_request`, `wait_for_route` + `run` main loop. `MissHandlerCtx` struct — 12 fields. NodeRuntime::spawn_route_miss_handler now 24 lines (was 171) | `node/routing/miss_handler.rs` (new) | ✅ done |
| 432.5b | **MailboxCleanup** deferred: 183--line `spawn_mailbox_cleanup` uses **25 Arc clones** + long tick body with inline eviction logic for 8+ subsystems. Proper extraction requires large ctx-struct and very careful operation with ownership. More likely is worth split the tick logic on named stages (`tick_memory_accounting`, `tick_evict_stale`, `tick_refresh_summary`) inside existing functions — lower risk. Deferred | `node/runtime.rs` (in place) | 📦 backlog |
| 432.6 | **ProxyTasks** ✅ **done**: `spawn_socks5_task` + `spawn_exit_proxy_task` (~120 lines inline) extracted in `node/proxy/tasks.rs` as `spawn_socks5(Socks5SpawnCtx)` / `spawn_exit_proxy(ExitProxySpawnCtx)`. Context-structs do coupling explicit. Call sites in runtime.rs — by 12 lines each instead of 40-80 | `node/proxy/tasks.rs` (new) | ✅ done |
| 432.7 | **NameAutoclaim** ✅ **done**: 300--line `spawn_name_autoclaim_task` with 2 nested closuress (`publish_record`, `do_mine_and_claim`) extracted in `node/name/autoclaim.rs` (414 lines). Closures unrolled in 5 named free-functions: `try_republish_stored_record`, `publish_record`, `mine_and_claim`, `persist_record_to_config`, `compute_sleep_until_next_renewal`, `handle_contention`. `AutoclaimContext` struct — 12 fields from `NodeRuntime`, caller one argument. NodeRuntime::spawn_name_autoclaim_task now 30 lines (was 317) | `node/name/autoclaim.rs` (new) | ✅ done |
| 432.8 | **ConfigReload**: separate `ReloadEngine` — hot path for config changes with explicit diff-apply | `node/config/reload.rs` | ~200 lines |

**Expected outcome:** `node/runtime.rs` shrinks to ~2000-2500 lines (orchestration + shared state containers), 8 new focused modules by 150-600 lines each. All hidden-state interactions surface on level explicit API boundaries.

**Not to touch:** session/dispatcher/mailbox/DHT — this already separate modules with clear boundaries.

### Pre-requisites before launch 432

- 431 closed (so that not to try refactor a moving goal)
- Testnet with 10+ nodes for regression validation
- Budget 2-3 weeks

## Epic 433. Final cleanup for code review

**Goal:** last pass by readability before the review final variant.

### Done

| # | Task | Before | After | Status |
|---|--------|------|-------|--------|
| 433.1 | `spawn_mailbox_cleanup` tick → 6 named phase-helpers (`tick_update_congestion_depth`, `tick_account_memory_and_evict_over_budget`, `tick_evict_expired_primary_stores` + `PrimaryEvictionCounts` struct, `tick_record_eviction_metrics`, `tick_evict_secondary_caches`, `tick_refresh_runtime_summary`, `tick_prune_completed_task_handles`).
