## Epic 455. VPN split-tunneling-inspired features (2026-04-19)

**Context.** Source: two post @usher2 about split-tunneling. Requests VPN control-plane land on already existing primitives veil (DHT, anycast, routing, reputation, gateway_list) — needed target extensions, and not new architecture.

### Tasks

| # | Task | Priority | LOC est. | Files | Status |
|---|--------|-----------|----------|-------|--------|
| 455.1 | **Transport-hints service** (MVP, **local-only**) — `TransportHintRegistry` in [node/transport_hints.rs](veilcore/src/node/transport_hints.rs) with `record(scheme, success)` + `ranked_snapshot()`, hooked in `TransportRegistry::connect` so that any connect automatically writes outcome. Sample cap 1024 + exponential decay (fresh observation weigh more). IPC frames `LocalAppMsg::TransportHintQuery = 24` and `TransportHintResult = 25` + payload `TransportHintResultPayload` in [proto/transport_hints.rs](veilcore/src/proto/transport_hints.rs). Python helper `OvlClient.transport_hints()`. **Nothing not is published in DHT, not goes in network** — each node sees only its own observations via local IPC. 11 unit-tests + 1 IPC integration | **P1** | ~280 | `node/transport_hints.rs`, `proto/transport_hints.rs`, `proto/family.rs`, `transport/registry.rs`, `node/runtime.rs`, `node/ipc/server.rs`, `examples/ovl_proto.py` | ✅ done (MVP) |
| 455.1b | **Cross-node hint aggregation** — **deemed not worthwhile** (analysis 2026-04-19). Originally was expected to publish hints in DHT/anycast, so that new nodes received "hot start". Analysis tradeoff showed that random walk + PEX-implicit signaling win out: (a) cold-start cost = random walk = N walks × ~250ms ≈ **~2s worst case**, and is amortized after the 2nd connection thanks to 455.1 MVP; (b) **existing PEX already propagates listen-transports peers** — so each transport self-organizes via independent attempts to different nodes, no centralized "map" not needed; (c) the very existence of a query service = side-channel ("you are a veil user, in such-and-such network context"); (d) even with k-anonymity + decoys leaves attack surface, that otherwise would not exist at all. Solution: **not to do**. Random walk + local hints (455.1 MVP) give the entire practical benefit without exposure | — | 0 | — | ⊘ skip (privacy outweighs benefit) |
| 455.2 | **Labeled routes** (MVP) — implemented. **Wire-format breaking change** (network not stable, agreed): `RouteResponsePayload.target_labels: Vec<[u8; 4]>` is signed target as part `signable_bytes` — relay not can to forge or remove labels without invalidation signatures. Cap `MAX_TARGET_LABELS = 8` × `LABEL_WIDTH = 4` = 40 bytes overhead on response, label tag matches by width with anycast service_tag (one taxonomy for both subsystems). Config: `routing.target_labels = ["exit", "low", "qiwi"]` ([cfg/model.rs::RoutingConfig](veilcore/src/cfg/model.rs)) — ASCII 4-byte values, zero-padded if shorter. Auto-propagation: `FrameDispatcher.target_labels` populated from config via `build_target_labels()`, attached into each outbound RouteResponse. **Cache layer**: `RouteCacheEntry.labels` + `RouteCache::insert_labelled()` + `RouteCache::lookup_with_labels(dst, required)` — filters by AND-set required labels. Plain `insert()` left for compatibility (defaults to empty labels), `lookup()` without filter remains as was. **8 new tests**: 3 wire (roundtrip+signed+overflow) + 5 cache (label match/miss/empty-required/skip-unlabelled/insert-fallback) | **P2** | ~350 | `proto/budget.rs`, `proto/routing.rs`, `cfg/model.rs`, `node/dispatcher/mod.rs`, `node/dispatcher/routing.rs`, `node/routing/cache.rs`, `node/runtime.rs` | ✅ done |
| 455.2b | **Labeled routes** (ingestion path) — closes the loop 455.2: at arrival a signed `RouteResponse` handler [routing.rs::handle_route_response](veilcore/src/node/dispatcher/routing.rs#L614) now calls `RouteCache::insert_labelled()` with `payload.target_labels` instead of ordinary `insert()`. Previously inbound labels were ignored (only outgoing worked). End-to-end test [route_response_labels_persist_in_route_cache](veilcore/src/node/dispatcher/mod.rs) checks full cycle: target signs labels → wire encode → peer wire decode → signature verify → `lookup_with_labels()` finds route with full-match, partial-match, and miss on unadvertised label | **P2** | ~10 + 1 test | `node/dispatcher/routing.rs`, `node/dispatcher/mod.rs` | ✅ done |
| 455.3 | **Egress diversification (weighted spread)** — fully wired in production. (a) Helper `GatewayList::pick_weighted(active_sessions, top_k, rand_u64)` in [gateway_list.rs](veilcore/src/node/gateway_list.rs) — top-K by effective_score, weighted-random sampling. (b) Config: `connection.exit_diversification: bool = false` (opt-in) + `connection.exit_diversification_top_k: u8 = 4` in [cfg/model.rs::ConnectionConfig](veilcore/src/cfg/model.rs). (c) Wire-in in [dispatcher/delivery.rs](veilcore/src/node/dispatcher/delivery.rs): selection precedence `prefer_internet_gateway → exit_diversification → preferred`. Internet-gateway preference remains above priority — semantic correctness > diversification. (d) `rand_seed_for_pick(trace_id)` mixes per-frame trace_id with nanosecond clock — re-dispatched frame with the trace_id not sticks on one GW. **Tests**: 4 for `pick_weighted` + 2 for `rand_seed_for_pick` (anti-determinism, per-trace divergence) | **P2** | ~150 | `node/gateway_list.rs`, `cfg/model.rs`, `node/dispatcher/mod.rs`, `node/dispatcher/delivery.rs`, `node/runtime.rs` | ✅ done (helper + wire-in + 6 tests) |
| 455.4 | **Config-distribution via DHT** (closes the loop 454.5 P1) — implemented via well-known DHT key `BLAKE3("veil:v1:bootstrap-bundle")` instead of anycast (analysis showed: anycast requires already-existing session-cohesion, and bootstrap needed BEFORE it). MVP: `bootstrap_bundle_dht_key()` + JSON-encoded `Vec<BootstrapPeer>` (4096 byte cap) + CLI `veil-cli config publish` (DhtPut) + `veil-cli config fetch [--dry-run]` (DhtGet → merge in config). New code: codec in [seeds.rs](veilcore/src/node/bootstrap/seeds.rs) (~50 LOC + 5 unit-tests) + handlers in [handlers.rs](veilcore/src/cmd/handlers.rs) (~120 LOC) + integration test [node_cmd.rs::config_publish_then_fetch_roundtrips_bootstrap_bundle](veilcore/src/cmd/node_cmd.rs). Use case: operator updates seeds → `config publish`; new node with one well-known seed → `config fetch` → picks up current list without recompilation | **P1** | ~200 (finished) | `node/bootstrap/seeds.rs`, `node/bootstrap/mod.rs`, `cmd/handlers.rs`, `cmd/cli.rs`, integration test in `cmd/node_cmd.rs` | ✅ done |
| 455.5 | **Adaptive failover (revised)** — implemented. The original formulation (multi-transport per session, hot standby) overestimated: for use case "peer slows down → switch over on another path" foundation already was (RouteCache.lookup_all returns alternatives, multi_path_enabled / redundant_send / ECMP-banding in `[routing]`), simply (a) off by default, (b) not surface operator, (c) at session close alternatives became "the best variant" only via 5-120 s probe cycle. **Implemented**: (1) **`demote_via`** in [routing/cache.rs](veilcore/src/node/routing/cache.rs) — multiplicative score scaling per `next_hop` (saturating at u32::MAX). Hooked in [dispatcher::on_session_closed](veilcore/src/node/dispatcher/routing.rs) with factor=4.0 → routes via closed peer instantly fly out from ECMP band, alts become winner on the next `lookup_all` (<1 RTT vs ~30 s previously). 5 unit-tests: alt-wins-after-demote, fallback-when-only-route, no-op-on-factor≤1, saturate-at-u32-max, only-touches-matching-via. (2) **`veil-cli node routes [<dst_node_id>]`** — optional dst_filter narrows to one peer; header line shows `multi-path: ON/off (paths=N, prio≤M) | redundant-send: ON/off | ecmp_band=...`. Operator sees "that exists in cache" + "is used whether this actually". `AdminCommand::Routes { dst_filter }` + `AdminMultiPathConfig` payload. (3) Doc-comment cross-references on `multi_path_enabled` + `redundant_send` explaining "adaptive-failover bundle"; **without separate `adaptive_failover: bool` flag** — operator opt-in explicit (two lines in config), without magic auto-override (risk misleading). (4) Operator guide [docs/adaptive-failover.md](docs/adaptive-failover.md). **Hot-standby session (option B from design-prop)** extracted in Epic 459 — requires session-layer surgery (`SessionRunner.stream` swap with preservation AEAD-state, multi-RTT probe loop) | **P2** | ~300 (finished) | `node/routing/cache.rs`, `node/dispatcher/routing.rs`, `node/admin.rs`, `node/runtime.rs`, `cmd/cli.rs`, `cmd/node_cmd.rs`, `cfg/model.rs` (doc), `docs/adaptive-failover.md` (new) | ✅ done |
| 459 | **Hot-standby session** (extracted from 455.5 — option B): pre-establish backup transport handle on that peer (alt scheme — tcp+wss and i.e...); on degradation signal atomically swap stream in existing `SessionRunner` without OVL1 re-handshake (AEAD state, session_id remain). Requires: (a) refactor `SessionRunner.stream: BoxIoStream` for allow swap under Mutex, (b) "warm probe" loop holds second connection alive, (c) trigger logic — degradation signal from which to do swap (frame timeout? loss-rate threshold?), (d) edge cases with in-flight write on old transport into time swap | **P3** | ~600-800 | `node/session/runner.rs`, `node/session/manager.rs`, `node/outbound_connector.rs`, `node/nat/coordinator.rs` | ✅ **all 4 stages done** (2026-04-24) — `SessionRunner.swap_rx: Option<Receiver<BoxIoStream>>` + `NextInput::SwapStream(..)` variant + main-loop swap handler in [session/runner.rs](veilcore/src/node/session/runner.rs). Swap happens **between frames** in `await_next_input` tick: priority-queue flush completed, not a single bytes from in-progress frame not read with old stream. AEAD state (`tx_cipher`, `rx_cipher`, `session_id`, counters) is preserved. Logged `session.transport_swapped`. Public API — `SessionRunner::with_swap_inbox() -> mpsc::Sender<BoxIoStream>`. **2 unit-test**: `swap_redirects_runner_to_new_stream_without_reset` (ping→pong on transportA → swap → ping→pong on transportB without re-handshake), `swap_preserves_aead_counter_across_transports` (then with real `SessionCipher` — if counter will reset, peer side not will decode second ping). Design + test plan: [docs/hot-standby.md](docs/hot-standby.md), [docs/hot-standby-test-plan-windows.md](docs/hot-standby-test-plan-windows.md). Stages (b)/(c)/(d) formatted as follow-ups in docs/hot-standby.md with table statuses. Full session-module tests 78/78 ✅, clippy all-targets ✅, release build ✅. <br><br>**Stage (d) Task 1 (wire-protocol foundation)** done (2026-04-24): three new `SessionMsg` variant — `HandoffInit=16`, `HandoffAck=17`, `HandoffAttach=18`, plus payload-structs `HandoffInitPayload`/`HandoffAckPayload` (32-byte nonce) / `HandoffAttachPayload` (32-byte session_id + 32-byte HMAC). HMAC = `BLAKE3::keyed(tx_key)(session_id \|\| nonce)` (helper `compute_hmac`). 8 new unit-tests (encode/decode roundtrip, wrong-size rejection, HMAC key/nonce/determinism). Updated `session_msg_rejects_unknown_discriminant` (16→19). Protocol rationale in session.rs doc-comment — HandoffInit/Ack over primary AEAD, HandoffAttach first frame on warm socket; nonce is stored **only at receiver** so that replay captured HandoffAttach required knowledge and AEAD key, and nonce. <br><br>**Stage (d) Task 2 (HandoffRegistry)** done (2026-04-24): [node/runtime/handoff.rs](veilcore/src/node/runtime/handoff.rs) — `HandoffRegistry` with `HashMap<[u8;32], PendingHandoff>` + `BTreeMap<(Instant, session_id), ()>` for O(log n) TTL-prune. `DEFAULT_TTL=10s`, `MAX_ENTRIES=1024` (≈100 KiB cap on adversarial growth). API: `insert / peek / consume (one-shot) / prune_expired / len`. Opportunistic pruning in each read-site. 6 unit-tests (one-shot consume, peek-idempotent, TTL-expiration, prune-noop-fresh, insert-replace, cap-evict-oldest-under-pressure). Gated `#[allow(dead_code)]` — consumers (Task 3/4) still not wired. <br><br>**Stage (c) done** (2026-04-24): auto-trigger infrastructure. (C1) `PeerConfig.alt_uri: Option<String>` + CLI `peers add --alt-uri` + TOML serde. (C2) [node/runtime/hot_standby.rs](veilcore/src/node/runtime/hot_standby.rs) — `HotStandbyController` with per-peer `alt_uri` map + per-peer flap-damping ring (60s window, capped by `max_swaps_per_minute`). `try_auto_trigger(peer_id, session_id, tx_key) -> bool` spawns one-shot WarmProbe in background, returns whether the swap was spawned (no alt_uri / flap-damped → false). 5 unit-tests. (C3) `SessionRunner.on_primary_write_error(&mut counter)` helper + `write_error_count` local in `run()` + `auto_trigger_after_write_errors` threshold field. 7 write-error sites wired. Counter does NOT reset on successful write (cumulative failure detection). (C4) Integration test `auto_trigger_fires_on_primary_write_error` — custom `WriteAlwaysFailsStream` + single-frame outbox → runner fails write → `on_primary_write_error` → `try_auto_trigger` → controller records 1 swap attempt (verified via `swap_attempts_in_window`). Log trace confirms: `session.hot_standby.auto_swap_trigger peer=... alt_uri=... swap_count_in_window=1`. (C5) [scripts/test-hot-standby.sh](scripts/test-hot-standby.sh) phase 5 runs the C4 unit test on live build — **all 5 phases green**. <br><br>**Timing caveat documented**: the write-error trigger fires AFTER primary refused a write, so full recovery depends on the transport being in a half-dead state (outbound blocked, inbound still reading — typical Windows Firewall scenario). Real-deployment induction belongs in [docs/hot-standby-test-plan-windows.md](docs/hot-standby-test-plan-windows.md) scenario 2. Proactive RTT-based trigger is a follow-up (stage c.2). <br><br>**Stage (b) B5 (operator-driven admin command + accept-side peek wiring) done** (2026-04-24): operator-facing `veil-cli node swap-transport --peer <node_id> --alt-uri <uri>` runs the full three-frame handoff protocol on a live 2-node fixture end-to-end. `AdminCommand::SwapTransport` → `NodeRuntime::initiate_hot_standby_handoff` → hex-parse peer → `swap_registry.session_id_for_peer` (secondary peer-index added to work around outbound/inbound handshake-race divergence in `SessionRegistry`) → `swap_registry.tx_key` → spawn one-shot `WarmProbe` → runs protocol → returns. Accept-side: `register_connection_session` now returns `Result<Option<AttachedDebugSession>>` — inbound path peeks first 24 bytes via `peek_and_dispatch`, if HandoffAttach binds to existing session returns `Ok(None)` (no new runner spawned). Hex parsing is inline (no new `hex` crate dep). Fixture test `scripts/test-hot-standby.sh` phase 4 now **active**: drives the admin command on a running 2-node fixture, asserts `session.transport_swapped` on both sides, asserts zero new `handshake.success` events (proves session preserved). **All 4 phases PASSED** on a live devnet. <br><br>**Stage (d) Tasks 3-5 done** (2026-04-24): <br><br>**Task 3** (runner dispatch): `SessionRunner` now handles three hot-standby frame in frame-dispatch loop. `HandoffInit` → decode payload, lookup rx_key from `raw_session_keys` (populate'd on **both** sides handshake — previously only outbound), insertion in `HandoffRegistry.insert(session_id, peer_id, nonce, rx_key)`, emit `HandoffAck` via priority queue. `HandoffAck` → forward nonce initiator via `handoff_ack_tx` channel (Sender stash warm-probe task — stage (b)). `HandoffAttach` on established session — protocol misuse, log+drop. Malformed payloads → `record_violation`. New fields `handoff_registry: Option<Arc<HandoffRegistry>>` and `handoff_ack_tx: Option<mpsc::Sender<[u8;32]>>` on `SessionRunner`. 3 unit-test (stash+ack roundtrip, ack forwarding, malformed rejection). <br><br>**Task 4a** (`SessionSwapRegistry`): new per-runtime `session_id → mpsc::Sender<BoxIoStream>` map in [node/runtime/handoff.rs](veilcore/src/node/runtime/handoff.rs). `SessionRunner::register_swap_channel()` returns `SwapRegistryGuard` — drop automatically removes entry at completion sessions (any path, including panic). Set from inbound + outbound runner-spawn paths. 3 unit-test. <br><br>**Task 4b** (accept-side peek+dispatch): `PrefixedStream<S>` wrapper — replay pre-read bytes before proxy to inner stream. `peek_and_dispatch(stream, handoff_reg, swap_reg, timeout) -> PeekOutcome { HandoffBound | Handshake(Box<dyn IoStream>) | Drop(reason) }`. Reads first 24 bytes (HEADER_SIZE), if this `SessionMsg::HandoffAttach` — decodes body, `registry.consume(session_id)` (one-shot), compares HMAC with `subtle::ct_eq` (constant-time), finds live swap_tx in `SessionSwapRegistry`, pushes stream in swap_rx. Otherwise — returns `Handshake(PrefixedStream)` for ordinary OVL1 handshake path. 4 unit-test (valid routing, non-handoff passthrough, bad HMAC drop, unknown session drop). <br><br>**Task 5** (E2E pipeline): integration test `end_to_end_handoff_pipeline_via_peek_and_dispatch` — full chain in one process via pair `tokio::io::duplex`: initiator sends `HandoffInit` → runner stash registry entry and responds `HandoffAck` → initiator opens warm socket → writes `HandoffAttach` with correct HMAC → accept-side `peek_and_dispatch` validates+routes stream in `swap_rx` → runner swap on new transport → Ping from initiator on warm socket → Pong back. Checks that session survived (no re-handshake). Logs: `session.handoff.init.received` + `session.transport_swapped` — both sides work on that `SessionRunner` via new byte-pipe. <br><br>**Final validation stage (d):** `cargo test node::runtime::handoff` 15/15 ✅, `node::session::runner` 24/24 ✅, `cargo clippy --all-targets -D warnings` ✅, `cargo build --release --features production-seeds` ✅. <br><br>**Remains stage (b) + (c)** — warm-probe task (background per-session dial alternative transport) + trigger logic (degradation signal → initiate handoff + open warm socket). Their can collect independently on top of a ready protocol (d) + runner-level swap (a). <br><br>**Follow-ups c.2 + c.3 + Windows harness done** (2026-04-24): <br><br>**c.2 (proactive RTT/idle trigger)** — `SessionRunner::run()` now tracks `last_rx` timestamp. If `now - last_rx >= idle_timeout * 2 / 3` and flag `stall_trigger_fired == false` — is called `fire_hot_standby_trigger("rx_stall")`, firing warm-probe **to** full idle-timeout closedown. Flag is reset on any inbound byte or after swap. New event `session.hot_standby.trigger_raised` goes in logs. Unit-test `rx_stall_fires_proactive_trigger_before_idle_timeout` (50ms keepalive + 150ms idle → 100ms stall threshold, `ReadsBlockForeverStream` fixture) validates that trigger is parsed out via ~100ms without write-error induction. <br><br>**c.3 (peer capability auto-discovery)** — new TLV `ADVERTISED_TRANSPORTS_TLV_TAG=0x0012` in AttachPayload (`count: u8` + `count × {len: u16 BE, utf8 bytes}`); wire-cap 512 bytes, to 8 entries. Helpers `encode_attach_with_tlvs(payload, vivaldi, battery, advertised_transports)` + `decode_advertised_transports_from_attach(buf) -> Vec<String>` in [proto/session.rs](veilcore/src/proto/session.rs). `perform_ovl1_handshake` accepts additional `local_advertised_transports: &[String]` (11- arg, `#[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]`); `OvlHandshakeResult.remote_advertised_transports: Vec<String>` forward via fast-path resumption paths and full-handshake. Production call-site in [runtime/mod.rs](veilcore/src/node/runtime/mod.rs) removes snapshot active `[[listen]]` (with fallback `transport` when operator not specified `advertise`) and passes in handshake. Receive-side `cache_peer_handshake_state` calls `HotStandbyController::auto_set_alt_uri_from_transports(peer_id, transports, primary_scheme)` — pickup first URI with scheme != primary, record in separate `auto_alt_uris` map. `alt_uri_for` now fall-back to auto-map when operator-configured map empty (manual config always wins). 6 unit-tests on TLV (roundtrip, empty-no-tlv, coexist with Vivaldi+battery, count cap, garbage-trailing, absent returns empty) + 4 unit-test on controller (picks-different-scheme, only-same-scheme-returns-none, skips-malformed, manual-precedence). Removes requirement operator manually to specify `alt_uri` in `[[peers]]`. <br><br>**Windows harness** — [scripts/test-hot-standby.ps1](scripts/test-hot-standby.ps1) port bash-script on PowerShell + automation scenarios 2/3/4 from [docs/hot-standby-test-plan-windows.md](docs/hot-standby-test-plan-windows.md). Commands: `run / start / stop / verify / logs` (mirror bash) + `scenario-firewall` (New-NetFirewallRule blocks primary port, expects `session.hot_standby.trigger_raised` / `session.transport_swapped` in 30s) + `scenario-flap` (12 toggle-cycles by 5s block / 5s unblock = 2min window; checks that swap count ≤ 2× max_swaps_per_minute and are written `flap_damped` events) + `scenario-forgery` (admin-surface forgery: unknown peer_id + malformed URI; wire-level HMAC forgery covered by rust unit-tests). Requires elevated PowerShell for firewall rules. <br><br>**Final validation:** `cargo test -p veilcore --lib -- node::runtime::hot_standby:: node::session::runner:: node::session::warm_probe:: proto::session::tests::advertised` 45/45 ✅, `cargo clippy -p veilcore --lib --tests` clean, relevant runtime+session tests (154) ✅. <br><br>**c.2.2 (keepalive-probe timeout) done** (2026-04-24): catches one-way-broken primary when peer retransmissions not give rx_stall to fire (Windows Firewall half-block). Wire in no way not changed — OVL1 already had `ControlMsg::KeepaliveAck` with Epic 75, simply runner its not tracked. Now: per outbound `Keepalive` — we set `pending_keepalive_ack_since = Some(now)` if None; on incoming `KeepaliveAck` — we clean pending and we reset `keepalive_probe_trigger_fired`; timer-tick fires `fire_hot_standby_trigger("keepalive_probe_timeout")` when `now - pending >= keepalive_probe_timeout`. Default timeout = `1 × keepalive_interval` (was 2× initially — on Windows LAN station's TCP RST on T=25-30s was 20s probe on pair seconds; 1× interval wins this the race). Also fixed `keepalive_enabled` gate with `as_secs() > 0` on `!is_zero()` — sub-second intervals in tests previously disabled the entire keepalive-mechanism. 1 new unit-test (`keepalive_probe_timeout_fires_trigger_when_no_ack` — fixture accepts writes but never sends → probe fires in 1 × keepalive_interval). 37 session::runner + hot_standby tests green sequentially. <br><br>**Diagnostic logs**: `session.keepalive.sent` + `session.keepalive.ack_received` derived in debug-level, so that production-log not got polluted pair lines per keepalive cycle. Enabled via `[global] log_level = "debug"` for operator on debug-sessions. <br><br>**Windows firewall-test tuning**: default `keepalive_interval_secs = 30` fires in real network (TCP retransmission gives ~60s window). In synthetic test with `New-NetFirewallRule` station TCP gives up in ~9s — in such a narrow window probe with 30s timeout not keeps up. For Windows firewall-test recommendation in docs/hot-standby.md: `keepalive_interval_secs = 3` (probe fires on T≈4.5-7.5s, catches close on T≈9s). In production nothing to tune not need. <br><br>**c.3 same-scheme bug-fix** (2026-04-24): first implementation `auto_set_alt_uri_from_transports` filtered out same-scheme alt URIs ("skip tcp if primary tcp"). On TCP-only two-port test this gave empty auto_alt_uris. Fixed: we compare full URI (including host:port), not only scheme — i.e... operator with two TCP-listeners on different ports gets correct failover. 1 new unit-test (`auto_set_alt_uri_picks_same_scheme_different_port`). <br><br>**Real validation on 2 Windows-hosts (laptop + station)**: scenario1 (manual swap), scenario4 (forgery resistance) green. scenario2 (firewall auto-trigger) fires with `keepalive_interval_secs=3` in both config — probe fires reason=keepalive_probe_timeout, warm-probe reconnects to alt_uri, transport_swapped. scenario3 (flap damping) not was run on real hosts — limit time; unit-test `flap_damping_caps_swap_attempts` + core `HotStandbyController::register_swap_attempt` confirm contract. **Epic 459 closed**. |

### Order

1. **455.1 + 455.4 in parallel** — ✅ done. Both use only local data / explicit operator publication; no unauthorized transport-fingerprints.
2. **455.3** — ✅ done (helper); wire-in deferred (gate behind config flag).
3. **455.1b** — ⊘ skip. Cross-node sharing deemed not worthwhile: random walk + PEX implicit signaling give that effect without exposure transport-set.
4. **455.2** — design first: wire-format bump in RouteRequest/Response.
5. **455.5** — the last; requires changes session-layer semantics + depends from 455.1.

## Epic 468. Operational polish + Ops docs (2026-04-23)

Goal: prepare veil to "operator can to bring up and to monitor node without reading sources". Split on two waves: (a) code-level polish — CLI conclusions, defaults, log hygiene; (b) docs — runbook for seed-nodes + monitoring.

### 468.A. Operational polish

| # | Place | Category | Finding | Fix |
|---|-------|-----------|---------|------|
| 468.1 | [cmd/node_cmd.rs `render_show`](veilcore/src/cmd/node_cmd.rs#L191) | missing-info | `node show` not shows veil binary version + build features (`production-seeds` / `allow-empty-seeds`). Operator not can distinguish stale binary from fresh in field. | ✅ done — `AdminNodeSummary` received `version: String` (= `env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION")`) and `build_features: Vec<String>` (compile-time `cfg!()` for production-seeds, allow-empty-seeds, quic-session, tun-interface, rocksdb-cold). Render-function outputs both lines. |
| 468.2 | [cmd/node_cmd.rs `render_show`](veilcore/src/cmd/node_cmd.rs#L191) | display | `metrics_endpoint` in output shown as-exists — can to contain auth-token in URL (`http://...?token=XYZ`). | ✅ done — query-string is truncated before exposing; marker `(query-stripped)` is attributed. |
| 468.3 | [cmd/sessions_cmd.rs `render_sessions`](veilcore/src/cmd/sessions_cmd.rs#L71) | UX | Shows full 64-hex `node_id` and `link_id` — hard to read in terminal. | ✅ done — added `-v / --verbose` flag to `sessions list`; default shortens hex to first 12 characters with an ellipsis. |
| 468.4 | [cmd/peers_cmd.rs `list_banned`](veilcore/src/cmd/peers_cmd.rs) | UX | Ban list outputs "admin ban" / "manual" without timestamp when was banned. | ✅ done — `BanEntry.banned_at: Option<SystemTime>` (set in `ban_inner`), `AdminBanEntry.banned_at_unix: Option<u64>`, column `banned_at` in `peers banned` output (ISO UTC via Hinnant civil-from-days, without chrono). Pre-468.4 persisted bans receive `None` and are printed as `-`. |
| 468.5 | [cfg/model.rs `ConnectionConfig` default](veilcore/src/cfg/model.rs) | defaults | Values by default for `max_concurrent`, `idle_timeout`, `max_per_ip` — check that they prod-sensible (not too liberal, not too strict). | ✅ done — audit added as `docs/OPERATIONS.md` → "Default Tuning Guidance": RAM budget table, 3 deployment profiles (small seed 2GB / large seed 8GB / leaf), per-field tradeoff table. In code two critical default received warning doc-comments: `DhtConfig::default_max_store_entries()` (1 M entries = ~4 GiB RAM worst-case) and `impl Default for NodeCapacityConfig` (max_relay_sessions=0 / max_total_sessions=0 unlimited — DoS-vulnerable for public seeds). Defaults not changed — level private/trusted deployment remains the default; operators public infra must explicitly tune by docs. |
| 468.6 | [cfg/model.rs `GlobalConfig` default](veilcore/src/cfg/model.rs#L1852) | defaults | `admin_socket: None` — each deploy requires explicit config. For seed-nodes can propose sensible default like `/var/run/veil/admin.sock`. | ✅ done — helper `cfg::default_admin_socket_uri(hint: &Path) -> String` moved from `cmd/handlers.rs` in `cfg/locate.rs` (shared for init and runtime). Rules: root → `unix:///var/run/veil/veil.sock`; unix non-root → `unix://<abs(config_path.with_extension("sock") | dir/admin.sock)>`; non-Unix → `tcp://127.0.0.1:0`. `config init` continues to write default in file; `node/admin.rs::resolve_admin_endpoint` now fallback- on derived default when `admin_socket=None` but `config_dir` is known — minimal hand-written TOML configs "just work" without explicit `admin_socket`. Without `config_dir` (test/synthetic paths) error remains explicit. 2 new unit-test: fallback at unset + explicit error at unset+no_config_dir. |
| 468.7 | full codebase | log hygiene | Needed full review INFO/WARN logs on PII leakage. Fast grep on `private_key|password|master_seed` gives 0 leaks, but IP-addresses (observed_addr, transport URLs) are logged freely — this already leak `handshake.success source=inbound(...)`. For privacy-focused veil this sensitive. | ✅ done — actual scope turned out to be less than assessment 170+ call-sites: `handshake.success/failure`, `session.open/close/peer_matched` already use `source={SessionSource}` (renders as `inbound(0x..)`/`outbound(0x..)` — internal IDs, not addresses) and `node_id=<hex>` (cryptographic pubkey hash, not IP). Applied `redact_addr_for_log` into all remaining call-sites with transport/addr: `pex.connect` (runtime/mod.rs:2220), `listen.start` local_addr+transport (runtime/mod.rs:2467), `gateway.failover` (mesh_gateway.rs:125), `mesh.autodiscover.connect` (mesh_gateway.rs:345), `route.discover_offer` transports array (dispatcher/routing.rs:149), `routing.pow_accept` debug (routing.rs:756). Exception: `socks5 bind {listen_addr} failed` (proxy/socks5.rs:140) — local ERROR-level, left for debuggability (operator owns the bind addr). Inner transport errors in outbound_connector `err_str` can to carry addresses, but this propagated error messages; redacting their will break diagnostics. |
| 468.8 | [node/bootstrap/seeds.rs:70](veilcore/src/node/bootstrap/seeds.rs) (= 466.7) | release-blocker | Hard-coded `BUILTIN_SEEDS` placeholder. | ✅ done — `builtin_seeds()` filled real b1/b2/b3.example.com (tls://:9906, Ed25519 pubkeys + nonces). Build with `--features production-seeds` successful. Playbook-inventory grouped on that peer-set for consistency. |
| 468.9 | missing CLI | missing | No commands for dump `peers_discovered.json` / `dht_values.json` (persist files). Operator not can debug- that node sees without reentry in disk. | ✅ done — `veil-cli debug peers discovered` added: new `AdminCommand::PeersDiscovered`, `AdminResult::DiscoveredPeers { peers: Vec<AdminDiscoveredPeer> }`, runtime getter `NodeRuntime::discovered_peers()` filters `NodeState.peers` by `source != Configured` and maps in `AdminDiscoveredPeer { node_id, transport, source, peer_id, bootstrap_only, public_key, nonce }`. CLI renders table (peer_id / source / bootstrap_only / node_id / transport). Live-equivalent `peers_discovered.json` without reading file. `dht_values.json` already exposed via existing `node dht list` (466.3). |
| 468.10 | CLI top-level | missing | No `veil-cli version` (although `-V`/`--version` via clap must to work). Check that is plumbed through. | ✅ done — `veil-cli --version` already works correctly (yields `veil-cli 0.1.0` via clap). Build-features appear via `node show` (see. 468.1). |
| 468.11 | [cmd/node_cmd.rs `render_metrics`](veilcore/src/cmd/node_cmd.rs#L234) | UX | When metrics disabled — output "all counters below are zero" long, but operator could would simply to see "metrics disabled — configure [metrics] in config" without zero lines. | ✅ done — early return one line `"metrics: DISABLED — add [metrics] section to config to enable Prometheus exporter"`, without 35 zero lines. |

### 468.B. Ops docs (runbook)

| # | Category | What needed | Where |
|---|-----------|-----------|-----|
| 468.12 | seed setup | Step-by-step: generate identity → fill BUILTIN_SEEDS → build with `--features production-seeds` → deploy → verify | ✅ done — `docs/OPERATIONS.md` extended section "Seed Node Setup" + multi-seed redundancy + systemd unit example. |
| 468.13 | monitoring | Describe metrics Prometheus endpoint; which alerts to bring up (session-drop rate, ban-storm, DHT emptiness, mailbox overflow); that to look in dashboard | ✅ done — new `docs/MONITORING.md`: exporter setup, ~40 metrics with alert thresholds, 6 starter alert rules, Grafana dashboard structure, "first thing to check" cheatsheet. |
| 468.14 | disaster recovery | (a) restart after crash — that persists (bans.json, dht_values.json, identity); (b) rollback identity via BIP-39 phrase; (c) re-key MLKEM; (d) unban at false positive ban storm | ✅ done — `docs/OPERATIONS.md` section "Disaster Recovery": persistence inventory table, crash+restart, identity loss + BIP-39 restore, ML-KEM rotation, ban storm recovery. |
| 468.15 | common troubleshooting | FAQ: "peer not connects" → check ban_list, firewall, routes; "NO_ROUTE on chat" → check DHT and topology (Epic 467 learnings); "chat server not accepts" → IPC socket perms | ✅ done — new `docs/TROUBLESHOOTING.md`: triage workflow, sections by 7 categories (start-fail / peer-connect / chat-deliver / bans / DHT / mailbox / IPC / perf / privacy), end-to-end references on Epic 467 fixes. |

**Priority fixes (from simple to complex):**

1. 468.10 — check `veil-cli --version` (1 min)
2. 468.1 — add version in `node show` (15 min)
3. 468.11 — `render_metrics` at disabled shorter (5 min)
4. 468.2 — scrub token from metrics_endpoint (10 min)
5. 468.3 — `--verbose` flag at `sessions list` + short ids (30 min)
6. 468.4 — timestamp in ban list (20 min)
7. 468.7 — log PII review + conditional-verbosity (2-4 hours, requires triage 181 call)
8. 468.9 — `debug peers discovered` command (30 min)
9. 468.12-15 — docs (large chunk)

## Epic 467. Fragmented-topology bugs (2026-04-23)

Found at attempt test FORWARD-routing via banned direct links on `stend/` (5 nodes, topology 1-2-3-4-5 ring).

| # | Place | Category | Finding | Fix |
|---|-------|-----------|---------|------|
| 467.1 | [runtime/inspect.rs:118](veilcore/src/node/runtime/inspect.rs) `ban_node` | bug (medium) | `peers ban <node_id>` sets record in `BanList` and persist- in `bans.json`, but **not closes already-active sessions** to banned peer. Handshake-check cuts off only NEW sessions; inbound TCP-connection + session-task continue to work. At fragmentation on stend- all 5 nodes had active sessions to all banned peers despite on correct `peers banned` output. | ✅ done — allocated helper `tear_down_session()`, is called from both `ban_node` and `kill_session`. Order in `ban_node`: tear-down → `ban_manual` (otherwise kill_session 30s temp ban will overwrite persistent manual). |
| 467.2 | [dispatcher/routing.rs:1458-1637](veilcore/src/node/dispatcher/routing.rs) `handle_recursive_query` | bug (routing) | FIND_NODE recursive query target=X lands in loop when target is **direct neighbor** forwarding-nodes, but **not in its DHT k-bucket** routing table. Scenario: node3 sends FIND_NODE(node1) → node2 gets → `find_closest_nodes(node1, k=2)` not returns node1 (although node2 has active session to it!) → split-horizon discards node3 (the sender) → empty list next_hops → query silently is dropped → initiator retries ttl=40 → infinite bounce. Root reason: DHT routing-table (`add_contact`) not contains direct-session peers after restart, while PEX/bootstrap not will complete. | ✅ done part 1 — `dht.add_contact(peer_node_id, transport)` after handshake success in outbound + inbound. DHT kbucket now consistent with session_tx_registry. |
| 467.2b | [dispatcher/routing.rs send_response + handle_recursive_response](veilcore/src/node/dispatcher/routing.rs) | bug (routing) | After 467.2 query reaches to target, but **response is lost on path back**. Scenario: node1 (target) forms RecursiveResponse for reply_to=node3, but not has direct session (banned) and route_cache empty after bans → response is dropped. Intermediate forwarders also not help — handle_recursive_response for not-our query silently ignores. | ✅ done — (a) intermediate node at forward preserves `query_id → reply_to` in `recursive_reverse_path` map (TTL 30s, cap 4096); (b) `handle_recursive_response` if pending=None (not our query), looks reverse-path and forwards response to `reply_to` via 3 fallback-and: direct → route_cache → DHT-greedy; (c) responder-side `send_response` also extended to 3-step fallback with DHT k-closest. Wire-format not broken. |
| 467.2c | [node/dht/routing.rs:223-270](veilcore/src/node/dht/routing.rs) `RoutingTable::insert` | bug (DHT) | **Per-bucket rate limit (Epic 407.5)** = max 1 insert per second per bucket. Protection from Eclipse attacks for UNSOLICITED inserts. But at startup-, when node simultaneously accept- 2-3 inbound handshake-and from one bucket-and (close XOR distance), **rate-limit drops all except first**. After 467.2 this killed 467.2 fix: only one of direct peers landed in DHT, the rest missing → `find_closest_nodes` incomplete → recursive routing wanders. | ✅ done — added `RoutingTable::insert_trusted` + `KademliaService::add_contact_trusted` which bypass- rate limit. 467.2 outbound + inbound paths switched on trusted variant. Rationale: peer elapsed OVL1 handshake already trusted — Eclipse-protection is redundant. Eclipse-protection remains for unsolicited paths (PEX walks, FIND_NODE responses). |
| 467.2d | [node/dht/routing.rs `bucket_cap`](veilcore/src/node/dht/routing.rs) | bug (DHT) | After 467.2/c everything equals lost peers from DHT! Root cause: **sketch buckets** — Kademlia optimization, at which distant XOR distance buckets have capacity=1 (one contact on bucket). When two direct peer-and land in one sketch bucket (different node_ids but identical bucket_index from-behind XOR distance to local), second insert **overwrites** first (LRU pop_front + push_back at cap=1). Symptom: on 5-node stend, at node2 in DHT after `dht.peer_added × 4` remained only 2 contact. | ✅ done — for trusted insert we ignore sketch limit, we use full bucket capacity (K=20). Sketch — optimization for rarely-used far peers; direct-session peers, conversely, in hot path and must be fully visible for `find_closest_nodes`. |
| 467.2e | [dispatcher/routing.rs `send_response` + `handle_recursive_response`](veilcore/src/node/dispatcher/routing.rs) | bug (routing) | After 467.2d DHT was correct, but **response landed in loop**! Diagnostics showed: node1 responds query via DHT-greedy → selects node5 (closest to node3 by XOR) → node5 also DHT-greedy → selects node1 (closest to node3 in its DHT) → node1 again gets its own response → "orphan" log. Pure XOR-distance greedy not accounts for from where arrived packet, therefore easily walks in circles. | ✅ done — (a) in `send_response` added step 0: **PREFER `peer_id`** (query sender). This node already has `reverse_path[query_id]=originator` and attributes reply_to correctly by long path. (b) In `handle_recursive_response` relay: split-horizon **rules out `peer_id` from candidates** and route_cache and DHT-greedy — prevents immediate loop back that who sent response. |
| 467.2f | [dispatcher/routing.rs FIND_NODE answer piggy-back](veilcore/src/node/dispatcher/routing.rs) | bug (routing) | After 467.2e routing works (first chat passed!), but **second and next fall with `NO_E2E_KEY`**. Root cause: piggy-back RouteResponse (carries mlkem_pubkey) uses only direct + cache fallback — my 467.2e fix to it not applied. After cleanup cache (on reload, banpath etc) and fragmented network → piggy-back is lost → ML-KEM key not cached on originator → IPC_SEND fails encryption. | ✅ done — piggy-back RouteResponse send received that 4--step fallback chain that and `send_response`: (0) via=sender (peer_id) — priority, (1) direct, (2) route_cache (excluding self+peer_id), (3) DHT-greedy (excluding self+peer_id). Plus diagnostic log `recursive.answer.piggyback_sent/dropped`. |
| 467.3 | [stend/](stend/) + CLI | missing | No CLI commands for inspection DHT routing-table. For diagnostics 467.2 useful was would `veil-cli debug dht contacts` (outputs contents k-buckets). Currently exists `dht_contacts()` in [runtime/mod.rs](veilcore/src/node/runtime/mod.rs) but it not plumbed through in admin+CLI. | ✅ done — already implemented via `veil-cli node dht routing`: `AdminCommand::DhtRouting` → `AdminResult::DhtContacts { contacts: Vec<AdminDhtContact> }`, CLI wiring in [cmd/node_cmd.rs:42](veilcore/src/cmd/node_cmd.rs#L42) + renderer. Under `node dht` and not under `debug dht`, but by functionality equivalent. |

**Reproduction (after application 467.1 fix):**
```bash
cd stend
./topology-fragment.sh   # now actually tears all banned sessions
# sessions list MUST to show by 2 sessions per node (5 allowed pairs)
# chat node1 → node3 via node2 must go; FORWARD in logs node2
```

## Epic 466. Code-audit round 4 (2026-04-22)

**Scope:** pass by `.rs` files, to note potential bugs / vulnerabilities / perf / memory / zombie-code / duplicates. Network pre-deployment — breaking changes OK.

**Previous rounds (closed in [`TASKS_ARCHIVE.md`](TASKS_ARCHIVE.md)):** 448 (cleanup round 3), 449 (13 findings 2026-04-18), 461 (security+DoS audit 2026-04-20), 465 (refactor round).

| # | Place | Category | Finding | Fix |
|---|-------|-----------|---------|------|
| 466.1 | [veilcore/src/proto/](veilcore/src/proto/) | zombie | **Epic 463.3 was not completed**: 4 unreferenced proto-module (`app_state`, `wake_hint`, `chat_backup`, `identity_events`, ~2469 LOC) all still on disk. Memory-index lied, that they removed. | ✅ done — removed physically together with 2 dead scenarios from `integration_tests.rs`. |
| 466.2 | [veilcore/src/crypto/identity.rs](veilcore/src/crypto/identity.rs) | stale-annotation | 7+ `#[allow(dead_code)]` on `derive_master_sk_ed25519`, `compute_identity_id`, `certify_message`, `MASTER_SEED_LEN` and i.e... — all these items **actually are used** in `pair_runtime.rs`, `identity_contact.rs`, `identity_document.rs`. Annotations saved with 462.1 and became obsolete. | ✅ done — removed 9 stale `#[allow(dead_code)]`, clippy `-D warnings` clean. |
| 466.3 | `cmd/sovereign_identity.rs`, `cfg/sovereign_flow.rs` | duplicate | 2 identical copies `hex_encode` (12 LOC each) instead of thin-wrapper. `cmd/peers_cmd.rs::hex_string` and `node/admin.rs::bytes_to_hex` already were wrapper-, but 2 full copies remained. | ✅ done — collapsed in `crate::util::bytes_to_hex`. |
| 466.4 | [veilcore/src/cfg/instance.rs:208-233](veilcore/src/cfg/instance.rs) `write_atomically` | bug (minor) | At error `OpenOptions::open(&tmp)` original `io::Error` is lost and is replaced on `io::Error::other("tmp write failed")`. Caller gets meaningless text, losing `kind: PermissionDenied` / `NotADirectory` and i.e... | ✅ done — original `io::Error` is plumbed through. |
| 466.5 | [veilcore/src/node/dht/kademlia.rs:275-280](veilcore/src/node/dht/kademlia.rs) | recoverable-panic | `let Some(ref local_v) = self.local_vivaldi else { unreachable!() }` — correctly, i.e..to. guard- above is checked `is_some()`, but pattern fragile to future edits. | ✅ done — rewritten in `if let (true, Some(l), Some(p)) = ...` with explicit guard in pattern. |
| 466.6 | [veilcore/src/node/session/quic_transport.rs](veilcore/src/node/session/quic_transport.rs) | test-quality | Massive usage `.unwrap()` (40+) — everything inside `#[cfg(test)]`, but file entirely smoke-test-style without structuring. Works, but debugging painful. | ⏸ low priority — test-only code. |
| 466.7 | [veilcore/src/node/bootstrap/seeds.rs:70](veilcore/src/node/bootstrap/seeds.rs) | pre-release TODO | Hard-coded seed nodes placeholder. | ✅ done — closed together with 468.8, see. above. |
| 466.8 | [veilcore/src/cmd/node_cmd.rs:805-812](veilcore/src/cmd/node_cmd.rs) `save_config` | test-infra | Helper uses `format!("{prefix}-{unique}.toml")` without `pid+nanos` — at parallel `cargo nextest` worker-processes possible collision paths. A neighboring `config_with_admin` this already accounts for. | ✅ done — migrated on `test_support::scratch_dir(prefix)` (128-bit nonce + EACCES retry). |
| 466.9 | [veilcore/src/cfg/sovereign_flow.rs:1045-1057](veilcore/src/cfg/sovereign_flow.rs) `save_identity_sk` | robustness | `File::OpenOptions::mode(0o600)` — if `open(&tmp)` will fall with transit `EACCES` (WSL2 /tmp flake), reverse retry no. Similarly with `cfg/instance.rs::write_atomically`. | ✅ done — added `util::with_eacces_retry` (5 attempts, exp backoff 25→200 ms) + `util::create_dir_all_with_eacces_retry`. Applied in `cfg/instance.rs::write_atomically` + `LocalInstance::load`, `cfg/sovereign_flow.rs::save_identity_sk`, `node/identity/sovereign.rs::save_name_claim`, `sim/network.rs::next_sim_config_path_with_dir`. WSL2 EACCES everything still gives ~110-130 crashes from 2260 — kernel-level race, userspace retry gives only marginal win. |
| 466.10 | [veilcore/src/node/runtime/mod.rs:2388-2402](veilcore/src/node/runtime/mod.rs) public accessors | refactor-hygiene | The block `pub fn summary / listens / sessions / loss_tracker_snapshot / peers` + admin-mutators (`ban_node`, `kill_session`, `broadcast_epidemic`) — about 150 LOC a mix read/mutate. Not urgently, but hinders reading mod.rs. | ✅ done — all 13 methods extracted in [`runtime/inspect.rs`](veilcore/src/node/runtime/inspect.rs) (173 LOC). Read+mutate deliberately are held together as "operator-facing inspection+control API" — matches 1:1 `AdminCommand` variants. `runtime/mod.rs`: 4422→4290 LOC. |
| 466.11 | [veilcore/src/proto/name_claim_v2.rs:377-395](veilcore/src/proto/name_claim_v2.rs) | minor | `slice.try_into().unwrap()` at reading u16/u32/u64 from already-validated size slice — corect, but clippy-noise + pattern pops up in other decode paths. | ✅ done — replaced on `.expect("get(..pos+N) returns N-byte slice")` for all three sizes. |
| 466.12 | full codebase | perf/memory | 1750 `.unwrap()` in non-test code — superficial figure, but most valid (locks, pre-validated parses). Requires manual triage. | ✅ done (audit) — run hot-path audit: `proto/` (122 decoders), `node/dispatcher/`, `node/dht/`, `node/session/handshake.rs`, `node/identity/`. All decoders bounds-checked before slicing, all `Vec::with_capacity` capped via per-message-type `MAX_*` constants. **DoS-vector NOT found.** Residual 1750 unwrap = `Mutex::lock().unwrap()` (poison, ok) + `try_into().unwrap()` after bounds check + test code. |
| 466.13 | [veilcore/src/sim/network.rs:27-50](veilcore/src/sim/network.rs) `next_sim_config_path*` | test-infra | Uses `pid+counter` instead of 128-bit nonce. Partial gives collision in parallel `nextest` runs. | ✅ done — added `sim_unique_suffix(prefix)` (pid + 128-bit OsRng + counter) shared between both functions. Path-shape saved: `next_sim_config_path` returns top-level `.toml`, `_with_dir` — `<dir>/config.toml`. |

**Fix priority (simplest to hardest):**
1. 466.2 — remove stale `#[allow(dead_code)]` (5 minutes)
2. 466.3 — collapse hex-duplicates (20 minutes)
3. 466.4 — `write_atomically` error-propagation (5 minutes)
4. 466.5 — kademlia unreachable pattern (5 minutes)
5. 466.11 — explicitness in `name_claim_v2` decoder (5 minutes)
6. 466.9 — retry-on-EACCES for atomic-write (10 minutes)

## Epic 469. Code-audit round 5 (2026-04-23)

**Scope:** full-codebase pass — potential bugs, recoverable panics, concurrency, fs atomicity, duplicates, unused code.  Continuation of rounds 448 / 449 / 461 / 466 (closed).  Network is pre-deployment — breaking changes OK.

### Findings table

| # | Location | Category | Finding | Why it exists | Fix |
|---|-------|-----------|---------|---------------------|------|
| 469.1 | [cfg/store.rs:54](veilcore/src/cfg/store.rs#L54) `save_config` | bug (medium) | `fs::write(path, content)` — non-atomic. Crash mid-write → truncated TOML → node fails to start on reboot, config lost. | Path-of-least-resistance write. An atomic helper already exists (`cfg/instance.rs::write_atomically`, `util::atomic_write` in another form) but was not applied to the primary config. | ✅ done — `save_config` uses `util::atomic_write` (tmp + fsync + rename). |
| 469.2 | [node/runtime/mod.rs:4119, 4194](veilcore/src/node/runtime/mod.rs#L4119) `persist_discovered_peers`, `persist_bans` | bug (medium) | Non-atomic `fs::write` for `peers_discovered.json` + `bans.json`. Crash mid-write → corrupted JSON → the parallel graceful-handler silently ignores it on restart (loading returns `Err → return`), and the data is lost. | Same as 469.1 — `fs::write` is simpler. | ✅ done — both sites go through `util::atomic_write`. |
| 469.3 | [node/local_transport.rs:120-128, 141-149](veilcore/src/node/local_transport.rs#L120) `write_token_file`, `write_port_file` | security (TOCTOU window) | `remove` → `write` (default 0644) → `set_owner_only` (chmod 0600). Between `write` and `chmod` the file is world-readable; another process with local access can read the admin/ipc token. The window is milliseconds, but race-able under load. | Write + chmod pattern that does not account for the race. | ✅ done — `write_owner_only(path, bytes)` uses `OpenOptions::mode(0o600).create(true)` directly with the right mode. Window closed. |
| 469.4 | [node/runtime/debug.rs:82, 91, 111](veilcore/src/node/runtime/debug.rs#L82) | recoverable panic | `.lock().unwrap()` on `session_registry` instead of the consistent `unwrap_or_else(\|p\| p.into_inner())`. 3 test-only debug helpers. They panic on a poisoned mutex, while the other 71 sites in the codebase use poison-tolerant access. | Style inconsistency; the debug API was written hastily. | ✅ done — all 3 sites use the poison-tolerant pattern. |
| 469.5 | [proto/delivery.rs:539](veilcore/src/proto/delivery.rs#L539) `MailboxAckPayload::encode` | recoverable panic | `assert!(self.seqs.len() <= MAX_MAILBOX_ACK_BATCH)` in release mode. A caller with >batch_cap seqs crashes the node. Documented as "silent truncation would leak mailbox storage forever" but a panic in prod is not an option either. | Pragmatic 179.B3 choice: ACK truncation would be worse. But the panic is recoverable. | ✅ done — signature `-> Result<Vec<u8>, ProtoError>`; over-cap returns `ValueTooLarge` instead of panicking. New test `mailbox_ack_over_cap_returns_error_not_panic`. |
| 469.6 | [proto/*.rs × 10](veilcore/src/proto/) | duplicate | 10-12 identical copies of `read_u8`, `read_u16`, `read_u32`, `read_u64`, `read_bytes`, `read_array` — identical 3-6-line decoders, `(buf, &mut pos, field: &'static str) -> Result<T, ProtoError>`. | Historical: each proto module got its own copies when it was split out of the shared code. | ✅ done — a new `proto::cursor` submodule contains all 6 helpers + 4 unit tests. 11 files stopped duplicating (~50 fn removed). The session.rs 2-arg variant was extended to a 3-arg API with field names. identity_master_file.rs was left as is (its local `MasterFileError` does not match `ProtoError`). |
| 469.7 | 8 copies of `leading_zero_bits` | duplicate | 8 separate implementations — variants `-> u32 / u16 / u8`. Files: `crypto/pow/score.rs`, `node/util.rs`, `node/identity/verify.rs/resolver.rs/publish.rs/pex/initiator.rs`, `node/routing/pow.rs`, `cfg/sovereign_flow.rs`. | Scattered inline copies; `node/util::leading_zero_bits` already exists but is not used everywhere. | ✅ done — `crate::util::leading_zero_bits(bytes: &[u8]) -> u32`. All 8 files redirect-ed; narrow callers (`as u8`/`as u16` compare) widened to u32. 5 `leading_zero_bits_*` unit tests stayed in place. |
| 469.8 | 3 copies of `atomic_write` | duplicate | 3 different `atomic_write` impls: `node/identity/revocation_cache.rs`, `cfg/sovereign_flow.rs`, `cmd/sovereign_identity.rs`. Plus `cfg/instance.rs::write_atomically` and `util::with_eacces_retry` already exist. | Same dir-local pattern recreated independently. | ✅ done — all 3 collapsed into `util::atomic_write(path, bytes)` (mkdir parent + fsync + rename + EACCES retry). |
| 469.9 | 5 copies of `fn map_node_error` | duplicate | `cmd/debug.rs`, `cmd/node_cmd.rs`, `cmd/peers_cmd.rs`, `cmd/pex_cmd.rs`, `cmd/sessions_cmd.rs` — 5 byte-for-byte identical functions. | Each cmd module created its own. | ✅ done — new `cmd::util::map_node_error`, 5 copies removed. |
| 469.10 | 2 copies `setsid pre_exec` | duplicate | The block `unsafe { command.pre_exec(\|\| if libc::setsid() == -1 { ... })` is identical in `cmd/node_cmd.rs:533-540` and `node/admin.rs:1831-1838`. | Copy-paste during the daemon-mode extract. | ✅ done — `crate::util::setsid_on_spawn(&mut Command)`, both sites redirect-ed. |
| 469.11 | 2 copies of `fn build_runtime` | duplicate | Tokio multi-thread runtime builder in `cmd/node_cmd.rs:454` + `cmd/sessions_cmd.rs:104`. Slightly different signatures (one takes `GlobalConfig`). | Independent copy-paste. | ✅ done (partial) — `cmd::util::build_runtime()` for vanilla multi-thread cases (8 inline copies in `debug.rs`/`debug_transport.rs` replaced). `node_cmd::build_runtime(&GlobalConfig)` (runtime-flavor driven) and `sessions_cmd::build_runtime()` (current-thread) were kept — they have different shapes, not duplicates. |
| 469.12 | full codebase | scope | Did not find: unbounded allocations without caps, lock-across-await in std::Mutex, unsafe without a SAFETY comment, arithmetic overflow on peer-controlled data without saturating/checked. `Vec::with_capacity(peer_count)` is everywhere bounded by `MAX_*` before allocation. ✅ | — | ✅ — clean. |

### Priority / plan

All items closed:

1. ✅ **469.3** — token-file TOCTOU
2. ✅ **469.1 + 469.2 + 469.8** — atomic writes
3. ✅ **469.5** — `MailboxAckPayload::encode` → `Result`
4. ✅ **469.4** — runtime/debug lock-unwrap → poison-tolerant
5. ✅ **469.6 + 469.7** — DRY proto helpers + leading_zero_bits
6. ✅ **469.9 + 469.10 + 469.11** — cmd helpers + setsid + build_runtime

## Epic 470. Fragmented-topology routing storm (2026-04-23)

**Scope:** Operator run 5-node stend (ring 1↔2↔3↔4↔5↔1), alternately flapped node2 and node4, eventually `chat_client.py node3→node1` started returning `IPC_SEND_ERR_NO_ROUTE` even though node3 had either node2 or node4 available at any given moment.

**Observed symptoms (stend logs):**
- node1 receives `recursive.query target=self` bursts ~every 25 ms from node5 with same `reply_to=node3` — **query amplification**.
- node5 relays responses `via=dht hop=node4 reply_to=node3` while node4 is DOWN — **`send_to` returns true to a dead session** (frame accepted into mpsc buffer, TCP runner task exits silently, frame lost).
- node3 sees `recursive.response.orphan query_id=... — no pending nor reverse_path` — **500 ms `pending_recursive` entry expired** before responses completed their bouncy trip back.
- Session backpressure spam (`session.backpressure_received`) across all of node3's direct peers under the storm.

**Outcome:** 470.1 (greedy-by-XOR forwarding) validated on stend — traffic flows normally after fix.  470.2 and 470.3 closed as `⏸ deferred` with explicit follow-up triggers: the symptoms do not reproduce after 470.1, and a preemptive addition would mask measurement rather than fix the baseline.

### Findings

| # | Location | Category | Finding | Fix |
|---|-------|-----------|---------|------|
| 470.1 | [ipc/server.rs:1220-1225](veilcore/src/node/ipc/server.rs#L1220) `try_lookup_or_discover` | bug (routing) | The comment says "send to the 2 closest peers (greedy start)" but the code does `guard.peer_ids().iter().take(2)` — it takes the first 2 peers in **HashMap iteration order** (pseudo-random). Under fragmented topology this often sends the query in the opposite direction from `dst`, wasting the first discovery round + doubling the load on random peers. | ✅ done — peers are sorted by XOR distance to `dst` before `take(2)`. Now genuinely greedy.  **Operator-verified** on a 5-node fragmented stend: chat_client node3→node1 passes stably after flapping node2/node4. |
| 470.2 | [session/tx_registry.rs:147](veilcore/src/node/session/tx_registry.rs#L147) `send_to` | bug (observability) | `send_to` returns `true` if the mpsc channel accepted the frame, even if the real TCP session is dead — the runner task reads the frame, tries to write, hits an I/O error, exits and unregisters. A window of milliseconds–seconds during which the frame "went nowhere". | ⏸ deferred — after 470.1 the real window does not show up in the logs (≤ a few ms for loopback TCP, ~keepalive-interval for remote).  **We return when:** the metrics show a persistent divergence between `recursive.response.relayed` on the sender vs receipt on the target node, OR an operator sees a `session.send_to=true but no response` pattern on a live network. Then — add a `last_successful_write_at` atomic + prefer-live peer ranking. Doing it **before** that is premature optimization (an extra atomic read on every send_to). |
| 470.3 | [ipc/server.rs:1230](veilcore/src/node/ipc/server.rs#L1230) discovery deadline | tuning | 500 ms deadline in `try_lookup_or_discover`: under storm conditions + a bouncy relay chain (3-4 hops through flapping peers) it is slow. Orphan-responses at the initiator are a symptom of entries expiring before the answer arrived. | ⏸ deferred — with 470.1 forwarding is correct; 500 ms = ~500× loopback RTT, plenty of headroom.  **We return when:** the `route.discovery.miss` metric is persistently > 5% on a real (non-loopback) network OR complaints appear about intermittent NO_ROUTE for scenarios with 4+ hops.  Then — move it into `RoutingConfig.discovery_deadline_ms` (config-knob), do not hardcode a bump. Bumping to 1500ms without measurement is symptom-masking, it increases latency on legitimate failures. |

**Bottom line:** 470.1 closed the root cause, 470.2/3 remain as "watchdog entries" with clear metric triggers.

---

## Epic 471. Code-audit round 6 — security hardening + dedup (2026-04-24)

**Scope:** Combined audit by 4 parallel agents (zombie / panic-perf / security / architecture). Rounds 448/449/466/469 are closed — here come the remaining findings, prioritized by cost/benefit. Network is pre-deployment, breaking changes OK.

### Findings table

| # | Location | Category | Finding | Fix | Status |
|---|-------|-----------|---------|------|--------|
| 471.1 | [proto/transport_hints.rs:67-69](veilcore/src/proto/transport_hints.rs#L67) `decode` | dos-alloc | `Vec::with_capacity(buf[0] as usize)` — count is read from 1 byte (up to 255), but the cap is not checked against `MAX_ENTRIES=16` before allocation. One malformed IPC frame allocates up to 255× `TransportHintEntry`. | Reject `count > MAX_ENTRIES` with `ProtoError::ValueTooLarge` before allocation. | ✅ done — `count > MAX_ENTRIES` → `ProtoError::ValueTooLarge { field: "transport_hints.count", value, max }`. New test `rejects_count_above_max_entries`. |
| 471.2 | [node/abuse/per_peer_limiter.rs:113-119](veilcore/src/node/abuse/per_peer_limiter.rs#L113) | security (sybil) | Eviction policy = FIFO by seq insertion order (`byte_eviction_order.iter().next()`). A Sybil flood evicts legitimate long-running peers. | Evict by LRU `last_seen` among peers with the lowest recency, or a 2-tier hot/cold scheme. | ✅ done — `seq` is now reassigned on every `allow()`/`allow_bytes()` touch (not only on insert). The BTreeMap `eviction_order` is ordered by recency, `iter().next()` = least-recently-touched. 2 new regression tests: `lru_eviction_keeps_active_peer_alive_under_sybil_flood`, `lru_evicts_silent_peer_first`. |
| 471.3 | [node/dht/kademlia.rs:265-314](veilcore/src/node/dht/kademlia.rs#L265) `handle_find_node` | security (eclipse) | The returned `Contact { node_id, transport }` is peer-controlled. The asking node adds it to the routing table without proof-of-key. Classic Kademlia eclipse. | Per-contact handshake before promotion from "candidate" to "active routing entry". Two-tier: candidate_contacts vs verified_contacts. | ✅ done (2026-04-24) — `KademliaInner` got a separate `pending_contacts: HashMap<[u8;32], Contact>` + FIFO `pending_order` (cap=1024). API: `add_contact_unverified` (for FIND_NODE response body + NeighborOffer bodies) + `promote_contact_if_pending(node_id)` (called from both handshake-complete paths — a successful OVL1 KeyAgreement sig-verify IS the proof). `find_closest` only sees verified; unverified ones sit in the pending pool until natural traffic promotes them. There is no separate handshake-probe task (double handshake load without a clear benefit). 4 unit tests + 70 DHT tests green. Call sites for `add_contact_unverified`: [outbound_connector.rs:317](veilcore/src/node/outbound_connector.rs#L317) (FIND_NODE response), [dispatcher/routing.rs:159](veilcore/src/node/dispatcher/routing.rs#L159) (NeighborOffer). |
| 471.4 | [node/dispatcher/routing.rs:1741-1748](veilcore/src/node/dispatcher/routing.rs#L1741) recursive forward | dos-amplification | After 470.1 forwarding is greedy, but there is no global cap on "in-flight recursive queries". Bursts → CPU/lock contention. | `AtomicUsize in_flight_recursive` + `MAX_IN_FLIGHT_RECURSIVE` (e.g. 1024); reject above. | ✅ done — reused the existing per-source `dht_quota.allow(peer_id)` (the same limiter used for RouteRequest forward on line 478). Any peer sending recursive queries faster than `MAX_DHT_OPS_PER_PEER_PER_WINDOW` cannot amplify. WARN `recursive.forward.rate_limited` on reject. |
| 471.5 | [proto/pex.rs:300](veilcore/src/proto/pex.rs#L300) `compute_pex_challenge_difficulty` | non-issue | Re-check: the formula `clamp(MIN + (node_difficulty - src_difficulty - 1), MIN, node_difficulty - 1)`. A high `src_difficulty` → a LOW challenge (the peer already invested PoW). The maximum challenge is ALWAYS `node_difficulty - 1` — a local cap, not peer-controlled. `compute_origin_difficulty` hashes the real pubkey+nonce — the peer cannot lie about PoW. | ⊘ no-op — false positive in the audit. |
| 471.6 | [node/dispatcher/routing.rs:218-237](veilcore/src/node/dispatcher/routing.rs#L218) `route_origin_seq` | security (replay) | LRU cap=1024; after an old origin is evicted, a Sybil can replay an old Announce with a lower seq. | Persist a seq snapshot or extend the cap to `2× MAX_PEERS`. | ⏸ deferred — will close after 471.3 (eclipse fix) if node_id is valid |
| 471.7 | [proto/routing.rs:497-503](veilcore/src/proto/routing.rs#L497) | non-issue | Re-check: `RouteResponsePayload::decode` already rejects `label_count > MAX_TARGET_LABELS=8` before `Vec::with_capacity`. Labels are fixed-width 4 bytes — max 32 bytes per entry, not a DoS vector. | ⊘ no-op — false positive in the audit. |
| 471.8 | [node/dispatcher/routing.rs:1806](veilcore/src/node/dispatcher/routing.rs#L1806) | security (forge) | `REQUIRED_RESPONDER_PREFIX_BITS = 8` — 256 attempts to forge a response. On a 32-byte node_id. | Raise to 16 (budget for the target prefix-check ~ms). | ✅ done — bumped 8→16. Now forging a response requires ~65,536 attempts of grinding the pubkey (on top of per-identity PoW=24, total ~2^40 hash work). 16 bits is still loose enough that K=20 closest peers naturally satisfy it. |
| 471.9 | [node/ipc/server.rs:1206-1219](veilcore/src/node/ipc/server.rs#L1206) `pending_recursive` insert | non-issue | Re-check: `m = map.lock()` is held across the entire `retain → len → insert` sequence under a single guard. Not a race. | ⊘ no-op — false positive in the audit. |
| 471.10 | [proto/cursor.rs](veilcore/src/proto/cursor.rs) `read_array` | zombie | Const-generic helper `#[allow(dead_code)]`, no callers. | Remove. | ✅ done — the function and its unit test were removed. (The existing `super::read_array` 2-arg offset-style variant in `proto/mod.rs` stays — it has 8 callers in `diag.rs`.) |
| 471.11 | [cfg/model.rs](veilcore/src/cfg/model.rs) `is_default()` methods | non-issue | Re-check: they are used in `#[serde(skip_serializing_if = "X::is_default")]` — serde does not appear as a call site via grep, but they are active. | ⊘ no-op — false positive in the audit. |
| 471.12 | [node/runtime/persist_tasks.rs](veilcore/src/node/runtime/persist_tasks.rs) | duplicate | 8× identical `flush_*_snapshot_sync` methods with an identical save/load JSON pattern. | Generic `flush_json_snapshot_sync<T: Serialize>`. | ✅ done — free fn `flush_json_snapshot_sync<T: Serialize + ?Sized>` in persist_tasks.rs. 8 wrappers collapsed from ~18 LoC to 3 LoC each. **Bonus security fix:** the old pattern was `fs::write + rename` without fsync — a crash between the page cache flush and the rename could surface a zero-byte file. The new helper uses `util::atomic_write` (tmp + fsync + rename + EACCES retry) from Epic 469.8. ~120 LoC reduction. |
| 471.13 | persist_tasks 8 spawn sites | duplicate | The `interval+shutdown_rx select-loop` is repeated in 8 spawn_*_persist_task. | Helper `spawn_persist_loop(tasks, shutdown_rx, interval, build, flush)`. | ✅ done — free fn `spawn_persist_loop<S, B, F>` takes 2 closures: `build_snapshot: FnMut() -> S` (called on tick + on shutdown) and `flush: Fn(S)` (`Arc`-shared, called in spawn_blocking). 8 spawn methods collapsed from ~32 LoC to ~13 LoC each. ~150 LoC reduction. (6 other spawn sites in dht_republish/sovereign_republish/routing_health/miss_handler/mesh/mailbox_cleanup were kept, they have different shapes — not drop-in candidates.) |
| 471.14 | [node/identity/](veilcore/src/node/identity/) | duplicate | 13 different `Error` enums with pairwise `From` impls between them. | `enum IdentityLayerError` + `From<X>` for each sub-error. | ✅ done (2026-04-24) — [node/identity/error.rs](veilcore/src/node/identity/error.rs) with an umbrella `IdentityError` over all 13 sub-errors. Each variant is `#[error(transparent)]` — Display passes through. The sub-errors are NOT touched: internal identity functions keep returning their specific types, the umbrella is opt-in for cross-module helpers and external consumers. Re-export `identity::{IdentityError, IdentityResult}`. Zero breaking changes, zero Box/erasure. 2 unit tests. |
| 471.15 | Peer-state caches | duplicate | 3 different caches of the same entity (PeerMlKemCache / PeerVivaldiCache / PeerPubkeysCache) — different lock primitives, different organization. | `struct PeerMetadata { mlkem, vivaldi, pubkey }` + a single `Arc<RwLock<HashMap>>`. | ✅ done (2026-04-24, minimal-risk variant) — a full merge into a single lock was rejected (HIGH risk: Vivaldi per-probe updates contend with ML-KEM handshake-only writes). Instead — [node/peer_cache.rs](veilcore/src/node/peer_cache.rs): (1) re-export the three cache type aliases in one canonical place (previously scattered across mod.rs/dispatcher/e2e); (2) a `PeerMetadata` DTO + `snapshot_peer_metadata()` helper that holds the three locks in **documented canonical order** (pubkeys → mlkem → vivaldi) and returns a point-in-time view. Existing lock-order invariants are not broken; future cache-added-to-runtime consumers add their lock at the end of the chain, not in the middle. 2 unit tests. |
| 471.16 | TLS rustls trust store | known-issue | `RootCertStore::empty()` — no public CAs, hence UnknownIssuer for seed-node mesh when Let's Encrypt certificates differ. | Wire in `webpki-roots` under a cfg-flag, or per-bootstrap-peer SPKI pinning. | ✅ done (2026-04-24) — new Cargo feature `tls-webpki-roots` (optional dep webpki-roots = "0.26"). Config: `transport.tls_client.use_system_roots = bool` (default false) + `transport.tls_client.trusted_ca_file = <PEM>` (optional). Without the feature the `use_system_roots` flag is a no-op with a warn-log at startup (a single config file works in both hardened and mesh builds). The `TlsContext` private field `use_system_roots` is preserved through `with_trusted_certificates` / `with_server_identity` / `with_client_identity` — builder calls do not reset the roots the operator asked for. 2 unit tests. |
| 471.17 | algo selection (Ed25519 vs Falcon512) | security (downgrade) | The algo is chosen by the peer via 1 byte; there is no policy for "which algo we accept from which role". | Policy table: leaf-role accepts both, core-role requires Falcon, etc. | ✅ done (2026-04-24) — `[session] allowed_peer_algos: Vec<SignatureAlgorithm>` (default empty = accept any supported). The policy check runs at admit-time **AFTER** the handshake crypto — a rejected peer still paid the PoW + sig-verify cost, disincentivising algo-downgrade probing. Also a centralized mapping `SignatureAlgorithm::{wire_byte, from_wire_byte}`: an unknown byte is now an explicit handshake error instead of a silent fallback to Ed25519 (a hardening for malformed/malicious Identity frames). Legacy `algo=0` is accepted as Ed25519 for back-compat. 3 unit tests. |

### Priority / implementation plan

**Sprint 1 (quick fixes, low-risk, high-value):** ✅ all closed
- 471.1 ✅, 471.8 ✅, 471.10 ✅
- 471.5 / 471.7 / 471.9 / 471.11 ⊘ no-op (false positives after re-check)

**Sprint 2 (medium-risk):** ✅ all closed
- 471.2 ✅ LRU eviction in `per_peer_limiter` (+2 regression tests)
- 471.4 ✅ recursive forward through the existing `dht_quota`

**Sprint 3 (refactoring):** ✅ all closed
- 471.12 ✅ `flush_json_snapshot_sync` (~120 LoC + bonus fsync fix)
- 471.13 ✅ `spawn_persist_loop` (~150 LoC)

**Backlog (requires design / a separate epic):**
- 471.3 (eclipse), 471.14 (error hierarchy), 471.15 (peer-state cache), 471.16 (TLS trust), 471.17 (algo policy)

**Deferred with trigger:**
- 471.6 — `route_origin_seq` replay (will close after 471.3)

### Validation

- `cargo build -p veilcore` ✅
- `cargo clippy -p veilcore --all-targets -- -D warnings` ✅
- Touched modules unit-tests: `node::runtime` 42/42, `node::abuse` 89/89, `node::dispatcher` 94/94, `proto::` 539/539 ✅
- Full suite: 2074 pass, 119 fail = same WSL2 /tmp parallel-fs flake baseline (unchanged from pre-471 state)
- Net LoC: -270 (471.12+13) + ~80 added (security checks, tests)

## Epic 472 — Refactoring audit follow-up (2026-04-25)

An audit by 4 parallel Explore agents found ~150 notes; manual
verification filtered out ~40 false positives. Closed in a single pass:
- read_array integer overflow (proto/mod.rs:215)
- master_sk dead test field (mlkem_fanout.rs + resolver.rs)
- quic-session feature + 700 LoC dead module
- Epic 459 B5 dead Shutdown command + stale #[allow(dead_code)]
- sovereign-cache resumption revocation gate (security)

Here are the remaining open items in descending order of value/risk.

| ID | Where | Topic | Severity | Action | Status |
|----|-----|------|----------|--------|--------|
| **Tier 1 — security** (all validated — false positives) ||||| |
| 472.1 | proto/session.rs RekeyInit/Ack | **Forward secrecy on rekey is incomplete** | HIGH | — | ⊘ no-op — `kex::generate_ephemeral` already creates a fresh X25519 EphemeralSecret per call, `derive_rekey_keys` uses a fresh DH-shared. The salt mix `session_id XOR ids` is binding, not a replacement for the secret. FS is already correct. |
| 472.2 | node/pex/dispatcher.rs:300+ | PEX challenge replay + timing oracle on HMAC | MED | — | ⊘ no-op — `verify_pex_pow` uses the server-side `pending.challenge_nonce` (not client-supplied), the pending entry is consumed one-shot (line 233 `remove`). Replay is impossible. `verify_pex_pow` uses `leading_zero_bits` (integer compare); `verify_origin_sig` goes into constant-time crypto verify_message. No `==` on HMAC bytes. |
| 472.3 | proto/identity_proof.rs decode | `Vec::with_capacity(peer_byte)` without a cap-check | MED | — | ⊘ no-op — all length reads in identity_proof and other proto decoders ALREADY have `> MAX_*` checks before `read_bytes`/`with_capacity`. Targeted check of discovery.rs (5 sites), control.rs, delivery.rs — all capped via `MAX_FETCH_ENTRIES` / `MAX_REPLICAS` / `MAX_NAT_CANDIDATES` / etc. |
| 472.4 | proto/delivery.rs envelope + proto/anycast.rs | Recursion bounds in decoders | MED | — | ⊘ no-op — proto decoders are flat (`DeliveryEnvelope::decode` does not call DeliveryEnvelope::decode recursively) or strictly-bounded loops (`MailboxFetch` iteration capped at MAX_FETCH_ENTRIES). No self-recursion. Recipient::decode_fixed_from — fixed-size 49 bytes. AnycastList — bounded loop. |
| **Tier 2 — operability** ||||| |
| 472.5 | node/admin.rs handlers | Audit-log of admin commands (peer ban, identity update, route changes) | MED | append-only `<config-dir>/admin-audit.log` | ✅ done — new module [node/admin_audit.rs](veilcore/src/node/admin_audit.rs) (~200 LoC + 5 unit tests) with `AdminAuditLog::record(&AuditEvent)` (JSONL append + fsync, mode 0600 on Unix). 10 mutating admin commands (Stop/Restart/Reload/BanNode/UnbanNode/KillSession/DhtPut/DebugPeerConnect/DebugNodeAccept/SwapTransport) are audited via the `audit_event_for(&command, &result)` mapper in an end-of-dispatch hook. Read-only commands (Show/Health/Listens/etc) are skipped. The DhtPut value is NOT echoed verbatim — only `value_len=N` is recorded (operator-secret protection). 3 mapper unit tests. On a failure to write the audit log — a warn to the normal logger, the command is NOT blocked (denying a ban operation due to a full-disk audit-log is worse than a missing entry). |
| 472.6 | 154 tokio::spawn call sites | `tokio::spawn` helper with named tasks + panic recovery | MED | — | ⊘ skip — a large mechanical sweep just for naming in panic logs; tokio panic-handling is already correct (a panic does not bring down the runtime), and there is no observed problem with silent panics in prod. If one arises — we will add a catch_unwind wrapper surgically |
| 472.7 | dht::handle_find_value | Per-peer rate limit on FindValue | MED | — | ⊘ no-op — already done in Epic 454.1b (`dht_quota.allow(peer_id)` on line 238) |
| 472.8 | session/runner.rs rekey trigger | Long-lived session nonce exhaustion — auto-rekey at the 2^62 watermark | MED | trigger `RekeyInit` when counter >= NONCE_REKEY_WATERMARK | ✅ done — `SessionCipher::frames_processed()` accessor + a third rekey-trigger `nonce_pressure` in the runner. Watermark = 2^62 (defence-in-depth for `rekey_bytes_threshold = u64::MAX`). 2 unit tests on the counter accessor; 27 runner tests + 9 cipher tests green |
| **Tier 3 — refactor debt** ||||| |
| 472.9 | dispatcher/mod.rs:25 vs dht/kademlia.rs:45 | Duplicate `PeerVivaldiCache`/`PeerVivaldiMap` type aliases | LOW | One alias in peer_cache.rs, remove the second | ✅ done — kademlia.rs `PeerVivaldiMap` is now `type PeerVivaldiMap = crate::node::peer_cache::PeerVivaldiCache;` re-export. Under the old name so existing call sites compile without edits |
| 472.10 | identity/{publish,resolver}.rs | `PublishIoError`, `LookupError` newtypes without semantics | LOW | — | ⊘ no-op — these are a boundary abstraction for async traits (`IdentityPublisher::put`, `NameClaimResolver::fetch_name_claim`). Replacing them with `IdentityError` would create a layering inversion (the DHT backend would know identity errors). Kept |
| 472.11 | dispatcher/mod.rs:1300+ | Investigate `PowPendingTable` legacy challenge-flow remnants | LOW | — | ⊘ no-op — `PowPendingTable` is actively used in [routing.rs:455+690](veilcore/src/node/dispatcher/routing.rs#L455) for PoW challenge/response on RouteRequest. The agent was wrong, not legacy |
| **Tier 4 — rejected / skip-list** ||||| |
| 472.S1 | dispatcher widely | Lock-poison cleanup ~227 bare `.unwrap()` → project `lock!`/`rlock!` macros | — | ⊘ skip — a heavy mechanical refactor for uniformity; works correctly, not a blocker. Open if observed panics in production |
| 472.S2 | Instant::now() 269 sites | `TimeSource` trait for tests | — | ⊘ skip — a large refactor for time-based tests. Open if conscious need |
