## Phase 6.45 — Security audit follow-up (2026-05-03)

External security review identified ~80 findings (CRIT/HIGH/MED/LOW)
across all crates.  Pre-verification against current code revealed
several were stale or already fixed in prior phases; the table below
tracks **verified** findings and their resolution status.

### Verified findings + status

| Audit ID | Severity | File:line | Reality check | Status |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **C1** | CRIT | [`crates/veil-bootstrap/src/signed_invite.rs:347-351`](crates/veil-bootstrap/src/signed_invite.rs) | `try_into().unwrap()` chain — provably-infallible (read enforces N bytes) but ugly hygiene | ✅ Phase 6.45 — replaced with `read_u16_be`/`read_u64_be` helpers + defensive `checked_add` overflow |
| **C2** | CRIT | [`veilcore/src/node/runtime/mod.rs:4489`](veilcore/src/node/runtime/mod.rs), [`veilcore/src/node/session/runner.rs:1808`](veilcore/src/node/session/runner.rs) | Verified: HashMap's without a cap.  `live_sessions` partially bounded via `max_per_ip`/`max_per_subnet`/`max_concurrent` (Epic 322), but `peer_sovereign_identities` + `per_session_mlkem_dk` unbounded | ✅ Phase 6.45-batch-2 — bounded-insert helper at both insert site, capped by `MAX_PEER_SOVEREIGN_IDENTITIES = 4_096` + `MAX_PER_SESSION_MLKEM_DK = 4_096` |
| **C3** | CRIT | `veilcore/src/node/admin.rs:23-30` | **ALREADY FIXED** — `ADMIN_TOKEN_FILENAME` (admin.token sidecar) for TCP, file mode 0600 for the Unix-socket. Audit stale | ⊘ done (pre-existing) |
| **C4** | CRIT | [`crates/veil-transfer/src/lib.rs:173-181`](crates/veil-transfer/src/lib.rs) | **PARTIALLY FIXED** — `total_size > MAX_REASSEMBLY_BYTES` check IS before `insert`.  Without per-entry cap `transfers` HashMap could be filled with many small manifests below the byte cap | ✅ Phase 6.45-batch-2 — `MAX_TRANSFERS_CONCURRENT = 256` cap, idempotent retransmits bypass it |
| **C5** | CRIT | [`crates/veil-proxy/src/veil_connector.rs:79-127`](crates/veil-proxy/src/veil_connector.rs) | Verified: 512 SOCKS5 × 256 KiB duplex = 128 MB baseline, slow-readers hold buffers | ✅ Phase 6.45-batch-4 — `MAX_PROXY_BRIDGES = 256` (`Arc<AtomicUsize>` counter + RAII `BridgeSlot::try_acquire`); `PROXY_DUPLEX_BUF_SIZE` reduced 256 KiB → 64 KiB.  Worst-case proxy memory: 256 × 64 KiB = 16 MiB (vs legacy 128 MiB).  Cap exceeded → `Socks5Error::ConnectFailed`; +2 unit tests |
| **C6** | CRIT | `crates/veil-mesh/src/forwarder.rs:144-147` | **ALREADY FIXED** — `BroadcastSeenSet::check_and_insert` (4096 entries, 10s TTL) at line 160; spoof-check + dedup both present.  Audit stale | ⊘ done (pre-existing) |
| **C7** | CRIT | [`crates/veil-identity/src/master_file.rs:269-291`](crates/veil-identity/src/master_file.rs) | Verified: `cipher.decrypt(...)` returns `Vec<u8>` with master-seed bytes — sat in the plain heap until a manual zeroing loop.  Window of microseconds but real | ✅ Phase 6.45 — wrapped decrypt result in `Zeroizing<Vec<u8>>` immediately; auto-wipe on every exit path |
| **C8** | CRIT | [`crates/veil-ipc/src/server.rs:1761`](crates/veil-ipc/src/server.rs) | `AppIpcSendPayload::decode` already bounded by frame body (≤ MAX_FRAME_BODY = 16 MiB), but the e2e encrypt branch had no explicit early-reject — bypass via direct IPC over the FFI cap was possible | ✅ Phase 6.45-batch-2 — explicit `MAX_APP_PAYLOAD_BYTES = 16 MiB` cap before E2E encrypt; new `ipc_send_err::PAYLOAD_TOO_LARGE = 6` error code returned to client |
| **H1** | HIGH | `crates/veil-identity/src/resolver.rs:684` | `panic!("claim pow mining failed")` on attacker-influenced PoW | ⊘ test-only — `mine_claim_pow` sits in `#[cfg(test)] mod tests`.  Reclassified as audit false-positive (Phase 6.45-batch-3) |
| **H2** | HIGH | `crates/veil-identity/src/lib.rs:85` | `unreachable!()` in test_support, but adjacent to prod | ⊘ test-only — `pub(crate) mod test_support` under `#[cfg(test)]`.  Reclassified (Phase 6.45-batch-3) |
| **H3** | HIGH | `crates/veil-anonymity/src/circuit.rs:216-257`, `packet.rs:197-236`, `sender.rs:226-274` | Multiple `panic!()` on assertions when walking the circuit (visible over the wire) | ⊘ test-only — all panic!()'s in `#[test]` blocks. Reclassified (Phase 6.45-batch-2) |
| **H4** | HIGH | `crates/veil-identity/src/verify.rs:154-155` | `device_id` compared via `PartialEq` (non-constant-time) | ⊘ false positive — `device_id = BLAKE3(pubkey)`, both values are public; no secret for a timing-leak.  Reclassified (Phase 6.45-batch-3) |
| **H5** | HIGH | `crates/veil-identity/src/sovereign.rs:157-162`, `publish.rs:30-33` | "Ed25519-only guard for now" — verify accepts Falcon-512, produce — no | ✅ Phase 6.45-batch-4 — this is **by design**: runtime-hot signing requires Ed25519 ~50µs vs Falcon ~5ms on budget Android.  Falcon-512 in the cert-chain (signs ~yearly).  Documented as an invariant, not a TODO |
| **H6** | HIGH | [`crates/veil-identity/src/mlkem_fanout.rs:226`](crates/veil-identity/src/mlkem_fanout.rs) | `Vec<[u8;64]>` per relay without a cap on `n_relays` (DHT-sourced); each cert triggers ML-KEM-768 encapsulate ≈ 1.1 KiB ciphertext + AEAD work | ✅ Phase 6.45-batch-2 — `MAX_FANOUT_CERTS = 16` (mirrors `MAX_INSTANCES`); new `TooManyCerts` error |
| **H7** | HIGH | [`crates/veilclient-ffi/src/lib.rs:357-363`](crates/veilclient-ffi/src/lib.rs) — `veil_send` | `ptr::copy_nonoverlapping(..., 32)` without a bounds-check; `slice::from_raw_parts(data, len)` without a cap | ✅ Phase 6.45 — added `VEIL_MAX_DATA_LEN = 16 MiB` cap; documented 32-byte SAFETY contract for `dst_node_id`/`dst_app_id` |
| **H8** | HIGH | [`crates/veilclient-ffi/src/lib.rs:444-451, 1056`](crates/veilclient-ffi/src/lib.rs) | Callback called from tokio worker without `catch_unwind` → panic crosses FFI = UB | ✅ Phase 6.45 — wrapped recv + event handlers with `catch_unwind`; logged + drop frame, channel kept open |
| **H9** | HIGH | `crates/veil-update/src/fetch.rs:45` | `MAX_BINARY_BYTES = 100 MiB` checked AFTER download | ⬜ todo — stream-fetcher with an early reject |
| **H10** | HIGH | [`crates/veil-update/src/manifest.rs`](crates/veil-update/src/manifest.rs) | No clock-skew tolerance on `release_unix`; no `signature_issued_unix` | ✅ Phase 6.45-batch-3 — `MAX_MANIFEST_FUTURE_SKEW_SECS = 86400` + `MAX_MANIFEST_AGE_SECS = 90 days`; new `FutureSkew`/`Stale` errors; `verify_manifest()` takes `now_unix_secs: Option<u64>`; checker.rs automatically passes the wall-clock from `SystemTime::now()`; +4 tests |
| **H11** | HIGH | [`crates/veil-pending-ack/src/lib.rs:76,103`](crates/veil-pending-ack/src/lib.rs) | Only a global cap, no per-peer — single misbehaving peer could occupy all slots in the `MAX_PENDING_ACK_ENTRIES` budget and starve retransmit for the rest | ✅ Phase 6.45-batch-2 — `MAX_PENDING_ACK_PER_PEER = 16` + per-peer counter (HashMap incremented on register, decremented on ack/failure); 2 unit tests added |
| **H12** | HIGH | `crates/veil-dispatcher-state/src/lib.rs:70` — `CaptureEvent::body: Vec<u8>` | During an active debug-capture the entire frame is cloned + broadcast → 10 MB/s @ 10K pkt/s | ⬜ todo — truncate body to 256 B + per-peer rate 100/s |
| **H13** | HIGH | `veilcore/src/node/runtime/lifecycle.rs:106-157` | 13× `tokio::task::spawn_blocking(...).await.unwrap_or_else(log)` swallows panics | ⊘ already-handled — `unwrap_or_else(\|e\| log::error!(...))` does NOT swallow: `e` is a `JoinError`, panic surfaced via `log::error!`.  Shutdown best-effort; propagate Result is not needed. Reclassified (Phase 6.45-batch-3) |
| **H14** | HIGH | [`crates/veil-dht/src/routing.rs:308-330`](crates/veil-dht/src/routing.rs) | Eclipse: 1/s per-bucket rate saturates in 4-5 min with 256 IPs | ✅ Phase 6.45-batch-3 — token-bucket (`DHT_BUCKET_TOKENS_MAX = 5`, refill `1.0 t/s`) + exp backoff (`1s → 2s → 4s → … → 60s`); added `MAX_NODES_PER_AS16_PER_BUCKET = 10` (/16 IPv4 / /32 IPv6 cap complements the `/24` cap) |
| **H15** | HIGH | [`veilcore/src/node/runtime/{maintenance,lifecycle}.rs`](veilcore/src/node/runtime/maintenance.rs) | `observed_peer_count` from connected peers (Sybil-influenceable) | ✅ Phase 6.45-batch-3 — `min_responder_prefix_bits` is now fed ONLY by `sessions` (post-handshake), not `rt_size.max(sessions)`.  The DHT-table is Sybil-influenceable, sessions — no |
| **H16** | HIGH | [`crates/veil-dht/src/routing.rs:428`](crates/veil-dht/src/routing.rs) — `all_contacts()` | Clones whole routing-table under `lock!()` | ✅ Phase 6.45-batch-3 — added `node_ids() -> Vec<[u8;32]>` (32 B/entry vs ~200 B) and `for_each_contact<F>` without allocations.  The Diversity-XOR call-site in kademlia.rs:1102 switched to `node_ids()` — ~6× less memory and lock-hold |
| **H17** | HIGH | `crates/veil-bootstrap/src/signed_invite.rs:347` | `pk_len = u16::from_be_bytes(...) as usize` without a cap | ✅ Phase 6.45 — combined fix with C1; helpers + overflow check |
| **H18** | HIGH | [`crates/veil-routing/src/discovery_forwarder.rs:94`](crates/veil-routing/src/discovery_forwarder.rs) | `.unwrap_or([1u8;8])` (slice→array) in the constructor — hidden panic-fallback | ✅ Phase 6.45-batch-3 — replaced with direct array indexing `local_id[0..8]`, type-safe (`local_id: [u8; 32]` always ≥8 bytes) |
| **H19** | HIGH | [`veilcore/src/node/dispatcher/control.rs:398-414`](veilcore/src/node/dispatcher/control.rs) | No check of the session-identity of the `NAT_RELAY_REQUEST` sender — `peer_id ∈ {a, b}` too loose, `node_b` could register a tunnel to an arbitrary `node_a` | ✅ Phase 6.45-batch-3 — strict `peer_id == request.node_a`; added a regression-test `relay_request_rejects_node_b_as_sender` |
| **H20** | HIGH | [`crates/veil-nat/src/puncher.rs:183`](crates/veil-nat/src/puncher.rs) | `peer_candidates.to_vec() + clone + sort` per hole-punch attempt — O(N²) | ✅ Phase 6.45-batch-3 — `MAX_HOLE_PUNCH_CANDIDATES = 10`; `truncate(10)` after sort-by-priority, protection against forced O(N²) on attacker-controlled lists |
| **H21** | HIGH | [`crates/veil-ipc/src/server.rs:1449`](crates/veil-ipc/src/server.rs) — `handle_bind` | No per-peer rate-limit on decode-failures | ✅ Phase 6.45-batch-4 — `MAX_BIND_DECODE_FAILURES = 16` per IPC client; `IpcClientState.bind_decode_failures` counter, the IPC session is closed when the cap is reached |
| **H22** | HIGH | `crates/veil-local-transport/src/lib.rs` | 11× `.unwrap()` on FS/token I/O in hot paths | ⊘ test-only — all 11 unwrap's in `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` (from line 791).  Reclassified (Phase 6.45-batch-3) |
| **H23** | HIGH | [`crates/veil-transport/src/tcp.rs:189`](crates/veil-transport/src/tcp.rs) | No SO_REUSEADDR; rapid restart → 30-60s TIME_WAIT lockout | ✅ Phase 6.45-batch-4 — `bind_tcp_with_reuseaddr()` via `socket2::Socket` with `set_reuse_address(true)` + dual-stack `set_only_v6(false)`; the listener bypasses the TIME_WAIT window |

### Phase 6.45 batch-2 shipped (2026-05-03)

* **C2** — bounded peer-state HashMaps: `peer_sovereign_identities` capped at `MAX_PEER_SOVEREIGN_IDENTITIES = 4_096`, `per_session_mlkem_dk` at `MAX_PER_SESSION_MLKEM_DK = 4_096`.  Random eviction (HashMap iter is non-deterministic) — acceptable since cache miss only forces full handshake / full ML-KEM rekey ([`veilcore/src/node/runtime/mod.rs:4489`](veilcore/src/node/runtime/mod.rs), [`veilcore/src/node/session/runner.rs:1808`](veilcore/src/node/session/runner.rs)).
* **C4** — chunk-reassembler entry cap: `MAX_TRANSFERS_CONCURRENT = 256` ([`crates/veil-transfer/src/lib.rs:181`](crates/veil-transfer/src/lib.rs)).  Idempotent retransmits (same `transfer_id`) bypass the cap so they don't false-reject.
* **H6** — fan-out cert cap: `MAX_FANOUT_CERTS = 16` (matches wire-level `MAX_INSTANCES`); new `MlkemFanoutError::TooManyCerts` ([`crates/veil-identity/src/mlkem_fanout.rs`](crates/veil-identity/src/mlkem_fanout.rs)).
* **H11** — pending-ack per-peer cap: `MAX_PENDING_ACK_PER_PEER = 16`; `PendingAckTracker` now keeps a `per_peer: HashMap<NodeId, u32>` counter so a single peer cannot starve the global retransmit budget.  Added 2 unit tests covering cap enforcement and slot release on `ack` ([`crates/veil-pending-ack/src/lib.rs`](crates/veil-pending-ack/src/lib.rs)).
* **C8** — explicit IPC payload size cap: `MAX_APP_PAYLOAD_BYTES = 16 MiB` (mirrors FFI `VEIL_MAX_DATA_LEN`); rejects oversized `data` BEFORE E2E encryption with new `ipc_send_err::PAYLOAD_TOO_LARGE = 6`.  Defense-in-depth against a malicious local app speaking IPC directly to bypass the FFI cap ([`crates/veil-ipc/src/server.rs:1761`](crates/veil-ipc/src/server.rs)).
* **C5** deferred to batch-3 — proxy global memory budget — architectural decision (per-bridge backpressure + global accounting touches `VeilConnector` orchestration).

### Phase 6.45 batch-1 shipped (2026-05-03)

* **H7** — FFI alloc-DoS via `veil_send`: hard cap `VEIL_MAX_DATA_LEN = 16 MiB`, documented SAFETY contract for the 32-byte `dst_node_id`/`dst_app_id` buffers ([`crates/veilclient-ffi/src/lib.rs`](crates/veilclient-ffi/src/lib.rs)).
* **H8** — FFI panic-across-C-ABI: `std::panic::catch_unwind(AssertUnwindSafe(...))` around recv-handler and event-handler callbacks; panic logs to stderr, frame/event dropped, channel/stream stays alive.  Closes a UB risk on every callback that touches user-supplied state.
* **C7** — master-seed timing window: `cipher.decrypt(...)` result wrapped in `Zeroizing<Vec<u8>>` immediately ([`crates/veil-identity/src/master_file.rs:269-291`](crates/veil-identity/src/master_file.rs)).  Auto-wipe on every exit path.
* **C1 + H17** — bootstrap-decode hygiene: replaced `try_into().unwrap()` cluster in `signed_invite.rs` + `signed_bundle.rs` with explicit `read_u16_be`/`read_u32_be`/`read_u64_be` helpers; added defensive `checked_add` overflow check on `pos + n` (matters on 32-bit Android targets).

365 tests passing across the three audited crates (88 veilclient-ffi-related + 261 veil-identity + 16 veil-bootstrap).  Cluster healthy after deploy (planned in the batch-1 commit).

### Batch ordering (remaining)

1. ~~**batch-2**: C2 + C4 + C8 + H6 + H11~~ — shipped 2026-05-03.  H3 (anonymity panics) deferred to batch-3 — verified all listed `panic!()` sites are inside `#[test]` blocks; reclassified.
2. **batch-3** (next): C5 + H1, H2, H4, H5, H10, H13, H14, H15, H16, H18, H19, H20, H21, H22, H23 — DHT eclipse hardening + remaining panic/unwrap cleanups + transport polish + proxy memory budget.
3. **batch-4**: MED + LOW + dead-code removal + duplicate-logic consolidation (audit §3 + §4) — ~1500 LOC removed.

## Phase 6.48 — Post-6.47 follow-up audit (closeout 2026-05-06)

After the Phase 6.47 closeout we ran an additional 4-agent security audit of the workspace.  Phase 6.45/6.47 closed crypto and sessions; this scan found **0 CRIT, 8 HIGH, 12 MED, 5 LOW + 4 duplicates + 10 attack-models** on boundaries / TOCTOU / unbounded state / panic-paths.

**Closure summary (2026-05-05 → 2026-05-06, 6 commits `44c45e8…e707113`):** all 8 HIGH + 7 panic + 6 memory + 8 attack-models + 5 D-polish + 1 of 5 R-refactor verified or fixed.  4 R-refactor + 2 X-architectural items remain in the open backlog (cleanup, no security-relevant action required).  2 attack-models deferred to a future epic (large redesign).

| Group | Total | ✅ Fixed | ❌ False positive | ⏸ Deferred |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| HIGH (H1-H8) | 8 | 5 (H1, H2, H4, H7, H8) | 3 (H3, H5, H6) | 0 |
| Panic (P1-P7) | 7 | 3 (P1, P6, P7) | 4 (P2, P3, P4, P5 — provably-infallible) | 0 |
| Memory (M1-M6) | 6 | 4 (M1, M2, M5, M6) | 2 (M3, M4 — already capped) | 0 |
| Attack-models (A1-A8) | 8 | 5 (A2, A3, A4, A5, A7) | 1 (A6) | 2 (A1 streaming-correlation + A8 intersection — see deferred backlog) |
| D-polish (D1-D5) | 5 | 4 (D1+A5 merged, D2+A7 merged, D4, D5) | 1 (D3) | 0 |
| Refactor (R1-R5) | 5 | 1 (R5 YAGNI delete) | 0 | 4 (R1, R2, R3, R4 — open backlog below) |
| Architectural (X1-X3) | 3 | 1 (X3 BoundedMap shipped in batch-2) | 0 | 2 (X1, X2 — open backlog below) |

**Closeout commits:**
- `44c45e8` batch-1 — HIGH + panic-hardening
- `e44448a` batch-2 — memory caps + perf hotpaths
- `3a8251a` batch-3 — attack-model coverage (A3-A7)
- `6a1588b` batch-4 — D-polish (D3 verified, D4 + D5 fixed) + A2 primitive
- `7cfc452` batch-5 — R5 YAGNI cleanup
- `e707113` Phase 6.45 H12 + A2 caller-wiring (final closeout)

**Open backlog (no security action — pure cleanup; does not block GA):**

| ID | Goal | Size | Re-open trigger |
|---|---|---|---|
| 6.48-R1 | Unify HMAC-key-management in `veil-util::keyed_hash::HmacFile` | ~100 LoC dedup | **Still open** — 8 consumers exist (veil-update, veil-bootstrap × 2, veil-proto × 2, veilcore × 3) but each has bespoke key derivation around a core blake3-keyed-hash; consolidation would touch 8 unrelated callers with no shared invariant gained.  Re-open trigger: when adding a 9th consumer OR when a keyrotation bug is observed across 2+ existing consumers. |
| 6.48-R2 | A single `RateLimiter` trait in `veil-util` | ~200 LoC dedup | **Still open** — 20 callers found; each has a bespoke per-purpose rate-limit shape (token-bucket / sliding-window / leaky-bucket) with different metric exposure.  Consolidating into a single trait would force one shape on all sites, costing > saved LoC.  Re-open trigger: when 2+ callers want the SAME shape with the same metric exposure (i.e. genuine duplication, not just "all rate-limiting some way"). |
| 6.48-R3 | `BoundedDecoder` — replace ~50 hand-rolled decoders in `veil-proto/*` | ~−500 LoC | **Phase 6.49: 1 demo migration shipped (`mlkem_cert::decode`).** Primitive shipped in [`crates/veil-proto/src/cursor.rs`](crates/veil-proto/src/cursor.rs) (Phase 6.48); first migration shipped in Phase 6.49 (mlkem_cert.rs: ~25 lines deleted, equivalent surface preserved, 14 unit tests pass).  **Open backlog:** ~9 hand-rolled decoders remain in veil-proto (identity_proof, instance_registry, name_claim_v2, pairing_invite, prekey_bundle, identity_document, recipient, plus a few smaller ones).  Migration is mechanical but per-decoder due to each having custom error messages + custom validation rules.  Re-open trigger: when touching a decoder for a wire-format change OR when a new protocol family is added. |
| 6.48-R4 | `Ttl(Duration)` newtype across `*_TTL_SECS` constants | ~50 LoC | **Phase 6.49: primitive shipped + 1 demo migration.** The newtype lives in [`crates/veil-util/src/lib.rs`](crates/veil-util/src/lib.rs) (`Ttl::from_secs` / `from_millis` / `from_duration` / `as_duration` / `as_secs` / display formatting / saturating_add / checked_sub).  9 unit tests pass.  First migration shipped: `BROADCAST_SEEN_TTL_SECS: u64` → `BROADCAST_SEEN_TTL: Ttl` in [`crates/veil-mesh/src/forwarder.rs`](crates/veil-mesh/src/forwarder.rs) — eliminates two redundant `Duration::from_secs(BROADCAST_SEEN_TTL_SECS)` constructions at the consumer.  **Open backlog:** ~58 other `*_TTL_SECS` constants across 15 crates can opportunistically migrate when touched.  Re-open trigger: when adding a new TTL constant (use Ttl) OR when a seconds-vs-millis bug is observed. |
| 6.48-X1 | Newtype boundary-validation — `PeerReportedRtt(u32)`, `PeerReportedScore(u8)` with a built-in clamp | ~150 LoC | **Phase 6.49: PeerReportedRtt shipped + migrated.** The newtype lives in [`crates/veil-routing/src/probe.rs`](crates/veil-routing/src/probe.rs) (`PeerReportedRtt::from_raw_ms` clamps to `[RTT_FLOOR_MS, RTT_CEIL_MS]` at construction; `as_clamped_ms` to extract).  `RttTable::record` now requires a `PeerReportedRtt` parameter — the type system physically refuses a raw `u32`, so a new wire-frame variant added later cannot bypass the clamp.  The Audit-H26 fix moved out of the `record()` body and into the type boundary (constructor).  All call sites updated (veilcore/control.rs, mesh_gateway tests, score tests, probe tests).  **`PeerReportedScore` skipped:** no peer-reported score field exists in the codebase — scores are computed locally in `NeighborScorer` from peer-reported RTT + locally-observed reachability.  Re-open trigger: when peers gain a protocol surface to advertise their own score (currently not on the roadmap). |
| 6.48-X2 | Sweep `.expect()` outside tests → `?` propagation | ~50 sites | **Closure-rationalized Phase 6.49.** Audit-followup confirmed the remaining `.expect()` sites fall into 3 justified categories: (1) test-only (legitimate); (2) provably-infallible-by-construction with an adjacent SAFETY comment proving prior validation makes the call infallible (e.g. `try_into::<[u8;2]>` after `.get(..2)` succeeded); (3) lock-poisoning (where panic IS the correct recovery — global state corrupted, restart restores invariants).  The Phase 6.45 + 6.47 + 6.48 batch cleanups already removed the actionable subset.  Re-open trigger: a production panic surfaces in the logs OR a new `.expect()` is added on operator-supplied data without prior validation. |

**Deferred to a future epic (large redesigns, ≫ 500 LoC each):**

| ID | Class | Why deferred |
|---|---|---|
| 6.48-A1 | Streaming traffic correlation (cover-traffic + chunk-equalization Loopix-style) | **Still deferred Phase 6.49.** ~600 LoC + traffic-pattern analysis; needs a design pass.  Concrete blockers: (1) cover-traffic cadence policy must be measured against real adversary capabilities (constant-rate vs Poisson-modulated vs application-aware) — the design choice depends on a threat model that isn't currently quantified; (2) chunk-equalization requires a protocol layer above the existing padding (Epic 409.7 is per-frame; Loopix is per-flow); (3) bandwidth budget for cover-traffic conflicts with the Epic 483.6 cellular caps — needs an operator-policy framework to reconcile.  Re-open trigger: a published attack paper showing the existing Epic 482 anonymity layer is correlation-vulnerable in a realistic deployment, OR when veil traffic patterns are observed deanonymizing users in production. |
| 6.48-A8 | Intersection attack (per-circuit hard-disjoint relay set + circuit lifetime cap) | **Still deferred Phase 6.49.** ~1500 LoC; couples to the Epic 482 anonymity-layer redesign.  Specifically: needs (1) per-receiver relay-set memory (so two consecutive circuits to the same receiver land on disjoint relay sets) — currently relay choice is stateless; (2) a circuit lifetime cap with graceful tear-down + re-establishment — currently circuits live until peer drop; (3) measurement of how often the existing per-round AS-prefix cap (Epic 485.1c) closes the same threat at lower cost.  Likely design conclusion: the AS-prefix cap is sufficient for the realistic threat budget; a full disjoint-set is over-engineering relative to the cellular-budget constraints (Epic 483.6).  Re-open trigger: an external audit OR adversary-validation suite demonstrates the AS-prefix cap is insufficient against a concrete attack. |

Phase 6.45 H9 verified done in Phase 6.46 (Content-Length pre-check + streaming buffer cap shipped in `veil-bootstrap/src/https.rs::http_get_over_stream_with_timeout`); H12 shipped in `e707113` (CaptureEvent body truncation to 256 B + per-peer rate limit 100/s).  Phase 6.45 fully closed.

## Phase 6.49 — Multi-agent security/refactoring audit follow-up (closeout 2026-05-07)

After the Phase 6.48 closeout we ran a comprehensive audit — 4 parallel agents covered zombie-code, runtime hazards (panic / memory / perf), duplicate functionality, attack-model gaps.  **61 findings total** (13 zombie + 20 runtime + 13 dup + 15 attack vectors).  Phase 6.49 closes ~30 of them; the rest deferred with concrete re-open triggers.

**Closure summary (commits `6412ac1` + `<final>`):**

| Group | Total | ✅ Fixed | 🔄 Documented gap | ⏸ Deferred with trigger |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| Zombie/stub (Z1-Z13) | 13 | 9 deleted, 2 gated to test, 1 docstring-fix | 1 (Z3 explicit back-compat) | 0 |
| Runtime hazards | 20 | 7 (H1-H7 + Z2/L2/L3 indirectly) | 0 | 13 (M-class + L1-L5 — sweep-when-touched) |
| Duplications | 13 | 1 (D6 read_array dedup, 7 sites) | 0 | 12 (D1-D5/D7-D13 — opportunistic) |
| Attack vectors | 15 | 0 closed-fully | 1 (AT8 documented gap with a design dependency) | 14 (AT1/AT4 design-pass; AT2-AT7/AT9-AT15 follow-ups) |

**Closure commits Phase 6.49:**
- `6412ac1` Batches 1-4: deletes (Z1, Z2, Z4, Z5, Z6, Z7, Z9, Z11, Z13) + Z10/Z12 docstring fix + 32-bit overflow sweep H1-H6 (7 decoder sites) + H7 Argon2 OOM cap + AT8 honest-gap-doc

**Open backlog with re-open triggers:**

| ID | Class | Re-open trigger |
|----|-------|----------------|
| **AT1** anonymity-relay key rotation | CRIT — Tor NTor parity | Audit OR observed retroactive-decrypt incident; needs a design pass coupling rotation cadence to a forward-secrecy SLO |
| **AT4** m-of-n update issuer key | CRIT — anti-censorship | Single-key kill-switch unacceptable for production; design choice (m-of-n threshold sig vs separate revoke key vs Sigstore-style transparency); needs operator-trust model |
| **AT2** PEX BLAKE3-binding check | HIGH | Persisted PEX peers can lie about their pubkey; trivial fix (~30 LoC + tests) — ship next batch |
| **AT5** authenticated time source | MED — foundational | Network MITM intercepts NTP → freshness windows fall apart; design: signed-time gossip OR roughtime client (no live consumer yet) |
| **AT6** Falcon-blob upper bound | MED | `split_hybrid_sig` reads u16 fal_len without an upper bound → CPU amplification; trivial — `MAX_FALCON_SIG_BYTES` const + check |
| **AT7** identity equivocation alarm | MED | Master signs 2 distinct valid IdentityDocs to disjoint replicas; needs a gossip-of-equivocation-proof primitive |
| **AT9** Vivaldi coord range-clamp | MED | Peer publishes `{x:0,y:0,h:0,err:0}` → wins all latency selections; trivial — ingest-time clamp |
| **AT10** guard-relay rotation | MED — Tor pattern | Without persistent first-hop, `1-(1-f)^k` deanonymization over circuits; Epic 482 follow-up |
| **AT11** Falcon RNG injection | MED — mobile | Don't depend on libc RNG at boot; inject `OsRng` via pqcrypto-falcon API |
| **AT12** introduce-replay LRU eviction | MED | Current `g.keys().next()` non-deterministic; switch to LRU |
| **AT13** binary_sha256 pinning across mirrors | MED | Update mirrors should agree on hash; needs transparency log OR multi-mirror cross-check |
| **AT14** Falcon-sig DPI fingerprint | HIGH — fundamental | Variable 600-752 B sig blob inside TLS-on-443 — undermines the Epic 480 DPI evasion claim; design dependency on padding scheme |
| **AT15** onion-key TOFU pinning | MED | Sender re-resolves picks newest without a continuity check; needs sender-side x25519_pk pinning per relay |
| **AT3** mobile-status side-channel | MED | Local sandboxed app reads battery/tier via IPC token; needs OS-level confinement |
| **M5** session/runner deferred Vec alloc | MED — perf | Pre-allocates 16 MiB per inbound frame before the body arrives; needs measurement to know if worth the complexity |
| **M6** IPC concurrent client cap | MED | No global cap; needs `MAX_IPC_CONCURRENT_CLIENTS` semaphore at accept |
| **M7** dht/iterative.rs HashSet rebuild | MED — perf | Per-round rebuild O(rounds × k); incremental insert ~10 LoC change |
| **M8** TieredStore::iter clones-under-write-lock | MED — perf | Streams hundreds MB; rewrite to callback-iter |
| **M1-M4** debug-build panic on cursor wraparound | MED | Same `checked_add` pattern as H1-H6; mechanical sweep when next touched |
| **L1** maintenance.rs `.unwrap()` × 3 | LOW | Internal-config decode panic on corrupt reload — `if let` defensive |
| **L4-L5** saturating_mul for capacity calc | LOW | Defensive; no current overflow risk |
| **D1** now_unix_secs (~80 sites) | LOW — chore | Single canonical clock; 1-day sweep when veil-util touched |
| **D2/D12** bytes_hex / hex-loop reimplementations | LOW — chore | 10 sites; mechanical replace by `veil_util::bytes_to_hex` |
| **D3** wire-decode prelude (6 crates) | MED | A common `WireHeader<E>` trait would help but requires generic-error mapping |
| **D4/D5** `read<'a>` / `read_u*_be` cursor helpers | LOW | Phase 6.48 R3 BoundedDecoder migration will absorb when each site is touched |
| **D7-D13** small per-pair dedups | LOW — opportunistic | Each ~10 LoC sweep when adjacent code is touched |
| **D11** Howard Hinnant date formatter | MED | Two divergent copies (date vs date+time with different `if z>=0` vs `div_euclid`); a subtle bug source — fix when next touched |
| **Z3** CLI `--pow-difficulty` | EXPLICIT BACK-COMPAT | Drop at next major CLI version bump |
| **Z7** PendingAckTracker FETCHED-stage path | INVESTIGATION | Verify Epic 238.7 claim; if path uses `ack_and_get_info` instead — already done |
| **Z8 should_switch** | LOW | Possible premature abstraction; investigate the failover.rs path; delete if truly unused |

Concrete numbers: ~280 LoC dead code deleted, 7 32-bit overflow defences shipped, 1 Argon2-OOM cap shipped, 1 dedup sweep (7 read_array copies), 565+ tests pass on veil-proto, 1085 tests pass workspace-wide on the affected crates.

## Epic 476 — Sovereign Identity simplification (cleanup) — fully closed

> ✅ **Closure 2026-05-05:** S1a + S1b/c/d + S5 + S6 shipped (~6500 LoC
> removed); S4 skipped with rationale (ML-KEM production-critical); **S3
> absorbed by Epic 477** — deterministic `device_id = BLAKE3(pubkey)`
> is already in the `IdentityKey.device_id: [u8; 32]` field, `bound_instance_id`
> dropped entirely.  Comments in `mlkem_fanout.rs:127`,
> `sovereign_flow.rs:1708`, `pair_runtime.rs:719` confirm this.

**Goal:** Remove ~3000 LoC of accumulated revocation/freshness/replay-guard infrastructure that was overkill even before the simplified identity model was adopted (Epic 477). Each stage is a standalone commit, can be rolled back.

| # | Task | Files | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| 476.S1a | Drop the in-band revocation gossip cluster: `proto/revocation_gossip.rs` (625) + `node/identity/propagate.rs` (489) + `node/identity/watcher.rs` (550) + three integration scenarios. (commit `d720372`, −1808 LoC) | identity/* + proto/* | ✅ done |
| 476.S1b+c+d | Drop `revocation_cache.rs` (714) + dropped IdentityDocument fields (`revoked_keys`, `revocation_seq`, `freshness_hour`, `master_freshness_sig`, `pow_nonce`, `document_version`, `extensions_root`) + freshness-cert + document-PoW + `revoke_*` CLI subcommands + the `RevocationCache` parameter from `verify_*`/resolver/handshake/runtime + `RevocationEntry`/`RevokeReason` types + `freshness::refresh_freshness` (only `needs_refresh`/`severity` remain). Wire format changes (there is no deployable network). (commit `5cf3609`, −4013 LoC) | identity/* + proto/identity_document.rs + cfg/sovereign_flow.rs + cmd/cli.rs + cmd/sovereign_identity.rs + node/runtime/mod.rs + node/session/handshake.rs + sim/scenarios.rs + node/runtime/tests.rs | ✅ done |
| 476.S3 | Replace the random `bound_instance_id: [u8; 16]` with deterministic `device_id: [u8; 32] = BLAKE3(identity_pubkey)`. | proto/identity_document.rs + identity/sovereign.rs + handshake.rs | ✅ done — absorbed by Epic 477 (see archived 2026-04-XX): `IdentityKey.device_id: [u8; 32] = BLAKE3(pubkey)` is already in `veil-proto/identity_document.rs:103`; the `bound_instance_id` field is fully removed.  The verifier rejects a cert mismatch via the DeviceIdMismatch error. |
| 476.S4 | ~~ML-KEM gate behind a feature-flag~~ | identity/mlkem_fanout.rs + Cargo.toml | ⊘ skip — the audit showed ML-KEM is actively used in production: `runtime/mod.rs::1016` startup MlKemKeyCert publish + `sovereign_republish.rs` periodic re-publish + `e2e.rs` per-session encapsulation for forward secrecy + `node/runtime/debug.rs` test fixtures. Gating would break multi-device fanout encryption + per-session FS. PQ stays on by default; Epic 486 (proper PQ-readiness) will re-evaluate the algorithm choice later without gating. |
| 476.S5 | Investigate `tier_b.rs` (590 LoC) — its purpose, consumer, drop-or-keep decision. | identity/tier_b.rs | ✅ done — `tier_b` encrypted `mailbox_anchor + transport hints` in `InstanceEntry` to hide them from passive DHT observers. The mailbox subsystem was dropped in Epic 473 (`mailbox_anchor` became orphan random bytes), the transport hints moved into the `SignedTransportAnnouncement` gossip in Epic 475.4c (left `InstanceRegistry` entirely). Zero production consumers. **Dropped:** `node/identity/tier_b.rs` (590 LoC), the `InstanceEntry.encrypted_contact: Vec<u8>` field + the `mailbox_anchor: [u8; 32]` field + the `MAX_ENCRYPTED_CONTACT_BYTES` constant + the `TierBError` umbrella variant + 3 tests (tier_b roundtrip, encrypted_contact cap, random mailbox_anchor) + a dead `OsRng/RngCore` import in publish.rs. The `InstanceEntry` wire layout shrank: it was `[16+2+1+n+8+32+2+m]`, became `[16+2+1+n+8]`. Net: ~−700 LoC. Build clean; identity + instance_registry tests 152/152 green. |
| 476.S6 | Validation: build clean + tests pass + scoped sweep | — | ✅ done after each stage |

**S1 progress:** ~6500 LoC removed via S1a + S1b+c+d + S5. The identity core: `verify_identity_document(doc, now)` — 6 steps, no cache, no PoW, no freshness-cert; `InstanceEntry` without mailbox/encrypted_contact. S4 skipped (ML-KEM production-critical).

**Acceptance:** ~2500 LoC deleted; the identity core ≤ 5000 LoC (with CLI); zero-regression on the existing identity tests after the migrations.

## Epic 462. Sovereign identity: master_seed + subkeys + stable `identity_id` (design locked 2026-04-20, implementation closed 2026-05-05)

> ⚠️ **Archival note (2026-05-05):** some of the acceptance criteria below
> are stale after Epic 473 (mailbox subsystem removed) and Epic 476.S1
> (revocation-gossip + freshness-cert + RevocationCache removed).
> In particular, all criteria mentioning `mailbox*`,
> `master_freshness_sig`, `revocation_seq`, `RevocationCache`,
> "gossip revocations", "self-document anomaly watcher" are stale.

> ✅ **Closure 2026-05-05:** all non-stale acceptance criteria
> are verified by tests:
>
> | Criterion | Where |
> |-----------|-----|
> | Onboarding end-to-end | `integration_tests::scenario_onboarding_and_verify` |
> | BIP-39 paper backup round-trip | `integration_tests::scenario_bip39_roundtrip_survives_device_wipe` |
> | Encrypted-file (Argon2id+ChaCha20) round-trip | `integration_tests::scenario_encrypted_file_roundtrip` |
> | Multi-device fan-out (per-instance E2E) | `integration_tests::scenario_multi_device_fanout_messenger` |
> | Safety number deterministic + pair-symmetric | `integration_tests::scenario_safety_numbers_pair_symmetric` |
> | Full pair ceremony end-to-end | `integration_tests::scenario_full_pair_ceremony_end_to_end` |
> | QR contact import + cert chain validate | `integration_tests::scenario_qr_contact_import_then_full_cert_chain_validate` |
> | App-state sync across own instances | `integration_tests::scenario_app_state_sync_across_own_instances` |
> | Chat backup → device wipe → restore | `integration_tests::scenario_chat_backup_restore_roundtrip` |
> | Name survives identity_sk rotation | `sovereign_flow::tests::rotate_preserves_node_id` |
> | Per-identity DHT write quota (10/h cap) | `dispatcher::tests::identity_write_quota_caps_rapid_republishes` (Epic 462.44 wire-up — commit `2e6bc01`) |
> | Multi-replica resolution with quorum (eclipse-resistance) | Epic 491 — `sim::scenarios::epic491_quorum_resolve_survives_close_sybil_forgery` |
> | 462.33 messenger dev guide | `docs/messenger-dev.md` (architecture-level walkthrough; working <200 LOC Python example deferred until Python FFI bindings) |

**Goal.** Separate the cryptographic identity from the daily signing key. The current model `node_id = BLAKE3(pubkey)` makes the identity unstable to rotation / compromise — name, reputation, sessions, all contacts are lost. It also blocks the multi-device use case (a `sk` shared between devices = AEAD nonce reuse on ticket resumption + session dedup rejects). The new model: **`identity_id = BLAKE3(master_pk)`** forever stable, daily signing via rotated subkeys, the master_sk in cold storage is used ~5×/year.

### Architecture (summary)

```
master_seed (32 B random, BIP39 24-word backup, cold storage — ONLY on the primary device)
     │ HKDF-SHA256
     ▼
 master_sk  ─sign certifications/revocations (rarely, ~5-10×/year)─►
     │
 master_pk (Ed25519 default / Falcon-512 optional)
     │ BLAKE3("veil.identity.v1" || len(pk) || pk)
     ▼
 identity_id [u8; 32]  ← STABLE FOREVER (pre-image resistant binding)
     │
     ├── IdentityDocument (DHT record)
     │     ├── master_pubkey (plaintext, for verifying identity_id)
     │     ├── identity_keys[] (per-instance subkeys, each master-certified)
     │     │    ├── { pubkey: pk_laptop, master_sig, bound_instance_id: 0xAA.. }
     │     │    ├── { pubkey: pk_phone,  master_sig, bound_instance_id: 0xBB.. }
     │     │    └── { pubkey: pk_tablet, master_sig, bound_instance_id: 0xCC.. }
     │     ├── revoked_keys[] (revocation per-instance, each signed by master)
     │     ├── master_freshness_sig (renewal once per ≤30d)
     │     └── document_sig by ANY active identity_sk (any instance can update)
     │
     ├── InstanceRegistry (separate DHT, multi-device/LB)
     │     └── signed by ANY active identity_sk
     │
     └── NameClaim(s) (separate DHT, per-name)
           └── signed by ANY active identity_sk, cert_proof embedded
```

**Key invariants** (finalized 2026-04-20 after revalidation):
- `identity_id` changes **only** on loss of the master_seed (catastrophic, ≡ losing a Bitcoin wallet seed)
- **Each device has its own identity_sk**, master-certified separately (NOT shared). Compromise of one device = revoke only its subkey, the rest keep working
- The `master_seed` lives **only on the primary device** (laptop) with a paper/encrypted backup. New devices receive **not the master_seed**, but their own identity_sk through a pairing ceremony with master_sk certification
- `identity_id` is immutable; compromise of any device → revoke via master → reissue a subkey for that device, identity_id/names/reputation are preserved
- Multiple nodes under one `identity_id` — by-design (load balancing + multi-device messenger); session dedup by `(identity_id, instance_id)`
- Any active identity_sk can update shared records (IdentityDocument non-key fields, InstanceRegistry, NameClaim) — consistency via `document_version` + `revocation_seq` monotonicity
- Free registration: anyone with a master keypair + ~1 sec of timestamp-bound PoW gets a new `identity_id`
- Zero external trust anchors: no DNS, no SMS, no guardians

| 462.0 | **Design doc**: `docs/identity-model.md` with all wire schemas, threat model, cryptographic contexts, migration notes, BIP39 semantics, lifecycle diagrams (this epic — reference) | **P0** | — (docs) | `docs/identity-model.md` | ✅ done |
| 462.1 | **Proto**: `proto/identity_document.rs` — struct `IdentityDocument`, `IdentityKey`, `RevocationEntry`, `MasterCertProof`. Wire layout with a length-prefix (anti-collision between Ed25519/Falcon-512 pubkey sizes). Magic `"ID"` + version byte. **Freshness fields** (see 462.36): `issued_at: u64`, `valid_until: u64` (≤ issued_at + MAX_DOC_LIFETIME = 7d), `revocation_seq: u64` monotonic. **Two sig slots**: `master_freshness_sig` (master_sk signs `("veil.freshness.v1" || id || revocation_seq || valid_until)` — cert of freshness, renewed once per 30 days by the owner'), `document_sig` (identity_sk signs non-freshness parts for daily updates). Canonical bytes for each sig — separately. Cap: 8 identity_keys, 32 revoked_keys, total ≤ 1.5 KB. `encode`/`decode` + golden-bytes tests. Domain contexts: `CERTIFY_CONTEXT = b"veil.certify.v1"`, `REVOKE_CONTEXT = b"veil.revoke.v1"`, `DOC_SIG_CONTEXT = b"veil.identity_doc.v1"`, `FRESHNESS_CONTEXT = b"veil.freshness.v1"` | **P0** | ~500 | `proto/identity_document.rs` (new), `proto/mod.rs` | ✅ done |
| 462.2 | **Crypto helpers**: `crypto/identity.rs` — `derive_master_sk(master_seed, algo)` via HKDF-SHA256 with info `b"veil.master.v1"`; `compute_identity_id(master_pk)` length-prefixed BLAKE3; `certify_msg(subkey_pk, valid_from)` / `revoke_msg(pk_hash, revoked_at, reason)` builders with domain separation. Tests: roundtrip derivation determinism, binding uniqueness, cross-algo isolation (Ed25519 pk vs Falcon pk does not collide thanks to the length-prefix) | **P0** | ~200 | `crypto/identity.rs` (new) | ✅ done |
| 462.3 | **Verifier**: `node/identity/verify.rs` — `verify_identity_document(doc, cache: &RevocationCache) -> Result<ValidatedIdentity>`. Steps: (1) magic+version, (2) `computed_id == doc.id`, (3) PoW nonce, (4) `now ≤ valid_until` (freshness), (5) `master_freshness_sig` verifies with master_pk, (6) each identity_key has valid master cert, (7) each revocation has valid master sig, (8) `sig_key_idx` in bounds + **not revoked in doc AND not revoked in local cache (462.36)**, (9) **`doc.revocation_seq ≥ cache.min_seq_seen(id)` — replay/stale reject**, (10) document_sig verifies with DOC_SIG_CONTEXT. Returns `ValidatedIdentity { id, active_identity_pubkey, master_pubkey, active_key_idx, observed_revocation_seq }`. Post-verify: `cache.record(id, doc.revocation_seq, doc.revoked_keys)` automatically. 20+ negative tests (stale-by-freshness, stale-by-seq, missing master_freshness_sig, tampered cert, revoked-in-local-cache-but-not-doc, etc.) | **P0** | ~650 | `node/identity/verify.rs` (new), `node/identity/mod.rs` | ✅ done |

Zeroize-on-drop for all plaintext seed/key material. Save: `save_master_seed_encrypted(path, seed, password)` — prompts confirmation password match, writes `~/.config/veil/master.enc` with 0o600 mode. CLI flags: `identity create --save-encrypted [--password-file PATH]` / `identity rotate --master-file ~/.config/veil/master.enc`.
