All 1973 lib tests pass (single-thread sweep); clippy clean. DHT publish/fetch loop + online/offline coalescing + scheduled republish land with the runtime wiring (library is pure DI — runtime plugs any DHT backend into the trait). **Runtime DHT publisher + startup one-shot shipped**: `node/identity/publisher_dht.rs::DhtBackedPublisher` is the thin `IdentityPublisher` adapter wrapping `Arc<KademliaService>` — `put(dht_key, value)` → `KademliaService::store_local` (full distributed put to K closest replicas is a future extension; for MVP the local shard is authoritative and peer lookups walk to the originator via Kademlia traversal). `NodeRuntime::start()` auto-publishes the loaded `IdentityDocument` at startup immediately after the session registry is created — peers walking the DHT keyspace can now retrieve the signed document without the legacy node_id path. 2 adapter tests + end-to-end library round-trip test through `publish_identity_document`. All 2110 lib tests pass single-threaded; clippy clean. **Scheduled republish loop shipped**: `spawn_sovereign_identity_republish_task` fires every 6 hours (matching the `IdentityDocument freshness` figure in `docs/identity-model.md`); no-op on legacy nodes. Routed through the standard `RuntimeService::SovereignIdentityRepublish` variant so both startup and reload paths spawn it consistently. First tick is consumed to avoid duplicating the one-shot startup publish; subsequent ticks re-publish both the identity document AND the single-entry `InstanceRegistry` via the `DhtBackedPublisher`. Graceful `debug` / `warn` logs per record per tick. **InstanceRegistry publish at startup shipped**: new `SovereignIdentity::build_and_sign_registry(reg_version, instances)` helper composes `build_instance_registry` + `sign_instance_registry` using the handle's active identity_sk + sig_key_idx + identity_id; `NodeRuntime::start` builds a single-entry registry for this node's instance (`reg_version = 1` for MVP — version persistence + monotonic bump across restarts is a follow-up, benign for now since peers tie-break on sig) and publishes alongside the document. Registry helper test proves sig verifies against the active subkey's pubkey via the canonical `INSTANCE_REGISTRY_SIG_CONTEXT` + canonical_signing_bytes shape. **MlKemCert publish at startup shipped**: new `SovereignIdentity::sign_mlkem_cert(mlkem_pubkey, valid_from, valid_until, cert_version)` helper wraps `sign_mlkem_cert` with `identity_id`, `active_instance_id`, `sig_key_idx`, `identity_sk`, and `document` from self. `NodeRuntime::start` certifies the node's own auto-loaded ML-KEM-768 encapsulation key (already in `mlkem_ek`) with a 30-day validity window and publishes the cert at the canonical `(identity_id, instance_id)` DHT slot. Helper test verifies the produced cert passes `verify_mlkem_cert` end-to-end (cert chain + window + subkey-in-doc). **6h republish loop extended** to refresh the cert with a fresh 30-day validity window on each tick — `valid_until` always rolls forward, so long-running nodes never let their cert window lapse. Thus all three canonical sovereign-identity records — `IdentityDocument`, `InstanceRegistry`, `MlKemKeyCert` — now publish together at startup and keep publishing every 6 h while the node runs. **NameClaim publish shipped**: new `SovereignIdentity::sign_name_claim(name, claimed_at_unix)` helper normalizes the name via `name_claim_v2::normalize_name` (rejects non-ASCII / homoglyphs), mines rarity-proportional PoW via `sign_name_claim`, signs with the active identity_sk. Disk persistence: `save_name_claim(veil_dir, claim)` writes `<veil_dir>/name_claims/<name>.bin` with tmp-then-rename atomicity; `load_persisted_name_claims(veil_dir) -> Vec<NameClaim>` scans the directory (returns empty when missing), deterministically sorts by name, and silently skips corrupt `.bin` files so one bad file doesn't block startup of the rest. Startup publish iterates the persisted claims and publishes each via `publish_name_claim`; the 6h republish loop re-scans the directory each tick so names claimed mid-session start publishing without a node restart. 5 new tests — sign produces valid claim, normalize rejects `alíce`, save/load round-trip, missing dir returns empty, corrupt file skipped but good claims still loaded. **CLI `identity claim-name <name>` shipped**: `veil-cli identity claim-name <name> [--veil-dir <path>]` loads the sovereign identity, calls `SovereignIdentity::sign_name_claim` (normalises name → mines PoW → signs with active identity_sk), persists via `save_name_claim` to `<veil_dir>/name_claims/<name>.bin`, and prints the claim + path + a note that the running daemon will publish on its next 6h republish tick. 4 CLI tests — happy-path writes file + emits message, rejects non-normalizable name (`alíce` homoglyph), reports `SovereignLoad` on unprovisioned dir (no panic, no corrupt write), idempotent on repeat (same filename overwritten cleanly). End-to-end messenger flow for `@alice` resolution is now usable: `veil-cli identity create` → `veil-cli identity claim-name alice` → restart daemon → `NameResolver::resolve("alice")` on Bob's node hits Alice's published claim in the DHT → resolves the full cert chain → returns `ValidatedIdentity`. **On-change republish shipped**: the 6-hour republish loop now runs a fast secondary poll every 60 s that stats `<veil_dir>/identity_document.bin`.  When the file's mtime advances (CLI `rotate` / `revoke` writes a new document), the loop reloads `SovereignIdentity::load_from_dir`, rebuilds the registry against the new subkey + sig_key_idx, and immediately re-publishes both document and registry via the `DhtBackedPublisher`.  Propagation time for a key-compromise revocation drops from `≤ 6h` to `≤ 60 s`.  Load failure (corrupt write mid-rotate, permissions, etc.) is logged `warn` and the previous in-memory identity is kept — no silent loss of service.  Logs emit `node.sovereign_identity.reloaded_on_change` on successful reload with both old and new `document_version` | **P1** | ~350 | `node/identity/publish.rs`, `node/identity/sovereign.rs`, `node/identity/publisher_dht.rs` (new), `node/task_registry.rs`, `node/runtime.rs`, `proto/mlkem_cert.rs`, `cmd/cli.rs`, `cmd/sovereign_identity.rs` | ✅ done |
| 462.13 | **NameClaim V2 proto**: `proto/name_claim_v2.rs` — struct with `name`, `identity_id`, `claimed_at`, `pow_nonce`, embedded `cert_proof`, `signing_identity_key_idx`, `sig`. Magic `"NM"` + version byte. **Character whitelist (ASCII-only MVP)**: only `[a-z0-9#_-]`, case-insensitive (normalize to lowercase before hashing). Max 64 bytes. Reject any Unicode characters — prevents homoglyph-typosquatting (`alíce` vs `alice`). `required_difficulty(name)` — rarity-proportional PoW (short ASCII alphabetic → 28-30, alphanumeric 5-7 chars → 22, Discord-style `alice#1234` → 14, long random → 12). Signed by **any active** identity_sk (not necessarily a specific instance). Replaces Epic 175 NameRecord. Wire migration (breaking). Future work: IDNA2008 Unicode support in a separate epic. **Implementation note**: proto+normalize+required_difficulty+dht_key all ship; legacy Epic 175 NameRecord removal is a follow-up (462.25) | **P1** | ~450 | `proto/name_claim_v2.rs` (new) | ✅ done |
| 462.14 | **Name resolver pipeline**: `node/identity/resolver.rs` — resolve `@name` → NameClaim → IdentityDocument → `ValidatedIdentity` + active subkey. Two-step DHT fetch with a cache (name → identity_id cached for TTL). Offline verification via the embedded cert_proof (single DHT round for "fast path" if the consumer already knows identity_id). **Implementation note**: resolver uses DI-style `NameLookup`/`IdentityLookup` async traits so production wires the DHT runtime and tests use in-memory fakes. Cache is capacity-bounded (4096) with oldest-touched eviction and a 5-minute TTL | **P1** | ~250 | `node/identity/resolver.rs` (new) | ✅ done |
| 462.15 | **Wire refactor**: `recipient_node_id: [u8; 32]` → `recipient: Recipient` everywhere. `struct Recipient { identity_id: [u8; 32], instance_tag: InstanceTag }`; `enum InstanceTag { Any, All, Specific([u8; 16]) }` — on-wire 17 bytes with a tag byte. Touches `DeliveryEnvelope`, `DELIVERY_FORWARD`, `TransitFramePayload`, `RouteCache`, `RouteAnnounce/Withdraw` origin, `PEX advertise`, `AnycastAdvertise`, `MailboxFetch`. The largest epic, wire-format breaking — one-shot migration. **Progress (phased)**: **Phase 0** ✅ — `Recipient`+`InstanceTag` proto types (21 tests). **Phase 1** ✅ — additive accessors (5 tests). **Phase 2** ✅ — 18 runtime consumers migrated. **Phase 3a** ✅ — field renamed across ~120 sites: `recipient_node_id` → `recipient_identity_id` on all four wire structs. **Phase 3b1** ✅ — `DeliveryEnvelope.recipient_identity_id: [u8; 32]` evolved to `DeliveryEnvelope.recipient: Recipient`.  Python script retargeted 34 construction sites from `recipient_identity_id: X` to `recipient: crate::proto::recipient::Recipient::any(X)`; `.recipient_identity_id` reads on `DeliveryEnvelope` instances now go through the `recipient_identity_id()` method or direct `.recipient.identity_id` field access; all ~1900 tests still pass. Wire format unchanged — encoder writes only `self.recipient.identity_id`, decoder wraps incoming bytes in `Recipient::any(...)`. Mailbox structs (`MailboxFetchPayload`, `MailboxFetchReplicaPayload`, `MailboxAckPayload`) keep `recipient_identity_id: [u8; 32]` unchanged — mailbox is identity-scoped, not instance-scoped. **Phase 3b-wire** (in progress) — evolve wire format to natively carry the `InstanceTag` byte (33 B unbounded / 49 B specific) so peers can actually request `InstanceTag::All` fan-out or `::Specific` targeting over the network.  Breaking wire-format change, gated behind dispatcher/session InstanceTag-awareness (462.20 / 462.17).  **Phase 3b-wire-1** ✅ — `DeliveryEnvelope` now carries the recipient via the new fixed-49-byte encoding (`Recipient::encode_fixed_into` / `decode_fixed_from`: identity_id (32) + tag byte (1) + instance_id (16, zero-padded for `Any`/`All`)).  Trade-off: chose fixed-49 over the variable 33/49 form so `OFFSET_ENDPOINT_ID` / `_CONTENT_ID` / `_CREATED_AT` / `_TTL_SECS` / `_PAYLOAD_LEN` / `_PAYLOAD` keep stable offsets (just shifted +17), preserving the dispatcher's fast-path TTL pre-decoder (`node/dispatcher/delivery.rs::is_envelope_expired`) without parsing the whole frame on every relay hop.  `DELIVERY_ENVELOPE_HEADER = 197` (was 180); `RECIPIENT_FIXED_SIZE = 49` const exposed.  Decoder rejects non-zero padding on `Any`/`All` tags as a protocol violation (signals encoder bug or wire tamper).  3 new wire tests — `roundtrip_preserves_instance_tag_all` (Specific too), `decode_rejects_nonzero_padding_on_any` — guard against accidental round-trip loss of the tag.  All 2269 lib tests pass single-threaded; clippy clean.  **Phase 3b-wire-2 inventory complete — no further wire migration needed**: post-3b-wire-1 audit of the originally-listed touchpoints showed every remaining struct is either (a) migrated transitively (`ForwardPayload` carries `DeliveryEnvelope` and inherits the new format end-to-end through every relay hop); or (b) addressing a different namespace — `TransitFramePayload`, `RouteAnnouncePayload`, `RouteWithdrawPayload`, `PexWalk`, `AnycastRecord` all carry session-layer `node_id` ([u8; 32], legacy keypair-derived) NOT sovereign `identity_id`, so the `Recipient` wrapper doesn't apply (instance-tagged routing for the legacy plane is by-design out of scope; legacy peers stay node_id-keyed); or (c) already exposes per-instance addressing via the Epic 462.19 `instance_id: [u8; 16]` separate field — `MailboxFetchPayload`, `MailboxAckPayload`, `MailboxFetchReplicaPayload` (mailbox is identity-scoped + per-instance cursor by design — the Recipient enum doesn't fit because mailbox doesn't have an "All instances" delivery semantic; instead it stores once per identity and serves multiple fetcher instances).  With 3b-wire-1 the only struct that genuinely needed `Recipient::encode_fixed_into` on the wire (because it's the only one that delivers to "all live instances") got it.  462.15 functionally complete.  **Runtime-integration scenario shipped + multi-instance fan-out gap fixed**: `sim/scenarios.rs::sovereign_identity_instance_tag_survives_runtime_layer` — 3-node mesh (alice device 0, alice device 1 restored from 0, charlie); `DeliveryEnvelope` with `Recipient::Any`/`All`/`Specific(_)` round-trips through encode→wire→decode preserving every `InstanceTag` variant; `debug_resolve_recipient` (new test-only `NodeRuntime` accessor over `SessionRegistry::resolve_recipient`) confirms the dispatcher resolver fans `Recipient::All` out to BOTH alice devices, `Specific(instance_a)`/`Specific(instance_b)` each hit exactly one (distinct) peer, bogus `Specific` returns empty, and `Any` lands on one of the live peers.  **Bug fixed**: pre-fix, the sim's `connect()` calls `reload_with` which wipes Charlie's `SessionRegistry` and the subsequent reconnect uses the session-resumption fast path (SESSION_TICKET) which bypasses the `IdentityProof` exchange — `validated_sovereign_identity` came back `None` and the second alice session never slotted into `by_identity_instance`.  Fix: a new `peer_sovereign_identities: Arc<Mutex<HashMap<peer_id, ValidatedIdentity>>>` cache lives on `NodeRuntime` (and is cloned through `SessionRuntimeContext`) — full handshakes write the binding into the cache, `cache_peer_handshake_state` restores it on resumption, so resumed sessions still slot into `by_identity_instance` and `Recipient::All` fans out correctly.  Cache survives `reload_with` (parallel to `peer_pubkeys`).  2277 lib tests + clippy clean | ✅ all wire phases done + multi-instance fan-out closed — `Recipient`+`InstanceTag` proto types (21 tests), additive accessors (5 tests), 18 runtime consumers migrated, ~120-site rename `recipient_node_id` → `recipient_identity_id`, `DeliveryEnvelope` field evolved to `Recipient`, fixed-49 wire format with `InstanceTag` round-trip preserved | `proto/recipient.rs`, `proto/delivery.rs`, `proto/budget.rs`, `proto/routing.rs`, `proto/pex.rs`, `proto/anycast.rs`, `node/dispatcher/*`, `node/routing/*`, `node/mailbox/*` | ✅ done |
| 462.16 | **Handshake refactor** (Epic 406 extension): `IdentityProof` payload in the OVL1 handshake — contains `identity_id` + `identity_pubkey` (current subkey) + `ephemeral_sig` (subkey signs X25519 ephemeral pk, anti-MITM) + `cert_proof` (master_pk + master_sig over identity_pk). Peer verify' without a DHT fetch. Cache peer `(identity_id → master_pk, active_identity_pk)`. Lazy revocation check via DHT when an anomaly appears. **Implementation note**: library layer ships — `proto/identity_proof.rs` (self-contained wire type: magic `IP`, v1, embeds `identity_id`, `master_pk`, inline `IdentityKey` fields + `master_sig`, `proof_valid_until_unix`, `freshness_hour`, `ephemeral_x25519_pk`, `ephemeral_sig`; 10 proto tests covering encode/decode round-trip, magic/version/truncation/trailing/oversize guards, zero-len sig rejection, ephemeral signing-message stability + anti-tamper binding); `node/identity/publish.rs::sign_identity_proof` producer (copies `IdentityKey` fields from the signed document, signs `EPHEMERAL_SIG_CONTEXT \|\| identity_id \|\| valid_until \|\| ephemeral_pk` with active `identity_sk`); `node/identity/verify.rs::verify_identity_proof` verifier (identity_id = BLAKE3(master_pk) binding, master-cert-chain inline verify via reconstructed `certify_message`, ephemeral-sig verify, `FRESHNESS_HOUR_SKEW`, `valid_until` window, local `RevocationCache` check — returns `ValidatedIdentity`); 9 producer/verifier tests — round-trip, wire round-trip → verify, MITM ephemeral swap, tampered identity pk (master-cert breaks), tampered master pk (identity_id mismatch), expired window, stale freshness hour, revoked subkey in cache, bad sig_key_idx. All 2058 lib tests pass single-threaded; clippy clean. **Runtime-wiring constraint discovered**: `KeyAgreementPayload` cannot be extended with a trailing-length-prefix `identity_proof` trailer — existing Epic 406 / OVL1 framing leaves non-structured bytes after `ephemeral_sig` on the wire and the current decoder tolerates them silently. An attempted additive wire trailer (explored 2026-04-21) misinterpreted those bytes as a fake `proof_len` and broke 3 real-handshake tests in `node::admin` (outbound session, sessions-list, debug-accept-routes). Reverted. **Correct integration**: carry the proof on a dedicated new session frame (`SessionMsg::IdentityProof`) sent between KA and SESSION_CONFIRM, or extend `IdentityPayload` via explicit version byte bump (NOT trailing append). **Dedicated-frame slot reserved**: `SessionMsg::IdentityProof = 15` variant landed in `proto/family.rs` with full `TryFrom<u16>` mapping and 4 discriminant-stability tests (every variant 0..15 round-trips, unknown 16+ errors cleanly, IdentityProof=15 confirmed). Existing `node/dispatcher/session.rs` catch-all handles the new variant gracefully as `NotHandled` (handshake-flow frames are consumed by the handshake state machine, not the steady-state dispatcher). **Frame-consumer helper shipped**: `node/identity/verify.rs::verify_identity_proof_frame(body, ka_ephemeral_pubkey, cache, now)` + `FrameProofError` enum — the exact one-call helper the runtime handshake invokes after reading the `IdentityProof` frame: (1) decode body via `IdentityProof::decode`, (2) cross-check `proof.ephemeral_x25519_pk == ka.ephemeral_pubkey` (critical anti-replay binding), (3) run full cert-chain + freshness + local-cache checks, returns `ValidatedIdentity`. 6 tests — matched-pk happy path, MITM pk-mismatch (proof bound to eph_a but KA said eph_b), garbage body, empty body, expired-proof (past valid_until), revoked-subkey-in-cache. **Capability-flag negotiation shipped**: `cap_flags::SUPPORTS_SOVEREIGN_IDENTITY = 1 << 7` in `proto/session.rs`; `CapabilitiesPayload::supports_sovereign_identity()` + `sovereign_identity_negotiated(&peer)` helpers. Bit is set by sovereign-identity-aware peers at handshake time; proof-frame exchange happens only when BOTH sides advertise it. Legacy peers advertise `0` in that bit and skip the frame entirely — no breaking wire change. 5 tests — stable bit value (`1 << 7`) with no overlap against other cap flags, helper reads bit correctly, `sovereign_identity_negotiated` requires both sides, survives encode/decode round-trip, legacy-peer backwards-compat (default-ctor caps have the bit unset). **Runtime provider struct shipped**: `node/identity/sovereign.rs::SovereignIdentity { document, sig_key_idx, identity_sk }` + `SovereignIdentityError` enum + `load_from_dir(veil_dir)` loader that reads `identity_document.bin` + `device_identity_sk.bin` (the exact disk layout `cfg/sovereign_flow::create_identity` writes), validates the SK↔subkey pubkey binding, rejects out-of-bounds sig_key_idx / in-doc-revoked active subkey / non-Ed25519 subkeys (Falcon deferred), and exposes `identity_id()`, `active_instance_id()`, `sign_proof(ephemeral_pk, valid_until, freshness_hour)` — the one-call emitter for the runtime handshake.  `Debug` impl redacts the SK. 10 tests — loader happy path (against a real `create_identity` output), missing-document/SK-file rejection, corrupted-document / wrong-SK-seed rejection, out-of-bounds idx, active-subkey-revoked-in-doc rejection, sign_proof → `verify_identity_proof_frame` end-to-end round-trip (the exact loop runtime will run), loaded document passes full `verify_identity_document`, SK redaction in Debug. Runtime handshake plumbing (teach `node/session/handshake.rs` to optionally take `&SovereignIdentity`, emit `SessionMsg::IdentityProof` after KA send, call `verify_identity_proof_frame` on receive, stash `ValidatedIdentity` on the session) is now the ONLY remaining runtime task — every library primitive, wire slot, error type, loader, and end-to-end test it needs is ready. Library-level equivalent scenario ships in `scenario_two_nodes_mutual_identity_proof_exchange` (462.23). **Runtime handshake wiring shipped**: `node/session/handshake.rs::perform_ovl1_handshake` accepts an optional `SovereignHandshakeCtx<'_>` parameter bundling the local `SovereignIdentity` + `RevocationCache` + `now_unix_secs`. When `Some`, the handshake (a) sets `cap_flags::SUPPORTS_SOVEREIGN_IDENTITY` in outgoing CAPABILITIES, (b) checks `caps.sovereign_identity_negotiated(&remote_capabilities)` after the mutual CAPS exchange, (c) emits `SessionMsg::IdentityProof` frame bound to the local KA ephemeral pk between KA and SESSION_CONFIRM when both sides advertised, (d) reads + verifies the peer's frame via `verify_identity_proof_frame`, (e) populates `OvlHandshakeResult::validated_sovereign_identity: Option<ValidatedIdentity>`. Legacy peers (or sessions without local sovereign material) skip the exchange entirely — no breaking wire change, no behavior change when `ctx = None`. `IdentityProof::decode` relaxed to tolerate trailing bytes (Epic 409.5 handshake padding). 8 call sites updated to pass `None` for the new param. 2 new handshake integration tests — `sovereign_proof_exchange_runs_end_to_end` creates two real sovereign identities via `create_identity`, drives the full OVL1 handshake over in-memory duplex streams, asserts both sides advertise the capability bit, both sides end up with each other's `ValidatedIdentity`, session keys still match; `sovereign_proof_exchange_skips_when_peer_lacks_support` proves asymmetric setup (one side with ctx, one without) falls back cleanly to the legacy path. All 2088 lib tests pass single-threaded; clippy clean. **Runtime auto-loader + handshake threading shipped**: `NodeRuntime` + `NodeServices` + `SessionRuntimeContext` all carry `sovereign_identity: Option<Arc<SovereignIdentity>>` + `revocation_cache: Arc<RevocationCache>`. At `start()`, the runtime auto-loads from `<veil_dir>/identity_document.bin` + `device_identity_sk.bin` via `SovereignIdentity::load_from_dir` (logs `node.sovereign_identity.loaded` with identity_id + instance_id); absent files → legacy mode with graceful log. Persistent `revocation_cache.bin` opens alongside so revocation pushes survive restarts. The single production `perform_ovl1_handshake` call site in `spawn_outbound_session` builds a `SovereignHandshakeCtx` from `runtime.sovereign_identity` when loaded and threads it into the handshake — every outbound session on a provisioned node now advertises `SUPPORTS_SOVEREIGN_IDENTITY` + exchanges the `IdentityProof` frame when the peer also advertises support. Both inbound (listener) and outbound (connect) paths carry the ctx through `SessionRuntimeContext` clones (3 construction sites patched consistently). **SessionRegistry identity index shipped**: `SessionEntry` gained `validated_sovereign_identity: Option<ValidatedIdentity>`. `SessionRegistry` maintains a secondary `by_identity: HashMap<[u8; 32], SessionId>` populated on insert (when present) and cleared on remove (with correct handling of the "same identity reconnects on a new session — old close must not evict the new entry" race). Public lookup surface: `get_by_identity_id(&[u8; 32])` returns the live `SessionEntry`; `peer_id_for_identity(&[u8; 32])` is the convenience wrapper delivery/mailbox code calls to map a sovereign addressee to the transport-level `peer_id` it already knows how to route to. `cache_peer_handshake_state` in `runtime.rs` forwards `r.validated_sovereign_identity` into the new `SessionEntry` field so every successful sovereign handshake is now indexable by `identity_id` across the node. 4 new SessionRegistry tests cover: sovereign entry is indexed + `peer_id_for_identity` returns the right peer, non-sovereign entry is NOT indexed (legacy peers), identity index is cleared on remove, reconnect-under-same-identity does not evict the newer entry when the older session's close fires later. All 2092 lib tests pass single-threaded; clippy clean. **Epic 462.16 is now functionally complete end-to-end** — a provisioned node authenticates sovereign identities on every OVL1 handshake and makes them addressable by `identity_id` across the runtime | **P0** | ~500 | `proto/identity_proof.rs`, `proto/family.rs`, `proto/session.rs::CapabilitiesPayload`, `node/identity/publish.rs`, `node/identity/verify.rs`, `node/identity/sovereign.rs` (new), `node/session/handshake.rs`, `node/session/manager.rs`, `node/runtime.rs` | ✅ done (library + wire + handshake + node loader + session index) |
| 462.17 | **Session dedup refactor**: key `(identity_id, instance_id)` instead of `node_id`. Multiple instances of one identity can simultaneously have sessions with the same peer'. The ticket binds to `peer_instance_id` — prevents AEAD nonce reuse on resumption. Breaking internal change, 5+ call sites. **Implementation note**: library layer ships — `SessionRegistry::by_identity_instance: HashMap<([u8; 32], [u8; 16]), SessionId>` replaces the single-session `by_identity` index from 462.16. Insert populates the composite key (when `ValidatedIdentity` is present); remove clears only if the slot still points at this session (correct race handling for reconnect-under-same-identity + delayed-old-close). Public API: `get_by_identity_instance(id, inst)` / `peer_id_for_identity_instance(id, inst)` (Specific routing), `peer_ids_for_identity(id) -> Vec<(instance_id, peer_id)>` (All fan-out), `peer_id_for_identity(id)` (Any — picks first, unspecified order documented as scoring follow-up). 4 new tests covering two-instances-coexist (laptop+phone under one identity both individually reachable + All sees both + Any returns one), specific-instance-missing, empty-fan-out, remove-one-leaves-others (+ the existing reconnect-eviction test still passes). **Ticket-layer `peer_instance_id` binding shipped**: `SessionTicket` gained `peer_instance_id: [u8; 16]`; plaintext wire grows 144 → 160; encrypted blob 172 → 188 (constants `SESSION_TICKET_ENCRYPTED_SIZE` kept as legacy + new `SESSION_TICKET_ENCRYPTED_SIZE_V2`). `TicketIssuer::decrypt` accepts both sizes; legacy 172-byte tickets decrypt with `peer_instance_id = [0; 16]` so pre-462.17 peers resume cleanly. New `TicketIssuer::issue_for_instance(session_id, peer_id, peer_instance_id, tx_key, rx_key)` gives sovereign-aware callers the binding; legacy 4-arg `issue` remains and stamps `[0; 16]` as the default. `runner.rs` ticket-size guard accepts both V1 and V2. 2 new tests — V2 round-trip preserves instance_id, hand-crafted 172-byte legacy blob decrypts to `peer_instance_id = [0; 16]`.  Note on `tx_registry`: no additional keying change is needed because each instance of a sovereign identity has a distinct transport-level `node_id` (BLAKE3 of its own `identity_sk` pubkey).  The existing `peer_id`-keyed `SessionTxRegistry` therefore intrinsically separates Alice's laptop (`node_id = hash(laptop_sk_pub)`) from Alice's phone (`node_id = hash(phone_sk_pub)`) — each gets its own outbox channel.  The sovereign-routing chain `(identity_id, instance_tag) → SessionRegistry → peer_id → tx_registry.send_to` is already correct end-to-end | **P0** | ~300 | `node/session/manager.rs`, `node/session/ticket.rs`, `node/session/runner.rs`, `node/runtime.rs`, `proto/session.rs::SessionTicket`, `proto/budget.rs` | ✅ done |
| 462.18 | **Reputation migrate**: keyed by `identity_id` instead of `node_id`. Survives rotation natively (identity_id stable). Shared between instance's of one identity (by design: identity-level reputation for LB). Migration: wipe existing reputation store (the network is not deployed). **Implementation note**: `ReputationTracker` API + docs updated to key on `identity_id` (rotation-stable, master-pk-derived); the 32-byte map shape stayed identical so call-site signatures don't churn.  `SessionRegistry::identity_id_for_peer(peer_id) -> Option<[u8; 32]>` (new helper) maps a session-layer `peer_id` to the validated sovereign `identity_id`; legacy peers without a sovereign identity fall back to `peer_id` as a degenerate identifier so legacy behaviour stays unchanged.  Three callers migrated: `runtime.rs` `session_opened` / `session_closed` (resolve identity BEFORE registry remove so the binding is still available) and `dispatcher/delivery.rs` ACK-gated relay credit (Epic 461.6).  Rotation-survival invariant verified by `reputation_survives_peer_id_rotation_under_stable_identity` test: 200 relay credits → rotation (peer_id changes, identity_id stable) → 200 more credits → ledger combined to 40 points (NOT split into two 20-point ledgers); a separate identity has its own ledger; multi-device (laptop + phone) ACKs combine on the SAME ledger.  Existing reputation tests (`relay_success_accumulates`, `eviction_at_capacity`, `evict_stale_*`) all pass against the renamed internals. 2271 lib tests + clippy clean | **P1** | ~200 | `node/reputation.rs`, `node/session/manager.rs` (`identity_id_for_peer`), `node/runtime.rs` (session open/close callers), `node/dispatcher/delivery.rs` (relay credit caller) | ✅ done |
| 462.19 | **Mailbox refactor** (multi-device sync foundation): mailbox keyed by `identity_id`. Shared inbox, per-instance cursor: `HashMap<instance_id, last_acked_seq>`. GC erases a message only after an ACK from ALL active instances OR after `instance_stale_after = 7d` force-advance. Support `InstanceTag::All` fan-out. **Implementation note**: library-layer foundation ships in `node/mailbox/instance_cursors.rs::InstanceCursorMap` — pure in-memory data structure keyed `(identity_id, instance_id) → (last_acked_seq, last_seen_unix)` with the canonical GC query `lowest_cursor(identity_id) -> Option<u64>` (minimum cursor across non-stale instances). API: `record_ack(id, inst, seq, now)` (monotonic — can't regress), `touch(id, inst, now)` (refresh last_seen without advancing cursor — used on fetch), `prune_stale(id, now, stale_after_secs)` (drops instances inactive > `DEFAULT_INSTANCE_STALE_AFTER_SECS = 7 days`; returns dropped ids; frees the identity entry when empty), `cursor_for_instance` / `active_instances` / `forget_instance`. 12 tests covering: ack monotonicity, lowest-cursor across 2+ instances picks min, none-when-unknown, zero-when-not-acked, touch refreshes without advancing, prune drops stale only, prune removes identity when empty, explicit forget, 3-instance fan-out semantics (all must ACK before GC), active_instances list, prune-missing-identity no-op. **`MailboxStore` now carries the tracker** as an `instance_cursors` field and exposes the full per-instance API: `record_instance_ack(id, inst, seq, now)` (monotonic, does NOT pop the queue — this is the key backwards-compat guarantee), `touch_instance(id, inst, now)`, `lowest_acked_cursor(id) -> Option<u64>` (the GC floor query), `cursor_for_instance(id, inst)`, `prune_stale_instances(id, now, stale_after_secs)`. Pure additive extension — legacy `ack` / `ack_seqs` still pop from the queue immediately, so existing callers see zero behaviour change. 7 new tests — per-instance ack does NOT pop, lowest-cursor queries tracker, none-for-unknown-identity, cursor_for_instance reads, touch refreshes last_seen survives prune, prune delegates to tracker, legacy ack still pops regardless of tracker state. **`MailboxBackend` trait extended** with `record_instance_ack` / `touch_instance` / `lowest_acked_cursor` / `cursor_for_instance` / `prune_stale_instances`.  Default impls forward to the legacy single-cursor `ack` path — safe no-op for backends that haven't been wired yet.  `InMemoryBackend` overrides delegate to the `MailboxStore` tracker; `WalBackend` forwards to its inner `InMemoryBackend` (WAL persistence of tracker state — new `WAL_INSTANCE_ACK` record type + replay — is a follow-up; MVP accepts at-least-once semantics on restart since delivery ACKs are already idempotent). 5 new backend tests — in-memory routes acks to tracker, in-memory ack does NOT pop queue, in-memory prune delegates cleanly, Wal forwards cleanly, `Box<dyn MailboxBackend>` trait-object dispatch picks the overridden impl (critical for service.rs handler routing). **Wire extension shipped**: `MailboxFetchPayload` gained `instance_id: [u8; 16]` field (wire size 40 → 56 bytes full, with 40 still accepted legacy); `MailboxAckPayload` gained a trailing 16-byte `instance_id` after the seqs array (backwards-compat: decoders that see exactly `34 + count*8` bytes default to `[0; 16]`; encoders always emit the trailer so the value propagates end-to-end). Budget constants split into `MAILBOX_FETCH_SIZE` (legacy 40) + `MAILBOX_FETCH_FULL_SIZE` (56) and `MAILBOX_ACK_MIN_SIZE` (legacy 34) + `MAILBOX_ACK_FULL_MIN_SIZE` (50). `MailboxAckPayload::single_for_instance(id, seq, inst)` convenience ctor tags a single-seq ACK with an explicit instance; legacy `::single(id, seq)` keeps `[0; 16]` default. 4 new proto tests — legacy 40-byte decode defaults to zero instance_id, full 56-byte round-trip preserves instance_id, legacy hand-built ack payload decodes with zero instance, ack with explicit instance round-trips. All ~15 construction sites across `node/dispatcher/*`, `node/mailbox/*`, and proto tests updated with explicit `instance_id: [0u8; 16]` legacy default. **Service handlers route per-instance acks** (`node/mailbox/service.rs`). `handle_fetch`: when `payload.instance_id != [0; 16]` the fetching instance is `touch_instance`-ed in the backend so it doesn't age out before its next ACK; zero instance_id is the legacy no-op path. `handle_ack`: when `instance_id` is non-zero the service calls `backend.record_instance_ack(recipient, instance, max(seqs), now)` — cursor advances but **queue is NOT popped**, so the entry stays reachable for other instances' later fetches; when `instance_id` is zero the service falls back to the legacy `ack_batch` → pop-on-ack path. Quota release (Epic 173.B) happens on both paths so the sender isn't penalised by a queue that's merely awaiting other-instance ACKs. 4 new service tests — fetch with instance_id touches tracker (cursor=0 seeded, last_seen=now), ack with instance_id advances cursor without popping (queue unchanged), ack with zero instance_id still pops (legacy path preserved), multi-instance laptop+phone ACK independently and `lowest_acked_cursor` returns the min.  **Slice 6 — `cleanup_expired` consults `lowest_acked_cursor`.** Before scanning queues for TTL eviction, the store prunes stale (> 7d inactive) per-instance cursors so a single offline instance never pins GC forever.  Then each entry is kept only if `instance_floor.map_or(true, |f| entry.seq > f) && (recipient_sleeping || ttl_unexpired)` — the per-instance floor takes precedence over sleep grace (once every instance has ACKed, the message is delivered end-to-end).  Recipients with no tracked instances (legacy single-device) keep the TTL-only behaviour unchanged.  4 new store tests — below-cursor drop, partial-ACK keeps msg, stale-instance prune unblocks GC, legacy no-instances falls back to TTL.  **All six slices green.  Epic 462.19 is functionally complete**: Alice's laptop and phone each ACK every inbound message independently, the mailbox drops messages only once every live instance has ACKed (or a laggard is pruned after 7 days), legacy single-device peers see zero behavioural change | **P1** | ~500 | `node/mailbox/instance_cursors.rs` (new), `node/mailbox/service.rs`, `node/mailbox/backend.rs`, `node/mailbox/store.rs`, `proto/delivery.rs::MailboxFetch`, `proto/delivery.rs::MailboxAckPayload`, `proto/budget.rs` | ✅ done |
| 462.20 | **Delivery dispatch with `InstanceTag`**: `Any` → resolve InstanceRegistry + `pick_weighted` picks one instance; `All` → fan-out to all active instances; `Specific(id)` → direct target. Integration with existing delivery paths. **Implementation note**: library-layer routing surface ships — `SessionRegistry::resolve_recipient(&Recipient) -> Vec<[u8; 32]>` is the single entry point the delivery dispatcher will call: `Any` → 0-or-1 peer_ids, `Specific(inst)` → 0-or-1 peer_ids, `All` → vec of every live `(instance, peer)` pair. Empty return = "no live session" → dispatcher falls through to mailbox / DHT paths. Legacy (non-sovereign) peers are not in `by_identity_instance` so they are deliberately invisible to sovereign routing (legacy routing must use `get_by_peer_id`). 5 tests — Any-returns-one, All-returns-every-instance, Specific-hits-one + missing-returns-empty, offline-identity-returns-empty-for-every-tag, legacy-peer-not-resolvable-by-identity. **Dispatcher integration surface shipped**: `FrameDispatcher` gained `session_registry: Option<Arc<Mutex<SessionRegistry>>>` (mirrors the existing `session_tx_registry` shape) populated by `NodeRuntime::start` via `shared_session_registry` — both dispatcher (read side) and runtime (write side populated by the handshake) share the same `Arc`. New helper `FrameDispatcher::resolve_sovereign_delivery_targets(&Recipient) -> Option<Vec<[u8; 32]>>` with the semantic: `None` → no registry wired (test/legacy build, fall back to `route_cache`); `Some(vec![])` → registry wired but no live session (fall back to mailbox/DHT); `Some(vec![peer_id, ...])` → dispatcher fans out. 3 tests — helper returns `None` without a wired registry (test dispatcher default), empty when identity is offline, and correct Any/Specific/All resolution when a live sovereign session exists. **Sovereign fast-path wired into `relay_forward`**: `try_sovereign_direct_forward(payload, sender_peer, traffic_class, relay_hops_out)` builds the forward frame (same Epic 411.1 single-allocation pattern as the legacy path, just 32-byte `next_hop_node_id` per target) and sends directly via `session_tx_registry.send_to`. Called at the top of `relay_forward` immediately after preconditions check — when the sender's identity is `Recipient::Any|Specific|All`-addressed and at least one live sovereign session exists (minus split-horizon + self-loop), delivery is 1 hop through an authenticated, known-alive session instead of however many hops `route_cache` scoring thinks. Falls through to the scored `route_cache` path unchanged on empty / all-filtered / send-fail. 3 new tests — live session hits fast-path (asserts the real frame with correct `next_hop_node_id` prefix lands in the outbox), no registry returns false, split-horizon filters the sender peer out. All 2108 lib tests pass single-threaded; clippy clean | **P1** | ~400 | `node/session/manager.rs`, `node/dispatcher/mod.rs`, `node/dispatcher/delivery.rs`, `node/runtime.rs` | ✅ done |
| 462.21 | **Tier B encrypted transports + encrypted mailbox_anchor** (optional, opt-in): `encrypted_contact: Option<Vec<u8>>` in InstanceEntry — AEAD(ChaCha20-Poly1305) with an HKDF key from **derived-from-master** identity-shared secret (not the individual identity_sk, since in 462.39 they are per-instance): derivation path `HKDF(master_pk_hash || "veil.tier_b_shared.v1", reg_version)` — all instances of one identity can decrypt, attackers outside the identity — no. The encrypted contact contains: `transports: Vec<String>`, `mailbox_anchor: [u8; 32]` (per 462.42 — anchor also encrypted so as not to leak location via Core geolocation). A consumer of the same identity decrypts it for direct sessions (low-latency multi-device sync). Config: `identity.multi_device.tier_b.enabled = false` (default), `last_seen_granularity_minutes = 60`, `publish_labels = false`, `dummy_entries = 0` (opt-in cover traffic). Privacy upgrade: DHT-observer sees only `(identity_id, instance_count, label-count, coarse last_seen)` — does not see mailbox anchor locations. **Implementation note**: library layer ships — `derive_tier_b_secret` via BLAKE3 `derive_key`, `encrypt_contact`/`decrypt_contact` with ChaCha20-Poly1305 bound by AAD to `(identity_id, instance_id, reg_version)`, inner `TierBContact { mailbox_anchor, transports }` wire format (magic `TB v1`) with caps of 8 transports × 256 B. Registry-publish wiring depends on 462.12 | **P2** | ~400 | `node/identity/tier_b.rs` (new) | ✅ done (library layer) |
| 462.22 | **Revocation propagation (immediate push)**: `node/identity/propagate.rs` — on compromise-revoke push the updated IdentityDocument + `RevocationEntry` to ALL known peers via direct sessions (do not wait for the DHT cycle). Rate-limited (burst 50, sustained 1/s) for anti-abuse. Recipients invalidate their local identity cache immediately + and write into the persistent revocation cache (462.36). **Implementation note**: library layer ships — `verify_and_apply_push` (identity_id-binding + master sig verify + cache merge; stale-seq silent no-op) and `RevocationPushRateLimiter` (per-peer token bucket, burst 50 / 1 per sec defaults, injected clock for tests). Session-layer delivery wiring depends on 462.16 | **P1** | ~250 | `node/identity/propagate.rs` (new) | ✅ done (library layer) |
| 462.23 | **Integration test suite**: `sim/scenarios/identity.rs` — (a) rotate identity_sk, verify name/reputation survived; (b) revoke compromised identity_sk, verify peers reject frames signed by old key; (c) master_seed restore on fresh device; (d) 2-device messenger scenario (send to identity → both receive, both ACK', mailbox GC correct); (e) 3-node LB (`InstanceTag::Any` distributes by score); (f) name resolution end-to-end (publish claim → remote resolve → verify cert chain). **Implementation note**: library-layer slice ships — `node/identity/integration_tests.rs` covers 17 scenarios end-to-end using in-memory fakes; **plus one production-code end-to-end test** in `node/session/handshake.rs::sovereign_handshake_feeds_session_registry_end_to_end` that drives two real sovereign handshakes over duplex streams (using the same `SovereignHandshakeCtx` production uses), runs the exact `SessionEntry` insertion pattern `cache_peer_handshake_state` uses, then confirms `SessionRegistry::resolve_recipient` returns the right peer for `Any` / `Specific` / `All` tag variants in both directions — the first test that validates the entire sovereign-identity runtime pipeline (handshake → auth → index → routing resolution) as a single unit: onboarding→verify, BIP-39 device-wipe recovery, encrypted-file round-trip, multi-device fan-out messenger flow, pair-symmetric safety numbers, app-state sync across instances, chat-backup restore, revocation-push blocks leaked key, anomaly watcher catches attacker-added device, freshness lifecycle, instance-state persistence, RevocationCache on-disk, Alice+Bob full exchange (fan-out + sync), no-cross-identity-leakage, **plus 5 runtime-composition scenarios**: (a) two-nodes mutual IdentityProof exchange — each side generates X25519 ephemeral, signs proof binding identity_sk→ephemeral_pk, peer verifies, both derive identical shared secret, MITM-tamper sanity check (this is the library-level shape of the 462.16 handshake); (b) full pairing ceremony end-to-end — source signs invite, renders QR URI, target scans, `hash_pair_secret(scanned) == invite.pair_secret_hash`, both sides complete X25519 + derive identical 6-digit OOB, fake-QR tamper check (defeats QR phishing); (c) QR contact import cross-validated against full cert-chain verifier — Alice publishes `veil:identity` URI, Bob scans, URI-level `identity_id == BLAKE3(master_pubkey)` gate, then full `verify_identity_document` agrees on every field; (d) **multi-device mailbox delivery** — Alice drops a message into Bob's mailbox; Bob's laptop fetches+ACKs with its instance_id (queue stays intact since phone hasn't seen it); Bob's phone then fetches the same message, ACKs with its instance_id; `cleanup_expired` drops the message exactly once both instances have ACKed; re-fetch by either returns empty; (e) **laggard-instance wait** — laptop ACKs, phone fetches but doesn't ACK, cleanup keeps the message, phone finally ACKs, cleanup drops it. All 2245 lib tests pass single-threaded; clippy clean. **Real-TCP runtime slice shipped (first two scenarios)**: `sim/network.rs` — new `SimNetworkBuilder::sovereign_identities(true)` option provisions each node with a fresh sovereign identity (`create_identity` into a per-node veil subdir) *before* `SimNode::start`, so `NodeRuntime::start`'s canonical `SovereignIdentity::load_from_dir` probe auto-picks them up.  Requires `next_sim_config_path_with_dir` (per-node subdirectory, isolates `identity_document.bin` + `device_identity_sk.bin` + MLKEM keypair + revocation cache between sibling nodes) + `provision_sovereign_identity_for_sim` (wraps `create_identity` with `#[cfg(test)]` PoW difficulty, no master encryption). `.name_claims(Vec<Option<String>>)` builder option + `provision_name_claim_for_sim` helper sign+persist a `NameClaim` between `create_identity` and `SimNode::start` so the runtime's startup scan picks it up and `publish_name_claim`'s the claim into the local DHT.  `NodeRuntime::sovereign_identity()` accessor + `NodeRuntime::debug_republish_sovereign_identity()` helper — the latter re-publishes doc + registry + ML-KEM cert + persisted name claims into the local DHT, matching the initial-startup publish path; needed in sim because `connect()` calls `reload_with` which swaps `self.dht` to a fresh `KademliaService` and wipes the pre-reload store. `sim/scenarios.rs::sovereign_identity_two_nodes_establish_session` — first runtime-level scenario: 2-node full-mesh with sovereign identities, asserts distinct `identity_id` + distinct `active_instance_id` + `identity_id == compute_identity_id(master_pubkey)` binding on both sides.  `sovereign_identity_name_claim_publishes_locally` — second scenario: node 0 pre-claims `@alice`, after mesh formation + `debug_republish_sovereign_identity`, asserts node 0's local DHT holds the claim at the canonical `NameClaim::dht_key("alice")` slot with a decoded `identity_id` matching node 0's sovereign identity, and that node 1 does *not* hold the claim (separate DHT instances).  **Cross-node replication shipped**: `node/dispatcher/discovery.rs` — unsigned-STORE whitelist now accepts the four sovereign-identity magics (`NM` / `ID` / `IR` / `MC`) with a cheap decode-sanity check at ingest; full signature / PoW / master-cert-chain verification is deferred to the resolver path (`NameLookup::fetch_name_claim` + equivalent) since recipients often lack the IdentityDocument context when the STORE arrives.  Malformed magic-prefixed bodies still bounce as Violation (prevents poisoning via forged magic header).  `node/runtime.rs` — `is_self_authenticating_dht_value` extended in parallel so the periodic republish loop actually propagates sovereign records to K-closest peers.  5 dispatcher tests cover the new paths: valid NameClaim accept (fabricated via real `create_identity` + `sign_name_claim`) + 4 malformed-body rejects (magic + junk for each of NM/ID/IR/MC).  `sim/scenarios.rs::sovereign_identity_name_resolves_over_dht` — third real-TCP scenario: node 0 claims `@alice`, after mesh formation + `debug_republish_sovereign_identity` + `debug_force_dht_republish`, the replicated STORE lands on node 1's local DHT, decodes to the same NameClaim with `identity_id` matching node 0's sovereign identity.  This closes the cross-node resolution gap.  `sovereign_identity_all_records_cross_replicate` — fourth real-TCP scenario: asserts every sovereign record type (`IdentityDocument` `ID`, `InstanceRegistry` `IR`, `MlKemKeyCert` `MC`, `NameClaim` `NM`) lands on node 1's local DHT at its canonical key, each decodes cleanly, and the `identity_id` (and `instance_id` for `MC`) binding holds.  Guards against whitelist regressions where one magic is accidentally stripped.  `sovereign_identity_mailbox_put_over_tcp` — fifth scenario: node 0 dispatches a `MAILBOX_PUT` frame targeting node 1's sovereign `identity_id` via `NodeRuntime::debug_send_mailbox_put` (new test-only helper that builds the plaintext frame + pushes onto the session outbox; the runner's `apply_tx_cipher` handles encryption before the wire).  Bob's dispatcher routes the PUT through `handle_mailbox_put` into `MailboxService.handle_put`, and `debug_mailbox_total_entries()` flips from 0 to 1 within 5s.  Proves the full identity-addressed runtime pipeline (wire ingress → session decrypt → dispatcher route → mailbox persist) works end-to-end over real TCP.  `sovereign_identity_mailbox_fetch_and_ack_round_trip` — sixth scenario: extends the put flow with the recipient-side fetch+ACK round-trip.  Bob's runtime calls `debug_mailbox_fetch(identity_id, [0;16], 0)` (new helper that wraps `MailboxService.handle_fetch`), decodes the backlog entry, verifies `envelope.payload` matches the bytes Alice sent, then calls `debug_mailbox_ack(identity_id, [0;16], seqs)` — legacy pop-on-ack semantics drain the mailbox to zero.  Closes the minimal "Alice sends → Bob receives + ACKs" end-to-end messenger round-trip over real TCP.  `sovereign_identity_multi_device_fetch_per_instance_gc` — closes 462.23 sub-task (d) end-to-end: Alice puts one envelope to Bob's mailbox; Bob's two devices (modeled as distinct `instance_id` values) each `fetch` independently with their own per-instance cursors and see the same payload; the first device's `ack` advances ITS cursor only — `cleanup_expired` keeps the entry because the second device hasn't ACKed; after the second `ack`, `cleanup_expired` drops the entry and subsequent fetches by either instance return empty.  Validates the Epic 462.19 per-instance cursor design end-to-end at the runtime layer over real TCP — single sender, single store, multi-device fetch with correct GC.  New `debug_mailbox_cleanup_expired` test-only helper triggers the GC pass synchronously (production runs it from a background tick).  `sovereign_identity_recipient_any_picks_highest_scored_instance` — closes 462.23 sub-task (e): score-based `InstanceTag::Any` distributes load across two Alice devices.  New `SessionRegistry::peer_id_for_identity_scored<F>` accepts a per-instance scorer closure and returns the highest-scoring peer with deterministic tie-break on `(instance_id, peer_id)` byte order; NaN scores demote to -∞ so a real score always beats them.  4 unit tests (highest wins, tie-break determinism, unknown-identity returns None, NaN handling) + 3-node sim scenario where Charlie scores Alice's two devices opposite ways and confirms `Any` picks the high-scored instance both times — covering the sub-task (e) load-distribution invariant.  Production scorers compose reputation + RTT + battery + role; the test fixtures show the contract.  `sovereign_identity_rotate_with_revoke_propagates` — seventh scenario: node 0's on-disk document is rotated with `revoke_current = true` mid-scenario (fresh subkey, prior subkey appended to `revoked_keys`, `document_version` → 2, `revocation_seq` → 1, re-signed + PoW re-mined); the republish helper now reloads from disk on every call so the v2 doc propagates without waiting for the production 60 s mtime-poll.  Node 1's local DHT flips from v1 (no revocations) to v2 with `revoked_keys.len() == 1`; `identity_id` stays stable across the rotation (proving the master-pk-derived id is rotation-invariant).  New sim infrastructure: `provision_sovereign_identity_for_sim` stashes raw master_seed bytes at `master_seed.sim` so rotate/revoke scenarios can re-derive `master_sk` without BIP-39 / password-file fixtures (`sim_read_master_seed(veil_dir)` helper).  `sovereign_identity_name_survives_rotate_with_revoke` — eighth scenario: after the rotate-with-revoke, Alice re-signs `@alice` under the new active subkey and overwrites the persisted claim; the republish helper reloads + propagates both the v2 doc and the re-signed claim.  Bob's copy of the claim binds to the same stable `identity_id`, is signed under a `signing_identity_key_idx` that is *not* listed in Bob's copy of `revoked_keys` (verified via `pubkey_hash` lookup), and the old pre-rotate subkey IS listed as revoked.  Closes the "name survives key rotation" invariant from the 462.23 sub-task (a) spec.  `sovereign_identity_old_claim_rejected_after_revoke` — ninth scenario, the security-inverse of the eighth: Alice rotates-with-revoke but INTENTIONALLY skips re-signing `@alice`.  Bob holds the pre-rotate claim (signed under subkey 0) plus the v2 document (subkey 0 revoked).  Manual `pubkey_hash` lookup against `revoked_keys` — the exact predicate `resolver::verify_name_claim` runs at resolve time — confirms the claim's `signing_identity_key_idx` is revoked, i.e., any resolver surfaces `NameClaimSigKeyRevoked` rather than trusting the signature.  Symmetric positive check asserts the new active sig_key_idx is NOT revoked, so a properly re-signed claim (the scenario 8 path) would still verify.  Closes 462.23 sub-task (b): "revoke compromised identity_sk, verify peers reject frames signed by old key" at the NameClaim layer.  `sovereign_identity_master_seed_restore_preserves_identity_id` — tenth scenario: closes 462.23 sub-task (c).  New `SimNetworkBuilder::restored_from(Vec<Option<usize>>)` option provisions opted-in nodes via `restore_identity` against another node's previously-saved `master_seed` (read from `master_seed.sim`).  In the test, node 1 is restored from node 0's seed: both nodes report the same `identity_id` + same `master_pubkey` (master-pk-derived → rotation-invariant → restore-invariant), but distinct `active_instance_id` (each device mints its own random tag at provisioning time) and distinct `identity_sk` subkey pubkeys (restore generates a fresh per-device subkey rather than transferring the source's).  Both subkeys are non-revoked on their local docs.  Session-layer node_ids still differ — proves session addressing remains independent of sovereign identity, so multiple devices sharing one identity can each maintain their own TCP sessions.  Mirrors the production "user lost device, recovers from BIP-39 paper backup" flow.  `sovereign_identity_pairing_ceremony_propagates_to_peer` — eleventh scenario: full pair ceremony e2e (Epic 462.30) integrated with the sim mesh.  Alice runs `PairingSource` on a free loopback port (loaded from her veil_dir's identity_sk + the sim master_seed); a side-channel `PairingTarget` dials it via real TCP and runs the full ceremony — both sides converge on the OOB code, target generates a fresh ed25519 SK + X25519 ephemeral, Alice master-certifies `target_pk`.  Alice's v2 IdentityDocument (with the appended IdentityKey) is persisted; the target's paired state is persisted via `save_paired_target_state` and the resulting veil_dir loads cleanly via `SovereignIdentity::load_from_dir` with the sig_key_idx override pointing at the new subkey + matching `identity_id`.  Alice republishes; Charlie (the bystander mesh peer) receives the v2 doc with both subkeys present, and Charlie's view of the appended subkey's pubkey matches the target's loaded subkey.  Closes the "new device pairs in and is visible to peers via the existing mesh" e2e proof — the sovereign-identity messenger's onboarding flow now works end-to-end on real TCP from QR scan through DHT propagation.  Remaining 462.23 follow-up scenarios (identity-addressed mailbox delivery + multi-instance LB + revocation propagation + pairing ceremony e2e) layer on this whitelist.  **All 6 sub-tasks closed**: (a) rotate+name-survives (scenarios 7 + 8), (b) revoke rejects old-sig (scenario 9), (c) master_seed restore on fresh device (scenario 10), (d) multi-device messenger with per-instance cursors (scenario 13), (e) score-based `InstanceTag::Any` LB (scenario 14 + 4 unit tests), (f) name resolution end-to-end (scenarios 2 + 3).  14 real-TCP sim scenarios total + 17 library-layer integration tests + 5 runtime-composition library tests + multi-instance fan-out bug closed via the `peer_sovereign_identities` cache. 2283 lib tests, clippy clean | **P0** | ~600 | `node/identity/integration_tests.rs`, `sim/network.rs` (`sovereign_identities` + `name_claims` + `restored_from` + per-node veil dir + `sim_read_master_seed`), `sim/scenarios.rs` (14 `sovereign_identity_*` scenarios), `node/runtime.rs` (`sovereign_identity()` accessor + `debug_republish_sovereign_identity()` + `debug_send_mailbox_put` / `_fetch` / `_ack` / `_cleanup_expired` + `debug_resolve_recipient` / `_any_scored` + `peer_sovereign_identities` cache + sovereign magics in `is_self_authenticating_dht_value`), `node/dispatcher/discovery.rs` (sovereign STORE whitelist), `node/dispatcher/mod.rs` (sovereign STORE tests), `node/session/manager.rs` (`peer_id_for_identity_scored`, `identity_id_for_peer`, `debug_identity_instance_keys`) | ✅ done |
